profile.domain: Marketing landing page for a residential solar installation company targeting homeowners in the US Sun Belt

project_goal.value: Convert Sun Belt homeowners into qualified solar installation leads via savings calculators and regional installer routing
project_goal.rationale: Core business objective is lead generation and installer matching

target_users.value: US Sun Belt homeowners aged 35-65 evaluating residential solar installation
target_users.rationale: Primary audience identified in market research

project_class.rationale: Static marketing page with no backend runtime required

capabilities.rationale: Only client-side rendering and analytics tracking needed

complexity_level.rationale: Single-page static site with embedded calculator and form

risk_level.rationale: No sensitive data processed on the page itself

data_sensitivity.rationale: No personal or financial data stored on this surface

external_exposure.rationale: Public marketing page accessible without authentication

access_model.value: Public, no authentication required
access_model.rationale: Landing page must be accessible to all potential leads

feature_scope.value: Solar savings calculator, financing options display, lead capture form with regional installer routing, testimonials, promo offers
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for conversion optimization

mvp_scope.value: Savings calculator, basic lead form, and installer region routing
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum set to test conversion hypothesis

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced, CSP headers, no sensitive data in client JS
security_baseline.rationale: Standard web security for public pages

frontend_stack.rationale: SSG output with React islands for calculator interactivity

backend_stack.rationale: No server-side runtime; forms post to third-party CRM webhook

data_platform.value: None - no database
data_platform.rationale: All lead data captured by CRM, not stored locally

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network
hosting_target.rationale: Global CDN with instant deploys and edge caching

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data retained on Vercel; CRM handles retention policy
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Data controller responsibility delegated to CRM provider

future_scope.value: A/B testing framework, personalized savings estimates by utility rate zone

constraints.value: No backend server, form submissions handled via third-party webhooks, Vercel free tier initially
constraints.rationale: Budget and operational simplicity constraints

observability_baseline.value: Vercel Analytics for page performance and visitor metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: Lightweight analytics sufficient for static landing page

execution_preference.value: Continuous deployment from main branch via GitHub Actions
execution_preference.rationale: Fast iteration on copy and layout changes

project.goal: Generate qualified solar installation leads from Sun Belt homeowners
project.target_users: US homeowners in AZ, TX, FL, NM evaluating solar ROI

backend.framework: Static site generation via next export
backend.api: None - form POST to HubSpot webhook

security.auth: None required
security.csp: Content-Security-Policy blocking inline scripts except calculator

devops.environments: preview per PR, production on main merge

open_questions.routing: Confirm installer region lookup uses zip code to region mapping JSON bundled in build

reasoner_reviews.product: The savings calculator is the core conversion driver — accurate payback period and estimated annual savings projections will directly impact lead quality. Financing option display should surface the most competitive rate tier prominently rather than listing all options equally.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js static export with React islands is the right call here. The zip-to-installer-region mapping should be bundled as a static JSON file at build time to eliminate any runtime API dependency. The lead form must POST directly to the HubSpot webhook with no server intermediary.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP headers must explicitly block third-party script injection while allowing Vercel Analytics and HubSpot form scripts. No personal data should land in browser localStorage or sessionStorage.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Vercel free tier bandwidth is adequate for a new landing page. GitHub Actions CI with next build and Lighthouse CI score gates ensures page performance stays above 90 on mobile.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan had thin observability — Vercel Analytics alone is insufficient for conversion funnel analysis. GTM event layer tracking calculator interactions and form partial completions must be added.

backend.service_design: No server-side runtime. All dynamic behavior is client-side React. Form submissions route directly to HubSpot CRM webhook via fetch POST from the browser.
backend.api_patterns: No API. Zip-to-installer-region resolution uses a bundled JSON lookup table updated at build time.
backend.failure_handling: Form submission errors display inline user feedback. Network failures trigger a retry with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts before showing fallback contact email.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 static export with page router. Single index page with scroll-linked sections: hero, savings calculator, financing options, testimonials, lead form, regional coverage map.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Hero with CTA to calculator, calculator section (zip code + monthly bill inputs producing savings estimate and payback period), financing carousel, social proof section, lead capture form with installer region pre-fill based on zip.
frontend.state_management: React useState for calculator inputs and financing selection. No global state manager needed.
frontend.performance_notes: next/image for all photos. Lazy-load testimonial section. Lighthouse CI mobile score gate at 90+ in CI pipeline.
frontend.frontend_risks: Calculator accuracy risk if utility rate data embedded in build becomes stale — must document refresh cadence.

security.auth_design: No authentication. Public page.
security.authorization_model: No authorization model. All content publicly readable.
security.abuse_prevention: HubSpot webhook rate limiting handles form spam. Honeypot field in lead form to catch bots. reCAPTCHA v3 optional for high-spam periods.
security.privacy_controls: No cookies set by first-party code. Vercel Analytics uses cookieless tracking. HubSpot script governed by privacy policy link.

data.entities: lead_submission (captured by HubSpot), savings_estimate (ephemeral client state), financing_option (static content), installer_region (static JSON), promo_offer (static content)
data.storage_design: No database. All static content in Git. Lead data owned by HubSpot CRM.
data.analytics_events: calculator_started, calculator_completed (with zip and estimated_savings), financing_option_viewed, form_started, form_submitted, form_error
data.retention_and_deletion: No data retained in Vercel infrastructure. HubSpot retention policy applies to leads.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel Edge Network with global CDN. next build outputs static HTML/CSS/JS. Zero runtime servers.
devops.environments: Preview deployments on every PR branch. Production on main merge. No staging environment needed for static page.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: install deps (bun install), lint (eslint), build (next build), Lighthouse CI mobile score check, Vercel CLI deploy.
devops.observability_stack: Vercel Analytics dashboard for Core Web Vitals and visitor stats. GTM with custom event layer for calculator and form funnel.
devops.rollback_strategy: Vercel instant rollback to previous deployment via dashboard or CLI.

ISS-001.title: No conversion funnel analytics beyond page views
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan had no GTM event tracking. Round 2 added Vercel Analytics but no granular funnel events for calculator and form interactions.

history.note: Vercel Analytics added but GTM events still missing

ISS-002.title: Stale utility rate data risk in embedded calculator
ISS-002.detail: The savings estimate relies on utility rate data bundled at build time. No refresh cadence or staleness warning has been specified.

focus_issues.title: Stale utility rate data risk in embedded calculator
focus_issues.detail: The savings estimate relies on utility rate data bundled at build time. No refresh cadence or staleness warning has been specified.

latest_recommendations: Add explicit documentation of utility rate data refresh cadence and a build-time data freshness check

previous_plan.title: Sun Belt Solar Landing Page v2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Next.js 14 static landing page on Vercel for residential solar lead generation across Sun Belt states, featuring a client-side savings calculator, financing options display, and HubSpot-integrated lead form with installer region routing.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Pure static export on Vercel Edge. React islands for calculator interactivity. No backend runtime.
previous_plan.technology_stack: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Vercel Analytics, GTM, HubSpot webhook.
previous_plan.data_model: No database. lead_submission, savings_estimate, financing_option, installer_region, promo_offer all static or CRM-managed.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a round 3 revision of a simple solar marketing landing page. The only unresolved issue is ISS-002 regarding stale utility rate data embedded at build time. The fix is straightforward: add a build-time data freshness assertion and document the quarterly rate data refresh process. The architecture is already well-specified as a Next.js static export with no backend runtime. Quality target is moderate — all sections should be solid with this final refinement addressed.

fix_report.action_taken: Added quarterly utility rate data refresh process documentation, build-time freshness check asserting rate file modified_at is within 90 days, and a UI staleness warning shown when estimated_rate_date is more than 60 days old
fix_report.expected_outcome: Calculator accuracy is maintained through documented refresh cadence and build pipeline will fail if rate data is stale before reaching production

target_output.title: Sun Belt Solar Lead Generation Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 statically exported landing page on Vercel Edge Network targeting US Sun Belt homeowners evaluating residential solar installation. Features a client-side savings calculator using bundled utility rate data, a financing options display, and a HubSpot-integrated lead capture form that routes qualified leads to regional installer partners. No backend runtime — all dynamic behavior runs in the browser.
target_output.architecture_overview: Pure static site architecture: Next.js 14 generates static HTML, CSS, and JavaScript at build time and deploys to Vercel Edge Network for global CDN delivery. The solar savings calculator runs entirely in the browser using zip code inputs to look up bundled installer regions and utility rate data. Lead form submissions POST directly to a HubSpot CRM webhook via client-side fetch. No server-side runtime exists — the only server interaction is Vercel's CDN serving static assets. A GitHub Actions pipeline enforces Lighthouse CI mobile score gates before each production deploy.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 with TypeScript, static export mode, Tailwind CSS for styling, React useState for calculator and form state. No backend framework or runtime. Analytics: Vercel Analytics (cookieless Core Web Vitals), Google Tag Manager with custom event layer for funnel tracking. Forms: HubSpot Forms API webhook. Build tooling: Bun as package manager, ESLint, Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions. Hosting: Vercel Edge Network with automatic preview deployments per PR.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Solar Savings Calculator: user enters zip code and average monthly electricity bill; client-side logic looks up utility rate from bundled JSON and calculates estimated annual savings, 25-year cumulative savings, and payback period in years. Financing Options Display: renders three financing tiers (cash purchase, solar loan, lease/PPA) with estimated monthly cost and ownership structure. Installer Region Routing: zip-to-region JSON maps entries to one of five Sun Belt installer partner territories. Lead Capture Form: name, email, phone, zip, and home ownership checkbox; submits to HubSpot with installer_region pre-filled. Promo Offer Banner: time-limited offer display driven by static config.
target_output.system_components: Vercel Edge CDN nodes serving pre-built HTML/CSS/JS bundles globally. Next.js static export build artifact stored in Vercel deployment. Bundled JSON data files: zip-to-installer-region map, utility-rate-by-state table (quarterly refresh), financing-options config. HubSpot CRM as the lead data sink receiving webhook POSTs. Google Tag Manager container managing analytics event firing. GitHub Actions CI runner executing build, lint, and Lighthouse checks.
target_output.workflows: Savings Estimation: homeowner enters AZ zip code 85001 and $180 monthly bill; client JS resolves state=AZ, looks up APS utility rate of $0.127/kWh from bundled rate table, estimates 7.2kW system size, calculates $1,560 annual savings and 8.2-year payback period, displays result inline without page reload. Lead Submission: after reviewing savings estimate, homeowner fills lead form; on submit, client JS appends installer_region=Southwest_AZ derived from zip lookup, POSTs JSON payload to HubSpot webhook endpoint, displays thank-you message and triggers GTM form_submitted event with estimated_savings value attached for CRM segmentation.
target_output.data_model: All data is static or CRM-managed. savings_estimate: ephemeral browser state (zip, monthly_bill_usd, state, utility_rate_per_kwh, estimated_system_kw, annual_savings_usd, payback_years, estimated_rate_date). financing_option: static content (type ENUM cash/loan/lease_ppa, monthly_cost_usd, ownership, key_benefits[]). installer_region: static JSON (zip_prefix, region_name, installer_partner, phone). lead_submission: captured by HubSpot (name, email, phone, zip, home_owner, installer_region, estimated_savings, source_utm). promo_offer: static config (headline, expires_at, badge_text). Rate file includes modified_at timestamp asserted by build script.
target_output.api_design: No server API. External integrations: POST https://api.hsforms.com/submissions/v3/integration/submit/{portalId}/{formId} — payload includes all lead fields plus hidden installer_region and estimated_savings fields. Vercel Analytics events via @vercel/analytics SDK. GTM dataLayer.push events: {event: 'calculator_completed', zip, estimated_savings_usd, payback_years} and {event: 'form_submitted', installer_region, lead_score_tier}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Content-Security-Policy header configured in next.config.js: default-src 'self', script-src allowing Vercel Analytics, GTM, and HubSpot scripts by explicit domain allowlist. HTTPS enforced by Vercel for all requests. No cookies set by first-party code. Honeypot hidden field in lead form to reject automated submissions. No personal data written to localStorage or sessionStorage. Privacy policy link in footer references HubSpot as data processor for lead information.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline on every push: bun install, eslint --max-warnings 0, next build, Lighthouse CI with mobile performance score gate of 90 and accessibility gate of 95, then Vercel CLI deploy to preview URL. On merge to main: same pipeline deploys to production. Build-time assertion script checks that utility rate data file modified_at is within 90 days; build fails with an explicit error message if rate data is stale. Quarterly rate data refresh: sustainability team updates src/data/utility-rates.json from EIA API, opens PR, CI validates freshness, merges to trigger production deploy. Rollback: Vercel dashboard instant rollback to prior deployment in under 30 seconds.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics dashboard monitors Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, FID) per deployment and by geographic region. GTM custom event layer fires calculator_started, calculator_completed, financing_option_viewed, form_started, form_submitted, and form_error events into GA4 for conversion funnel analysis. Lighthouse CI reports stored as GitHub Actions artifacts for each PR. Alert: Vercel email notification if deployment fails or error rate on static asset serving exceeds 1%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Hobby tier for initial launch: zero cost for static hosting and analytics up to 100GB bandwidth. Upgrade to Pro ($20/month) if traffic exceeds hobby limits. No database, no compute costs. HubSpot CRM cost is separate and team-managed. Scaling is inherent to Vercel Edge CDN with no configuration required.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (2 weeks): hero section, savings calculator with bundled AZ/TX/FL/NM utility rates, basic lead form with HubSpot webhook, installer region routing JSON, Vercel deployment with CSP headers, Lighthouse CI gate. Phase 2 Optimization (4 weeks post-launch): GTM event layer with full funnel tracking, financing options carousel, testimonials section, promo offer banner with expiry logic. Phase 3 Personalization (future): A/B testing via Vercel Edge Config, personalized savings estimates by utility rate zone using updated EIA data feeds.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode enforced. ESLint with next/recommended ruleset, zero warnings policy in CI. Tailwind CSS class ordering enforced by prettier-plugin-tailwindcss. Lighthouse CI score gates: mobile performance 90+, accessibility 95+. Utility rate data file must include modified_at field; build script asserts freshness. Component co-location: calculator logic in src/features/calculator/, form logic in src/features/lead-form/. No third-party analytics scripts added without security review of CSP implications.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: utility rate data in bundled JSON becomes stale between quarterly refreshes, causing inaccurate savings estimates. Mitigation: build-time freshness assertion blocks deploys with data older than 90 days; UI displays estimated_rate_date with note 'rates updated quarterly'. Risk: HubSpot webhook downtime causes lead form submissions to fail silently. Mitigation: client-side retry with exponential backoff and fallback email display after 3 failed attempts. Tradeoff: no server-side form handling means no server-side validation or spam filtering beyond honeypot; acceptable given low sensitivity of pre-qualification data.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed zip-to-installer-region mapping bundled as static JSON at build time — no runtime API call needed. Confirmed HubSpot as the CRM and lead data controller. Confirmed Vercel Analytics as the primary observability tool with GTM supplementing funnel event tracking. Rate data refresh cadence set to quarterly with build-time freshness enforcement.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision resolving utility rate data staleness risk for solar landing page. Static architecture well-specified.

project_goal.value: Web application for small municipal water utilities to track infrastructure maintenance schedules, report water quality test results, and manage regulatory submission deadlines
project_goal.rationale: Core operational need replacing spreadsheets and paper logs

target_users.value: Utility operators, field technicians, supervisors, and compliance administrators at small municipal water systems
target_users.rationale: Primary end users identified in stakeholder interviews

project_class.rationale: Browser-based operational tool with admin panel, no native mobile requirements

capabilities.rationale: Full-featured operational web app with role-based administration

complexity_level.rationale: CRUD-heavy with compliance workflow logic and calendar scheduling

risk_level.rationale: Internal operational data with regulatory reporting obligations

data_sensitivity.rationale: Water quality and infrastructure data is internal but not classified as personal or financial

external_exposure.rationale: All access requires authenticated utility staff login

access_model.value: RBAC with roles: operator, field_tech, supervisor, compliance_admin, system_admin
access_model.rationale: Different staff levels need different data access and action permissions

feature_scope.value: Infrastructure asset registry, maintenance task scheduling, water quality test logging with MCL checks, regulatory submission deadline calendar, compliance document uploads, staff user management
feature_scope.rationale: Full operational scope for small water utility management

mvp_scope.value: Asset registry, maintenance scheduling, water quality test entry with EPA MCL flag detection, and deadline calendar view
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable to replace current spreadsheet workflows

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, bcrypt password hashing, session-based auth with 8-hour expiry, CSRF protection
security_baseline.rationale: Standard secure web baseline for internal operational system

frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise and component ecosystem

backend_stack.rationale: Lightweight REST API matching team skillset

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16
data_platform.rationale: Relational data with strong referential integrity for compliance records

hosting_target.value: DigitalOcean App Platform
hosting_target.rationale: Cost-effective managed hosting within small utility budget

privacy_retention_policy.value: Water quality test records retained 10 years per EPA SDWA requirements, maintenance logs retained 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: EPA Safe Drinking Water Act record-keeping mandate

future_scope.value: Mobile app for field technicians, automated EPA eDWR submission integration, public-facing water quality report page

constraints.value: Budget under $400/month hosting, must support 20-50 concurrent users, single-region deployment
constraints.rationale: Small utility budget and low concurrency requirements

observability_baseline.value: New Relic APM, structured logging, email alerts for MCL exceedance detection failures
observability_baseline.rationale: New Relic is already licensed for the utility's IT systems

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery with 2-week sprints, demo to utility manager every 4 weeks
execution_preference.rationale: Utility prefers incremental delivery to validate workflows before full rollout

compliance_context.value: EPA Safe Drinking Water Act (SDWA) reporting, state-level water quality regulations, Consumer Confidence Report (CCR) annual publication requirements
compliance_context.rationale: Risk level medium plus internal data sensitivity requires compliance tracking

project.goal: Track infrastructure maintenance, water quality tests, and regulatory deadlines for small municipal water utilities
project.target_users: Utility operators and compliance staff

backend.framework: Express with Zod validation

security.auth: Auth0 with RBAC roles

open_questions.mobile_access: Field technicians may need mobile access - deferred to Phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: Core workflows around water quality test logging and deadline tracking directly replace error-prone spreadsheet processes. The MCL exceedance auto-flagging is the highest-value feature for compliance staff.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Express on Node 20 is adequate for 20-50 concurrent users on App Platform. No caching layer needed at this scale. PostgreSQL referential integrity is important for linking test results to sampling sites and assets.
reasoner_reviews.security: Auth0 RBAC covers the five defined roles. CSRF protection is required for form submissions. Audit logging of who entered which test result is important for regulatory defensibility.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: $400/month hosting budget is achievable on App Platform with a managed PostgreSQL add-on. The small user count does not require auto-scaling at MVP.

backend.service_design: Single Express app with domain modules: assets, maintenance, water_quality, regulatory, users. Each module owns its routes, controllers, and service layer.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful JSON with versioned routes under /api/v1. Zod schemas for request validation on all write endpoints.
backend.failure_handling: Global Express error handler with structured error responses. Postgres connection pool with retry on startup.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with React Router v6, lazy-loaded route modules for assets, quality, scheduling, and admin sections.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login, Asset Registry, Maintenance Calendar, Water Quality Test Entry, MCL Exceedance Review, Regulatory Deadline Calendar, Admin User Management.
frontend.state_management: React Query for all server state, minimal local state for form drafts.

security.auth_design: Auth0 SPA SDK with PKCE flow. JWTs validated by Express middleware on all protected routes.
security.authorization_model: Five RBAC roles enforced at route level. Compliance_admin and system_admin can access all data. Operators and field_techs scoped to their assigned water system.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Append-only audit_log table recording user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, and timestamp for all test result entries and regulatory submissions.

data.entities: water_system, infrastructure_asset, maintenance_task, water_quality_test, sampling_site, mcl_standard, regulatory_submission, compliance_deadline, audit_log
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on DigitalOcean Managed DB with daily automated backups. File uploads for compliance documents stored in DigitalOcean Spaces.
data.retention_and_deletion: 10-year retention for water quality test records. 5-year retention for maintenance logs. Archived records flagged but not deleted.

devops.deployment_topology: DigitalOcean App Platform with separate components for React static build and Node API service. Managed PostgreSQL add-on.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint, test, build on PR. Deploy to production on merge to main. Database migrations run via node-pg-migrate as a pre-deploy step.
devops.observability_stack: New Relic APM agent on Node service. Structured JSON logs shipped to New Relic Logs. Alert on MCL exceedance detection errors and p99 latency >3s.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a municipal water utility compliance web app on DigitalOcean. Key decisions: single Express app with domain modules sufficient for 20-50 users, Auth0 RBAC for the five-role model, PostgreSQL for referential integrity between test results and sampling sites. Keeping architecture simple to match small utility budget and team capacity. Weak quality row - some sections are thin.
target_output.title: Municipal Water Utility Compliance Tracker
target_output.executive_summary: A web application for small municipal water utilities built on React 18 and Node.js 20 Express, hosted on DigitalOcean App Platform. Enables operators to log water quality test results with automatic EPA MCL exceedance detection, manage infrastructure maintenance schedules, and track regulatory submission deadlines via a compliance calendar.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-page React 18 application communicating with a Node.js 20 Express REST API on DigitalOcean App Platform. PostgreSQL 16 on managed database service stores assets, test results, and compliance records. Auth0 handles authentication with RBAC for five staff roles. File attachments for regulatory documents stored in DigitalOcean Spaces. New Relic APM monitors API performance. No caching layer required at the 20-50 concurrent user scale.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, React Router v6. Backend: Node.js 20, Express, Zod request validation, node-pg-migrate. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on DigitalOcean Managed DB. Auth: Auth0 with RBAC. Storage: DigitalOcean Spaces for compliance document uploads. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Monitoring: New Relic APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Infrastructure Asset Registry for water mains, pumps, treatment units, and sampling sites with maintenance history. Maintenance Task Scheduler with due date tracking and technician assignment. Water Quality Test Entry with automatic comparison against EPA MCL lookup table and exceedance flagging. Regulatory Submission Deadline Calendar with assigned responsible staff and document upload. Admin panel for staff user management and role assignment.
target_output.system_components: DigitalOcean App Platform serving React static build and Node Express API as separate components. PostgreSQL 16 managed database. DigitalOcean Spaces for document storage. Auth0 tenant for authentication. New Relic APM agent on the API service.
target_output.workflows: Water Quality Test Entry: Field tech logs test result for a sampling site, selecting contaminant and entering measured concentration. System queries mcl_standard table for the pollutant and compares value; if concentration exceeds MCL, status set to exceedance and supervisor notified via email. Regulatory Deadline Management: Compliance admin opens deadline calendar, selects an upcoming SDWA submission, assigns responsible staff member, uploads draft document to DigitalOcean Spaces, and marks submission as complete. Deadline status updates propagate to dashboard.
target_output.data_model: water_system (id, name, pwsid, state, tier, created_at). infrastructure_asset (id, water_system_id, asset_type ENUM main/pump/treatment/storage/sampling_site, label, install_date, last_inspection_date). maintenance_task (id, asset_id, description, scheduled_date, assigned_tech_id, status ENUM pending/in_progress/complete). water_quality_test (id, sampling_site_id, contaminant_code, measured_value, unit, tested_by_id, tested_at, mcl_status ENUM pass/exceedance/pending_review). mcl_standard (contaminant_code PK, contaminant_name, mcl_value, unit, regulatory_basis). regulatory_submission (id, water_system_id, submission_type, due_date, assigned_to_id, status ENUM upcoming/in_progress/submitted/overdue, document_url). compliance_deadline (id, water_system_id, deadline_type, due_date, notes). audit_log (id, user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, timestamp).
target_output.api_design: RESTful under /api/v1. GET /assets?water_system_id= (asset list), POST /assets (create asset), GET /maintenance-tasks?asset_id= (task list), POST /maintenance-tasks (schedule task), PATCH /maintenance-tasks/:id (update status), POST /water-quality/tests (log test result), GET /water-quality/tests?site_id=&from=&to= (test history), GET /water-quality/exceedances (open exceedances), GET /regulatory/deadlines?water_system_id= (deadline list), PATCH /regulatory/submissions/:id (mark submitted). Auth0 JWT required on all routes.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth0 PKCE flow for SPA authentication. Five RBAC roles enforced at Express route middleware level. CSRF tokens on all state-changing form submissions. Append-only audit_log for test entries and regulatory submissions for SDWA defensibility. TLS 1.3 enforced at App Platform edge. DigitalOcean Spaces bucket policy restricts access to authenticated API service only. EPA Safe Drinking Water Act record retention enforced via soft-delete with retention date field.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: ESLint, Jest unit tests, React build on PR. On merge to main: run node-pg-migrate migrations, deploy Node API component, deploy React static build to App Platform. DigitalOcean Managed DB with automated daily backups retained 7 days. Single production environment - no staging at MVP given budget constraints. Rollback by redeploying previous container image via App Platform UI.
target_output.observability: New Relic APM on Node API with transaction traces for test entry and exceedance detection routes. Structured JSON logs with user_id and water_system_id context. Alerts: MCL exceedance detection function error rate >0%, API p99 latency >3s, database connection failures. Email notifications to compliance admin on new exceedance detection.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $280/month: App Platform Basic ($25 API + $5 static), Managed PostgreSQL Basic ($15), DigitalOcean Spaces ($5), Auth0 free tier (up to 7500 MAU), New Relic free tier. Well within $400/month budget. Scaling: upgrade to App Platform Professional tier if concurrent users exceed 50 in future.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Asset registry with infrastructure types, maintenance task scheduling, water quality test entry with MCL auto-check, regulatory deadline calendar, Auth0 RBAC for all five roles, GitHub Actions CI/CD. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Compliance document uploads to Spaces, exceedance review workflow with supervisor sign-off, Consumer Confidence Report template generator. Phase 3: Mobile field app for technicians, EPA eDWR automated submission integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode on frontend and backend. Zod schemas co-located with route handlers. Jest unit tests for MCL comparison logic and RBAC middleware. PR requires one approval before merge. node-pg-migrate for all schema changes - no manual DDL.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: No staging environment at MVP - production incidents harder to diagnose. Mitigation: Feature flags for new workflows. Risk: Auth0 free tier limit of 7500 MAU - larger utilities would exceed this. Mitigation: Budget for Developer plan if utility grows. Tradeoff: No caching layer simplifies ops but limits future API response time improvements if user count grows beyond 50.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose App Platform over Droplets for managed runtime and automatic TLS. Selected Auth0 over self-hosted Keycloak given small team. PostgreSQL over SQLite for multi-user concurrency. MCL lookup table approach chosen over hard-coded constants for maintainability.

project_goal.value: Modernize legacy SCADA-connected emissions monitoring by replacing flat-file exports with a queryable REST JSON API for environmental compliance officers and third-party auditors
project_goal.rationale: Core modernization objective replacing error-prone FTP flat-file transfers with structured API access

target_users.value: Environmental compliance officers and third-party environmental auditors
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of emissions data requiring structured access for regulatory reporting

project_class.rationale: Pure API backend with no browser UI required; consumers integrate programmatically

capabilities.rationale: API service with data ingestion batches, OAuth2 auth, and partner-facing endpoints

complexity_level.rationale: SCADA integration, legacy flat-file parsing, EPA compliance rules, and partner API surface add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Regulatory reporting accuracy and confidential industrial facility data require high risk controls

data_sensitivity.rationale: Facility emissions data is commercially sensitive and subject to regulatory confidentiality requirements

external_exposure.rationale: API accessed by credentialed third-party auditors and compliance officers, not the general public

access_model.value: OAuth2 client credentials for partner systems, API key fallback for legacy auditor tools, mTLS for SCADA ingest endpoint
access_model.rationale: Partner integrations require machine-to-machine OAuth2; mTLS secures the SCADA ingest boundary

feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set required for EPA Clean Air Act reporting workflow replacement

mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum needed to replace manual FTP-to-spreadsheet workflow for pilot facilities

security_baseline.value: mTLS for SCADA ingest, OAuth2 client credentials for API consumers, AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 for all API traffic, no data stored in logs
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential industrial data and partner-facing exposure require defense-in-depth

backend_stack.rationale: Team expertise in Java ecosystem; Spring Batch is mature for scheduled file processing

data_platform.rationale: Relational model suits normalized pollutant and facility data; Redis handles high-throughput ingest deduplication

hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers, no EC2 patching overhead; RDS for managed HA and automated backups

project.goal: Replace SCADA flat-file exports with queryable emissions JSON API
project.target_users: Environmental compliance officers and third-party auditors

backend.framework: Spring Boot 3 with Spring Batch and Spring Security

security.auth: OAuth2 client credentials, mTLS for SCADA ingest endpoint

data.cache: Redis 7 for rate limiting and idempotency

constraints.scada: Must parse legacy CSV and fixed-width flat files without device changes

open_questions.realtime: Real-time MQTT ingest deferred to post-MVP

reasoner_reviews.product: Scope is well-bounded for MVP with 3 pollutant types and core facility query endpoints. Replacing FTP flat-file transfers is the highest-value deliverable. Auditor query pagination is critical for large date ranges spanning multiple reporting periods.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Spring Boot 3 with Spring Batch is a natural fit for scheduled flat-file ingest. The fixed-width parser requires careful column mapping per SCADA device model. Recommend a facility-scoped partition strategy in the batch jobs to enable parallel ingest for multi-facility deployments. The read path for auditor queries should use a dedicated read replica to isolate from ingest write load.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS on the SCADA ingest endpoint is non-negotiable given the industrial boundary. OAuth2 client credentials flow is correct for machine-to-machine partner access. Emissions records must never appear in application logs -- field-level log masking is required. Audit log entries must be append-only and stored in a separate schema with restricted write access.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Jenkins is already operational which reduces CI setup cost. Budget of $3000/month supports Fargate plus RDS Multi-AZ. The 10-year retention requirement for emissions records demands a tiered storage strategy -- hot data on RDS, cold data archived to S3 with Parquet format for cost efficiency.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The plan has a notable flaw: the data_model section uses generic entity names and omits the compliance_threshold entity entirely, which is central to the EPA flag annotation workflow. The api_design section also lacks the compliance flag field in the response schema example, breaking alignment with the feature scope. These omissions create contract violations.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot 3 monolith with domain packages: ingest (flat-file parsing, SCADA adapter), emissions (reading normalization, threshold evaluation), facilities (facility registry, reporting periods), audit (append-only event log), api (REST controllers, OpenAPI spec)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful v1 under /api/v1, OpenAPI 3.1 spec auto-generated via SpringDoc, versioned via URL path, paginated responses using cursor-based pagination for large emissions datasets
backend.failure_handling: Dead-letter S3 prefix for malformed flat files, Spring Batch job restart from last checkpoint on failure, idempotency keys in Redis to prevent duplicate emissions_reading inserts on retry
backend.backend_risks: Fixed-width SCADA format variations across device models may require per-facility column mapping configuration -- plan for a facility_parser_config table to avoid hardcoded column offsets
backend.background_jobs: Spring Batch jobs: ScadaFlatFileIngestJob (scheduled nightly, reads from S3 drop bucket, parses CSV and fixed-width via ItemReaders, validates pollutant codes against NAAQS pollutant registry, upserts emissions_readings), ReconciliationJob (weekly, cross-checks ingested totals against SCADA device checksums)

security.auth_design: Cognito OAuth2 authorization server issuing client_credentials tokens for partner systems, token introspection endpoint for API gateway validation, mTLS certificate pinning on /ingest/scada endpoint
security.authorization_model: Scope-based: emissions:read for auditors, emissions:write for ingest service account, admin:all for internal ops, facility-scoped claims to restrict auditors to contracted facilities
security.secrets_management: AWS Secrets Manager for DB credentials and OAuth2 client secrets, 30-day rotation, no secrets in environment variables or flat files
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_events table in separate PostgreSQL schema with INSERT-only grants, logs: user_id, facility_id, action, endpoint, ip_address, timestamp; CloudTrail for AWS API actions; all application logs field-masked to exclude pollutant values

data.entities: facilities (id, name, naics_code, epa_facility_id, state, region, active), emissions_readings (id, facility_id, pollutant_type_id FK, reading_value NUMERIC, unit, measurement_period_start, measurement_period_end, source_file_hash, compliance_flag BOOL, ingested_at), pollutant_types (id, code VARCHAR, name, naaqs_standard, unit), compliance_thresholds (id, facility_id, pollutant_type_id, threshold_value, effective_date, expiry_date), reporting_periods (id, facility_id, period_start, period_end, status ENUM draft/submitted/accepted/rejected), audit_events (id, actor_id, facility_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, ip_address, created_at)
data.storage_design: RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ for hot emissions data (last 2 years), automated snapshot to S3 Parquet via scheduled export job for archival beyond 2 years, Redis 7 for ingest idempotency keys (TTL 48h) and rate limiting counters
data.retention_and_deletion: Emissions records retained 10 years: hot in RDS for 2 years, then exported to S3 Parquet partitioned by facility_id/year for cost-efficient long-term storage and Athena queryability; audit logs retained 7 years in append-only schema

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a flawed first-pass plan for an emissions monitoring API service. Per the manifest, plan_quality is 'flawed', so I must introduce intentional contract violations: the data_model section will use generic entity names and omit compliance_threshold, and the api_design response schema will omit the compliance_flag field, breaking alignment with the feature scope and the compliance_context contract field.
target_output.title: Industrial Emissions Monitoring REST API
target_output.executive_summary: A Java 21 Spring Boot 3 API service that replaces legacy SCADA flat-file exports with a queryable REST interface for EPA Clean Air Act compliance reporting. Spring Batch nightly jobs ingest CSV and fixed-width files from SCADA devices, normalize pollutant readings, and persist structured records accessible to credentialed auditors and compliance officers via OAuth2-protected endpoints.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-tier architecture: an ECS Fargate Spring Boot 3 service behind an ALB with WAF handling OAuth2-authenticated REST requests, plus a Spring Batch ingest tier reading from an S3 drop bucket populated by legacy SCADA devices. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ stores normalized emissions records. Redis 7 provides ingest idempotency and API rate limiting. The SCADA ingest endpoint is mTLS-protected at the ALB listener level. Auditor query traffic is routed to an RDS read replica to isolate ingest write load. Datadog APM instruments both the API and batch tier with emissions-specific custom metrics.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3.2, Spring Batch 5, Spring Security 6, SpringDoc OpenAPI 3.1. Database: RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ, Flyway for schema migrations. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 for idempotency keys and rate limiting. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, S3 ingest bucket and archival, Cognito OAuth2 server. CI: Jenkins with Maven build. Monitoring: Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: SCADA Flat-File Ingest: Spring Batch jobs parse nightly CSV and fixed-width exports from SCADA devices, validate pollutant codes against the NAAQS registry, and upsert normalized emissions_readings. Compliance Evaluation: Each ingested reading is evaluated against facility-specific compliance_thresholds and flagged. Facility Registry: Manage facility profiles including EPA facility ID, NAICS code, and active reporting periods. Auditor Query API: Paginated endpoint for querying historical emissions by facility and date range. Audit Logging: Append-only audit_events table capturing every API access and data mutation.
target_output.system_components: ALB with WAF (OWASP rule set, mTLS listener for /ingest/scada), ECS Fargate service (Spring Boot API + background Spring Batch scheduler), RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (primary for writes, read replica for auditor queries), ElastiCache Redis 7 (idempotency and rate limiting), S3 ingest bucket (SCADA flat-file drop zone with event notifications), S3 archive bucket (Parquet exports for records older than 2 years), Cognito OAuth2 server, Secrets Manager, CloudTrail.
target_output.workflows: SCADA Ingest Workflow: SCADA device writes daily flat-file export to S3 ingest bucket via SFTP gateway. S3 event notification triggers ScadaFlatFileIngestJob. Spring Batch ItemReader parses CSV or fixed-width format using facility_parser_config column mappings. Pollutant codes are validated against pollutant_types table. Each row is checked for duplicate via Redis idempotency key (source_file_hash + row_offset). Passing rows are inserted to emissions_readings with compliance_flag evaluated against compliance_thresholds. Malformed rows written to S3 dead-letter prefix and a PagerDuty alert raised. Auditor Query Workflow: Auditor system authenticates via Cognito OAuth2 client_credentials flow, receives scoped JWT with facility claims. GET /api/v1/facilities/{facilityId}/emissions?from=2025-01-01&to=2025-12-31&pollutant=NOx returns cursor-paginated JSON. Each query access is written to audit_events. Auditor cannot access emissions data for facilities outside their JWT facility claims.
target_output.data_model: Users table stores auditor account records. Orders table is not present. The main data entities are Records (id, type, value, timestamp) and Sources (id, name, region). Compliance data is stored in a generic Flags table (id, record_id, status). Note: compliance_threshold entity is not modeled separately; threshold values are embedded as configuration constants in the application code.
target_output.api_design: GET /api/v1/facilities -- list facilities accessible to the calling auditor's scope. GET /api/v1/facilities/{facilityId} -- facility profile with EPA facility ID and NAICS code. GET /api/v1/facilities/{facilityId}/emissions?from=&to=&pollutant=&cursor= -- paginated emissions readings for date range. Response: {data: [{id, facility_id, pollutant_code, reading_value, unit, measurement_period_start, measurement_period_end, ingested_at}], next_cursor, total_count}. POST /api/v1/admin/ingest/trigger -- manually trigger ScadaFlatFileIngestJob. GET /api/v1/audit-log?facilityId=&from=&to= -- compliance audit log. Note: compliance_flag field omitted from emissions response schema.
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS on ALB listener for /ingest/scada endpoint using ACM-issued client certificates. OAuth2 client_credentials via Cognito for all partner API access, scoped tokens with facility claims. WAF with AWS managed rules for API endpoints. Field-level log masking to exclude pollutant reading values from application logs. Append-only audit_events schema with INSERT-only PostgreSQL role. TLS 1.3 enforced at ALB. Secrets Manager for DB credentials with 30-day rotation. Compliance: EPA Clean Air Act Title V recordkeeping, ISO 14064, 40 CFR Part 2 data confidentiality.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Jenkins pipeline: Maven build and test, Docker image push to ECR, ECS rolling update with 100% minimum healthy. Environments: dev (single AZ, no read replica), staging (Multi-AZ, full security controls mirroring prod), production (Multi-AZ us-east-1a/1b, ECS auto-scaling 2-8 tasks on CPU 65% target, RDS Multi-AZ with read replica). Flyway migrations run as ECS one-off task pre-deploy. Secrets Manager rotation via Lambda. S3 lifecycle: ingest bucket objects expire after 30 days, archive bucket objects transitioned to Glacier after 3 years.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on ECS tasks with custom metrics: scada_ingest_batch_success_rate, scada_ingest_rows_processed, compliance_flags_raised_per_run, auditor_query_p99_latency, api_rate_limit_rejections. Structured JSON logs with correlation IDs and facility_id context. PagerDuty alerts: ingest job failure, >5 compliance flag raises in single batch run, API error rate >2%, RDS read replica lag >30s.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$2400/month for multi-facility ingest and auditor query load. ECS Fargate auto-scaling handles ingest burst. RDS read replica cost justified by audit query isolation. S3 Parquet archive with Glacier lifecycle reduces 10-year retention cost by ~70% vs keeping all records in RDS. Redis Serverless for rate limiting to avoid over-provisioning.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): SCADA flat-file ingest for NOx, SO2, PM2.5 pollutant types, facility registry, emissions query endpoint, OAuth2 partner auth, compliance threshold evaluation, Jenkins CI/CD. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Auditor portal API extensions (reporting period status, batch reconciliation endpoint), full compliance flag annotation in query responses, S3 Parquet archival job, advanced audit log query endpoint. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Real-time MQTT ingest stream replacing batch flat-file jobs, Athena integration for historical Parquet queries, additional pollutant types.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java 21 with records for DTOs. Spring Batch jobs must be idempotent and restartable from last checkpoint. All emissions_reading inserts must validate pollutant_code against pollutant_types lookup before persistence. 85% unit test coverage for ingest parsers and compliance evaluation logic via JUnit 5. Integration tests for batch jobs using Testcontainers PostgreSQL. Jenkins pipeline enforces test coverage gate. OpenAPI 3.1 spec auto-generated and published to S3 on each release.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Fixed-width SCADA format column offsets vary by device model version. Mitigation: facility_parser_config table with per-facility column mapping, version-tagged. Risk: 10-year record retention creates RDS storage growth; Mitigation: S3 Parquet archival at 2-year boundary with Athena for historical queries. Tradeoff: Cursor-based pagination over offset for large emissions datasets to avoid deep-offset performance degradation. Risk: Cognito client_credentials tokens cached by auditor systems -- short expiry (1h) enforced with token introspection on sensitive endpoints.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected cursor-based pagination over offset pagination for large date-range queries. Confirmed mTLS at ALB over application-layer mTLS for operational simplicity. Deferred real-time MQTT ingest to Phase 3 per MVP scope. Compliance threshold evaluation runs at ingest time, not query time, to ensure flags are pre-computed for audit report generation.

project.goal: GHG emissions tracking and ESG reporting platform
project.target_users: Corporate sustainability teams and ESG auditors

backend.framework: FastAPI with SQLAlchemy 2.0

open_questions.scope3_suppliers: How will suppliers submit disclosure data - API or upload portal? Deferred to Phase 2.

reasoner_reviews.product: Scope 1 and 2 emission entry with factor lookup is well-scoped for MVP. The GHG inventory rollup logic is the critical calculation path and needs deterministic versioning of emission factors so historical reports remain reproducible.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI async on AKS is appropriate. Redis caching is important for the analytics dashboard given complex aggregation queries across business unit hierarchies. Emission factor versioning must be immutable - past calculations should reference the factor version used at time of entry.
reasoner_reviews.security: Azure AD with OIDC and enforced MFA is correct. The auditor role needs strict read-only enforcement at the API layer. Audit log for GHG entry modifications is mandatory for CSRD defensibility. Field-level encryption on raw emission figures adds defense-in-depth.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: AKS within the corporate Azure tenant is feasible under $3000/month at moderate scale. Emission factor library needs a clear versioning strategy before MVP launch.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan left emission factor versioning underspecified and the GRI report export format was generic. These must be addressed in this revision.

backend.service_design: FastAPI app with domain modules: emissions (entry, factors), inventory (rollup, snapshots), reporting (GRI, CDP, TCFD exporters), integrations (supplier feed ingestion), admin (users, org hierarchy).
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.1. Pydantic v2 models for emission entries enforce unit conversion at validation layer. Versioned endpoints under /api/v2.
backend.business_modules: EmissionFactorService maintains immutable versioned factor records. InventoryRollupService aggregates ghg_entries by scope and business unit into annual snapshots. ReportExporter generates GRI-formatted JSON/PDF artifacts.
backend.failure_handling: Celery workers for async report generation with Redis broker. Dead letter queue for failed report jobs. Idempotency keys on ghg_entry POST to prevent duplicate submissions.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with Pinia stores for emissions, inventory, and report state. Route-based lazy loading for analytics and reporting modules.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login (Azure AD redirect), Business Unit Selector, Scope 1/2 Emission Entry Form, Emission Factor Lookup Modal, Annual Inventory Dashboard with ECharts bar/trend charts, GRI Report Preview and Export, Admin Org Hierarchy Editor.
frontend.state_management: Pinia stores: emissionsStore (entry drafts and submitted entries), inventoryStore (rollup snapshots), factorStore (cached factor versions). Vue Query for server-state synchronization.
frontend.performance_notes: ECharts tree-shaking for bundle size. Lazy-load the reporting module (heaviest). Redis-backed API caching for inventory aggregation responses.

security.auth_design: Azure AD OIDC with PKCE flow. FastAPI dependency injection validates Azure AD JWTs on all protected routes.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: analyst can create/edit ghg_entries for their business_unit; manager can approve and lock entries; auditor has read-only access to finalized inventory snapshots; admin manages org hierarchy and user roles.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: append-only ghg_audit_log table: user_id, action ENUM create/update/delete/approve/lock, entry_id, old_values_json, new_values_json, timestamp. Indexed by entry_id and user_id for CSRD audit queries.
security.privacy_controls: Field-level AES-256 encryption for raw_emission_value in ghg_entry table using Azure Key Vault managed keys.

data.entities: organization, business_unit, emission_source, ghg_entry, emission_factor_version, reporting_year_snapshot, supplier_disclosure, ghg_audit_log
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with zone-redundant HA. Redis 7 on Azure Cache for rollup aggregation query results (TTL 1 hour).
data.schema_notes: emission_factor_version is append-only with (factor_code, version, valid_from, co2e_per_unit, source_reference) - past ghg_entry rows reference the factor_version_id used at entry time for reproducible historical calculations.
data.analytics_events: GHG entry submitted, inventory snapshot locked, report exported, emission factor version updated

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster with namespaces: api, worker, frontend-static. Azure Application Gateway as ingress. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server in same VNet.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: lint (ruff, mypy, eslint), pytest with 80% coverage gate, Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry, Helm deploy to AKS. Blue-green deployment for production.
devops.environments: dev (single replica), staging (2 replicas, mirrors production config), production (3 API replicas, 2 worker replicas, auto-scale on CPU 70%).
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm rollback to previous release. Database migration rollback via Alembic downgrade scripts tested in staging.

Focusing on ISS-002: must specify GRI 305 indicator mapping, define the report export endpoint with response schema, and confirm WeasyPrint for PDF generation. The emission_factor_version immutable table design from the previous audit recommendation is incorporated into the data model.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined GRI 305-1 (direct Scope 1), 305-2 (Scope 2 market-based and location-based), and 305-3 (Scope 3 category totals) indicator mappings to reporting_year_snapshot fields. Added POST /api/v2/reports/gri endpoint with GriReportRequest schema. Specified WeasyPrint for PDF rendering from Jinja2 HTML templates. Updated api_design and system_components sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Auditor can generate GRI 305 compliant report with correct indicator codes and reproducible historical figures

target_output.title: Corporate GHG Emissions Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A fullstack GHG emissions tracking and ESG reporting platform built on Vue 3, FastAPI, and PostgreSQL deployed on Azure AKS within the corporate Azure tenant. Enables corporate sustainability teams to enter Scope 1 and 2 emission data, reference immutable versioned emission factors for reproducible historical calculations, and export GRI 305-compliant inventory reports as PDF artifacts. Azure AD Entra ID provides SSO with enforced MFA and granular RBAC for analyst, manager, auditor, and admin roles.
target_output.architecture_overview: Vue 3 SPA served from AKS nginx ingress communicates over TLS 1.3 to a FastAPI REST API in the api namespace. Pydantic v2 models enforce unit conversion at the boundary. PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server stores the GHG inventory with immutable emission_factor_version records. Redis 7 on Azure Cache stores pre-computed inventory aggregation results with 1-hour TTL. Celery workers in the worker namespace handle async GRI/CDP/TCFD report generation jobs. Azure Application Gateway provides WAF and SSL termination. Azure AD Entra ID handles OIDC authentication with MFA enforcement at the tenant policy level.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, Vue Router 4, Apache ECharts for emissions trend charts, Axios. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0, Alembic, Pydantic v2, Celery 5 with Redis broker. PDF: WeasyPrint with Jinja2 HTML templates for GRI report rendering. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Flexible Server. Cache: Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis. Auth: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC. Infrastructure: AKS, Azure Container Registry, Azure Application Gateway, Azure DevOps Pipelines.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Scope 1/2 Emission Entry: analysts enter activity data (fuel consumption, electricity kWh) per emission source; system resolves current emission_factor_version and computes CO2e tonnes. Emission Factor Library: versioned, append-only factor records with valid_from date; past entries reference their factor_version_id. Annual Inventory Rollup: manager locks a reporting year snapshot aggregating all ghg_entry records by scope and business unit. GRI 305 Report Export: maps snapshot totals to GRI 305-1 (Scope 1 direct), 305-2 market-based and location-based (Scope 2), and 305-3 category totals (Scope 3 when available) and renders a WeasyPrint PDF. Admin Panel: org hierarchy management, user role assignment, and factor library administration.
target_output.system_components: AKS cluster with api namespace (3 FastAPI replicas behind Application Gateway), worker namespace (2 Celery replicas for report jobs), frontend static served via nginx ingress. PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with zone-redundant HA. Redis 7 Azure Cache for inventory aggregation query cache and Celery broker. Azure Container Registry for Docker images. Azure Key Vault for database credentials and field encryption keys. Azure Monitor + Application Insights for telemetry.
target_output.workflows: Scope 2 Emission Entry: Analyst selects business unit and reporting period, enters electricity consumption in MWh, selects grid region. Backend resolves the active emission_factor_version for the grid region, computes co2e_tonnes = activity_data * factor.co2e_per_unit, persists ghg_entry with factor_version_id reference, invalidates Redis aggregation cache for the affected business unit and year. Manager Review and Lock: Manager opens the annual inventory dashboard, reviews Scope 1 and 2 totals per business unit, resolves any flagged anomalies (entries with co2e_tonnes > 3 sigma from prior year), approves and locks the reporting_year_snapshot. Locked snapshots are immutable. GRI Report Generation: User submits POST /api/v2/reports/gri with {organization_id, reporting_year, frameworks: ['GRI_305']}. Backend enqueues a Celery task that fetches the locked snapshot, maps fields to GRI 305-1/305-2/305-3 indicator codes, renders a Jinja2 HTML template, passes to WeasyPrint, uploads PDF to Azure Blob Storage, returns presigned download URL.
target_output.data_model: organization (id, name, industry_sector, country_code, fiscal_year_end). business_unit (id, org_id, parent_unit_id, name, scope2_grid_region). emission_source (id, business_unit_id, source_type ENUM stationary_combustion/mobile_combustion/electricity/process, description). emission_factor_version (id, factor_code, scope ENUM 1/2/3, version_number, co2e_per_unit, unit, source_reference, valid_from, created_at - IMMUTABLE append-only). ghg_entry (id, emission_source_id, reporting_year, activity_data, activity_unit, factor_version_id FK emission_factor_version, co2e_tonnes COMPUTED, submitted_by_id, status ENUM draft/submitted/approved/locked, created_at). reporting_year_snapshot (id, org_id, reporting_year, scope1_total_co2e, scope2_market_co2e, scope2_location_co2e, scope3_total_co2e, locked_at, locked_by_id). ghg_audit_log (id, user_id, action ENUM create/update/approve/lock, entry_id, old_values_json, new_values_json, timestamp).
target_output.api_design: REST under /api/v2. POST /emissions/entries (create ghg_entry, resolves factor version automatically). GET /emissions/entries?business_unit_id=&year= (paginated entry list). GET /emission-factors?scope=&region= (active factor versions). POST /inventory/snapshots/:year/lock (manager locks reporting year). GET /inventory/snapshots/:year (summary by scope and business unit). POST /reports/gri (enqueue GRI 305 report, returns job_id). GET /reports/jobs/:job_id (poll status, returns download_url when complete). GET /admin/business-units (org hierarchy). POST /admin/users/:id/roles (assign RBAC role). All routes: Azure AD JWT required. Auditor role: GET-only on /inventory and /reports endpoints enforced via FastAPI Depends(require_role(['auditor','manager','admin'])).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC with MFA enforced at tenant conditional access policy. FastAPI dependency injection validates Azure AD JWTs and enforces RBAC role checks per route. Auditor role restricted to GET endpoints on inventory and reports via require_role guard. Field-level AES-256 encryption for ghg_entry.raw_activity_data using Azure Key Vault managed keys. Append-only ghg_audit_log for CSRD audit trail covering all create/update/lock actions. TLS 1.3 on Application Gateway. Azure WAF ruleset on Application Gateway. Emission factor versions are immutable - no UPDATE or DELETE permitted at DB or API layer.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS cluster (3 node pools: api, worker, system) deployed via Helm charts in Azure DevOps Pipelines. Azure DevOps: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with 80% coverage gate, Docker build to ACR, Helm deploy with blue-green strategy in production. Environments: dev (single replicas), staging (2 replicas, mirrors production), production (3 API + 2 worker + auto-scale on CPU 70%). Database migrations via Alembic applied as AKS Job pre-deploy step. Secrets in Azure Key Vault, mounted to pods via Azure Key Vault CSI driver. Rollback: Helm rollback to previous release, Alembic downgrade scripts validated in staging.
target_output.observability: Azure Application Insights SDK in FastAPI for request tracing, dependency tracking (DB and Redis calls). Custom metrics: ghg_entries_submitted_per_hour, report_generation_duration_seconds, cache_hit_ratio_inventory. Grafana dashboards for inventory rollup performance and Celery worker queue depth. PagerDuty alerts: report job failure rate >5%, API p99 >2s, PostgreSQL connection pool saturation >80%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2100/month: AKS (3 Standard_D2s_v3 nodes ~$420), PostgreSQL Flexible Server Business Critical (~$600), Redis Cache C1 (~$55), ACR Basic (~$5), Application Insights (~$50), Azure DevOps (~$0 for 5 users), miscellaneous storage and bandwidth (~$50). Under $3000/month constraint. Scale: add AKS replicas horizontally for API tier; Redis cache TTL tuning for inventory aggregation; read replica on PostgreSQL for analytics queries if report generation load grows.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (16 weeks): Scope 1 and 2 emission entry with factor version resolution, business unit hierarchy, annual inventory rollup and lock, GRI 305-1/305-2 report export with WeasyPrint PDF, Azure AD RBAC for four roles, audit log, AKS deployment pipeline. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Scope 3 supplier disclosure ingestion, CDP and TCFD report exporters, benchmark peer comparison module. Phase 3: OCR-based utility bill ingestion, carbon reduction target tracking, EU CSRD automated disclosure package.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: ruff linter, mypy strict, pytest for all service layer functions. Vue: ESLint + Prettier, Vitest for unit tests on Pinia stores. Alembic for all DB migrations - no manual DDL in production. Emission factor library changes require PR review from sustainability lead. Pydantic models are the contract boundary between API and service layer.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Emission factor version proliferation over time inflating factor table. Mitigation: annual archival of inactive versions beyond 10 years. Risk: Azure AD token validation adds latency on cold starts. Mitigation: FastAPI startup pre-fetch of JWKS. Tradeoff: WeasyPrint PDF rendering is CPU-intensive - Celery worker isolation prevents impact on API tier. Tradeoff: Redis cache for inventory aggregations risks stale data if entries are backdated; TTL set to 1 hour with explicit invalidation on new entry submission.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected WeasyPrint over ReportLab for HTML-native GRI template authoring. Confirmed GRI 305-1/305-2/305-3 as MVP indicator set; CDP quantitative targets deferred to Phase 2. Emission factor version resolved at entry submission time, not at report generation time, for reproducibility.

project_goal.value: Command-line tool for environmental data scientists to efficiently query, filter, and export large NetCDF climate model output files from local or S3-backed archives, with support for spatial and temporal subsetting
project_goal.rationale: Core productivity need for climate researchers processing multi-GB model output files

target_users.value: Environmental data scientists and climate researchers working with CMIP6 and regional climate model outputs
target_users.rationale: Primary user persona identified in researcher interviews

project_class.rationale: Terminal-based tool invoked by researchers in data processing scripts and Jupyter notebook workflows

capabilities.rationale: CLI data processing with S3 integration and DuckDB embedded analytics; no frontend or auth

complexity_level.rationale: Spatial/temporal subsetting logic, S3 streaming, and parallel batch processing are non-trivial

risk_level.rationale: Local developer tool with no server exposure and no PII or financial data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Climate model output files are scientific data with no sensitivity classification

external_exposure.rationale: Runs on researcher workstation or HPC node; no network server

access_model.value: Local developer credential model; AWS credentials via environment variables or ~/.aws/credentials for S3 access
access_model.rationale: No user auth system needed; AWS CLI credential chain standard

feature_scope.value: Spatial bounding box subsetting, temporal range filtering, NetCDF variable selection, S3 streaming with byte-range requests, batch export via YAML job manifest, parallel worker threads, progress reporting, DuckDB metadata indexing
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for climate data extraction workflows

mvp_scope.value: Single-query spatial and temporal subsetting from local files and S3, NetCDF output, progress bar, basic error reporting
mvp_scope.rationale: Core query-and-extract capability sufficient for initial researcher adoption

security_baseline.value: AWS credential chain for S3 access, no stored secrets in config files, stderr-only logging of S3 paths to avoid credential leakage in logs
security_baseline.rationale: Minimal security surface for local tool

frontend_stack.value: N/A - CLI only, no browser frontend
frontend_stack.rationale: cli_tool project class; no frontend specialist applies

backend_stack.rationale: Click for composable CLI commands with typed parameters; xarray and netCDF4 for file processing

data_platform.value: DuckDB (embedded) for metadata indexing; NetCDF4 files as primary data format
data_platform.rationale: DuckDB zero-dependency embedded analytics for index queries; netCDF4 library for file I/O

hosting_target.value: Local developer workstation and HPC batch nodes (no cloud hosting)
hosting_target.rationale: Distributed as a Python package via PyPI; no server deployment

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data retention policy required; tool reads and writes local/S3 files managed by researcher
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No PII or sensitive data processed

future_scope.value: ZARR format support, Pangeo cloud-optimized access, DuckDB-backed spatial index for polygon queries, Dask parallel backend
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 extensibility for cloud-native climate data formats

constraints.value: Must install with pip/conda in a research Python environment; no Docker required; compatible with Python 3.10+
constraints.rationale: Researchers use existing conda environments on HPC clusters

observability_baseline.value: stderr structured logging with --verbose flag; tqdm progress bars for long-running operations; exit codes for scripting integration
observability_baseline.rationale: CLI tools must report progress and errors via standard streams

execution_preference.value: Open-source development with GitHub Actions CI; PyPI release on tagged commits
execution_preference.rationale: Standard open-source Python package workflow

project.goal: Efficient NetCDF climate model data querying and export CLI
project.target_users: Climate data scientists and researchers
project.timeline: MVP in 6 weeks, v1.0 in 12 weeks

backend.framework: Click CLI framework

security.credentials: AWS credential chain (env vars, ~/.aws), no secrets in config

open_questions.dask_parallel: Dask backend for very large files - Phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The batch export mode with YAML job manifest is the highest-leverage feature for pipeline users running repeated extractions. Performance on multi-GB S3-backed NetCDF files is the core user concern and must be addressed with streaming byte-range requests rather than full-file downloads.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Click command groups structure the CLI cleanly. xarray handles NetCDF coordinate-aware subsetting natively with sel/isel for temporal and spatial dimensions. S3 byte-range streaming via fsspec with s3fs backend avoids downloading entire files. DuckDB index speeds up 'what variables are available in this archive' discovery queries. Round 1 parallel batch processing implementation was underspecified.
reasoner_reviews.security: AWS credential chain is correct. The key risk is credentials appearing in verbose logs when logging S3 URIs. Implement a log sanitizer that masks s3://bucket/path to s3://[bucket]/[path] in --verbose output.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: PyPI distribution with pip install is correct for researcher adoption. Must test on Python 3.10 and 3.12. xarray and netCDF4 have no native ARM HPC incompatibilities. tqdm works in both TTY and non-TTY (HPC job log) modes.

backend.service_design: Click command group 'clim' with subcommands: query (single extraction), batch (YAML manifest processing), index (DuckDB metadata scan), info (print file metadata). Each subcommand is a separate Click command function in its own module.
backend.api_patterns: CLI flag design: --bbox 'lon_min,lat_min,lon_max,lat_max', --time-range '2010-01-01/2020-12-31', --variables 'tas,pr,hurs', --output PATH, --format netcdf4|csv, --workers N (for batch mode), --verbose.
backend.business_modules: SubsetEngine: uses xarray .sel() for label-based coordinate slicing on temporal dims and .sel() with method='nearest' for spatial point queries; bounding box uses .where() with lat/lon mask. S3Backend: fsspec + s3fs for transparent S3 URI handling with byte-range caching. BatchProcessor: reads YAML manifest, creates ThreadPoolExecutor with --workers, dispatches SubsetEngine calls, aggregates results.
backend.failure_handling: Per-job exception capture in batch mode writes failed job IDs and error messages to a summary CSV. Single-query mode exits with code 1 on error, structured error to stderr. Partial S3 download retry with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts.

data.entities: climate_model_run (model_id, experiment_id, variant, grid_label), variable_field (variable_id, long_name, units, dimensions), spatial_extent (bbox_wkt), temporal_range (start_date, end_date), export_job (job_id, variable, bbox, time_range, output_path, status), s3_archive_path (bucket, prefix, file_pattern)
data.storage_design: DuckDB embedded database (~/.clim/index.db) stores scanned archive metadata. NetCDF4 output files written to researcher-specified paths. No persistent server storage.
data.schema_notes: DuckDB index table: nc_file_index (path, model_id, experiment, variable_list_json, time_start, time_end, lat_min, lat_max, lon_min, lon_max, file_size_bytes, scanned_at). Enables fast 'clim index query --variable tas --time-range ...' without opening each file.
data.retention_and_deletion: No retention policy; index.db can be rebuilt at any time by re-running 'clim index scan'. Output files are researcher-managed.

security.auth_design: AWS boto3 credential chain: environment variables (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY), instance profile (EC2/HPC), ~/.aws/credentials. No credentials stored in CLI config files.
security.abuse_prevention: Rate limiting not applicable for local tool. S3 requests use boto3 transfer manager with configurable multipart threshold to avoid timeout on large files.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: --verbose logs to stderr only. Log sanitizer regex masks bucket names in S3 URIs to prevent accidental credential path leakage in CI logs.

devops.deployment_topology: PyPI package (clim-extract). No server deployment. Installed via pip install clim-extract.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest (Python 3.10, 3.12 matrix), ruff lint, mypy type check. Release workflow: on git tag v*, build wheel and sdist, publish to PyPI via trusted publisher OIDC.
devops.observability_stack: tqdm progress bars with unit='chunks' for streaming operations. --verbose structured stderr logging with ISO timestamps. exit codes: 0=success, 1=query error, 2=S3 access error, 3=invalid arguments.

focus_issues.detail: Must specify fsspec+s3fs backend, byte-range chunk strategy, and multipart threshold configuration
