target_output.title: Climate NetCDF Query CLI (clim-extract)
target_output.executive_summary: A Python CLI tool (clim-extract) for climate data scientists to subset, filter, and export NetCDF climate model output files from local storage or S3 archives. Built on Click, xarray, and fsspec+s3fs for transparent S3 byte-range streaming. DuckDB embedded index accelerates archive discovery queries. Batch mode processes YAML job manifests in parallel using ThreadPoolExecutor with per-job failure isolation.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-process Python CLI application installed via pip install clim-extract. Click command group 'clim' dispatches to subcommands (query, batch, index, info). SubsetEngine wraps xarray for coordinate-aware spatial and temporal slicing. S3Backend wraps fsspec with the s3fs storage backend, enabling xarray to open S3-hosted NetCDF files via HTTP Range requests without staging the full file locally. DuckDB runs embedded as ~/.clim/index.db for metadata index queries. BatchProcessor uses concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor for parallel job execution. No network server, no daemon process.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12 (compatible with 3.10+). Click 8 for CLI command structure and typed parameters. xarray 2024.x with netCDF4 engine for coordinate-aware file I/O and subsetting. fsspec 2024.x + s3fs for S3 URI transparent file access with HTTP Range requests. DuckDB 0.10 embedded for metadata index. boto3 for S3 credential resolution and transfer manager configuration. tqdm for streaming progress bars. PyPI distribution via trusted publisher OIDC.
target_output.functional_feature_map: clim query: single extraction with --bbox, --time-range, --variables, --output, --format, --chunk-size flags. Streams relevant NetCDF chunks from S3 or local disk and writes filtered output file. clim batch: reads YAML job manifest, dispatches extractions in parallel via ThreadPoolExecutor, writes per-job status to summary.csv. clim index scan: crawls local or S3 archive, extracts metadata from each NetCDF file header, stores in DuckDB nc_file_index. clim index query: SQL-like filtering on nc_file_index to find files matching variable/time/spatial criteria without opening files. clim info: prints variables, dimensions, coordinate ranges, and file size for a single NetCDF file.
target_output.system_components: Click CLI entry point (clim/__main__.py). SubsetEngine (clim/engine.py): xarray Dataset.sel() for label-based temporal slicing on 'time' dimension; bounding box via Dataset.where(lat_mask & lon_mask). S3Backend (clim/s3.py): fsspec.open() with s3fs protocol, open_local=False for streaming; chunk_size passed as storage_options={'requester_pays':False,'default_block_size':chunk_size_bytes}. BatchProcessor (clim/batch.py): ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=--workers), futures.as_completed() for progress aggregation, per-future exception capture. DuckDB index (clim/index.py): DuckDB.connect('~/.clim/index.db'), nc_file_index table with spatial and temporal bounds for each scanned file.
target_output.workflows: Single S3 Query: Researcher runs 'clim query --bbox -10,35,10,55 --time-range 2010-01-01/2020-12-31 --variables tas,pr s3://cmip6-archive/MPI-ESM1-2-HR/historical/ --output subset.nc --chunk-size 8MB'. S3Backend opens the archive path via fsspec+s3fs, xarray lazily loads chunks via HTTP Range requests in 8MB blocks. SubsetEngine applies temporal sel() and spatial where() masks. tqdm progress bar tracks chunks written. Output written to subset.nc. Exit code 0. Batch Export: Researcher creates jobs.yaml with 12 entries each specifying variable, bbox, time_range, output_path. Runs 'clim batch --manifest jobs.yaml --workers 4'. BatchProcessor creates ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4), submits 12 SubsetEngine futures, collects results via as_completed(). Failed jobs write error message to summary.csv without aborting remaining jobs. Archive Discovery: Researcher runs 'clim index scan s3://cmip6-archive/ --model MPI-ESM1-2-HR'. Index module streams file listing from S3, opens each NC file header with xarray (no data loaded), writes metadata row to DuckDB nc_file_index. Query: 'clim index query --variable tas --time-range 2010/2020' returns matching file paths instantly.
target_output.data_model: DuckDB nc_file_index: (path TEXT, model_id TEXT, experiment_id TEXT, variant_label TEXT, variable_list_json TEXT, time_start DATE, time_end DATE, lat_min FLOAT, lat_max FLOAT, lon_min FLOAT, lon_max FLOAT, file_size_bytes BIGINT, scanned_at TIMESTAMP). export_job (in-memory dataclass): job_id, variable, bbox_tuple, time_range_tuple, output_path, status ENUM queued/running/complete/failed, error_message. climate_model_run (metadata extracted from NC global attributes): model_id, experiment_id, variant_label, grid_label, nominal_resolution. No persistent relational DB beyond DuckDB index file.
target_output.api_design: CLI command surface (not HTTP API). 'clim query [OPTIONS] SOURCE': --bbox FLOAT,FLOAT,FLOAT,FLOAT, --time-range DATE/DATE, --variables TEXT (comma-separated), --output PATH (default stdout for CSV), --format netcdf4|csv, --chunk-size TEXT (default 8MB, parsed to bytes). 'clim batch --manifest PATH --workers INT --output-dir PATH'. 'clim index scan SOURCE --model TEXT --experiment TEXT'. 'clim index query --variable TEXT --time-range DATE/DATE --bbox FLOAT,FLOAT,FLOAT,FLOAT'. 'clim info SOURCE'. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 query error, 2 S3 access error, 3 invalid arguments. All errors to stderr.
target_output.security_and_compliance: AWS credentials via boto3 credential chain: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY env vars, EC2 instance profile, or ~/.aws/credentials. No credentials written to CLI config or log files. S3 URI log sanitizer in --verbose mode replaces s3://bucket-name/ with s3://[bucket]/ to prevent accidental bucket name exposure in shared CI logs. No authentication system required for local tool. Researcher responsible for S3 bucket access policies.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: PyPI package clim-extract distributed via pip install clim-extract. GitHub Actions CI: pytest matrix (Python 3.10, 3.12), ruff linting, mypy type checking, test with local synthetic NetCDF fixtures generated by test setup. Release workflow: on git tag v*, build wheel and sdist via python -m build, publish to PyPI via trusted publisher OIDC (no stored API tokens). No server deployment or container image. HPC compatibility tested on Rocky Linux 8 with conda Python 3.11.
target_output.observability: tqdm progress bars with unit='chunks' and dynamic miniters for S3 streaming operations. --verbose flag enables structured stderr log lines: ISO timestamp, subcommand, source path (sanitized), operation, duration_ms. Batch mode writes summary.csv: job_id, status, output_path, duration_seconds, error_message. Exit codes propagate to calling scripts and CI pipelines.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost - runs on researcher workstation or HPC node. S3 egress costs are researcher-managed based on data volume. ThreadPoolExecutor --workers defaults to 4; researchers on high-bandwidth HPC can increase to 16-32. For files >10GB, DuckDB index ensures only matching files are opened, minimizing unnecessary S3 HEAD requests.
target_output.phased_implementation: v0.1 MVP (6 weeks): clim query with local file subsetting (bbox + time-range), NetCDF4 output, tqdm progress, basic error reporting, GitHub Actions CI, PyPI alpha release. v0.5 (4 weeks): S3 streaming via fsspec+s3fs with byte-range requests, --chunk-size flag, DuckDB index (scan + query subcommands), --verbose log sanitizer. v1.0 (4 weeks): clim batch with YAML manifest and parallel workers, summary.csv reporting, clim info subcommand, Python 3.10 compatibility validation, PyPI stable release.
target_output.development_guidelines: ruff linter and mypy strict mode enforced in CI. pytest with synthetic NetCDF fixtures created via xarray.Dataset.to_netcdf(). All S3 interactions mocked via moto in tests. CLI entry points tested with Click's CliRunner. No hardcoded S3 bucket names in source code. DuckDB index path configurable via CLIM_INDEX_PATH env var.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: xarray lazy loading of S3 NetCDF can exhaust memory if bounding box is very large. Mitigation: --max-output-size flag with 2GB default aborts query before writing. Risk: DuckDB index becomes stale if S3 archive is updated without re-scanning. Mitigation: --force-rescan flag; index entries include scanned_at timestamp. Tradeoff: ThreadPoolExecutor over Dask for simplicity at v1.0; Dask backend planned for Phase 2 to handle files >50GB.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected fsspec+s3fs over direct boto3 streaming for xarray integration compatibility. Confirmed DuckDB over SQLite for embedded analytics index - faster spatial range queries. Batch concurrency model: ThreadPoolExecutor chosen over asyncio for simplicity with blocking xarray I/O.

metadata.notes: Climate NetCDF CLI tool revision round 2 - moderate quality, ISS-002 S3 streaming now specified with fsspec+s3fs byte-range detail.

profile.domain: Voluntary Carbon Market Credit Retirement Automation

project_goal.value: Automation tool that scales carbon credit retirement workflows for voluntary carbon market platforms, automatically matching buyer purchase orders with available credit batches, executing retirements on registry APIs, and issuing retirement certificates
project_goal.rationale: Core operational need to automate manual registry retirement workflows at scale

target_users.value: Voluntary carbon market platform operators and buyer organizations purchasing carbon credits through automated workflows
target_users.rationale: B2B automation tool used by platform ops and buyer API integrations

project_class.rationale: Background automation service with no user-facing frontend; processes purchase orders and registry API calls

complexity_level.rationale: Idempotent retirement workflows, multi-registry API orchestration, and financial transaction reconciliation are non-trivial

risk_level.rationale: Financial transactions on regulated carbon registries require high reliability and audit trail

data_sensitivity.rationale: Carbon credit retirement transactions are financial instruments subject to audit requirements

access_model.rationale: Automation tool with partner-facing API requires strong service identity

feature_scope.rationale: Full automation workflow from order to certificate

mvp_scope.rationale: MVP targets Verra VCS as first registry integration; Gold Standard added in Phase 2

security_baseline.value: mTLS for all registry API connections, JWT validation on inbound webhooks, idempotency keys on all registry API calls, append-only audit ledger for all retirement transactions
security_baseline.rationale: Financial transaction integrity and partner-facing service security requirements

data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for transactional integrity on retirement records; Redis for idempotency key cache and job queue

constraints.rationale: Registry API constraints and financial integrity requirements

observability_baseline.rationale: High-risk financial automation requires proactive alerting

execution_preference.rationale: Continuous delivery with registry sandbox validation

project.goal: Automate carbon credit retirement from purchase order to certificate
project.target_users: Carbon market platform operators and buyer organizations
project.timeline: MVP Verra VCS integration in 10 weeks

backend.framework: Standard library net/http + goroutines
backend.api: Internal webhook receiver and registry API clients

security.service_auth: mTLS for registry connections, JWT for inbound webhooks
security.idempotency: Idempotency keys on all retirement API calls

data.cache: Redis 7 on Memorystore for idempotency key cache
data.queue: Redis RPUSH/BLPOP for retirement job queue

devops.hosting: Cloud Run (API) + Cloud Run Jobs (batch reconciliation)

open_questions.gold_standard: Gold Standard API authentication model - deferred to Phase 2

confirmed_decisions.cert_format: PDF via Go HTML template + headless Chrome

reasoner_reviews.product: The idempotency guarantee is the most critical correctness property. A duplicate retirement on Verra VCS cannot be undone and represents a financial loss. The matching algorithm for credit batches must be deterministic and auditable - same purchase order must always produce the same batch selection.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go standard library net/http with goroutines is appropriate for controlled concurrency with the 10/minute Verra rate limit. Redis RPUSH/BLPOP queue with a single consumer goroutine gated by a rate limiter token bucket is the correct pattern. PostgreSQL advisory locks on credit_batch rows during matching prevents race conditions. Round 1 missed specifying the certificate issuance mechanism.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS certificate rotation for Verra API connections needs an automated process via GCP Certificate Authority Service. JWT validation on inbound buyer webhooks must verify the buyer_organization_id in the token matches the purchase_order.buyer_id to prevent cross-buyer order injection. Append-only retirement_ledger is critical.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Verra VCS 10/minute rate limit requires a leaky bucket implementation. Cloud Run min-instances=1 prevents cold starts on inbound purchase order webhooks. Reconciliation Cloud Run Job can run nightly without always-on cost.

backend.service_design: Single Go binary with three goroutine groups: webhook receiver (HTTP server), retirement worker (rate-limited queue consumer), reconciliation worker (Cloud Run Job triggered nightly). Domain packages: orders, matching, registry, certificates, reconciliation.
backend.api_patterns: Inbound: POST /webhooks/purchase-orders (JWT auth, idempotency header required). Internal: no public API surface. Registry client: HTTP client with mTLS, retry with exponential backoff, idempotency key header on every retirement call.
backend.business_modules: MatchingEngine: selects credit_batch rows by project_type and vintage_year using PostgreSQL SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED for concurrency safety. RegistryClient (Verra): constructs retirement payload, sends with idempotency key, parses confirmation serial number. CertificateService: generates PDF retirement certificate from Go HTML template rendered via headless Chrome via CDP. ReconciliationJob: pulls Verra confirmation log for prior day, joins against retirement_ledger, flags status mismatches.
backend.failure_handling: Retirement worker: on registry API 5xx, requeue with exponential backoff up to 5 attempts, then move to dead_letter_queue. On 4xx (e.g., credit batch already retired), mark retirement_event as failed_duplicate, alert PagerDuty. Idempotency: Redis SETNX on idempotency key before every Verra API call.

data.entities: purchase_order, credit_batch, registry_account, retirement_event, certificate, buyer_organization, reconciliation_run
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with high-availability regional instance. Redis 7 Memorystore for idempotency key SET (TTL 48h) and retirement job queue (RPUSH/BLPOP).
data.schema_notes: retirement_event (id UUID, purchase_order_id, credit_batch_id, registry_account_id, verra_serial_number, idempotency_key UNIQUE, status ENUM pending/submitted/confirmed/failed_duplicate/failed, submitted_at, confirmed_at). credit_batch (id, project_type, vintage_year, available_quantity, locked_quantity, registry_account_id). certificate (id, retirement_event_id, pdf_s3_key, issued_at). retirement_ledger (append-only audit table mirroring retirement_event state transitions).
data.retention_and_deletion: retirement_event and certificate records retained 10 years. reconciliation_run logs retained 7 years. No deletion permitted on financial records.

security.auth_design: Inbound webhook JWT validated against buyer_organization JWKS endpoint. mTLS client certificates for Verra API loaded from GCP Secret Manager, rotated every 90 days via Cloud Scheduler + Secret Manager rotation function.
security.authorization_model: Service-to-service only. Buyer JWT must contain buyer_organization_id claim matching purchase_order.buyer_id. No human user RBAC.
security.abuse_prevention: Inbound webhook rate limit: 100 purchase orders/minute per buyer_organization_id via Redis sliding window counter. Idempotency key SETNX in Redis prevents concurrent duplicate retirement submissions.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Append-only retirement_ledger records every state transition for each retirement_event with timestamp, trigger source, and registry response code. Cloud Audit Logs for GCP resource access. Structured JSON log shipping to Cloud Logging with retirement_event_id correlation.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run service (min-instances=1, max-instances=10) for webhook receiver and retirement worker. Cloud Run Job for nightly reconciliation triggered by Cloud Scheduler. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL in same VPC via Private Service Connect. Redis Memorystore in same VPC.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build: go vet, go test (unit + integration against Verra VCS sandbox), docker build, push to Artifact Registry, deploy to Cloud Run. Integration tests use Verra VCS sandbox environment with synthetic purchase orders.
devops.rollback_strategy: Cloud Run revision rollback via gcloud run services update-traffic. Database migrations via golang-migrate applied as Cloud Run Job pre-deploy step; rollback scripts validated in staging.
devops.cost_controls: Cloud Run scales to zero between bursts. Cloud SQL uses db-custom-2-8GB instance (~$150/month). Redis Memorystore Basic M1 (~$35/month). Expected total <$500/month at 500 retirements/day.

ISS-001.title: Certificate issuance mechanism not specified

ISS-002.title: mTLS certificate rotation process absent

history.note: mTLS mentioned but lifecycle unspecified

focus_issues.title: mTLS certificate rotation process absent

Define mTLS rotation lifecycle with GCP Secret Manager
Add certificate delivery webhook or download endpoint

previous_audits.summary: Strong Go architecture with correct idempotency and rate-limiting design. Certificate issuance and mTLS rotation are significant gaps that must be addressed before production.
strengths: Redis SETNX idempotency pattern is correct
PostgreSQL SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED for batch matching is well-designed
Rate-limited retirement worker with leaky bucket is appropriate for Verra 10/min constraint

previous_plan.title: Carbon Credit Retirement Automation - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Go 1.22 automation service on Cloud Run handling carbon credit retirement from purchase order to registry confirmation. Redis rate limiter and idempotency keys prevent duplicate Verra API calls. Nightly reconciliation Cloud Run Job.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: mTLS for Verra API connections (rotation not specified). JWT for inbound buyer webhooks. Append-only retirement_ledger.
previous_plan.system_components: Cloud Run webhook receiver, Redis job queue, retirement worker goroutines, Cloud Run Job reconciliation. Certificate generation: mentioned but unspecified.

target_output.thinking_summary: Revision round 2 for the carbon credit retirement automation. Two focus issues: ISS-001 (certificate issuance) and ISS-002 (mTLS rotation). For ISS-001: specify headless Chrome via Go chromedp library for PDF rendering from a Go HTML/CSS template containing Verra serial number, project type, vintage year, quantity, buyer org name, and issuance date; certificates stored in Cloud Storage and delivery URL sent via buyer notification webhook. For ISS-002: mTLS client certificates stored in Secret Manager with 90-day TTL; Cloud Scheduler triggers a Cloud Function to renew via GCP Certificate Authority Service and update the Secret Manager version. No frontend applies to this automation_tool.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified Go chromedp library for headless Chrome PDF rendering. Defined certificate template contents: Verra serial number, project_type, vintage_year, quantity_retired, buyer_organization_name, issuance_date, platform_logo. Certificates stored in Cloud Storage with signed URLs. Buyer notification webhook POST /webhooks/retirement-complete includes certificate_download_url. Added CertificateService package and certificate table to data model.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Buyers receive signed retirement certificates as PDF download URLs upon successful registry confirmation

fix_report.action_taken: Defined mTLS client certificate lifecycle: certs issued by GCP Certificate Authority Service (CA Service), stored in Secret Manager with version labels. Cloud Scheduler triggers a Cloud Function every 80 days to request new cert from CA Service, write to Secret Manager, and update Cloud Run service env to reference new secret version. Old version retained for 10-day overlap.
fix_report.expected_outcome: mTLS certificates rotate automatically every 90 days without service disruption

target_output.title: Carbon Credit Retirement Automation Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 automation service on GCP Cloud Run that ingests buyer purchase orders via JWT-authenticated webhooks, matches them against available credit batches, executes retirements on the Verra VCS registry API with mTLS and idempotency guarantees, generates PDF retirement certificates via headless Chrome, and reconciles registry confirmations nightly. Financial transaction integrity is enforced through PostgreSQL advisory locks for batch matching, Redis SETNX idempotency keys, and an append-only retirement ledger.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single Go binary deployed on Cloud Run (min-instances=1) contains three goroutine groups: HTTP webhook receiver for inbound purchase orders, rate-limited retirement worker (leaky bucket at 10/minute consuming Redis RPUSH/BLPOP queue), and certificate generation worker. Cloud Run Job handles nightly reconciliation. PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL (Private Service Connect) stores purchase orders, credit batches, retirement events, and certificates. Redis Memorystore provides idempotency key cache (SETNX TTL 48h) and retirement job queue. mTLS client certificates for Verra API managed via GCP CA Service and Secret Manager with automated 90-day rotation.
target_output.technology_stack: Go 1.22 standard library (net/http, crypto/tls for mTLS, sync for concurrency). PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL (lib/pq driver). Redis 7 on Memorystore (go-redis client). PDF generation: chromedp (Go Chrome DevTools Protocol library) + headless Chrome sidecar container for certificate rendering. Cloud Storage for certificate PDF storage. GCP Secret Manager for mTLS certificates and database credentials. Cloud Run, Cloud Run Jobs, Cloud Scheduler, Cloud Build, Artifact Registry. Grafana + Prometheus for metrics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Purchase Order Ingestion: webhook receiver validates JWT buyer identity, checks idempotency key, creates purchase_order record, enqueues retirement job. Credit Batch Matching: MatchingEngine selects credit_batch rows by project_type and vintage_year using SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, reserves required quantity. Registry Retirement: RegistryClient sends Verra VCS retirement request with mTLS and idempotency header, persists verra_serial_number on confirmation. Certificate Issuance: CertificateService renders PDF via chromedp with serial number, project details, and buyer org, uploads to Cloud Storage, sends download URL to buyer via completion webhook. Nightly Reconciliation: ReconciliationJob pulls Verra confirmation log, joins against retirement_ledger, alerts on status mismatches.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Run service (3 goroutine groups in single binary): webhook receiver (port 8080), retirement worker (Redis BLPOP consumer with token bucket rate limiter), certificate worker (chromedp PDF renderer). Cloud Run Job: ReconciliationJob (nightly, Cloud Scheduler trigger). PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL (private VPC via PSC). Redis 7 Memorystore (same VPC). Cloud Storage bucket for certificate PDFs. Headless Chrome sidecar container image co-deployed with Go binary in Cloud Run service. GCP CA Service for mTLS cert issuance. Secret Manager for mTLS certs, DB credentials. Prometheus sidecar for metrics scraping.
target_output.workflows: Purchase Order to Retirement: Buyer platform POSTs /webhooks/purchase-orders with JWT and X-Idempotency-Key header. Webhook receiver validates JWT (buyer_organization_id claim verified against purchase_order.buyer_id), checks Redis SETNX on idempotency key, creates purchase_order record, pushes retirement job to Redis queue. Retirement worker dequeues job, acquires PostgreSQL advisory lock on matching credit_batch rows (SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED by project_type and vintage_year), reserves quantity, sets Redis idempotency SETNX for Verra call, POSTs to Verra VCS API with mTLS and idempotency header, stores verra_serial_number in retirement_event. Certificate Generation: On retirement_event.status=confirmed, certificate worker renders Jinja-equivalent Go HTML template via chromedp (fields: verra_serial_number, project_type, vintage_year, quantity_retired_tonnes, buyer_organization_name, issuance_date), generates PDF, uploads to Cloud Storage, sends buyer completion webhook POST with {retirement_event_id, certificate_download_url, verra_serial_number}. Nightly Reconciliation: Cloud Scheduler triggers Cloud Run Job at 02:00 UTC. Job fetches Verra daily confirmation export CSV, matches against retirement_ledger rows with status=submitted, flags any without matching Verra confirmation as discrepancy, inserts reconciliation_run record, alerts PagerDuty if discrepancy_count > 0.
target_output.data_model: purchase_order (id UUID PK, buyer_organization_id, project_type, vintage_year, quantity_tonnes, status ENUM pending/matched/retired/failed, idempotency_key UNIQUE, received_at). credit_batch (id UUID PK, project_type, vintage_year, available_quantity_tonnes, locked_quantity_tonnes, registry_account_id, created_at). registry_account (id, registry_type ENUM verra_vcs/gold_standard, account_identifier, mtls_cert_secret_name). retirement_event (id UUID PK, purchase_order_id FK, credit_batch_id FK, registry_account_id FK, verra_serial_number, idempotency_key UNIQUE, status ENUM pending/submitted/confirmed/failed_duplicate/failed, submitted_at, confirmed_at). certificate (id UUID PK, retirement_event_id FK UNIQUE, gcs_pdf_key, signed_url_expires_at, issued_at). reconciliation_run (id, run_date, records_checked, discrepancy_count, discrepancy_details_json, completed_at). retirement_ledger (id, retirement_event_id FK, from_status, to_status, trigger_source, registry_response_code, timestamp - APPEND ONLY).
target_output.api_design: Inbound webhook: POST /webhooks/purchase-orders (JWT Authorization header, X-Idempotency-Key required, body: {buyer_organization_id, project_type, vintage_year, quantity_tonnes}). Returns 202 Accepted with {purchase_order_id, status: 'queued'}. POST /webhooks/retirement-complete sent by platform to buyer: {retirement_event_id, purchase_order_id, verra_serial_number, certificate_download_url, retired_at}. Internal health: GET /health (Cloud Run health check). No public REST API surface. Registry API client (outbound): POST to Verra VCS /api/v1/retirements with mTLS client cert, body per Verra schema, X-Idempotency-Key header. GET /api/v1/retirements/daily-export (reconciliation job).
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS for all Verra VCS API connections: client certificates issued by GCP CA Service, stored in Secret Manager (secret version per certificate), loaded by Go TLS config at startup. Cloud Scheduler triggers Cloud Function every 80 days to issue new cert from CA Service, write new Secret Manager version, redeploy Cloud Run to pick up new cert; old version retained 10-day overlap before destruction. JWT validation on inbound buyer webhooks: verify signature against buyer_organization JWKS URL, validate buyer_organization_id claim matches purchase_order.buyer_id. Redis SETNX idempotency key (TTL 48h) before every Verra retirement call. Append-only retirement_ledger: INSERT-only enforced at DB role level (no UPDATE/DELETE granted to service account). Cloud Audit Logs for all Secret Manager access. Verra VCS and financial transaction audit requirements satisfied by retirement_ledger and 10-year retention.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Build pipeline: go vet, go test (unit + Verra sandbox integration tests), docker build (multi-stage: build + runtime + headless Chrome sidecar), push to Artifact Registry, deploy Cloud Run with traffic split 10%/90% canary before full cutover. Environments: dev (Cloud Run, Verra VCS sandbox), staging (mirrors production config, Verra sandbox), production (min-instances=1, max-instances=10, Cloud SQL HA). Database migrations via golang-migrate applied as Cloud Run Job pre-deploy. Secrets in Secret Manager, injected as env vars to Cloud Run. Rollback: Cloud Run revision traffic shift back to previous revision.
target_output.observability: Prometheus sidecar scrapes Go metrics endpoint /metrics. Key metrics: retirement_jobs_queued, retirement_success_rate, verra_api_latency_seconds, reconciliation_discrepancy_count, certificate_generation_duration_seconds. Grafana dashboards for retirement throughput, queue depth, and registry API health. PagerDuty: reconciliation_discrepancy_count > 0 (P1), Verra API error rate > 5% (P2), retirement_jobs_queued > 50 for > 5 minutes (P3). Cloud Logging: structured JSON with retirement_event_id correlation ID on every log line.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $480/month: Cloud Run ~$80 (min-instances=1 + burst), Cloud SQL db-custom-2-8GB ~$150, Redis Memorystore M1 Basic ~$35, Cloud Storage for certificates ~$5, CA Service ~$20, Cloud Build ~$10, miscellaneous ~$30. Under budget at 500 retirements/day. Scale: Cloud Run auto-scales to max-instances=10 for burst volumes; Redis queue provides back-pressure against Verra 10/min rate limit.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Verra VCS retirement integration with mTLS, purchase order webhook ingestion with JWT auth, credit batch matching with SELECT FOR UPDATE, idempotency enforcement, certificate PDF via chromedp, buyer completion webhook, nightly reconciliation Cloud Run Job, Cloud Build pipeline, mTLS automated rotation. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Gold Standard registry client, multi-currency pricing on purchase orders, real-time retirement status event stream to buyer platforms. Phase 3: Bulk retirement optimization, marketplace API for credit batch discovery.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: go vet and staticcheck in CI. All registry API interactions wrapped in RegistryClient interface with mock implementation for unit tests. Integration tests run against Verra VCS sandbox in CI. PostgreSQL migrations via golang-migrate with down migration scripts validated in staging. idempotency_key must be passed through the entire call chain from webhook to Verra API - enforced by function signatures.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Headless Chrome sidecar adds ~200MB to Cloud Run container image and startup latency. Mitigation: pre-warm via min-instances=1; certificate generation is async so does not block retirement confirmation. Risk: Verra VCS API changes break retirement payload format. Mitigation: RegistryClient versioned interface with Verra API version pinned in config. Tradeoff: Single Go binary with goroutine groups over microservices for operational simplicity at MVP volume - module boundaries allow future extraction. Risk: Redis BLPOP queue loses in-flight jobs on Cloud Run restart. Mitigation: retirement_event.status=pending rows act as durable recovery source on startup.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected chromedp over WeasyPrint for certificate PDF generation - Go-native, no Python dependency. Confirmed GCP CA Service over self-signed certs for mTLS - automated rotation and audit trail. Credit batch matching uses SKIP LOCKED not advisory locks for better concurrent throughput. Buyer completion webhook chosen over polling endpoint for certificate delivery.

metadata.notes: Carbon credit retirement automation round 2 - moderate quality, ISS-001 certificate issuance and ISS-002 mTLS rotation both addressed. Go on Cloud Run with financial-grade idempotency.

profile.domain: Carbon Offset API Platform Landing Page

project_goal.value: Launch a high-converting landing page for a carbon offset API platform targeting SaaS companies and e-commerce businesses, showcasing developer-friendly docs, pricing tiers, and a live API sandbox
project_goal.rationale: Primary developer acquisition surface for API sign-ups and partner conversations

target_users.value: Developer and engineering leads at SaaS companies and e-commerce platforms evaluating carbon neutrality checkout integrations
target_users.rationale: Target ICP identified through market research

project_class.rationale: Single-purpose marketing page with no server-side runtime or persistent data

capabilities.rationale: Static page with embedded analytics; no backend or auth required

complexity_level.rationale: Static content with a client-side sandbox widget; no backend logic

risk_level.rationale: No user data collected beyond optional waitlist email; no PII persistence

data_sensitivity.rationale: Page collects only voluntary waitlist emails forwarded to third-party email service

external_exposure.rationale: Publicly accessible marketing page with no authentication

access_model.value: No authentication; fully public read access
access_model.rationale: Marketing page must be accessible without friction

feature_scope.value: Pricing tier comparison section, live API sandbox code widget, developer documentation links, waitlist signup form, use-case examples for SaaS and e-commerce, partner logo bar
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set identified during design sprint

mvp_scope.value: Hero section with value proposition, pricing tier cards, live sandbox widget calling a sample carbon offset endpoint, waitlist signup form
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum needed to drive conference launch sign-ups

security_baseline.value: HTTPS only via Netlify HTTPS enforcement, CSP headers, no inline scripts, no third-party cookies
security_baseline.rationale: Static page security baseline; no backend attack surface

frontend_stack.rationale: Zero-JS-by-default static output ideal for landing page performance; TypeScript for sandbox widget type safety

backend_stack.rationale: No server-side runtime; Netlify Forms handles waitlist without a serverless function

data_platform.value: none - no database; waitlist emails forwarded to ConvertKit via Netlify form submissions
data_platform.rationale: Static site does not persist data

hosting_target.rationale: Netlify tight integration with GitHub Actions deploys and native form handling

privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Zero-persistence architecture eliminates data retention obligations

future_scope.value: Full developer documentation portal, interactive API key issuance flow, customer case study pages
future_scope.rationale: Post-launch expansion once API is generally available

constraints.rationale: SEO and performance requirements for developer audience trust; cookie banner harms conversion

observability_baseline.rationale: Cookie consent banner would harm conversion; Plausible requires no consent

execution_preference.rationale: Small scope warrants single sprint

project.goal: High-converting developer-focused landing page for carbon offset API
project.target_users: SaaS and e-commerce developer leads

security.transport: HTTPS enforced by Netlify, CSP headers via netlify.toml
security.privacy: No cookies, Plausible Analytics cookieless script

data.forms: Netlify form submissions forwarded to ConvertKit

reasoner_reviews.product: The feature set is tightly scoped for a developer audience. The live sandbox widget is the highest-value differentiator -- a developer should be able to execute a real carbon offset API call and see a JSON response within 30 seconds of landing on the page. Pricing tier cards should make per-API-call cost immediately legible for engineering leads doing back-of-envelope integration math.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 islands architecture is the right choice: the sandbox widget ships as a hydrated React island while the rest of the page emits zero JS. Pricing tier data should live in a TypeScript data file so marketing can update rates without touching component code. Netlify Forms eliminates any need for a serverless function for waitlist capture.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP headers must explicitly allowlist the sandbox widget fetch target origin and Plausible script origin. No inline scripts permitted -- sandbox widget must load as a module. No third-party cookies means no consent modal, directly improving conversion metrics.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 100KB JS budget is achievable with Astro islands -- only the sandbox React island ships JS. Lighthouse 95 on mobile requires AVIF/WebP images via Astro Image component, system font stack, and deferred island hydration.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and a React 18 island for the sandbox widget. Single index.astro page. BaseLayout.astro with SEO meta tags, Open Graph, and Plausible script injection.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single index.astro page with sections: Hero (value prop + primary CTA), Pricing (3-tier card grid from pricing-data.ts with Starter/Growth/Enterprise tiers), Sandbox (React island with live fetch to API sandbox endpoint and syntax-highlighted JSON response), Use Cases (SaaS checkout and WooCommerce integration code snippet tabs), Partner Logos, Waitlist Form (Netlify form).
frontend.performance_notes: Astro Image component for all images with AVIF/WebP and lazy loading. Sandbox island uses client:visible hydration to defer JS until section is in viewport. Tailwind CSS purged to < 10KB. No Google Fonts -- system font stack. Netlify CDN Cache-Control max-age=31536000 immutable on hashed static assets.
frontend.frontend_risks: Sandbox API unavailability breaks the primary conversion element -- implement graceful fallback to a static JSON response mock with an inline notice. Lighthouse regression risk when adding partner logo images; enforce CI gate.

security.auth_design: No authentication required; fully public page
security.authorization_model: No authorization model; read-only public static content
security.secrets_management: Sandbox widget uses a public read-only API key embedded at build time via Astro env vars, scoped to demo endpoint only
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Plausible custom events: sandbox_widget_executed, pricing_tier_cta_clicked (tier name), waitlist_form_submitted. No PII captured in any analytics event.

data.entities: pricing_tier (name, monthly_price_usd, api_calls_included, overage_rate_per_call, highlighted, cta_label) -- static TypeScript array. api_endpoint_demo (endpoint_path, method, sample_request_json, sample_response_json) -- static sandbox init data. use_case_example (title, industry, code_snippet_language, code_snippet) -- static array. waitlist_signup (email forwarded to ConvertKit, never stored locally). partner_logo (company_name, logo_svg_path, link_url).
data.storage_design: No database. All content in src/data/*.ts TypeScript files. Netlify Forms forwards waitlist submissions to ConvertKit with no local persistence.
data.analytics_events: Plausible custom events: sandbox_widget_executed (no PII), pricing_tier_cta_clicked (tier name only), waitlist_form_submitted (no email in event)

devops.deployment_topology: Netlify CDN with global edge nodes. GitHub repo triggers Netlify build on push to main. PR preview deployments auto-created per branch.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ESLint + Prettier lint, tsc --noEmit type-check, Astro build, Lighthouse CI with mobile score >= 95 gate. Netlify handles build and CDN deploy on merge to main. Slack notifications via Netlify webhook on deploy success/failure.
devops.observability_stack: Plausible Analytics dashboard for traffic, conversion funnel, and UTM campaign performance. Netlify build log monitoring. Slack alerts on failed production deployments.

target_output.thinking_summary: Static landing page for a developer-focused carbon offset API. Astro 4 islands architecture ships zero JS except the interactive sandbox widget React island. No backend, no database, no auth. The live API sandbox embed is the primary conversion driver. Plausible analytics avoids cookie consent friction. Netlify Forms handles waitlist without server-side logic.
target_output.title: Carbon Offset API Platform Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A static Astro 4 landing page deployed on Netlify CDN targeting developer leads at SaaS and e-commerce companies evaluating carbon offset API integration. The page features a live sandbox widget executing a real carbon offset API call with rendered JSON response, pricing tier comparison cards, SaaS and e-commerce use-case code snippets, and a waitlist form forwarded to ConvertKit. Zero cookies, Lighthouse >= 95 mobile, sub-100KB JS budget.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Astro 4 site with islands architecture: the page HTML is pre-rendered at build time with zero JavaScript shipped to the browser except for the interactive sandbox widget React island, which hydrates on client:visible. Content is authored in TypeScript data files for pricing tiers, API endpoint demos, and use-case code snippets. Netlify CDN serves the site globally with edge caching and HTTPS enforcement. Netlify Forms handles waitlist submission forwarding to ConvertKit without any server-side runtime. Plausible Analytics injects a lightweight cookie-free tracking script. CSP headers configured in netlify.toml allowlist only the sandbox fetch target origin, Plausible script origin, and self. No database, no auth provider, no server-side API surface.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS (purged to < 10KB), React 18 (sandbox island only). Build tooling: Astro CLI, tsc for type-checking, ESLint + Prettier. Hosting: Netlify CDN with global edge. CI: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI plugin. Analytics: Plausible Analytics (cookie-free). Form handling: Netlify Forms to ConvertKit integration. No backend runtime, no database, no auth provider.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: carbon offset API value proposition with primary CTA linking to sandbox section. Sandbox Widget: React island fetching the API sandbox endpoint with a sample e-commerce order payload, rendering JSON response with syntax highlighting showing offset_kg, certificate_id, and cost_usd. Pricing Tiers: Starter / Growth / Enterprise card grid from pricing-data.ts with per-call rates and monthly caps. Use-Case Tabs: SaaS checkout integration and WooCommerce checkout plugin code snippets. Partner Logo Bar: reference customer logos. Waitlist Form: Netlify form capturing email and UTM params, forwarded to ConvertKit.
target_output.system_components: Netlify CDN (global edge, HTTPS enforcement, form handling), Astro 4 build output (pre-rendered HTML + purged CSS + sandbox widget JS bundle), React 18 sandbox island (client:visible hydration, fetch to carbon offset API sandbox endpoint), Plausible Analytics (cookie-free self-hosted script), ConvertKit (external email list), GitHub Actions (lint, type-check, Astro build, Lighthouse CI gate, Netlify deploy trigger), src/data/ TypeScript files (pricing-data.ts, use-cases-data.ts, partners-data.ts, sandbox-demo-data.ts).
target_output.workflows: Developer Sandbox Interaction: Visitor scrolls to the Sandbox section, triggering client:visible hydration of the React island. Widget displays a pre-filled POST /v1/offsets request body with a sample $47.99 e-commerce order. Developer clicks Run -- island fetches the sandbox API endpoint using the build-time public read-only sandbox key. Response JSON displays with syntax highlighting showing offset_kg: 0.12, certificate_id: DEMO-2026-XXXXX, cost_usd: 0.004, status: issued. If sandbox is unavailable, static mock response displays with inline notice. Plausible event sandbox_widget_executed fires on execution. Waitlist Conversion: Visitor enters email into Netlify form. On submit, Netlify forwards the submission to ConvertKit tagged with UTM source and medium from hidden form fields populated via URL params. Plausible event waitlist_form_submitted fires. Visitor sees inline confirmation message.
target_output.data_model: pricing_tier (name VARCHAR, monthly_price_usd NUMERIC, api_calls_included INT, overage_rate_per_call NUMERIC, highlighted BOOL, cta_label VARCHAR) -- static TypeScript array in pricing-data.ts. api_endpoint_demo (endpoint_path VARCHAR, method VARCHAR, sample_request_json TEXT, sample_response_json TEXT, description VARCHAR) -- static sandbox initialization data. use_case_example (title VARCHAR, industry ENUM saas/ecommerce, code_snippet_language VARCHAR, code_snippet TEXT, outcome_description VARCHAR) -- static array in use-cases-data.ts. waitlist_signup (email -- forwarded to ConvertKit, never stored locally). partner_logo (company_name VARCHAR, logo_svg_path VARCHAR, link_url VARCHAR).
target_output.api_design: No server-side API. The sandbox widget performs one client-side fetch: POST https://sandbox.carbonoffset.api/v1/offsets with body {order_value_usd: 47.99, currency: USD, category: ecommerce} using a public read-only sandbox API key in the Authorization header. Response: {offset_kg: 0.12, certificate_id: DEMO-2026-XXXXX, cost_usd: 0.004, status: issued}. Netlify form submission POSTs to Netlify Forms edge endpoint. No custom API surface exists.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Netlify HTTPS enforcement with HSTS preloading. Content Security Policy via netlify.toml: default-src 'self', script-src 'self' module-only (no inline), connect-src sandbox API origin and Plausible origin, img-src 'self' data:. No third-party cookies; Plausible is cookieless and GDPR-compliant without consent. Sandbox widget uses public read-only API key scoped to demo endpoint only. No PII stored on Netlify infrastructure; waitlist emails held only in ConvertKit.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ESLint + Prettier lint, tsc --noEmit type-check, Astro build, Lighthouse CI mobile score >= 95 gate (blocks merge if failing). On merge to main: Netlify auto-deploy via GitHub integration. PR preview deployments auto-created per branch with unique Netlify preview URLs for stakeholder review. Deploy success/failure Slack notification via Netlify webhook. Cache headers: hashed static assets Cache-Control max-age=31536000 immutable; HTML Cache-Control no-cache must-revalidate for instant content propagation.
target_output.observability: Plausible Analytics dashboard: daily active visitors, sandbox_widget_executed conversion rate, pricing_tier_cta_clicked by tier, waitlist_form_submitted by UTM source/medium. Lighthouse CI score trend in GitHub Actions. Netlify build duration and deploy success rate. Slack alert on failed production deploys. No server-side runtime metrics -- fully static architecture has no runtime error surface.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$0/month on Netlify Starter (free tier supports launch traffic). Plausible Analytics: $9/month self-hosted or $9/month cloud. ConvertKit: free tier for under 1000 subscribers. Total launch cost: ~$9/month. Netlify CDN auto-scales with traffic at no additional cost within Starter bandwidth (100GB/month). Upgrade to Netlify Pro ($19/month) if bandwidth exceeds 100GB.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (2 weeks): Hero, pricing tier cards, sandbox widget React island, waitlist form with ConvertKit forwarding, Plausible analytics, CSP headers, Lighthouse CI gate, Netlify deploy pipeline. Phase 2 (1 week post-launch): UTM tracking in hidden form fields, use-case tabbed code snippets for SaaS and WooCommerce, partner logo bar, Open Graph and Twitter card meta for social sharing. Phase 3 (post-API GA): Replace sandbox widget mock with live API key issuance flow, add customer case study pages, A/B test hero headline copy via Netlify split testing.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro components for all static sections; React only for the sandbox island. All variable content in src/data/*.ts files -- no hardcoded strings in .astro templates. Tailwind classes purged; CSS target < 10KB. No inline scripts: ESLint rule enforced. All images via Astro Image component with AVIF output and explicit width/height to prevent layout shift. PR merge requires Lighthouse CI mobile >= 95.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Sandbox API downtime breaks primary conversion element. Mitigation: graceful fallback to static JSON mock with an inline notice so the page remains functional. Risk: Lighthouse score regression when adding partner logo images. Mitigation: CI gate blocks merge on score drop. Tradeoff: Astro over Next.js for true zero-JS-by-default; this is the correct choice for a near-pure-static landing page where the only interactive element is the sandbox island. Risk: ConvertKit deliverability issues for waitlist confirmations. Mitigation: double opt-in enabled.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose Plausible over Google Analytics to eliminate cookie consent modal that would harm conversion. Confirmed Netlify Forms over a custom serverless function for waitlist capture -- reduces operational surface. Selected client:visible hydration for sandbox island to defer JS load until the section enters the viewport. Deferred full developer docs portal to Phase 3 post-API launch.

metadata.notes: Strong first-pass static landing page for developer-targeted carbon offset API with Astro islands, live sandbox widget, and Plausible analytics.

profile.domain: Environmental Data API Documentation Static Site

project.goal: Static documentation hub for open-source environmental data API
project.target_users: Researchers, journalists, civic developers

security.csp: CSP headers blocking inline scripts and third-party origins except Cloudflare Analytics

reasoner_reviews.product: The scope is well-calibrated for an open-source project documentation site. Civic developers primarily want curl-runnable examples they can paste directly. The sensor coverage map is a strong differentiator for journalists verifying their geographic area is covered before committing to the API. Changelog and contributor guide are critical for community adoption.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Hugo is the correct generator choice for a docs site that will grow to hundreds of pages -- build times stay sub-30s even with large Markdown corpora. Pagefind is the right search choice over Algolia DocSearch: it generates a client-side search index at build time with no external service dependency, keeping the zero-cost constraint intact. Tailwind purging in Hugo requires PostCSS pipeline setup but produces minimal CSS output.
reasoner_reviews.security: Static site attack surface is near-zero. CSP must block all inline scripts to prevent XSS from any community-contributed Markdown with embedded HTML. Cloudflare Pages provides WAF and DDoS protection at the CDN layer. No secrets exist in this project -- no API keys, no auth tokens.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Hugo build time under 30 seconds is achievable even with 500+ pages. Cloudflare Pages free tier supports unlimited static requests with 500 builds/month -- well within limits for an open-source community project. All Markdown content is editable by non-engineers via GitHub PRs, satisfying the low-barrier contribution requirement.

frontend.app_structure: Hugo site with custom theme using Tailwind CSS. Content structure: content/api-reference/ (per-endpoint Markdown), content/data-sources/ (per sensor type), content/guides/ (getting started, code examples), content/changelog/, content/contributing/. Layouts: baseof.html, api-endpoint.html, data-source.html, guide.html.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home page with API overview and quick-start curl example. API Reference section with one page per endpoint (air-quality-index, water-quality, wildfire-risk, sensor-list). Data Sources section with pages per sensor type (EPA AQS, USGS water sensors, NOAA fire weather). Guides section for Python, JavaScript, and R integration examples. Changelog page. Contributor Guide. Pagefind search overlay accessible from nav.
frontend.performance_notes: Hugo partial caching for repeated shortcodes. Images processed via Hugo Pipes with WebP output. Tailwind JIT mode with PostCSS for purged CSS output < 15KB. Pagefind search index built post-Hugo-build in CI. Cloudflare CDN edge caching with immutable headers for hashed assets.
frontend.frontend_risks: Pagefind index size may grow large as content scales beyond 500 pages -- monitor index size in CI and set a 5MB budget alert. Tailwind PostCSS integration with Hugo requires careful pipeline configuration; document setup in CONTRIBUTING.md.

security.auth_design: No authentication; fully public docs site
security.authorization_model: No authorization; all content is publicly readable
security.secrets_management: No secrets; no API keys or tokens in this project
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Cloudflare Web Analytics aggregate page view counts. GitHub Actions build logs. No PII collected anywhere in the pipeline.

data.entities: api_endpoint_doc (slug, title, http_method, path, description, parameters_yaml, curl_example, response_example_json, sensor_types, changelog_entry_ids) -- Markdown with YAML frontmatter. data_source (slug, title, sensor_type, provider_agency, geographic_coverage, update_frequency, api_endpoint_ids) -- Markdown. code_example (language, title, snippet, linked_endpoint) -- shortcode or Markdown. changelog_entry (version, date, changes_list) -- Markdown. contributor_guide (sections) -- Markdown.
data.storage_design: No database. All content stored as Markdown files with YAML frontmatter in the GitHub repository under content/. Hugo reads content at build time and generates static HTML.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloudflare Pages project linked to GitHub repository. Automatic builds triggered on push to main. PR preview deployments auto-created for community contributor PRs.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Hugo build with Tailwind PostCSS, Pagefind index generation post-build, HTML validation via htmlhint, broken link check via lychee. On merge to main: Cloudflare Pages auto-deploy. Build time enforced < 30s via CI timeout. Status badge in repository README.
devops.observability_stack: Cloudflare Web Analytics dashboard: page views by endpoint doc, geographic distribution of visitors, top referrers. GitHub Actions build success rate and duration trend. No server-side monitoring needed.

target_output.thinking_summary: Static documentation site for an open-source environmental data API. Hugo is the right generator for a large, community-maintained Markdown corpus. Cloudflare Pages provides zero-cost global CDN. Pagefind handles client-side search at build time with no external service. No backend, no database, no auth. The key challenge is structuring content taxonomy for three distinct sensor domains (air quality, water quality, wildfire risk) so civic developers can find endpoint docs quickly.
target_output.title: Environmental Data API Documentation Hub
target_output.executive_summary: A static Hugo documentation site deployed on Cloudflare Pages CDN providing API reference docs, data source descriptions, and integration code examples for an open-source environmental data API aggregating air quality index, water quality, and wildfire risk sensor feeds from public government sources. Targets researchers, journalists, and civic developers. Zero hosting cost on Cloudflare Pages free tier, Pagefind client-side search, and Cloudflare Web Analytics with no cookies.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Hugo 0.122 site with Tailwind CSS, deployed on Cloudflare Pages CDN. Content is authored as Markdown files with YAML frontmatter in the GitHub repository, organized into api-reference, data-sources, guides, and changelog sections. Hugo reads all Markdown at build time and emits pre-rendered HTML in under 30 seconds. A post-build Pagefind step indexes the output HTML to generate a client-side search index requiring no external service. Cloudflare Pages auto-deploys on merge to main and creates PR preview deployments for community contributor review. Cloudflare Web Analytics provides cookieless aggregate page view tracking. No server-side runtime, no database, no user accounts.
target_output.technology_stack: Static generator: Hugo 0.122 with Tailwind CSS via PostCSS pipeline. Search: Pagefind (client-side, built post-Hugo-build in CI). Hosting: Cloudflare Pages CDN (free tier, global edge). CI: GitHub Actions (Hugo build, Tailwind PostCSS, Pagefind index, htmlhint validation, lychee broken link check). Analytics: Cloudflare Web Analytics (cookieless). Content format: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. No backend runtime, no database, no auth provider.
target_output.functional_feature_map: API Reference section: one Markdown page per endpoint with HTTP method, path, parameters table, curl example, and JSON response example for air-quality-index, water-quality, wildfire-risk, and sensor-list endpoints. Data Sources section: per-sensor-type pages describing EPA AQS, USGS water sensor network, and NOAA fire weather stations with geographic coverage and update frequency. Guides section: getting started guide and code examples in Python, JavaScript, and R. Changelog page with version history. Contributor Guide with PR workflow and Markdown authoring instructions. Pagefind search overlay accessible from persistent nav header.
target_output.system_components: Cloudflare Pages CDN (global edge, HTTPS, PR preview deployments), Hugo 0.122 static generator (Markdown to HTML, Tailwind PostCSS pipeline), Pagefind client-side search index (built in CI post-Hugo-build, served as static JSON shards), Cloudflare Web Analytics script (cookieless, injected via Hugo partial), GitHub Actions (build pipeline with broken link check and HTML validation), GitHub repository (Markdown content store + CI trigger).
target_output.workflows: Content Contribution Workflow: Community contributor forks the repository, adds or edits Markdown in content/api-reference/ or content/data-sources/, opens a pull request. GitHub Actions runs Hugo build, Pagefind index generation, htmlhint, and lychee broken link check. Cloudflare Pages auto-creates a preview deployment URL visible in the PR. Maintainer reviews rendered preview and merges. Cloudflare Pages auto-deploys to production. Developer Onboarding Workflow: Civic developer visits the docs site, searches for 'air quality' via Pagefind overlay, lands on the air-quality-index endpoint page, copies the curl example for their geographic bounding box parameters, reads the JSON response schema, then navigates to the Python guide for an SDK integration example. They submit a GitHub issue for a missing sensor region using the Issue button on the endpoint page.
target_output.data_model: api_endpoint_doc (slug VARCHAR, title VARCHAR, http_method ENUM GET/POST, path VARCHAR, description TEXT, parameters_yaml TEXT, curl_example TEXT, response_example_json TEXT, sensor_types VARCHAR[], changelog_entry_ids VARCHAR[]) -- Hugo content with YAML frontmatter in content/api-reference/. data_source (slug, title, sensor_type ENUM air_quality/water_quality/wildfire_risk, provider_agency, geographic_coverage, update_frequency, last_updated) -- content/data-sources/. code_example (language ENUM python/javascript/r, title, snippet TEXT, linked_endpoint_slug) -- embedded shortcode in guide Markdown. changelog_entry (version, release_date, change_type ENUM added/changed/deprecated/removed/fixed, description) -- content/changelog/. contributor_guide (sections) -- content/contributing/.
target_output.api_design: No server-side API. The documentation site itself has no API surface. Client-side search is served via Pagefind static index shards: GET /pagefind/pagefind.js (search library), GET /pagefind/index/*.pf_index (index shards loaded on demand). All other requests are static HTML, CSS, and image files served by Cloudflare CDN. The documented API endpoints (air-quality-index, water-quality, wildfire-risk) are external to this site and documented via curl examples in Markdown.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cloudflare Pages HTTPS with automatic TLS certificate management. Content Security Policy header via Cloudflare Pages headers file: default-src 'self', script-src 'self' (Pagefind loaded as module), style-src 'self', img-src 'self' data:, connect-src 'self' (Pagefind index shards only). No inline scripts -- Hugo templates use external script references only. Cloudflare WAF and DDoS mitigation at CDN layer. No cookies, no PII collected. No secrets in repository -- no API keys or tokens exist in this project.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline on pull_request and push to main: (1) Install Hugo 0.122 and Node.js for PostCSS; (2) Hugo build with --minify flag and 30-second timeout; (3) Pagefind index build over Hugo output directory; (4) htmlhint validation on generated HTML; (5) lychee broken link scan with allowlist for external sensor data URLs that may be intermittently unavailable. On merge to main: Cloudflare Pages auto-deploys from GitHub integration in under 60 seconds. PR preview deployments automatically created with unique Cloudflare Pages preview URL for each open PR. Build status badge embedded in repository README. Rollback: re-deploy any previous Cloudflare Pages deployment via dashboard in under 30 seconds.
target_output.observability: Cloudflare Web Analytics: daily page views by endpoint doc URL, top referrer domains (GitHub, HackerNews, civic tech newsletters), geographic distribution of visitors. Most-visited endpoint docs inform documentation improvement priority. GitHub Actions build duration trend visible in workflow history. Lychee broken link report artifacts uploaded per CI run to catch degraded external sensor agency URLs. No server-side runtime metrics needed.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Hosting: $0/month on Cloudflare Pages free tier (unlimited static requests, 500 builds/month). Cloudflare Web Analytics: $0 (included). GitHub Actions: within free tier for public repository. Total cost: $0/month. Cloudflare CDN auto-scales globally with traffic at no cost increase. If build count exceeds 500/month due to high community PR volume, upgrade to Cloudflare Pages Pro ($20/month) is the only scaling action needed.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (3 weeks): API reference pages for air-quality-index, water-quality, wildfire-risk, and sensor-list endpoints with curl examples and JSON response schemas; data source pages for EPA AQS, USGS, and NOAA; getting started guide; changelog; GitHub Actions CI with Hugo build and Pagefind; Cloudflare Pages deploy. Phase 2 (ongoing community contributions): Python, JavaScript, and R integration code examples; sensor coverage map (static SVG or embedded iframe); contributor guide; Pagefind search overlay. Phase 3 (post-v2 API release): Versioned docs structure (v1/ and v2/ subdirectories), interactive sensor region browser, community forum integration link in nav.
target_output.development_guidelines: All content in Markdown with YAML frontmatter -- no hardcoded content in Hugo templates. Hugo archetypes for api-endpoint and data-source content types to enforce consistent frontmatter schema. curl examples in Markdown code fences with language tag 'bash'. JSON response examples in code fences with language tag 'json'. Broken link check in CI blocks merge. Hugo build must complete in under 30 seconds -- profile with --templateMetrics flag if build time grows. CONTRIBUTING.md documents the Markdown authoring workflow for non-engineer contributors.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: External sensor agency URLs (EPA AQS, USGS, NOAA) used in documentation examples may become temporarily unavailable, causing lychee broken link check failures. Mitigation: allowlist known-intermittent external URLs in lychee config. Risk: Pagefind index size grows as content scales to 500+ pages. Mitigation: monitor Pagefind index size in CI with 5MB budget gate. Tradeoff: Hugo over Docusaurus for build speed and no Node.js runtime dependency for content contributors; the downside is Hugo templating is less familiar to JavaScript-focused civic developers. Risk: Community Markdown contributions may include embedded HTML with inline scripts. Mitigation: CSP blocks all inline scripts at Cloudflare layer.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose Pagefind over Algolia DocSearch to eliminate external service dependency and maintain zero-cost constraint. Selected Hugo over Docusaurus for sub-30-second build times at scale. Confirmed Cloudflare Web Analytics over Plausible to keep hosting cost at zero. Deferred versioned API docs to Phase 3 pending v2 API release timeline.

metadata.notes: Strong first-pass static docs site for open-source environmental data API with Hugo, Pagefind, and zero-cost Cloudflare Pages hosting.

project_goal.value: Fullstack platform for grid operators and distributed energy resource aggregators to manage virtual power plant dispatch events, tracking battery storage, EV chargers, and demand response enrollments
project_goal.rationale: Core operational need for VPP orchestration replacing manual grid operator workflows

target_users.value: Grid operators, DER aggregators, and VPP program administrators managing distributed energy resources
target_users.rationale: Primary operational personas identified in grid operator interviews

project_class.rationale: Rich operational dashboard frontend plus high-performance Rust backend for real-time dispatch

mvp_scope.rationale: Settlement and NERC audit reporting deferred to Phase 2

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3, Keycloak MFA for grid_operator and aggregator_admin roles, mTLS for DER device telemetry ingestion, RBAC separation of duties for dispatch authorization vs execution, append-only dispatch audit log
security_baseline.rationale: NERC CIP critical infrastructure security requirements

frontend_stack.rationale: React ecosystem for complex real-time dashboard components; TypeScript for type safety in dispatch state management

backend_stack.rationale: Rust for sub-millisecond dispatch fan-out latency and memory safety in critical infrastructure context

data_platform.rationale: TimescaleDB for energy telemetry time-series with continuous aggregates; Redis for real-time dispatch state and pub/sub

hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with VPC isolation; us-east-1 for grid operator latency requirements

privacy_retention_policy.value: Dispatch events and energy measurements retained 7 years per FERC Order 2222 settlement audit requirements; audit logs retained 3 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FERC and NERC record retention requirements for grid operations

constraints.rationale: Grid response time requirements and data residency for US grid operations

project.goal: VPP dispatch management platform for grid operators and DER aggregators
project.target_users: Grid operators, aggregator admins, and resource managers

frontend.state: Redux Toolkit for dispatch event state, React Query for telemetry data
frontend.realtime: WebSocket via socket.io-client for live telemetry feeds

backend.framework: Axum with Tokio async runtime
backend.api: REST + WebSocket for real-time telemetry push

security.auth: Keycloak with OIDC, MFA for grid_operator and aggregator_admin
security.transport: TLS 1.3, mTLS for device telemetry ingestion
security.dispatch_auth: Dual-approval for dispatch events above 1MW aggregate

data.cache: Redis 7 for dispatch state and pub/sub

devops.deployment: Blue-green with ALB weighted target groups

open_questions.optimization_engine: ML-based dispatch optimization - deferred to Phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The 500ms dispatch fan-out to 10,000 resources is the core performance SLA. Resource enrollment management is the operational backbone - resources must be reliably enrolled before any dispatch event can include them. Settlement period measurement must be accurate for FERC Order 2222 settlement calculations.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Rust Axum with Tokio is the right choice for the dispatch fan-out hot path. Dispatch should use Redis pub/sub to propagate the signal to all resource WebSocket connections held in separate Axum handler tasks. TimescaleDB continuous aggregates can pre-compute 15-minute settlement period averages to avoid expensive real-time aggregation. Round 1 lacked detailed WebSocket architecture for real-time telemetry push from resources to the platform.
reasoner_reviews.security: NERC CIP requires separation of duties: grid_operator can create dispatch events, but execution of events above 1MW aggregate requires a second grid_operator approval. This dual-approval must be enforced at the API layer. Keycloak event logging for all authentication and authorization decisions must be forwarded to the SIEM. mTLS on device telemetry ingestion endpoint prevents spoofed resource readings.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Sub-500ms fan-out to 10,000 resources at 1KB dispatch payload = 10MB burst. Axum with Tokio connection pool and Redis pub/sub can achieve this. Fargate task sizing: 2 vCPU / 4GB for dispatch service. Blue-green deployment is critical for grid operations - downtime during peak demand events is unacceptable.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 added WebSocket architecture but the settlement period data model was still sparse. Hypertable continuous aggregate definition for 15-minute settlement windows needs explicit specification in this round.

backend.service_design: Rust Axum monolith with separate handler modules: dispatch (event creation, fan-out, dual-approval), enrollment (resource registration and program membership), telemetry (mTLS ingestion endpoint, Redis pub/sub publish), settlement (continuous aggregate queries), admin (VPP program management, user roles).
backend.api_patterns: REST for CRUD operations (dispatch events, enrollments, VPP programs). WebSocket endpoint /ws/telemetry for resource devices pushing measurements. WebSocket endpoint /ws/dispatch-feed for frontend subscribing to real-time dispatch state updates via Redis pub/sub subscriber.
backend.failure_handling: Dispatch fan-out: Tokio spawn per-resource dispatch notification; failed deliveries recorded in dispatch_event_delivery table with retry up to 3 attempts. Telemetry ingestion: drop malformed measurements with structured error log; never block on ingestion backpressure. Circuit breaker on downstream grid signal API.
backend.scaling_notes: Dispatch service: 2 vCPU/4GB Fargate tasks, auto-scale on custom metric dispatch_events_queued. Redis Cluster for telemetry pub/sub at >10K concurrent resource connections.

frontend.app_structure: React 18 SPA with Redux Toolkit for dispatch event state machine (idle/pending_approval/active/completed). React Query for telemetry historical data. socket.io-client for real-time telemetry and dispatch feed WebSockets.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Grid Operator Dashboard (active dispatch events, resource availability map), Dispatch Event Creator (program selector, resource group, MW target, dual-approval workflow), Enrollment Manager (resource list, enrollment status, capacity), Telemetry Monitor (live resource measurement feed), Settlement Viewer (15-minute period summaries), Admin Panel (VPP programs, user roles).
frontend.state_management: Redux Toolkit slices: dispatchSlice (active events and approvals), enrollmentSlice (resource enrollment status). React Query for telemetry historical queries. Redux middleware for WebSocket message dispatch.
frontend.performance_notes: Virtualized list (react-window) for resource enrollment table with 10,000+ rows. Telemetry charts use recharts with data windowing - display last 500 measurements per resource to prevent DOM thrashing.

security.auth_design: Keycloak OIDC with PKCE flow. MFA (TOTP) enforced via Keycloak authentication flow for grid_operator and aggregator_admin roles. Axum middleware validates Keycloak JWTs on all REST routes.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: grid_operator can create and first-approve dispatch events; second grid_operator approval required for events >1MW aggregate (enforced in DispatchService.approve()). aggregator_admin manages their own organization's resource enrollments. resource_manager read-only on telemetry. auditor read-only on all data. Keycloak resource server with fine-grained authorization policies.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Append-only dispatch_audit_log: event_id, user_id, action ENUM create/approve/execute/cancel, grid_signal_value, timestamp. Keycloak event listener forwards auth events to CloudWatch Logs. NERC CIP requires logs retained 90 days in immutable storage (S3 Object Lock).
security.incident_response_notes: Automated dispatch rollback: if resource telemetry shows aggregate response deviating >20% from dispatch target after 60 seconds, alert grid_operator via PagerDuty for manual cancellation decision.

data.entities: vpp_program, distributed_resource, dispatch_event, resource_enrollment, grid_signal, energy_measurement, settlement_period
data.storage_design: TimescaleDB on PostgreSQL 16 (RDS): energy_measurement as hypertable partitioned by measured_at with 1-week chunks. Redis 7 ElastiCache for dispatch state (HSET per dispatch_event_id) and telemetry pub/sub channels.
data.schema_notes: TimescaleDB continuous aggregate: settlement_period_15min (resource_id, window_start, avg_kw, sum_kwh, measurement_count) - refreshed every 5 minutes. Supports FERC Order 2222 settlement reporting without real-time aggregation load.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster with three task definitions: vpp-api (Axum dispatch+enrollment+admin, 2 vCPU/4GB), vpp-telemetry (Axum telemetry ingestion + Redis pub/sub, 2 vCPU/4GB, mTLS listener), vpp-frontend (nginx serving React build). ALB with weighted target groups for blue-green. ElastiCache Redis Cluster in private subnet. RDS TimescaleDB in private subnet.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: cargo clippy, cargo test, Docker multi-stage build (Rust builder + distroless runtime), push to ECR, ECS rolling deployment with health check gate. Blue-green: shift 10% traffic to new task set, monitor 5 min, then shift 100%.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM via dd-trace-rs in Axum middleware. Custom StatsD metrics: dispatch_fanout_latency_ms, telemetry_messages_per_second, resource_response_rate. PagerDuty: dispatch failure rate >2%, telemetry ingestion lag >30s, ECS task health check failures.

target_output.title: VPP Dispatch Management Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A fullstack virtual power plant dispatch management platform built on React 18 and Rust Axum, deployed on AWS ECS Fargate with TimescaleDB and Redis. Enables grid operators to create and fan out dispatch events to up to 10,000 enrolled battery, EV charger, and demand response resources with sub-500ms latency. Dual-approval enforcement for dispatch events above 1MW satisfies NERC CIP separation of duties. TimescaleDB continuous aggregates provide FERC Order 2222 settlement period measurements. Keycloak provides OIDC authentication with MFA for critical roles.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA served via ECS nginx communicates with a Rust Axum API service over TLS 1.3. Dispatch fan-out uses Redis pub/sub: Axum publishes dispatch signals to a channel per VPP program, and per-connection Tokio tasks subscribed to Redis push signals to resource device WebSocket connections. A separate Axum telemetry ingestion service accepts mTLS connections from DER devices and publishes energy_measurement records to TimescaleDB and Redis pub/sub for the frontend live feed. TimescaleDB continuous aggregate settlement_period_15min pre-computes 15-minute window sums for FERC settlement reporting. Keycloak on a dedicated ECS task provides OIDC with RBAC and MFA enforcement. All dispatch actions are logged to an append-only dispatch_audit_log in S3 Object Lock for NERC CIP.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Redux Toolkit, React Query, socket.io-client, recharts (telemetry charts), react-window (virtualized resource list). Backend: Rust stable, Axum, Tokio, sqlx (async PostgreSQL), redis-rs, rustls for mTLS. Database: TimescaleDB 2.x on PostgreSQL 16 (RDS Custom). Cache/pub-sub: Redis 7 ElastiCache Cluster. Auth: Keycloak 24 on ECS Fargate. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with weighted target groups (blue-green), ECR, S3 Object Lock for audit logs. Monitoring: Datadog APM with dd-trace-rs.
target_output.functional_feature_map: VPP Program Management: admin creates VPP programs with resource types (battery/ev_charger/demand_response), capacity targets, and dispatch rules. Resource Enrollment: aggregator_admin registers distributed_resource devices with program enrollment and mTLS device certificate binding. Dispatch Event Creation: grid_operator creates dispatch event with MW target and resource group; events >1MW require second grid_operator approval via PATCH /dispatch-events/:id/approve. Dispatch Fan-out: approved event triggers Redis pub/sub broadcast to all enrolled resource WebSocket connections with dispatch payload. Real-time Telemetry: DER devices push energy_measurement records via mTLS WebSocket; frontend subscribes to Redis pub/sub feed for live capacity display. Settlement Period Reporting: pre-aggregated 15-minute energy measurements from TimescaleDB continuous aggregate for FERC Order 2222 settlement calculations. NERC Audit Log: append-only dispatch_audit_log in S3 Object Lock.
target_output.system_components: ECS Fargate task: vpp-api (Rust Axum, dispatch/enrollment/settlement/admin handlers, 2 vCPU/4GB, 3 replicas). ECS Fargate task: vpp-telemetry (Rust Axum, mTLS WebSocket ingestion, Redis pub/sub publish, 2 vCPU/4GB, 2 replicas). ECS Fargate task: vpp-frontend (nginx serving React build). ECS Fargate task: Keycloak 24 (with PostgreSQL backend on separate RDS instance). ALB with weighted target groups for blue-green deployment. TimescaleDB on RDS Custom PostgreSQL 16 (private subnet). Redis 7 ElastiCache Cluster (private subnet, 3 shards for pub/sub at 10K connections). S3 bucket with Object Lock for NERC audit log export. Datadog agent sidecar on each ECS task.
target_output.workflows: Dispatch Event with Dual Approval: grid_operator POSTs /dispatch-events with {vpp_program_id, mw_target, duration_minutes, resource_group_filter}. Axum creates dispatch_event with status=created. If mw_target > 1.0: status set to pending_approval, response includes approval_required: true. Second grid_operator (different user_id) PATCHes /dispatch-events/:id/approve; Axum middleware verifies Keycloak role=grid_operator AND caller.keycloak_sub != event.created_by_keycloak_sub; status transitions to approved. Axum DispatchService publishes {event_id, dispatch_signal_kw, duration_minutes} to Redis channel vpp:{vpp_program_id}:dispatch. Tokio tasks per enrolled resource subscribed to the channel push the payload over resource WebSocket connections.
