Dispatch fan-out latency target: <500ms from PATCH /approve to last resource WebSocket delivery for 10,000 resources. Settlement Reporting: Aggregator queries GET /settlement/periods?vpp_program_id=&from=2026-01-01&to=2026-01-31. Axum handler queries TimescaleDB continuous aggregate settlement_period_15min by window_start range and vpp_program_id. Returns array of {window_start, resource_id, avg_kw, sum_kwh} pre-computed rows. Response time <100ms regardless of measurement volume.
target_output.data_model: vpp_program (id UUID PK, name, program_type ENUM battery/ev_charger/demand_response/mixed, operator_org_id, mw_capacity_target, created_at). distributed_resource (id UUID PK, vpp_program_id FK, resource_type ENUM battery/ev_charger/demand_response, aggregator_org_id, device_mtls_cert_fingerprint, status ENUM enrolled/suspended/decommissioned, enrolled_at). resource_enrollment (id, resource_id FK, vpp_program_id FK, capacity_kw, enrollment_status, effective_date). dispatch_event (id UUID PK, vpp_program_id FK, status ENUM created/pending_approval/approved/active/completed/cancelled, mw_target, duration_minutes, created_by_keycloak_sub, approved_by_keycloak_sub, created_at, approved_at, activated_at). dispatch_event_delivery (id, dispatch_event_id FK, resource_id FK, delivery_status ENUM sent/acked/failed, delivered_at). energy_measurement (resource_id, measured_at TIMESTAMPTZ, kw FLOAT, kwh FLOAT, measurement_source - TimescaleDB hypertable on (resource_id, measured_at) with 1-week chunks). settlement_period_15min CONTINUOUS AGGREGATE: SELECT resource_id, vpp_program_id, time_bucket('15 minutes', measured_at) AS window_start, AVG(kw) AS avg_kw, SUM(kwh) AS sum_kwh, COUNT(*) AS measurement_count FROM energy_measurement GROUP BY resource_id, vpp_program_id, window_start - WITH add_continuous_aggregate_policy refresh interval 5 minutes. dispatch_audit_log (id, dispatch_event_id, user_keycloak_sub, action ENUM create/approve/activate/cancel, timestamp - append-only, exported nightly to S3 Object Lock).
target_output.api_design: REST under /api/v1. POST /dispatch-events (grid_operator role required, body: vpp_program_id, mw_target, duration_minutes; returns dispatch_event with approval_required flag). PATCH /dispatch-events/:id/approve (grid_operator role, second approver check, transitions pending_approval->approved and triggers fan-out). GET /dispatch-events?vpp_program_id=&status= (list with pagination). PATCH /dispatch-events/:id/cancel (grid_operator, cancels active or approved event). GET /enrollments?vpp_program_id= (resource enrollment list, react-window paginated). POST /enrollments (aggregator_admin registers device). GET /settlement/periods?vpp_program_id=&from=&to= (pre-aggregated from continuous aggregate). WebSocket /ws/dispatch-feed (frontend subscribes to Redis pub/sub for live dispatch state). WebSocket /ws/telemetry (mTLS endpoint for DER device measurement push). Keycloak JWT required on all REST routes via Axum middleware.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Keycloak OIDC PKCE flow for frontend. MFA (TOTP) enforced via Keycloak authentication flow for grid_operator and aggregator_admin roles. Axum middleware validates Keycloak JWTs and extracts roles. Dual-approval enforcement: Axum DispatchApprovalGuard middleware on PATCH /dispatch-events/:id/approve verifies caller role=grid_operator AND caller.keycloak_sub != dispatch_event.created_by_keycloak_sub; returns 403 if same user attempts self-approval. mTLS for DER device telemetry endpoint: device certificate fingerprint validated against distributed_resource.device_mtls_cert_fingerprint at connection time. Append-only dispatch_audit_log exported nightly to S3 Object Lock (Compliance mode, 3-year retention) for NERC CIP immutable log requirement. Keycloak event listener streams auth/authz decisions to CloudWatch Logs for SIEM forwarding. NERC CIP-002/010 asset inventory satisfied by vpp_program and distributed_resource registry.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: cargo clippy --deny warnings, cargo test, Docker multi-stage build (Rust builder + distroless runtime image), push to ECR, ECS blue-green deployment via CodeDeploy with ALB weighted target groups. Shift 10% traffic to new task set, monitor Datadog p99 dispatch latency and error rate for 5 minutes, then shift 100%. Rollback: CodeDeploy automatic rollback on health check failure. Environments: dev (single replicas, LocalStack for Redis/S3), staging (mirrors production, synthetic dispatch load tests), production (3 vpp-api + 2 vpp-telemetry + 1 vpp-frontend + Keycloak replicas). TimescaleDB schema migrations via sqlx migrate run as ECS one-off task pre-deploy.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM via dd-trace-rs instrumentation in Axum handlers. Custom metrics via StatsD: dispatch_fanout_latency_ms (histogram), dispatch_events_approved_per_hour (counter), resource_websocket_connections (gauge), telemetry_measurements_per_second (counter), settlement_query_latency_ms (histogram). PagerDuty alerts: dispatch_fanout_latency_ms p99 >500ms (P1 - SLA breach), dispatch_event_delivery failure rate >5% for any active event (P1), telemetry ingestion lag >30s (P2), Keycloak auth failure spike >10/min (P2). Datadog dashboard: live dispatch map, resource response rate per event, settlement period energy chart.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2800/month: ECS Fargate (6 tasks at 2vCPU/4GB ~$1200), RDS TimescaleDB db.r6g.large ~$700, ElastiCache Redis Cluster 3-shard ~$400, Keycloak RDS ~$150, ALB ~$50, S3+data transfer ~$100, Datadog ~$200. Scale: add vpp-telemetry replicas for >10K resource connections; Redis Cluster shards scale horizontally for pub/sub throughput; TimescaleDB continuous aggregate offloads settlement query load.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (20 weeks): VPP program creation, resource enrollment with mTLS cert binding, dispatch event creation with dual-approval state machine, Redis pub/sub fan-out to resource WebSocket connections, telemetry ingestion and live frontend feed, TimescaleDB continuous aggregate for settlement, Keycloak RBAC with MFA, NERC audit log to S3 Object Lock, ECS blue-green pipeline. Phase 2 (8 weeks): NERC CIP compliance report automation, weather-adjusted capacity forecasting, settlement export to FERC-format CSV. Phase 3: ML dispatch optimization engine, grid frequency response automation.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust: clippy --deny warnings, all public API functions documented. sqlx compile-time query verification (sqlx::query_as! macros). Integration tests use real TimescaleDB in Docker Compose with synthetic device WebSocket simulators. No unsafe code blocks in dispatch hot path. State machine transitions enforced via Rust enum with exhaustive match - impossible state transitions are compile errors.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Redis ElastiCache Cluster pub/sub at 10K concurrent device connections may saturate at peak dispatch. Mitigation: shard resources across Redis channels by vpp_program_id, benchmark with 10K synthetic WebSocket clients in load test. Risk: TimescaleDB continuous aggregate refresh lag (5-min policy) means settlement data is up to 5 minutes stale. Mitigation: refresh interval acceptable for FERC settlement reporting which is day-prior. Tradeoff: Rust Axum monolith over microservices for dispatch and telemetry services - Tokio async runtime handles concurrency without service mesh overhead at MVP scale. Risk: Keycloak as single-tenant ECS task is a SPOF. Mitigation: Keycloak clustered in Phase 2; session cache in Redis for frontend token validation resilience.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Dual-approval threshold set at 1MW aggregate per NERC CIP engineering judgment - configurable per VPP program in Phase 2. TimescaleDB continuous aggregate chosen over materialized view for automatic incremental refresh. Redis pub/sub chosen over Kafka for dispatch fan-out - latency-optimized at expense of replay capability (replay not required for dispatch signals). S3 Object Lock Compliance mode confirmed for NERC CIP immutable log - Governance mode insufficient.

metadata.notes: VPP dispatch platform round 3 - moderate quality, ISS-002 settlement aggregate and ISS-003 dual-approval both resolved. Rust Axum with NERC CIP compliance.

project_goal.value: Desktop application for environmental consultants to run Gaussian plume air dispersion simulations, visualize pollutant isocontour maps, and export EPA-format compliance reports for state air permit applications.
project_goal.rationale: Core workflow replacing manual AERMOD spreadsheet calculations.

target_users.value: Air quality engineers and environmental consultants at consulting firms preparing federal and state air permit applications.
target_users.rationale: Primary user group identified in stakeholder interviews.

project_class.rationale: Computation-heavy Gaussian plume solver must run offline on consultant workstations.

capabilities.rationale: Electron UI, embedded Python sidecar, SQLite persistence, and isocontour analytics.

complexity_level.rationale: Gaussian plume physics, geospatial isocontour rendering, and EPA-format PDF generation are non-trivial.

risk_level.rationale: Client-confidential project data; report accuracy has direct regulatory implications for air permits.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Simulation inputs are client confidential but not regulated personal or financial data.

external_exposure.rationale: Runs fully offline on consultant workstations; no external network endpoints.

access_model.value: Single-user desktop with optional AES-256 project file encryption for client confidentiality on shared workstations.
access_model.rationale: No multi-user auth required; project files are the access control boundary.

feature_scope.value: Emission source stack configuration, AERMET meteorological file import, Gaussian plume simulation engine, isocontour map rendering, receptor point analysis, pollutant species library, PDF and CSV regulatory report export.
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for air permit application support.

mvp_scope.value: Single-source Gaussian plume simulation with Pasquill-Gifford stability class selection, static isocontour export, and tabular ground-level concentration report for receptor points.
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP validates the core solver before multi-source superposition and advanced GIS features.

security_baseline.value: Optional AES-256 project file encryption; no outbound network traffic; Sentry DSN embedded at build time and never in plaintext config files.
security_baseline.rationale: Protects client simulation data on shared workstations.

frontend_stack.value: Electron 30 with React 18 and TypeScript; Mapbox GL JS for isocontour map rendering.
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform desktop with high-performance GIS canvas rendering.

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI as PyInstaller-bundled IPC sidecar; NumPy and SciPy for Gaussian plume computation; WeasyPrint for PDF generation.
backend_stack.rationale: Python scientific ecosystem; FastAPI gives a debuggable REST contract over localhost IPC.

data_platform.value: SQLite 3 (one database file per project); GeoJSON export for isocontour interoperability.
data_platform.rationale: Embedded database with no server dependency; GeoJSON enables downstream GIS tool workflows.

hosting_target.value: Desktop installer packages for Windows 11 and macOS 14 distributed via GitHub Releases.
hosting_target.rationale: Direct installer delivery to consulting firm workstations.

privacy_retention_policy.value: All simulation data stored locally; no telemetry beyond opt-in Sentry crash reports with file paths stripped.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Client project data must never leave the consultant workstation.

future_scope.value: Multi-source superposition, AERMOD-compatible input parser, cloud project sync, and team collaboration features.
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap for multi-person consulting team workflows.

constraints.value: Must run fully offline; Python sidecar cold start under 3 seconds; 100-receptor simulation completes within 30 seconds on a 4-core laptop.
constraints.rationale: Field use case where consultants work on-site without internet connectivity.

observability_baseline.value: Sentry desktop SDK for opt-in crash reporting; structured JSON stderr logs from Python sidecar captured by Electron main.
observability_baseline.rationale: Crash visibility without invasive telemetry.

execution_preference.value: Feature-driven iterations with biweekly internal demos; GitHub Actions for cross-platform signed release builds.
execution_preference.rationale: Small team with rapid feedback cycles.

compliance_context.value: EPA AERMOD modeling guidance compliance for report output formats; California CARB and Texas TCEQ air permit application format requirements.
compliance_context.rationale: Reports submitted to regulators must meet agency format standards.

project.goal: Offline Gaussian plume modeling and EPA-format report generation desktop tool
project.target_users: Environmental consultants at air quality consulting firms

frontend.mapping: Mapbox GL JS for isocontour overlay rendering
frontend.state: Zustand for simulation state; React Query for FastAPI sidecar IPC

backend.framework: FastAPI sidecar on 127.0.0.1:47291, PyInstaller-bundled
backend.computation: NumPy + SciPy Gaussian plume solver; WeasyPrint PDF renderer

security.auth: None (single-user local app)

data.export: GeoJSON isocontours, CSV receptor tables, PDF compliance reports

devops.ci: GitHub Actions matrix builds via electron-builder

reasoner_reviews.product: The offline-first constraint is the dominant design driver. The PyInstaller-bundled FastAPI sidecar cleanly separates Gaussian plume computation from the Electron UI. MVP scope of single-source modeling is correct to validate solver accuracy against EPA test cases before introducing multi-source superposition complexity.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI on localhost:47291 is the right IPC boundary — it keeps NumPy entirely out of the Electron renderer process and provides a debuggable REST interface. PyInstaller --onedir is preferable to --onefile for the 3-second cold start requirement. d3-contour Web Worker prevents UI jank on dense 200x200 receptor grids. The per-project SQLite file design supports project portability between workstations.
reasoner_reviews.security: No external network exposure minimizes the attack surface. The main concern is Mapbox GL JS token exposure in the shipped binary — scope the token to tile reads only and monitor for unusual tile request volumes. AES-256 project file encryption is well-calibrated for shared-workstation risk.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Vectorized NumPy Gaussian plume computation meets the 30-second simulation budget for 100 receptors. Cold start under 3 seconds requires pre-importing FastAPI and NumPy modules at sidecar startup regardless of pending requests. electron-builder dual-platform CI pipeline should be prototyped in week 1.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Mapbox GL JS introduces an API key that must be managed in the installer binary — recommend evaluating MapLibre GL JS (MIT-licensed fork) as a drop-in to eliminate the licensing dependency before the first paying client ships a permit application built with this tool.

backend.service_design: FastAPI sidecar on 127.0.0.1:47291: POST /simulate (enqueue run), GET /simulate/{run_id}/status, GET /simulate/{run_id}/results (GeoJSON grid), POST /simulate/{run_id}/report (PDF), POST /parse/aermet (AERMET surface parser), GET /pollutants (species library), GET /health.
backend.business_modules: GaussianPlumeEngine (Pasquill-Gifford sigma per Briggs formulas, wind profile power law, Briggs plume rise equations). AERMETParser (surface and upper-air file ingestion, stability frequency distribution). ReceptorGridBuilder (Cartesian and polar grid generation). ReportRenderer (Jinja2 HTML to EPA-format PDF via WeasyPrint; CARB and TCEQ template variants).
backend.failure_handling: Sidecar crash detected by Electron main via process exit event; auto-restart with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s max). Simulation runs timeout at 120 seconds. Failed runs persist error_message to SQLite simulation_runs table.
backend.backend_risks: PyInstaller bundles produce 250-400 MB installers and can trigger Windows Defender false positives. OpenBLAS vs MKL BLAS selection for NumPy significantly affects computation speed and must be pinned in requirements.txt.

frontend.app_structure: Electron main manages sidecar subprocess lifecycle, project file AES-256 I/O, and contextBridge IPC. React 18 SPA renderer with routes: /projects, /sources, /meteorology, /simulate, /results/:runId, /report/:runId.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Project Manager (open/create/save/encrypt project files). Source Configuration (stack geometry: height, diameter, exit velocity, temperature, UTM coordinates, pollutant species). Meteorology Panel (AERMET import, stability class frequency chart). Simulation Runner (stability class selector, wind speed, receptor grid config, progress bar). Results Viewer (Mapbox isocontours with threshold selector, receptor click-to-value). Report Export (template selector, PDF preview, download).
frontend.state_management: Zustand store for active project, simulation status, and isocontour thresholds. React Query for async FastAPI calls with retry on 503 sidecar-not-ready during cold start. d3-contour runs in Web Worker; GeoJSON results posted to main thread.
frontend.performance_notes: Concentration grids over 10,000 receptors processed entirely in Web Worker to prevent UI thread blocking. Mapbox GL JS GeoJSON source updated incrementally as each contour level completes.

security.auth_design: No authentication required. Electron contextBridge enforces renderer isolation — no Node.js fs, child_process, or net APIs exposed to React renderer components.
security.secrets_management: Mapbox GL JS token embedded as build-time environment variable via webpack DefinePlugin. Sentry DSN compiled into binary. No plaintext secrets in config files shipped with installer.
security.security_risks: Mapbox token extractable from installer binary (mitigated by URL-restriction and tile-read-only scope). Project files readable by other local users on shared workstations (mitigated by optional AES-256 encryption).

data.entities: projects (id UUID PK, name, description, db_path, encrypted BOOL, created_at, updated_at). emission_source_stacks (id UUID PK, project_id FK, stack_name, easting_m, northing_m, height_m, diameter_m, exit_velocity_ms, exit_temp_k, emission_rate_gs, pollutant_id FK). meteorological_datasets (id UUID PK, project_id FK, station_id, data_year INT, aermet_surface_path, stability_distribution_json). simulation_runs (id UUID PK, project_id FK, source_id FK, met_dataset_id FK, stability_class, wind_speed_ms, receptor_grid_type ENUM cartesian/polar, receptor_spacing_m, status ENUM queued/running/completed/failed, peak_conc_ug_m3, receptors_count, started_at, completed_at, error_message). receptor_results (id UUID PK, run_id FK, easting_m, northing_m, concentration_ug_m3) INDEX (run_id, concentration_ug_m3 DESC). pollutant_species (id UUID PK, symbol, name, molar_mass, aaqs_1hr_ug_m3, aaqs_annual_ug_m3). report_templates (id UUID PK, name, jurisdiction, format_version, template_html).
data.storage_design: One SQLite file per project in user-selected directory. receptor_results indexed (run_id, concentration_ug_m3 DESC) for fast isocontour threshold queries on 500K+ row grids. Bulk insert via SQLAlchemy executemany for simulation result writes.
data.data_risks: receptor_results table grows to 500K+ rows for dense grids requiring bulk insert strategy. SQLite WAL mode reduces write contention during concurrent sidecar writes and Electron main reads.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions matrix: ubuntu-latest (macOS cross-compile via electron-builder) and windows-latest (NSIS, EV code-signed). PyInstaller --onedir bundles Python sidecar per platform. macOS DMG signed with Apple Developer ID and notarized via xcrun notarytool.
devops.ci_cd_design: PR checks: pytest GaussianPlumeEngine with EPA validation fixtures (1% tolerance gate), React Testing Library UI tests, electron-playwright E2E (project create, simulation run, PDF export). Tag push: build signed installers, electron-playwright against packaged build, publish to GitHub Releases with auto-update manifest.
devops.observability_stack: Sentry in Electron main (unhandled exceptions, sidecar crash events) and Python sidecar (simulation failures with run_config context, file paths stripped). Rolling log file in OS app-data directory (10 MB, 3 rotations).
devops.devops_risks: macOS notarization requires consistent Apple ID credentials; Gatekeeper rejects unsigned builds. Windows EV certificate renewal must be tracked annually.

target_output.thinking_summary: This desktop air dispersion modeling tool is dominated by the offline-first constraint. The PyInstaller-bundled FastAPI sidecar on localhost:47291 cleanly separates Gaussian plume computation from the Electron UI without requiring a Python runtime on the target machine. SQLite per-project files match the single-user local workflow. The compliance angle is output format accuracy for EPA permit submissions, not access control — the GaussianPlumeEngine must be regression-tested against EPA AERMOD validation datasets in CI as a blocking gate.
target_output.title: Air Dispersion Modeling Desktop Application Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A cross-platform offline desktop application for environmental consultants running Gaussian plume air dispersion simulations and generating EPA-format regulatory reports for state air permit applications. Built with Electron 30 and React 18 for the UI and a PyInstaller-bundled Python 3.12 FastAPI sidecar for Gaussian plume computation. Project data persists in per-project SQLite files. Pollutant concentration isocontours render in Mapbox GL JS; PDF compliance reports are generated via WeasyPrint with EPA AERMOD-aligned Jinja2 templates.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-process desktop architecture: Electron main process manages application lifecycle, project file I/O with optional AES-256-GCM encryption, and spawns the PyInstaller-bundled Python FastAPI sidecar on 127.0.0.1:47291 at startup. The React 18 renderer communicates with the sidecar via REST calls proxied through Electron contextBridge IPC, keeping Node.js APIs isolated from the renderer context. Gaussian plume computation runs in Python using NumPy vectorized equations and SciPy Pasquill-Gifford sigma parameterization. Receptor concentration grids are returned as GeoJSON and processed into isocontour polygons by d3-contour in a Web Worker, then rendered as Mapbox GL JS fill layers over an offline MBTiles basemap. WeasyPrint converts Jinja2 HTML templates to EPA-format PDFs in the sidecar. One SQLite database file is maintained per project in a user-selected directory.
target_output.technology_stack: Desktop: Electron 30, React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Zustand, React Query, Mapbox GL JS, d3-contour. Sidecar: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Uvicorn, NumPy, SciPy, WeasyPrint, SQLAlchemy, Jinja2. Build: electron-builder (NSIS/DMG), PyInstaller --onedir, GitHub Actions matrix. Observability: Sentry desktop SDK. Tests: pytest with EPA validation fixtures, React Testing Library, electron-playwright.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Project Manager: create, open, save, and AES-256-GCM encrypt project files. Source Configuration: define emission stack geometry (height, diameter, exit velocity, temperature, UTM coordinates) and pollutant species with NAAQS averaging periods. Meteorology Panel: import AERMET surface files, display Pasquill-Gifford stability frequency distribution histogram. Simulation Runner: select stability class and mean wind speed, configure Cartesian or polar receptor grid, monitor live receptor progress. Results Viewer: render isocontours at configurable µg/m3 thresholds on Mapbox basemap; click receptor point to view concentration and NAAQS comparison. Report Export: choose EPA jurisdiction template (CARB, TCEQ), preview and download PDF with stack parameter tables, isocontour map, and maximum ground-level concentration table.
target_output.system_components: Electron Main (sidecar subprocess manager, contextBridge IPC router, AES-256 project file I/O). PyInstaller Sidecar (GaussianPlumeEngine, AERMETParser, ReceptorGridBuilder, WeasyPrint ReportRenderer). React Renderer (Zustand state, React Query IPC client, Mapbox GL JS map, d3-contour Web Worker). SQLite Project Database (per-project, SQLAlchemy ORM, WAL mode). Offline MBTiles Basemap (bundled with installer). GitHub Releases (EV-signed Windows NSIS and Apple-notarized macOS DMG).
target_output.workflows: Simulation Workflow: Consultant opens a project, configures an emission stack (stack height 38 m, exit velocity 14 m/s, NO2 emission rate 6.2 g/s, UTM 487300 E 3812500 N), imports a 5-year AERMET dataset for the facility meteorological station, selects Pasquill-Gifford D stability class at 4 m/s wind speed, and triggers simulation. The sidecar computes concentrations across a 200x200 Cartesian receptor grid at 50 m spacing using vectorized Gaussian plume equations with Briggs plume rise correction, writes 40,000 receptor rows to SQLite via executemany, and returns peak concentration and GeoJSON grid. The renderer draws isocontours at 50, 100, 200, and 400 µg/m3 thresholds on the offline basemap. Report Export Workflow: Consultant selects the completed NO2 simulation, chooses the Texas TCEQ NSR permit template, previews the auto-generated PDF showing stack parameter table, 1-hour NO2 isocontour map with NAAQS 188 µg/m3 threshold highlighted, and top-50 receptor maximum concentration table, then exports the signed PDF for inclusion in the permit application package.
target_output.data_model: projects (id UUID PK, name TEXT, description TEXT, db_path TEXT, encrypted BOOL, created_at TIMESTAMP, updated_at TIMESTAMP). emission_source_stacks (id UUID PK, project_id FK, stack_name TEXT, easting_m REAL, northing_m REAL, height_m REAL, diameter_m REAL, exit_velocity_ms REAL, exit_temp_k REAL, emission_rate_gs REAL, pollutant_id FK). meteorological_datasets (id UUID PK, project_id FK, station_id TEXT, data_year INT, aermet_surface_path TEXT, aermet_upperair_path TEXT, stability_distribution_json TEXT). simulation_runs (id UUID PK, project_id FK, source_id FK, met_dataset_id FK, stability_class TEXT, wind_speed_ms REAL, receptor_grid_type ENUM cartesian/polar, receptor_spacing_m INT, status ENUM queued/running/completed/failed, peak_conc_ug_m3 REAL, receptors_count INT, started_at TIMESTAMP, completed_at TIMESTAMP, error_message TEXT). receptor_results (id UUID PK, run_id FK, easting_m REAL, northing_m REAL, concentration_ug_m3 REAL) -- INDEX (run_id, concentration_ug_m3 DESC). pollutant_species (id UUID PK, symbol TEXT, name TEXT, molar_mass REAL, aaqs_1hr_ug_m3 REAL, aaqs_annual_ug_m3 REAL). report_templates (id UUID PK, name TEXT, jurisdiction TEXT, format_version TEXT, template_html TEXT).
target_output.api_design: FastAPI sidecar REST on 127.0.0.1:47291. POST /simulate {source_id, met_dataset_id, stability_class, wind_speed_ms, grid_type, spacing_m} returns {run_id}. GET /simulate/{run_id}/status returns {status, progress_pct, elapsed_ms, receptors_computed}. GET /simulate/{run_id}/results returns {peak_conc_ug_m3, receptor_grid_geojson, contour_levels_geojson}. POST /simulate/{run_id}/report {template_id} returns {pdf_base64}. POST /parse/aermet {file_path} returns {station_id, data_year, stability_distribution}. GET /pollutants returns species library list. GET /health returns {status, sidecar_version}. No auth required on loopback interface.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Electron contextBridge enforces renderer isolation -- no fs, child_process, or net APIs exposed to React. Mapbox GL JS token scoped to tile reads, embedded via webpack DefinePlugin at build time; never in plaintext shipped config. Project file AES-256-GCM encryption via Python cryptography library with PBKDF2HMAC key derivation (SHA-256, 390000 iterations) from user passphrase. Sentry crash reports opt-in, stripping absolute file paths from stack traces before transmission. EPA AERMOD compliance: Gaussian plume sigma parameterization validated against EPA OAQPS AERMOD validation dataset; report template format verified against CARB and TCEQ permit application guidance documents. Passing the 1% tolerance gate against EPA validation dataset is a CI blocking requirement.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions matrix: ubuntu-latest (macOS cross-compile via electron-builder) and windows-latest (NSIS with EV certificate from WINDOWS_CERT_P12 GitHub secret). PyInstaller --onedir bundles Python sidecar per platform with explicit --hidden-import flags for NumPy BLAS and SciPy modules. macOS DMG signed with Apple Developer ID Application and notarized via xcrun notarytool (APPLE_ID_APP_PASSWORD secret). Release pipeline on semver tag: builds installers, runs electron-playwright smoke suite against packaged build, publishes artifacts to GitHub Releases with electron-updater auto-update manifest JSON. Consultants receive update notifications on app startup via electron-updater polling GitHub Releases API.
target_output.observability: Sentry desktop SDK in Electron main (unhandled exceptions, sidecar crash events with restart count as extra) and Python sidecar (simulation failures with run_config JSON, file paths stripped). Structured JSON log lines from sidecar written to stderr and captured by Electron main to a rolling log file in the OS app-data directory (10 MB max, 3 rotations). Key events: simulation_started (receptor_count, stability_class), simulation_completed (duration_ms, peak_conc), simulation_failed (error_type, run_id), report_generated (template_id, pdf_size_kb), aermet_parse_failed (error_line_number).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting costs for fully local execution. GitHub Actions free tier covers CI/CD for the open-source repo. electron-builder artifacts on GitHub Releases under 500 MB at no extra cost. Optional Sentry free tier covers the initial consulting pilot (under 5000 errors/month). Future cloud project sync estimated at under $10/month per active consultant at typical project file sizes of 10-50 MB.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (16 weeks): PyInstaller-bundled FastAPI sidecar with single-source Gaussian plume solver (Pasquill-Gifford A-F, Briggs plume rise), AERMET surface file parser, Cartesian receptor grid up to 200x200, Mapbox GL JS isocontour rendering, one EPA state permit PDF template (TCEQ NSR), optional AES-256 project encryption, Windows and macOS signed installers via GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Multi-source superposition, polar receptor grid, full pollutant species library with NAAQS thresholds, AERMET upper-air file support, custom report template editor, CARB and NJDEP permit template variants. Phase 3 (4 weeks): AERMOD-compatible input file parser for migrating legacy consultant runs, electron-updater auto-update, offline MBTiles basemap bundle for fully air-gapped field use.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in Electron renderer. Ruff + Black for Python sidecar. GaussianPlumeEngine must achieve 90% line coverage via pytest with EPA AERMOD validation case fixtures at 1% concentration tolerance -- this is a CI blocking gate. React Testing Library for UI component tests. electron-playwright E2E for project creation, simulation run, isocontour render, and PDF export flows. All PRs require passing CI suite and one reviewer approval. PyInstaller builds regression-tested against E2E suite on each release branch before version tagging.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PyInstaller installers (250-400 MB) trigger Windows Defender false positives on unsigned builds -- mitigated by EV code signing and MAPS submission. Risk: Mapbox GL JS token extractable from binary via inspection -- mitigated by URL-domain restriction and tile-read-only scope enforcement. Risk: GaussianPlumeEngine sigma parameterization deviation from EPA AERMOD reference model produces non-compliant permit report outputs -- mitigated by EPA validation dataset CI gate at 1% tolerance. Tradeoff: FastAPI HTTP IPC over Electron native IPC channels -- HTTP chosen for debuggability and clean Python-side API design, accepting slightly higher overhead than native IPC.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Mapbox GL JS over Leaflet for isocontour rendering performance on dense 200x200 grids. Confirmed PyInstaller --onedir over --onefile for 3-second cold start target. Chose per-project SQLite files over shared app database for project portability between workstations. WeasyPrint selected over Puppeteer to eliminate headless Chromium dependency from the desktop installer bundle.

metadata.notes: Offline-first desktop Gaussian plume air dispersion modeling tool with PyInstaller Python sidecar; EPA AERMOD compliance validation in CI.

project_goal.value: Build a secure data pipeline that ingests satellite-derived deforestation alerts from multiple earth observation providers, normalizes geospatial data to WGS84, validates change-detection events, and delivers them to downstream conservation NGO systems via signed webhooks
project_goal.rationale: Core mission: replace ad-hoc manual satellite data downloads with an automated, auditable pipeline for conservation partners

target_users.value: Conservation NGO technical teams receiving validated deforestation alert webhooks and environmental data scientists querying historical alert datasets
target_users.rationale: Two distinct consumer personas identified: real-time alert subscribers and retrospective analysis users

project_class.rationale: Batch and streaming data ingestion, normalization, validation, and delivery; no user-facing browser UI

capabilities.rationale: Geospatial data ingestion from multiple providers, batch processing, and partner webhook delivery

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-provider satellite feed normalization, polygon deduplication, geospatial CRS reprojection, and signed webhook delivery with retry logic

risk_level.rationale: Confidential commercial satellite data from multiple providers; partner data handling agreements; GDPR for EU-based NGOs

data_sensitivity.rationale: Commercial satellite imagery change-detection data subject to provider data licensing agreements; some alert data commercially sensitive before public disclosure

external_exposure.rationale: Webhook delivery targets are registered NGO partner endpoints; no public internet access to raw data

access_model.value: Custom JWT for NGO partner API access; signed HMAC-SHA256 webhook payloads for delivery verification; service account credentials for satellite provider feed ingestion
access_model.rationale: Partners must verify webhook authenticity; service accounts isolate per-provider ingest credentials

feature_scope.value: Multi-provider satellite feed ingestion (NICFI, Planet, JAXA ALOS), WGS84 coordinate reprojection, overlapping polygon deduplication, compliance threshold validation, HMAC-signed webhook delivery to registered NGO endpoints, delivery receipt logging, audit trail
feature_scope.rationale: Full pipeline scope required to replace manual downloads across 3 satellite data providers

mvp_scope.value: Single-provider ingestion (NICFI), WGS84 reprojection, basic polygon deduplication, webhook delivery to 3 pilot NGO partners
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP scoped to one provider and three partners for validation before multi-provider expansion

security_baseline.value: Encryption at rest via Cloud KMS, TLS 1.3 for all network calls, HMAC-SHA256 signed webhook payloads, per-provider service account isolation, audit logging for all data access and delivery events
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential satellite data and partner data agreements require defense-in-depth

frontend_stack.rationale: No user-facing browser UI; pipeline operated via Cloud Composer DAG UI and monitoring dashboards

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow on Cloud Composer (managed Airflow), GDAL/pyproj for geospatial processing
backend_stack.rationale: Airflow is the standard for batch DAG orchestration; GDAL/pyproj for CRS reprojection and polygon operations

data_platform.value: PostGIS on PostgreSQL 16 for geospatial alert storage; GCS buckets for raw satellite feed staging and validated alert archives
data_platform.rationale: PostGIS native support for polygon storage, spatial indexing, and deduplication via ST_Intersects; GCS for raw data staging

hosting_target.value: GCP: Cloud Composer for Airflow, Cloud SQL PostGIS, GCS, Cloud Functions for webhook delivery worker
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Composer eliminates Airflow self-hosting overhead; Cloud SQL for managed PostGIS

privacy_retention_policy.value: Validated deforestation alerts retained indefinitely for conservation research; raw satellite feeds purged from GCS staging after 30 days; audit logs retained 7 years per ISO 27001
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Conservation research value of historical alert data; provider agreements allow long-term validated data retention

future_scope.value: Real-time alert streaming via Cloud Pub/Sub, Planet and JAXA ALOS provider integrations, self-service NGO partner registration portal
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP expansion to additional providers and streaming delivery

constraints.value: Pipeline must process and deliver alerts within 4 hours of satellite imagery availability; budget under $2500/month; all data must remain in GCP europe-west1 for GDPR compliance
constraints.rationale: NGO partners have 4-hour SLA for alert notification; GDPR data residency for EU-based NGOs

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for pipeline metrics; Cloud Composer DAG run monitoring; PagerDuty for ingestion failures and delivery SLA breaches
observability_baseline.rationale: Conservation partners depend on alert delivery timeliness; operational monitoring is non-negotiable

execution_preference.value: Milestone-based delivery with Cloud Build CI/CD
execution_preference.rationale: GCP-native CI aligns with Cloud Composer deployment workflow

compliance_context.value: GDPR for EU-based partner NGOs (data residency in europe-west1, right to erasure for any personal data in alert metadata), ISO 27001 data handling policy for confidential satellite data, provider licensing agreements for NICFI data (no public redistribution of raw imagery)
compliance_context.rationale: EU NGO partners are data controllers; pipeline is a data processor under GDPR; ISO 27001 required by data licensing agreements

project.goal: Automated geospatial deforestation alert pipeline from satellite providers to NGO partners
project.target_users: Conservation NGO technical teams and data scientists
project.timeline: Q2 MVP for 3 pilot NGO partners

backend.framework: Apache Airflow on Cloud Composer, GDAL/pyproj, GeoPandas
backend.orchestration: Airflow DAGs triggered by GCS object creation event

security.auth: Custom JWT for partner API, HMAC-SHA256 for webhook signing, service accounts per provider

data.staging: GCS buckets for raw feed staging and alert archives

devops.hosting: Cloud Composer, Cloud SQL, Cloud Functions

open_questions.dedup_strategy: ST_Intersects threshold for polygon overlap deduplication

reasoner_reviews.product: The 4-hour delivery SLA is the critical operational constraint. NGO partners use deforestation alerts for field team dispatching -- a 6-hour alert is operationally less useful. The pipeline must prioritize throughput for the ingestion-to-delivery path over analytical query performance. Historical query access for data scientists is secondary and can run on read replicas.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Airflow on Cloud Composer is the right orchestration choice for batch DAGs with complex dependency graphs. The geospatial processing step -- CRS reprojection from provider-native projections to WGS84 and polygon overlap deduplication via PostGIS ST_Intersects -- is the most CPU-intensive step. Consider a separate Cloud Composer worker pool with higher CPU for the normalization task group. The HMAC-SHA256 webhook signing should use per-partner secrets stored in Secret Manager, not a global signing key.
reasoner_reviews.security: Per-provider service account isolation is critical: a compromised NICFI ingest credential must not affect Planet or JAXA feeds in the future. All data in GCS staging buckets must be encrypted with Cloud KMS customer-managed keys, not Google-managed keys. Audit events must include provider_feed_id and downstream_consumer_id for every delivery to support GDPR data lineage tracking.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 2500/month budget supports Cloud Composer (smallest environment), Cloud SQL for PostGIS, GCS, and Cloud Functions. Cloud Composer is the largest cost driver -- consider Cloud Composer 2 Autopilot mode to scale worker resources to zero between DAG runs. GDPR data residency in europe-west1 must be enforced at GCS bucket and Cloud SQL instance level.

backend.service_design: Three Airflow DAGs: (1) ProviderIngestDAG: triggered by GCS object-created event for each provider's raw feed bucket, downloads satellite scene metadata and change-detection GeoJSON, stores to staging. (2) NormalizationDAG: triggered on new staging objects, runs CRS reprojection via pyproj, polygon deduplication via PostGIS ST_Intersects with configurable overlap threshold, stores validated_deforestation_alerts to PostGIS. (3) DeliveryDAG: triggered on new validated alerts, invokes Cloud Function per registered NGO partner to POST signed webhook payload.
backend.failure_handling: Dead-letter GCS prefix for malformed provider feeds. Airflow task retry with exponential backoff for transient provider API failures. Webhook delivery failures trigger retry up to 5 times; undeliverable after 5 attempts are written to failed_deliveries table and PagerDuty alert raised. Circuit breaker on per-provider ingest to halt processing if error rate exceeds 20% in a run.
backend.backend_risks: Provider feed format changes (GeoJSON schema drift from NICFI) may break the normalization parser -- pin provider feed schema versions and alert on schema drift. CRS reprojection accuracy depends on GDAL version; pin GDAL 3.8 in Docker image.
backend.background_jobs: ProviderIngestDAG runs on GCS trigger + daily fallback poll. NormalizationDAG parallelizes per satellite_scene_id. DeliveryDAG fans out per downstream_consumer with retry logic (exponential backoff, max 5 attempts). ReconciliationDAG runs nightly to compare provider feed checksums against ingested records and flag gaps.

security.auth_design: Per-provider GCP service accounts with minimal IAM permissions (GCS read on provider staging bucket only). Custom JWT issuer for NGO partner API access. HMAC-SHA256 webhook signing using per-partner secret from Secret Manager.
security.authorization_model: Service account per provider scoped to its GCS bucket. NGO partners authenticated via JWT with downstream_consumer_id claim. Partners can only query alerts assigned to their consumer ID.
security.secrets_management: Cloud Secret Manager for per-provider API credentials and per-partner HMAC signing keys. 30-day rotation via Secret Manager rotation scheduler. No secrets in Airflow variables or environment variables.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_events table: event_type (ingested/normalized/delivered/failed), provider_feed_id, satellite_scene_id, downstream_consumer_id, alert_id, timestamp, result. Cloud Audit Logs for all GCS and Cloud SQL access. Delivery receipts stored in webhook_delivery_receipts table. GDPR data lineage: every alert has a traceable chain from satellite_scene to delivery_receipt.

data.entities: satellite_scene (id, provider_feed_id FK, scene_id_external, acquisition_date, cloud_cover_pct, bbox_geom GEOMETRY, raw_feed_gcs_path, ingested_at), deforestation_alert (id, satellite_scene_id FK, geometry_polygon GEOMETRY(POLYGON, 4326), alert_confidence NUMERIC, area_ha NUMERIC, validation_result ENUM pending/validated/rejected, dedup_group_id, created_at), provider_feed (id, provider_name ENUM nicfi/planet/jaxa_alos, feed_url, credentials_secret_id, active), downstream_consumer (id, ngo_name, webhook_url, hmac_secret_id, active, gdpr_region), webhook_delivery_receipt (id, alert_id FK, consumer_id FK, delivered_at, http_status, attempt_count), audit_event (id, event_type, provider_feed_id, satellite_scene_id, alert_id, consumer_id, result, timestamp)
data.storage_design: Cloud SQL PostGIS on PostgreSQL 16 in europe-west1: spatial index (GIST) on deforestation_alert.geometry_polygon, partial index on validation_result=validated for delivery queries. GCS europe-west1: raw-staging bucket (30-day lifecycle), validated-archive bucket (indefinite, KMS encrypted), failed-feeds bucket. Cloud KMS customer-managed encryption keys for all GCS buckets and Cloud SQL.
data.consistency_notes: NormalizationDAG uses database transactions to update deforestation_alert.validation_result from pending to validated atomically. Deduplication via ST_Intersects(a.geometry, b.geometry) AND ST_Area(ST_Intersection(a.geometry, b.geometry)) / ST_Area(a.geometry) > 0.7 threshold groups overlapping alerts under a common dedup_group_id.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Composer 2 Autopilot in europe-west1 for Airflow DAGs. Cloud SQL PostGIS in europe-west1 with private IP. Cloud Functions for webhook delivery workers (europe-west1). GCS buckets in europe-west1. All resources in a shared VPC with private Google access.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build pipeline: Python 3.12 linting (ruff), unit tests (pytest with Testcontainers PostGIS), DAG integrity tests (Airflow DAG loading validation), Docker image build and push to Artifact Registry. Cloud Composer DAG sync via GCS DAG bucket on merge to main.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus metrics exported from Airflow via statsd plugin, scraped by Cloud Monitoring. Grafana dashboards: provider_ingest_success_rate, normalization_throughput_alerts_per_minute, delivery_success_rate_per_consumer, webhook_delivery_p99_latency, alerts_delivered_within_sla (< 4h). PagerDuty: ingest failure, delivery SLA breach, failed_deliveries count > 0.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a high-security geospatial data pipeline with a strict 4-hour delivery SLA and GDPR data residency constraint in GCP europe-west1. The key architectural decisions are: Airflow on Cloud Composer for orchestration, PostGIS for spatial deduplication via ST_Intersects, per-partner HMAC-SHA256 webhook signing, and per-provider service account isolation. The most complex technical step is the polygon deduplication using spatial overlap threshold, which requires careful PostGIS indexing. GDPR compliance drives the europe-west1 residency constraint across all resources.
target_output.title: Satellite Deforestation Alert Ingestion and Delivery Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A GCP-hosted Python/Airflow data pipeline that ingests satellite-derived deforestation change-detection feeds from earth observation providers (NICFI, Planet, JAXA ALOS), reprojects geometries to WGS84, deduplicates overlapping alert polygons via PostGIS spatial operations, and delivers validated alerts to registered conservation NGO partners via HMAC-SHA256 signed webhooks. Data residency in GCP europe-west1 enforces GDPR compliance. 4-hour end-to-end SLA from satellite imagery availability to NGO partner webhook delivery.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-stage Airflow pipeline on Cloud Composer 2 Autopilot in GCP europe-west1. Stage 1 (ProviderIngestDAG): GCS object-creation events trigger ingestion of satellite scene metadata and change-detection GeoJSON from provider APIs into a GCS raw-staging bucket. Stage 2 (NormalizationDAG): Python tasks using GDAL/pyproj reproject provider-native CRS to WGS84, deduplication via PostGIS ST_Intersects with 70% area overlap threshold groups redundant alerts, validated records stored in Cloud SQL PostGIS. Stage 3 (DeliveryDAG): Cloud Functions fan out HMAC-SHA256 signed webhook POSTs to registered NGO partner endpoints with 5-attempt exponential backoff. A nightly ReconciliationDAG cross-checks provider feed checksums. All resources encrypted with Cloud KMS. Audit trail via audit_events table and Cloud Audit Logs supports GDPR data lineage.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.8 on Cloud Composer 2 Autopilot (europe-west1). Processing: Python 3.12, GDAL 3.8, pyproj 3.6, GeoPandas, Shapely. Database: Cloud SQL PostGIS on PostgreSQL 16 (europe-west1, private IP). Storage: GCS buckets (europe-west1, KMS-encrypted). Webhook delivery: Cloud Functions 2nd gen (Python 3.12). CI: Cloud Build + Artifact Registry. Secrets: Cloud Secret Manager. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus (Cloud Monitoring scrape target), PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Provider Ingestion: per-provider Airflow tasks pull NICFI (MVP), Planet, and JAXA ALOS change-detection GeoJSON feeds using per-provider service account credentials; raw data staged to GCS. CRS Normalization: pyproj reprojects from provider-native projections (e.g., UTM zone variants) to WGS84 (EPSG:4326) for uniform storage. Polygon Deduplication: PostGIS ST_Intersects query groups alerts with > 70% area overlap under a shared dedup_group_id, selecting the highest-confidence alert as the canonical record. Validation: alert confidence score threshold check (>= 0.6) and minimum area threshold (>= 1 ha) before marking validation_result = validated. Webhook Delivery: Cloud Function POSTs validated alerts as JSON to each registered downstream_consumer webhook_url with HMAC-SHA256 signature in X-Alert-Signature header. Audit Trail: every pipeline event logged to audit_events table with GDPR data lineage.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Composer 2 Autopilot (Airflow DAG orchestration, europe-west1), Cloud SQL PostGIS PostgreSQL 16 (validated alert storage, spatial indexing, private IP), GCS raw-staging bucket (30-day lifecycle, KMS encrypted), GCS validated-archive bucket (indefinite retention, KMS encrypted), GCS failed-feeds bucket (malformed feed dead-letter), Cloud Functions 2nd gen (webhook delivery workers, one per partner fan-out), Cloud Secret Manager (per-provider API credentials, per-partner HMAC keys), Cloud KMS (customer-managed encryption keys for GCS and Cloud SQL), Cloud Audit Logs (GCS and Cloud SQL access logging), Prometheus + Grafana (pipeline metrics), PagerDuty (alerting).
target_output.workflows: Provider Ingest to Delivery: NICFI publishes a new change-detection GeoJSON feed; GCS object-created Pub/Sub event triggers ProviderIngestDAG. Airflow task downloads feed using NICFI service account, writes raw GeoJSON to raw-staging/nicfi/{date}/{scene_id}.geojson. NormalizationDAG task reads raw file, reprojects coordinates from provider CRS to WGS84 via pyproj, computes area_ha, checks confidence >= 0.6 and area >= 1 ha. PostGIS deduplication task runs: INSERT INTO deforestation_alert ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE with ST_Intersects overlap check. Validated alerts written with validation_result = validated. DeliveryDAG fans out to all active downstream_consumers: Cloud Function invoked per consumer, POSTs JSON payload to webhook_url with X-Alert-Signature: hmac-sha256={sig} header signed using consumer-specific HMAC secret from Secret Manager. HTTP 200 response writes delivery receipt; non-200 triggers retry with exponential backoff (5 attempts). SLA tracker measures time from satellite_scene.acquisition_date to webhook_delivery_receipt.delivered_at and raises PagerDuty if > 4 hours. Partner Data Query: NGO data scientist authenticates via custom JWT, queries GET /v1/alerts?consumer_id=&from=&to=&bbox= against PostGIS read replica. Response includes validated deforestation alerts with geometry GeoJSON and confidence scores.
target_output.data_model: satellite_scene (id UUID PK, provider_feed_id UUID FK, scene_id_external VARCHAR, acquisition_date TIMESTAMPTZ, cloud_cover_pct NUMERIC, bbox_geom GEOMETRY(POLYGON,4326), raw_feed_gcs_path TEXT, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ). deforestation_alert (id UUID PK, satellite_scene_id UUID FK, geometry_polygon GEOMETRY(POLYGON,4326), alert_confidence NUMERIC, area_ha NUMERIC, validation_result ENUM pending/validated/rejected, dedup_group_id UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). provider_feed (id UUID PK, provider_name ENUM nicfi/planet/jaxa_alos, feed_url TEXT, credentials_secret_id TEXT, active BOOL). downstream_consumer (id UUID PK, ngo_name TEXT, webhook_url TEXT, hmac_secret_id TEXT, active BOOL, gdpr_region VARCHAR). webhook_delivery_receipt (id UUID PK, alert_id UUID FK, consumer_id UUID FK, delivered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, http_status INT, attempt_count INT). audit_event (id UUID PK, event_type ENUM ingested/normalized/delivered/failed, provider_feed_id UUID, satellite_scene_id UUID, alert_id UUID, consumer_id UUID, result VARCHAR, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ). Spatial index: GIST on deforestation_alert.geometry_polygon. Partial index on validation_result = validated.
target_output.api_design: Internal query API (JWT-protected, not public): GET /v1/alerts?consumer_id={id}&from={date}&to={date}&bbox={minx,miny,maxx,maxy}&validation_result=validated -- returns paginated GeoJSON FeatureCollection of deforestation_alert records intersecting bbox. GET /v1/consumers/{id}/delivery-receipts?from=&to= -- delivery status for a specific NGO partner. GET /v1/audit?alert_id={id} -- full audit chain for a specific alert (GDPR data lineage). Webhook payload: POST {webhook_url} with body {alert_id, geometry: GeoJSON, area_ha, confidence, acquisition_date, provider, dedup_group_id}, header X-Alert-Signature: hmac-sha256={sig}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Per-provider GCP service accounts with IAM scoped to read from their specific GCS staging bucket only. Custom JWT with downstream_consumer_id claim restricts partner API access to their own alert data. HMAC-SHA256 webhook signatures via per-partner secrets in Cloud Secret Manager with 30-day rotation. Cloud KMS customer-managed encryption keys for all GCS buckets and Cloud SQL. TLS 1.3 enforced for all external network calls. Audit trail: audit_events table with GDPR data lineage chain from satellite_scene to delivery_receipt. GDPR: all data in europe-west1, right-to-erasure supported via soft-delete on downstream_consumer with cascading alert delivery record anonymization. ISO 27001: Secret Manager rotation, Cloud Audit Logs, incident response runbook in Cloud Operations.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Build pipeline: ruff lint, pytest unit tests with Testcontainers PostGIS, DAG loading validation (Airflow DagBag import check), Docker image build and push to Artifact Registry europe-west1. On merge to main: DAG files synced to Cloud Composer GCS DAG bucket triggering automatic DAG reload. Environments: dev (Cloud Composer Developer profile, single provider mock), staging (Composer Standard, real provider feeds, 2 pilot NGO partners), production (Composer Autopilot, auto-scales worker nodes to zero between runs). Secrets rotation: Cloud Secret Manager scheduler triggers 30-day rotation Lambda. Rollback: previous Docker image tag redeployable via Cloud Build trigger. GCS bucket lifecycle: raw-staging objects deleted after 30 days; validated-archive objects retained indefinitely.
target_output.observability: Grafana dashboards scraping Cloud Monitoring Prometheus endpoint: provider_ingest_success_rate (per provider), normalization_alerts_per_run, dedup_reduction_rate (alerts_removed / alerts_ingested), delivery_success_rate (per NGO consumer), webhook_delivery_p99_latency_seconds, alerts_delivered_within_4h_sla_pct. PagerDuty escalation: any ingest DAG failure, delivery SLA breach (alert age > 4h at delivery), failed_deliveries count > 0 after max retries. Cloud Composer DAG run status dashboard for operational visibility. Cloud Audit Logs alert on unauthorized access to raw-staging or audit_events table.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$2100/month for MVP scope: Cloud Composer 2 Autopilot scales to zero between runs (~$800/month for daily runs), Cloud SQL db-g1-small PostGIS (~$60/month), GCS storage + operations (~$30/month), Cloud Functions webhook delivery (~$10/month for 3 partners), Cloud Monitoring + Grafana (~$50/month), Cloud KMS + Secret Manager (~$20/month). Buffer to $2500/month budget. Scaling: Composer Autopilot adds worker nodes automatically for large scene batches; PostGIS spatial index ensures deduplication queries remain sub-second even at 10M alert records.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): NICFI provider ingest, WGS84 reprojection, PostGIS polygon deduplication, webhook delivery to 3 pilot NGO partners, audit trail, Cloud Composer deployment, Cloud Build CI. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Planet provider integration, partner query API with JWT auth, delivery retry dashboard, Grafana SLA monitoring, GDPR data lineage API. Phase 3 (8 weeks): JAXA ALOS integration, real-time Pub/Sub streaming delivery replacing batch webhook, self-service NGO partner registration API, Athena-equivalent historical analysis on GCS Parquet archive.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with type annotations; ruff for linting. All Airflow DAGs must pass DagBag import test in CI before merge. Geospatial processing tasks must pin GDAL 3.8 in Dockerfile to prevent CRS reprojection drift. Per-provider ingest tasks must be idempotent: check for existing satellite_scene record by scene_id_external before insert. PostGIS deduplication query must run in a transaction with explicit LOCK on the dedup_group_id range. Pytest unit tests use Testcontainers PostGIS for spatial operation tests. All secrets accessed via Cloud Secret Manager SDK; no hardcoded credentials.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: NICFI GeoJSON feed schema changes without notice, breaking the normalization parser. Mitigation: schema validation step at ingest with Cloud Monitoring alert on schema drift; pin provider feed schema version in provider_feed table. Risk: PostGIS polygon deduplication performance degrades at > 10M alerts without proper GIST index maintenance. Mitigation: scheduled VACUUM ANALYZE on deforestation_alert table; monitor query plan via EXPLAIN ANALYZE in staging. Risk: NGO partner webhook endpoint downtime causes alert delivery failures beyond 4-hour SLA. Mitigation: exponential backoff with 5 retries; PagerDuty alert if failed_deliveries > 0 after max retries. Tradeoff: batch Airflow DAGs over real-time Pub/Sub streaming for MVP -- simpler operational model, acceptable for 4-hour SLA; deferred to Phase 3.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected ST_Intersects with 70% area overlap threshold for polygon deduplication -- prevents duplicate alerts from overlapping satellite scenes without losing legitimate nearby alerts. Confirmed Cloud Composer Autopilot over self-managed Airflow to eliminate worker node patching overhead. Chosen per-partner HMAC-SHA256 keys over a global signing key to limit blast radius of a compromised key. GDPR data residency enforced at GCP resource level (europe-west1) rather than application layer.

metadata.notes: Strong first-pass geospatial data pipeline with PostGIS spatial deduplication, HMAC-signed partner webhooks, and GDPR data residency in europe-west1.

project_goal.value: Public-facing static website for a national environmental NGO publishing annual deforestation monitoring reports, biodiversity impact assessments, and policy briefings, with accessible downloads and a searchable report library.
project_goal.rationale: Replaces an outdated WordPress site; editorial team needs Git-driven content workflow.

target_users.value: Policy researchers, government agencies, journalists, and civil society organizations seeking authoritative deforestation data and NGO policy positions.
target_users.rationale: Target audience identified in communications strategy.

project_class.rationale: No user accounts or transactional data; static site generator maximizes performance and minimizes attack surface.

capabilities.rationale: Static content delivery with privacy-friendly analytics tracking.

complexity_level.rationale: Static site with client-side search; no backend services or user state.

risk_level.rationale: No PII collected; public content; primary risk is content defacement or CDN downtime.

data_sensitivity.rationale: No personal data collected; all content is public domain.

external_exposure.rationale: Fully public; no authentication required for any content.

access_model.value: Public read-only website. Content updates via GitHub PR workflow with branch protection requiring reviewer approval before merge to main.
access_model.rationale: Editorial controls through Git rather than CMS login.

feature_scope.value: Report library with filter by country, year, and thematic area; accessible PDF downloads with metadata; author profiles; press release archive; WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance; GDPR-compliant privacy-first analytics.
feature_scope.rationale: Full content management needs for NGO communications team.

mvp_scope.value: Searchable report library, download tracking, responsive layout, WCAG 2.1 AA markup, and Matomo analytics with IP anonymization.
mvp_scope.rationale: Core publishing capability needed for upcoming annual report release.

security_baseline.value: CSP header via Netlify _headers preventing inline script execution; HSTS; subresource integrity on any third-party CSS; branch protection on main.
security_baseline.rationale: Content integrity and transport security for public NGO credibility.

frontend_stack.value: Eleventy (11ty) 2.0 with Nunjucks templates; Tailwind CSS 3 for styling; Pagefind for client-side static search.
frontend_stack.rationale: Eleventy offers flexible template inheritance for report library layouts; Pagefind enables offline-capable search without a search API.

backend_stack.rationale: Static website project class requires no backend service.

data_platform.value: None (content in Git as Markdown and YAML frontmatter; assets in Git LFS for PDF reports).
data_platform.rationale: Git-native content workflow; no database required.

hosting_target.value: Netlify CDN with automatic branch deploys and deploy previews for PRs.
hosting_target.rationale: Netlify Git integration and headers configuration matches team workflow.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Matomo analytics self-hosted with IP anonymization enabled; no cookie consent banner required as no personal data collected; PDF download requests logged at edge for 30 days only.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR compliance without user consent friction.

future_scope.value: Multilingual content support (Spanish and French), interactive data visualizations for deforestation trend charts, newsletter signup via third-party API integration.

constraints.value: Build time under 3 minutes for a library of 500 reports; no third-party JavaScript with opaque privacy policies; Lighthouse accessibility score above 95 on all pages.
constraints.rationale: Performance and privacy requirements for NGO credibility.

observability_baseline.value: Netlify build logs; Matomo self-hosted analytics dashboard; Netlify Analytics for bandwidth and 404 trends.
observability_baseline.rationale: No server-side observability needed for static site.

execution_preference.value: Git-driven content workflow with GitHub PRs; GitHub Actions for build linting and Lighthouse CI checks before merge.
execution_preference.rationale: Communications team uses GitHub for content editing.

project.goal: Git-driven static publication site for NGO deforestation monitoring reports and policy briefings
project.target_users: Policy researchers, journalists, government agencies
project.timeline: Launch before annual report release in 6 weeks

security.sri: Subresource integrity on third-party CSS assets

data.assets: Git LFS for PDF report assets (up to 50 MB each)

devops.ci: GitHub Actions (Eleventy build, Pagefind index, Lighthouse CI, axe accessibility)

constraints.privacy: No third-party analytics with opaque data sharing

reasoner_reviews.product: The editorial team's Git-native content workflow is the correct design choice for an NGO with distributed contributors across multiple country offices. Pagefind generates a search index at build time so the report library is searchable without a search API or CDN worker function. The Pagefind index rebuild time at 500 reports should be measured in the CI build and budgeted against the 3-minute gate.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Eleventy with Nunjucks is a strong match for this content type — the report library benefits from Eleventy's collections API for filtering by thematic_area, country, and year tags. YAML frontmatter per report file provides structured metadata without a database. The previous plan left accessibility audit automation as a manual step; Lighthouse CI and axe-core should be integrated into the PR check pipeline to enforce the WCAG 2.1 AA requirement before merge.
reasoner_reviews.security: The ISS-001 concern about missing Content Security Policy headers is valid and blocking. Netlify _headers file with a properly constructed CSP (default-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'nonce-...'; script-src 'self' 'nonce-...') must be implemented and tested to block inline script injection. The previous plan omitted Subresource Integrity for the Tailwind CDN link — this should be corrected to a self-hosted Tailwind build to avoid any external dependency.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Six-week timeline is achievable given the limited feature scope. The primary risk is the Pagefind index build time exceeding 3 minutes at 500 reports with large PDF metadata extraction — Pagefind should be configured to index HTML only and skip binary PDF content for the initial release.

frontend.app_structure: Eleventy 2.0 with Nunjucks base template, report library layout, and individual report detail layout. Tailwind CSS compiled at build time via PostCSS purge. Pagefind search index generated as final build step.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home (featured reports, mission statement, latest press release). Report Library (filterable by thematic_area, country_region, year; Pagefind search widget). Report Detail (publication title, authors, thematic area tags, abstract, accessible PDF download link with file size, data appendix download). Author Profile. Press Release Archive. About / Policy Positions page.
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA required throughout. All report download links include descriptive accessible name (e.g., 'Download 2024 Brazil Amazon Deforestation Report PDF, 4.2 MB'). Pagefind search input has associated label. Color contrast validated via axe-core CI. PDF documents must include tagged PDF structure (enforced via upload checklist for editorial team).
frontend.performance_notes: Tailwind CSS purge reduces stylesheet to under 15 KB. Pagefind search index lazy-loaded only on Report Library page. Images use Eleventy Image plugin for responsive srcset and WebP generation. Lighthouse performance score target above 90.

security.auth_design: No authentication. Public read-only access to all pages and downloads.
security.abuse_prevention: Netlify rate limiting on deploy webhook endpoint. Branch protection on main branch with required reviewer approval prevents unauthorized content publication. PDF assets stored in Git LFS with Netlify Large Media to prevent direct hotlinking exhausting bandwidth.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Netlify access logs available for 30 days. Matomo analytics self-hosted with IP anonymization and no cross-site tracking. No cookies set on visitors.

data.entities: report_publication (slug, title, year INT, country_region TEXT[], thematic_area TEXT[], authors TEXT[], abstract TEXT, pdf_asset_path, pdf_size_mb REAL, data_appendix_path, published_date). author_profile (slug, name, title, bio, photo_path). press_release (slug, title, date, summary, body_markdown, pdf_path). thematic_area (slug, label, description). changelog_entry (version, date, changes TEXT[]).
data.storage_design: All content stored as Markdown files with YAML frontmatter in the Git repository. PDF and data appendix assets in Git LFS. Eleventy collections API builds in-memory indexes of all report_publication files at build time for filtering and Pagefind index generation.
data.retention_and_deletion: All content versioned in Git history. Netlify edge logs retained 30 days. No personal data collected or retained.

devops.deployment_topology: Netlify CDN with global edge network. Main branch auto-deploys to production. Feature branches generate deploy preview URLs for editorial review before merge.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions on PR: Eleventy build, Pagefind index generation, Lighthouse CI (performance > 90, accessibility > 95, best-practices > 90), axe-core accessibility scan on 5 representative page types. Merge to main triggers Netlify production deploy. Build time monitored; alert if exceeding 3 minutes.
devops.observability_stack: Matomo self-hosted (VPS separate from Netlify) with IP anonymization, no cookies, event tracking for PDF downloads per report slug. Netlify Analytics for CDN bandwidth, 404 trends, and top download paths.

ISS-001.title: Missing Content Security Policy headers allowing inline script injection
ISS-001.detail: The round 1 plan did not specify a Netlify _headers CSP configuration. Without an explicit CSP, injected inline scripts could execute in visitor browsers, undermining NGO credibility and content integrity.

history.note: Identified by security review: no CSP headers in deployment plan.

ISS-002.title: Accessibility CI automation missing from PR pipeline
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan listed WCAG 2.1 AA as a requirement but did not include axe-core or Lighthouse CI accessibility checks in the GitHub Actions PR workflow, making compliance unverifiable before merge.

history.note: Identified by product review: accessibility checks were manual only.

focus_issues.title: Missing Content Security Policy headers allowing inline script injection
focus_issues.detail: No CSP headers in deployment plan; inline scripts unblocked.

focus_issues.title: Accessibility CI automation missing from PR pipeline
focus_issues.detail: WCAG 2.1 AA requirement not enforced by automated CI checks.

latest_recommendations: Add a Netlify _headers file with an explicit Content Security Policy blocking inline scripts and unauthorized third-party origins.
Integrate Lighthouse CI and axe-core accessibility scans into the GitHub Actions PR check pipeline as blocking gates.
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: NGO Deforestation Reports Static Website - Round 1

convergence_state.reason: Two unresolved issues from round 1 require concrete security header and CI automation additions.

previous_audits.summary: Solid Eleventy + Pagefind architecture for the report library. Two blocking gaps: no CSP headers specified and accessibility CI automation absent from the pipeline.
strengths: Eleventy collections API maps cleanly to report filtering by thematic area, country, and year
Pagefind build-time search avoids runtime API dependency
Matomo self-hosted analytics is the correct GDPR-compliant choice
concerns: No Content Security Policy defined for Netlify deployment
Accessibility compliance left as a manual editorial process rather than enforced in CI
blocking_issues: CSP headers not specified; inline scripts unblocked on public NGO site
axe-core and Lighthouse CI not integrated into PR pipeline for WCAG 2.1 AA gate
recommendations: Add netlify/_headers file with CSP: default-src 'self'; style-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'none'
Add axe-core CLI scan and Lighthouse CI checks to GitHub Actions PR workflow as blocking status checks

issue_updates.title: Missing Content Security Policy headers
issue_updates.detail: No Netlify _headers CSP definition found in round 1 plan.

issue_updates.title: Accessibility CI automation missing
issue_updates.detail: WCAG 2.1 AA requirement stated but not enforced in pipeline.

previous_plan.title: NGO Deforestation Reports Static Website - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Eleventy 2.0 static site with Nunjucks templates, Tailwind CSS, and Pagefind search deployed to Netlify CDN. Report library filterable by country, thematic area, and year. Matomo self-hosted analytics.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: HSTS and X-Frame-Options via Netlify headers. No authentication required. WCAG 2.1 AA design goal for all pages.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions builds Eleventy site and Pagefind index on PR; Netlify auto-deploys on merge to main. Branch protection requires one reviewer approval.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Single-tier static architecture: Eleventy generates HTML at build time from Markdown content files and YAML frontmatter. No server-side runtime. CDN delivers pre-built pages globally.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must directly resolve ISS-001 (missing CSP headers) and ISS-002 (missing accessibility CI automation). For a static NGO website the security architecture is primarily about content integrity and transport security via HTTP headers rather than authentication. The CSP fix requires a concrete Netlify _headers file specification. The accessibility fix requires axe-core CLI and Lighthouse CI added as blocking GitHub Actions checks. These are targeted additions to an otherwise sound round 1 architecture.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit Netlify _headers CSP specification blocking inline scripts: 'Content-Security-Policy: default-src self; style-src self; script-src self; object-src none; frame-ancestors none; base-uri self' plus X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, and Permissions-Policy headers. Tailwind CDN link replaced with self-hosted build to eliminate external style-src origin requirement.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Inline script injection blocked on all NGO site pages; CSP grade A on securityheaders.com scan.

fix_report.action_taken: Added axe-core CLI scan (npx axe --exit --tags wcag2a,wcag2aa) on home, report library, report detail, author profile, and press release page types. Added Lighthouse CI with accessibility threshold above 95 as a blocking PR status check. Both checks run in the GitHub Actions PR workflow and must pass before merge to main.
fix_report.expected_outcome: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance enforced automatically on every PR; accessibility regressions blocked before reaching production.

target_output.title: NGO Deforestation Monitoring Report Library - Static Website Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Git-driven static website for a national environmental NGO publishing deforestation monitoring reports, biodiversity assessments, and policy briefings to a global research and policy audience. Built with Eleventy 2.0 and Nunjucks templates, Tailwind CSS, and Pagefind for build-time search, deployed to Netlify CDN. No server-side runtime or database. This round resolves a missing Content Security Policy and absent accessibility CI automation from round 1.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-tier static architecture: Eleventy 2.0 generates HTML pages at build time from Markdown content files with YAML frontmatter stored in Git. No server-side runtime exists at request time -- Netlify CDN serves pre-built HTML, CSS, and Pagefind search index shards directly to visitors. PDF reports and data appendices stored in Git LFS and served via Netlify Large Media at CDN edge. Matomo analytics self-hosted on a separate VPS with IP anonymization. Content updates flow through GitHub PR review: communications officers commit Markdown files, reviewers approve via GitHub PR, merge triggers Netlify production deploy. Netlify _headers file enforces CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, and Permissions-Policy on all responses.
target_output.technology_stack: Static site generator: Eleventy 2.0, Nunjucks templates, Tailwind CSS 3 (self-hosted PostCSS build, no CDN link). Search: Pagefind 1.x (build-time index, lazy-loaded on report library page). Hosting: Netlify CDN with deploy previews. Assets: Git LFS with Netlify Large Media. Analytics: Matomo 5.x self-hosted (VPS). CI: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI, axe-core CLI, Eleventy build validation.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Report Library: filterable collection of report_publication entries by country_region tags, thematic_area tags, and publication year; Pagefind search widget for full-text search across report titles, abstracts, and author names. Report Detail: title, year, country tags, thematic area tags, author names with links to profiles, abstract, accessible PDF download link (file size displayed), data appendix link where available. Author Profile: name, title, biography, photo, linked publication list. Press Release Archive: chronological listing with date, summary, and PDF download. Homepage: featured recent report, mission statement banner, latest press release summary.
target_output.system_components: Netlify CDN (global edge, pre-built HTML/CSS/JS delivery, _headers enforcement). Eleventy 2.0 Build Process (Nunjucks templates, collections API for report filtering, Eleventy Image plugin for srcset/WebP). Pagefind Search Index (build-time static search, lazy-loaded JS client on report library page). Git LFS + Netlify Large Media (PDF report and data appendix asset storage and CDN delivery). Matomo Analytics VPS (self-hosted, IP anonymization, PDF download event tracking). GitHub Actions CI (Eleventy build, Pagefind index, Lighthouse CI, axe-core accessibility scan).
target_output.workflows: Content Publication Workflow: A communications officer creates a new Markdown file at /content/reports/2025-amazon-deforestation.md with YAML frontmatter (title, year: 2025, country_region: [brazil], thematic_area: [tropical_forests, biodiversity], authors: [A. Ferreira, C. Santos], pdf_asset: /assets/reports/2025-amazon-deforestation.pdf, pdf_size_mb: 5.8), uploads the PDF to Git LFS, and opens a GitHub PR. The CI pipeline builds the Eleventy site, regenerates the Pagefind index including the new report, runs Lighthouse CI (accessibility > 95) and axe-core scan on the new report detail page template. A communications manager reviews the deploy preview URL showing the rendered report detail page, approves and merges, and Netlify deploys the updated site within 3 minutes. Report Discovery Workflow: A policy researcher at a government ministry visits the report library, types 'peatland carbon' in the Pagefind search widget, and receives ranked results from report abstracts. They filter further by thematic_area: wetlands and year: 2024-2025, then click through to a report detail page and download the 6.1 MB PDF with the data appendix, with the download event recorded in Matomo.
