previous_plan.title: City Climate Action Plan Website - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Hugo static site with Bootstrap 5, deployed to Cloudflare Pages. CSP header added in round 2 but includes unsafe-inline for Bootstrap compatibility. Cloudflare Web Analytics for cookie-free page view tracking.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Cloudflare Pages _headers file: CSP with script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; HSTS; X-Frame-Options DENY. Branch protection on main stated as required.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions Hugo build on PR; Cloudflare Pages auto-deploys on merge to main. Branch protection required but settings not documented.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 must fix ISS-001 by removing unsafe-inline from script-src. The root cause was Bootstrap 5 inline onclick handlers in Hugo templates. The fix requires migrating all Bootstrap interactive components (navbar collapse, accordion milestones, modal PDF previews) to use data-bs-toggle and data-bs-target attributes exclusively so no inline JS is needed. After that migration, script-src can be set to 'self' only with a report-uri endpoint. ISS-002 requires documenting the specific GitHub branch protection rule settings in the deployment section. Both are concrete, targeted fixes for a weak static site plan.

fix_report.action_taken: Audited all Hugo templates and removed all inline onclick, onsubmit, and onload handlers. Migrated Bootstrap 5 navbar collapse to data-bs-toggle='collapse' data-bs-target='#navbarMenu'. Migrated milestone accordion to data-bs-toggle='collapse' data-bs-target='#milestone-{id}'. Migrated PDF preview modal to data-bs-toggle='modal' data-bs-target='#pdfModal'. Bootstrap JS self-hosted via Hugo Pipes bundle (no CDN link). CSP script-src updated to 'self' only (removing unsafe-inline). Added report-uri https://csp.cityname.gov/report to CSP header for violation monitoring.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CSP script-src 'self' blocks all inline script injection on official city domain; securityheaders.com grade A; CSP violations logged to city IT monitoring endpoint.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit GitHub branch protection rule specification to deployment_and_operations: require 1 reviewer from the CODEOWNERS city-it team, require all GitHub Actions status checks (hugo-build, html-validate, axe-accessibility) to pass before merge, disallow force push to main, disallow deletion of main, require linear history (no merge commits). CODEOWNERS file added to repository root mapping /* to @city-it-team.
fix_report.expected_outcome: City IT can verify and enforce the documented branch protection settings in GitHub repository settings; all content changes require IT approval before publication.

target_output.title: City Climate Action Plan - Public Information Website Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A static public information website for a city climate action plan, publishing emissions inventories by sector, policy milestone trackers, and resident action guides to city residents and journalists. Built with Hugo and Bootstrap 5 deployed to Cloudflare Pages CDN. No server-side runtime or database. This round removes the unsafe-inline script-src vulnerability from the Content Security Policy by migrating Bootstrap components to data-attribute initialization, and documents specific GitHub branch protection settings for city IT enforcement.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-tier static architecture: Hugo builds HTML pages at build time from Markdown content files and TOML data files stored in Git. No server-side runtime exists at request time -- Cloudflare Pages CDN serves pre-built HTML globally with DDoS protection included. Policy milestone data and emissions inventory tables are stored as TOML in the Hugo data/ directory and rendered via Hugo range templates at build time. Content updates require a GitHub PR reviewed and approved by city IT via branch protection before merge triggers Cloudflare Pages production deploy. Cloudflare Pages _headers enforces CSP (script-src 'self' only, no unsafe-inline), HSTS, X-Frame-Options, and X-Content-Type-Options on all responses. Cloudflare Web Analytics provides cookie-free page view metrics without collecting personal data.
target_output.technology_stack: Static generator: Hugo 0.125, Bootstrap 5.3 (self-hosted via Hugo Pipes bundle, no CDN link), Go HTML templates. Content format: Markdown with TOML frontmatter; Hugo data/ TOML files for milestone and inventory data. Hosting: Cloudflare Pages CDN. CI: GitHub Actions (hugo-build, html-validate, axe-core). Analytics: Cloudflare Web Analytics (cookie-free). Security: Cloudflare Pages _headers CSP with report-uri.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Emissions Inventory: sector category pages (buildings, transportation, waste, energy supply) showing CO2e tonnes by year with year-over-year percentage change table sourced from Hugo data/inventory.toml. Milestone Tracker: accordion-style listing of all climate policy commitments with status badge (on_track, delayed, completed), target year, lead department, and description -- status driven from data/milestones.toml. Resident Action Guides: three static guide pages (home energy efficiency, sustainable transport choices, food system and waste reduction) with sector-specific step lists and estimated individual impact. Progress Indicators: homepage summary cards showing total citywide emissions reduction percentage vs baseline year and count of completed vs open milestones. Publication Archive: chronological list of PDF policy documents with download links.
target_output.system_components: Cloudflare Pages CDN (global edge delivery, DDoS protection, _headers enforcement, deploy previews). Hugo Build Process (Go templates, Bootstrap 5 self-hosted via Pipes, content from Markdown/TOML, HTML generation). GitHub Repository (Markdown content, TOML data files, CODEOWNERS, _headers config, Hugo config). GitHub Actions CI (hugo-build, html-validate, axe-core blocking checks). Cloudflare Web Analytics (cookie-free, no personal data). Cloudflare CSP Report URI (violation logging endpoint).
target_output.workflows: Content Update Workflow: A city communications officer creates a TOML update to data/milestones.toml changing a milestone status from 'on_track' to 'completed' for the '2025 EV charging network expansion' milestone. She opens a GitHub PR; GitHub Actions runs Hugo build validation, html-validate, and axe-core accessibility scan -- all must pass. The city IT reviewer sees the deploy preview URL showing the updated milestone tracker page with the new 'completed' badge and approves the PR. Merge triggers Cloudflare Pages to rebuild and deploy the updated site in approximately 45 seconds. CSP Violation Monitoring Workflow: If a browser-injected ad or third-party extension attempts to execute an inline script on the city domain, Cloudflare CSP report-uri receives a violation report from the browser. City IT reviews the report in the Cloudflare dashboard security events section to assess whether a supply chain compromise or browser extension is responsible.
target_output.data_model: policy_milestone stored in data/milestones.toml: id TEXT, title TEXT, target_year INT, status ENUM on_track/delayed/completed, sector TEXT, lead_department TEXT, last_updated DATE, description TEXT. emissions_inventory in data/inventory.toml: sector TEXT, year INT, co2e_tonnes INT, percent_change_yoy REAL, data_source TEXT. action_guide as Markdown in content/guides/: TOML frontmatter with slug, title, sector, impact_estimate; body is step-by-step Markdown. sector_category in data/sectors.toml: slug, label, icon_class. publication_year in data/publications.toml: year INT, documents [{title, pdf_path, published_date}]. All data versioned in Git; no database.
target_output.api_design: No server-side API. All data served as pre-rendered HTML from Cloudflare Pages CDN. PDF documents served as static files from /public/documents/{slug}.pdf via Cloudflare edge. CSP violation reports sent by browsers to POST https://csp.cityname.gov/report (Cloudflare Worker endpoint logging to Cloudflare D1 for city IT review -- separate from the static site itself). Cloudflare Web Analytics beacon via Cloudflare-managed script (allowed by CSP script-src 'self' since Cloudflare injects it from the same origin). No form submission endpoints.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cloudflare Pages _headers file enforces on /*: Content-Security-Policy (default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; font-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'none'; base-uri 'self'; report-uri https://csp.cityname.gov/report); Strict-Transport-Security (max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload); X-Frame-Options DENY; X-Content-Type-Options nosniff; Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin. Bootstrap JS self-hosted via Hugo Pipes bundle -- no CDN script-src origin required. All Bootstrap interactive components (navbar collapse, milestone accordion, PDF modal) use data-bs-toggle and data-bs-target attributes exclusively; zero inline event handlers in templates. CODEOWNERS file maps all paths to @city-it-team requiring IT approval on every PR. GitHub branch protection: require 1 city-it reviewer, require hugo-build + html-validate + axe-accessibility status checks, disallow force push, disallow branch deletion.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloudflare Pages project linked to GitHub repository via webhook. Feature branches generate automatic preview deploy URLs included in PR comments. GitHub Actions PR workflow (required status checks blocking merge to main): hugo-build (runs hugo --minify, fails on any template error), html-validate (scans all generated HTML for invalid markup), axe-accessibility (axe-core CLI --tags wcag2a,wcag2aa on milestone, inventory, guide, and home page templates -- must return zero violations). CODEOWNERS requires approval from @city-it-team. Merge to main triggers Cloudflare Pages production deploy (average 45 seconds for 100-page Hugo site). GitHub branch protection settings: Require a pull request before merging, Require approvals: 1 (from CODEOWNERS city-it-team), Require status checks to pass (hugo-build, html-validate, axe-accessibility), Require branches to be up to date before merging, Restrict pushes that create matching branches: disabled, Allow force pushes: disabled, Allow deletions: disabled.
target_output.observability: Cloudflare Web Analytics dashboard (cookie-free, no personal data) shows page views by URL path, top entry pages, and geographic distribution of visitors. Cloudflare Pages build logs available in Cloudflare dashboard for each deploy. Cloudflare security events dashboard shows CSP violation reports received at report-uri endpoint with browser user-agent and blocked directive. GitHub Actions run history provides audit trail of all Hugo builds, validation runs, and deploy triggers. No server-side APM or structured log aggregation required for a static site at this scale.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloudflare Pages free tier covers unlimited sites and requests for city government use. Hugo builds complete in under 60 seconds for a 100-page site well within Cloudflare Pages build minute allowances. PDF document hosting at Cloudflare Pages static asset edge, no additional CDN cost. Cloudflare Web Analytics free tier. Total monthly cost: $0 for expected city government traffic volumes. If PDF download bandwidth exceeds Cloudflare free limits, upgrade to Pro tier ($20/month) for higher bandwidth allowance.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (4 weeks): Hugo site with Bootstrap 5 self-hosted, milestone tracker (data/milestones.toml), emissions inventory pages (data/inventory.toml), three resident action guides, CSP headers (script-src 'self' no unsafe-inline), GitHub branch protection with CODEOWNERS, GitHub Actions CI (hugo-build, html-validate, axe-core), Cloudflare Web Analytics, Cloudflare Pages deploy. Phase 2 (8 weeks post-launch): Progress indicator charts using Chart.js (self-hosted, no CDN), publication archive with filtered PDF library, press release section, sector landing pages with featured guides. Phase 3: Multilingual support (Spanish via Hugo i18n), interactive emissions data visualization, Cloudflare D1 integration for dynamic milestone filtering without a full backend.
target_output.development_guidelines: Hugo templates must use zero inline event handlers -- all Bootstrap interactive components must use data-bs-toggle and data-bs-target exclusively. This is enforced by a grep check in the GitHub Actions pipeline (grep -r 'onclick' layouts/ should return no results). Bootstrap JS bundled via Hugo Pipes from local node_modules; no CDN links in templates. TOML data files validated via schema check script on PR (Python script checking required fields in milestones.toml and inventory.toml). axe-core accessibility scan is a non-negotiable blocking gate on every PR. Milestone status values are constrained to on_track/delayed/completed by a Hugo template assertion that fails the build on unknown values.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Bootstrap 5 future minor version may reintroduce inline event handler patterns in documentation examples, causing template authors to copy them -- mitigated by the grep CI check blocking inline handlers. Risk: Hugo data/milestones.toml growing to hundreds of entries could slow build time -- mitigated by Hugo's fast template rendering (100 milestones adds under 5 seconds). Tradeoff: Cloudflare Pages over Netlify for government hosting -- Cloudflare's Anycast DDoS protection is more robust for a high-profile government domain, accepting slightly different CI/CD integration compared to Netlify deploy hooks.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Cloudflare Web Analytics over Matomo self-hosted (Cloudflare option requires zero infrastructure and is cookie-free by design). Confirmed Hugo over Jekyll (Hugo build speed advantage at scale). Bootstrap 5 data-attribute initialization confirmed sufficient for all required interactive components (accordion milestones, collapsible navbar, PDF modal) without unsafe-inline. CODEOWNERS approach chosen over GitHub environment protection rules for simplicity in city IT administration.

metadata.notes: Round 3 city climate action plan static site; removes unsafe-inline CSP (ISS-001) via Bootstrap data-attribute migration; documents branch protection settings (ISS-002); weak quality appropriate for simple static site.

profile.domain: Climate Risk - Flood Risk Intelligence API

project_goal.value: API service providing real-time flood risk scores and inundation depth estimates for insurance underwriters and municipal planners, integrating live river gauge data, storm drain sensor feeds, and elevation models.
project_goal.rationale: Modernizes batch-report-based flood risk assessment with sub-500ms API responses.

target_users.value: Insurance underwriters querying risk scores per property coordinate and municipal planners subscribing to flood threshold event webhooks.
target_users.rationale: Dual user segments with different API consumption patterns identified in partner discovery.

project_class.rationale: Pure backend API with no customer-facing UI; consumed by partner integration teams.

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, public_api, realtime, integrations
capabilities.rationale: REST scoring API, PostGIS spatial queries, OAuth2 auth, webhook event delivery, live sensor feed ingestion.

complexity_level.rationale: Sub-500ms spatial risk queries, real-time sensor ingestion, webhook fan-out, and elevation model integration require advanced engineering.

risk_level.rationale: Flood risk scores used in insurance underwriting decisions; data accuracy and API availability directly impact financial and safety outcomes.

data_sensitivity.rationale: River gauge telemetry and inundation models licensed from government agencies under restricted redistribution agreements.

external_exposure.rationale: API accessed by credentialed insurance carrier and municipal partner organizations; not open to public registration.

access_model.value: OAuth2 client credentials flow with per-partner API keys; rate limits enforced per client_id; IP allowlisting for underwriter integrations.
access_model.rationale: Partner-facing API with known client organizations; IP allowlisting provides additional control layer.

feature_scope.value: GET flood risk score by coordinate (lat/lon), GET inundation depth estimate polygon, POST webhook subscription for flood zone threshold events, GET river gauge status feed, GET historical flood event query by date range and geographic bounding box.
feature_scope.rationale: Core API surface for insurance underwriting and municipal planning use cases.

mvp_scope.value: Synchronous risk score endpoint by coordinate and OAuth2 client credentials authentication; webhook subscriptions deferred to v1.1.
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP validates risk scoring pipeline accuracy before adding event-driven webhook complexity.

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all API traffic; OAuth2 client credentials; API gateway rate limiting; audit logging of all scoring requests with client_id and coordinate.
security_baseline.rationale: Partner-facing API with licensed data requires strong access control and query audit trail.

frontend_stack.value: None (API service with no UI; partner integrations consume the REST API directly).
frontend_stack.rationale: api_service project class; no frontend component.

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin HTTP framework; PostGIS spatial queries via pgx driver.
backend_stack.rationale: Go performance characteristics suit sub-500ms spatial query latency requirement; Gin is lightweight with good middleware ecosystem.

data_platform.value: PostGIS on PostgreSQL 16 for geospatial flood zone data and elevation models; Redis 7 for gauge reading cache (5-minute TTL).
data_platform.rationale: PostGIS ST_DWithin and ST_Intersects for sub-second spatial queries; Redis absorbs high-frequency gauge read requests.

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1 behind an ALB; PostGIS on RDS; Redis on ElastiCache.
hosting_target.rationale: Managed infrastructure with VPC isolation for partner-facing confidential data API.

privacy_retention_policy.value: API query audit logs retained 2 years; gauge telemetry retained 7 years for historical flood event analysis; no personal data stored (coordinates are property locations, not linked to individuals).
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FEMA NFIP data use agreement requires audit trail; long retention for actuarial lookback analysis.

future_scope.value: ML-enhanced risk scoring incorporating storm surge forecasts, real-time precipitation radar, and property-level building footprint elevation adjustments.

constraints.value: Flood risk score endpoint p99 latency under 500ms; PostGIS spatial queries optimized with GiST indexes; River gauge data refreshed from USGS NWM API every 5 minutes.
constraints.rationale: Insurance underwriting SLA requires sub-500ms responses; USGS API polling cadence.

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with distributed traces; structured JSON logging with client_id and request_id; PagerDuty alerts for p99 latency above 400ms or error rate above 0.5%.
observability_baseline.rationale: Partner SLA requires proactive alerting before breach.

execution_preference.value: API-first development with OpenAPI 3.1 spec before implementation; GitHub Actions CI/CD with progressive delivery.
execution_preference.rationale: Partner integrations depend on stable API contract.

compliance_context.value: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls (AU, SI, AC control families) required for API service providing flood risk intelligence to insurance underwriters and government municipal planners; API clients operating in regulated insurance sectors must sign Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) confirming permissible use of risk scores in underwriting decisions; SOC 2 Type II audit trail covering all scoring requests with client_id and coordinate logged for 2 years to support regulatory enquiry; flood risk scores classified as confidential proprietary models — client agreements must include NDA and prohibit model reverse engineering; API rate limiting and abuse detection required to prevent bulk extraction of proprietary inundation models; export controls reviewed for dual-use risk data in jurisdictions with disaster management restrictions.
compliance_context.rationale: High risk level and confidential data sensitivity for a flood risk API used by insurance underwriters and municipal planners triggers NIST 800-53 controls, DPA obligations with regulated clients, and proprietary model protection requirements under commercial agreements.

project.goal: Real-time flood risk scoring REST API for insurance and municipal planning partners
project.target_users: Insurance underwriters and municipal emergency planners

backend.framework: Gin HTTP framework
backend.spatial: PostGIS pgx driver for ST_DWithin flood zone queries

security.auth: OAuth2 client credentials via Cognito app client
security.rate_limiting: API Gateway per client_id throttling (insurance: 1000/min, municipal: 200/min)
security.ip_allowlist: Cognito resource server with IP condition for underwriter clients

constraints.gauge_refresh: USGS NWM API polled every 5 minutes

reasoner_reviews.product: The dual-segment API design (synchronous scoring for underwriters, webhook event subscriptions for municipal planners) is the right shape. The MVP decision to defer webhooks is sensible -- synchronous scoring is the core value proposition and the more technically risky latency challenge. Webhook fan-out can land in v1.1 once scoring accuracy is validated with pilot insurance carriers.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go + Gin + PostGIS is the right stack for sub-500ms spatial queries. The critical performance path is the ST_DWithin flood zone intersection query -- GiST spatial indexes are mandatory and their creation must be explicitly included in the migration scripts, not treated as optional. Redis gauge cache TTL of 5 minutes matches the USGS NWM polling cadence and prevents cache stampede on the gauge refresh cycle.
reasoner_reviews.security: Partner-facing API with licensed government data requires a full audit trail. The audit_log table capturing every scoring request with client_id, coordinate, score, and timestamp is essential for FEMA NFIP data use agreement compliance. IP allowlisting for insurance carrier integrations adds defense-in-depth beyond OAuth2 token validation.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 500ms p99 target is achievable with GiST-indexed PostGIS queries for a flood zone database of realistic size (50K-500K polygon features). The USGS NWM API polling job must handle API unavailability gracefully -- stale gauge readings must be served from Redis with a staleness flag in the API response rather than returning errors to partners.

backend.service_design: Gin router with middleware chain: JWT auth middleware (Cognito public keys), rate limit middleware (Redis token bucket per client_id), request ID injection, structured logger. Handlers: FloodRiskHandler (spatial query), GaugeStatusHandler (Redis cache read), HistoricalFloodHandler (PostGIS date range + bbox query).
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1 spec-first. Versioned at /v1/. Request/response validation via oapi-codegen generated types. Pagination via cursor-based tokens for historical flood queries.
backend.business_modules: FloodRiskEngine (ST_DWithin query on flood_zone, elevation lookup, confidence interval computation). GaugeFeedPoller (USGS NWM API polling every 5 minutes, Redis cache write). RiskScoreComposer (combines flood zone proximity, gauge reading delta, and elevation model into composite risk_score 0-100 with flood_depth_m estimate).
backend.failure_handling: USGS NWM API unavailability: serve stale Redis gauge readings with staleness_minutes in API response. PostGIS query timeout: return 503 with Retry-After header. Circuit breaker on USGS API client with 30-second open state.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool app clients for machine-to-machine OAuth2 client credentials. Each partner organization issued a client_id and client_secret. Tokens validated at Gin middleware layer using Cognito JWKS endpoint. Token expiry: 1 hour.
security.authorization_model: Scope-based authorization: flood:read (risk score, gauge status), flood:history (historical queries), flood:subscribe (webhook subscriptions, deferred to v1.1). Scopes assigned per partner tier.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every scoring request logged to audit_log (request_id, client_id, lat, lon, risk_score, flood_depth_m, response_time_ms, timestamp). Audit log written asynchronously to avoid adding latency. Retained 2 years per FEMA NFIP data use agreement.
security.security_risks: Coordinate precision in audit logs could enable reverse-engineering of client underwriting strategies -- mitigate by truncating logged coordinates to 3 decimal places (approximately 100m precision) while preserving full precision for risk computation.

data.entities: flood_zone (id UUID, zone_type ENUM AE/X/VE, geometry GEOMETRY(MultiPolygon, 4326), flood_freq_100yr BOOL, flood_freq_500yr BOOL, source TEXT, ingested_at TIMESTAMP) GiST INDEX. elevation_model (id UUID, grid_cell GEOMETRY(Point, 4326), elevation_m REAL, resolution_m INT, source TEXT) GiST INDEX. river_gauge (id UUID, usgs_gauge_id TEXT, name TEXT, geometry GEOMETRY(Point, 4326), watershed TEXT). gauge_reading (id UUID, gauge_id FK, stage_ft REAL, flow_cfs REAL, flood_stage_ft REAL, reading_at TIMESTAMP, source ENUM usgs_nwm/storm_drain). risk_score_cache (coordinate_hash TEXT PK, risk_score INT, flood_depth_m REAL, flood_zone_type TEXT, cached_at TIMESTAMP, expires_at TIMESTAMP). api_client (client_id TEXT PK, organization_name TEXT, tier ENUM insurance/municipal, ip_allowlist TEXT[], created_at TIMESTAMP). audit_log (request_id UUID PK, client_id TEXT FK, lat_truncated REAL, lon_truncated REAL, risk_score INT, flood_depth_m REAL, response_time_ms INT, timestamp TIMESTAMP).
data.storage_design: PostGIS tables flood_zone and elevation_model with mandatory GiST spatial indexes (CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY). Redis cache for gauge_reading (key: usgs_gauge_id, TTL: 300 seconds) and risk_score_cache (key: coord_hash_with_gauge_version, TTL: 300 seconds).
data.data_risks: Flood zone geometry polygons from FEMA NFIP are licensed data; redistribution via API responses requires per-partner data use agreement. Elevation model data from USGS 3DEP is public domain but requires attribution in API responses per license terms.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster: api-service task (Go Gin, 2 vCPU, 4 GB) and gauge-poller task (USGS NWM polling job, 0.5 vCPU, 1 GB). ALB in front of api-service with TLS termination. RDS PostgreSQL 16 multi-AZ with PostGIS. ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster mode. VPC with private subnets for RDS and Redis; public subnet ALB only.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: go test, golangci-lint, oapi-codegen validation, Docker build, push ECR, ECS rolling deploy. Staging environment requires manual approval gate before production deploy. Database migrations via golang-migrate on ECS one-off task.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM traces on all Gin handlers. Custom metrics: flood_risk_score_latency_ms histogram, gauge_cache_hit_rate, usgs_api_success_rate, audit_log_write_rate. PagerDuty: p99 latency > 400ms OR error_rate > 0.5% OR gauge_staleness_minutes > 15.
devops.devops_risks: ECS Fargate cold start latency for the api-service can contribute to p99 spikes during scale-out events -- mitigate by maintaining minimum 2 running tasks and using Fargate Spot only for the gauge-poller task.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a partner-facing flood risk API with high risk and confidential licensed data -- both conditions require compliance_context in the contract, but the contract is missing it. The plan also erroneously includes a frontend specialist subplan (developer portal) even though this is an api_service project class where FrontendAgent would not run. Both are intentional flaws in this training row representing a flawed-quality plan. The architecture itself is otherwise sound: Go + Gin + PostGIS GiST for sub-500ms spatial queries, Redis gauge cache matching USGS polling cadence, audit logging for FEMA data use agreement compliance.
target_output.title: Flood Risk Intelligence API Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A partner-facing REST API providing real-time flood risk scores and inundation depth estimates to insurance underwriters and municipal planners. Built with Go 1.22 and Gin on AWS ECS Fargate, with PostGIS for geospatial flood zone queries and Redis for river gauge data caching. OAuth2 client credentials authentication per partner organization. Sub-500ms scoring endpoint using GiST-indexed PostGIS spatial queries against FEMA NFIP flood zone polygons and USGS elevation model data.
target_output.architecture_overview: API-only service architecture with no frontend component. Go Gin HTTP handlers behind AWS ALB with TLS 1.3 termination in a VPC private subnet configuration. The flood risk scoring path: Gin handler authenticates OAuth2 token via Cognito JWKS, reads current gauge readings from Redis (5-minute TTL populated by a separate Fargate gauge-poller task polling USGS NWM API), executes a PostGIS ST_DWithin query against GiST-indexed flood_zone polygons for the requested coordinate, performs elevation lookup, composes a risk_score (0-100) and flood_depth_m estimate, logs the request to audit_log asynchronously, and returns the response. The gauge-poller task runs as a separate ECS Fargate task on a 5-minute cron schedule via ECS Scheduled Tasks, writing gauge readings to Redis and PostgreSQL. RDS PostgreSQL 16 with PostGIS serves flood zone geometry, elevation model, and historical gauge data. The developer portal UI mentioned in the specialist subplans section is out of scope for this api_service -- no frontend deployment exists.
target_output.technology_stack: API: Go 1.22, Gin 1.9, pgx v5 (PostGIS driver), go-redis v9, oapi-codegen for OpenAPI 3.1 type generation. Auth: AWS Cognito (OAuth2 client credentials, JWKS validation). Spatial: PostGIS 3.4 on RDS PostgreSQL 16. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB (TLS 1.3), ECR, RDS multi-AZ, VPC with private subnets. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, golang-migrate. Observability: Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Flood Risk Score: accepts lat/lon coordinate, returns risk_score (0-100), flood_zone_type (AE/X/VE/none), flood_depth_m estimate, nearest_gauge_name, gauge_stage_ft, gauge_staleness_minutes. Inundation Estimate: returns inundation polygon GeoJSON for the 100-year flood scenario intersecting the query coordinate vicinity. River Gauge Status: returns current stage, flow, flood stage threshold, and staleness for gauges within a bounding box. Historical Flood Events: paginated query of past flood zone activations by bounding box and date range. Webhook Subscriptions (v1.1): partner organizations subscribe to gauge threshold crossing events with configurable stage thresholds and delivery URLs.
target_output.system_components: ALB (TLS 1.3 termination, WAF rules). ECS Fargate API Task (Go Gin handlers, Cognito JWT middleware, Redis rate limiting, Datadog APM sidecar). ECS Fargate Gauge-Poller Task (USGS NWM API client, Redis gauge cache writer, PostgreSQL historical gauge writer). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with PostGIS (flood_zone GiST index, elevation_model GiST index, gauge_reading, audit_log). ElastiCache Redis 7 (gauge readings TTL 300s, risk_score cache TTL 300s). Cognito User Pool (OAuth2 client credentials app clients per partner). Datadog APM + PagerDuty alerting.
target_output.workflows: Insurance Underwriter Scoring Workflow: Underwriter's integration sends GET /v1/risk-score?lat=29.7604&lon=-95.3698 with Bearer token. Gin middleware validates Cognito JWT (scope: flood:read), checks IP allowlist for the client_id, and applies Redis token bucket rate limiting (1000 req/min for insurance tier). FloodRiskEngine executes PostGIS: SELECT zone_type, ST_Distance(geometry, ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(-95.3698, 29.7604), 4326)) FROM flood_zone WHERE ST_DWithin(geometry, ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(-95.3698, 29.7604), 4326), 0.001) ORDER BY distance LIMIT 1 (GiST index makes this sub-50ms). Elevation lookup from elevation_model. Current gauge stage read from Redis cache. RiskScoreComposer returns {risk_score: 73, flood_zone: AE, flood_depth_m: 1.4, nearest_gauge: Harris County Addicks, gauge_stage_ft: 28.3, gauge_staleness_minutes: 2}. Audit log written asynchronously. Total response time: 180ms p50, 380ms p99. Gauge Staleness Handling: When USGS NWM API is unavailable, GaugeFeedPoller catches the error, retains last Redis values, and sets staleness_minutes based on time since last successful write. API responses include gauge_staleness_minutes allowing partners to apply their own staleness threshold logic.
target_output.data_model: flood_zone (id UUID PK, zone_type TEXT CHECK IN (AE,X,VE,X500,none), geometry GEOMETRY(MultiPolygon,4326) GiST INDEX, flood_freq_100yr BOOL, flood_freq_500yr BOOL, source TEXT, fips_code TEXT, ingested_at TIMESTAMP). elevation_model (id UUID PK, grid_cell GEOMETRY(Point,4326) GiST INDEX, elevation_m REAL, resolution_m INT, source TEXT). river_gauge (id UUID PK, usgs_gauge_id TEXT UNIQUE, name TEXT, geometry GEOMETRY(Point,4326), watershed TEXT, flood_stage_ft REAL). gauge_reading (id UUID PK, gauge_id FK, stage_ft REAL, flow_cfs REAL, reading_at TIMESTAMP, source TEXT). api_client (client_id TEXT PK, organization_name TEXT, tier ENUM insurance/municipal, ip_allowlist INET[], scopes TEXT[], created_at TIMESTAMP). audit_log (request_id UUID PK, client_id TEXT, lat_truncated REAL, lon_truncated REAL, risk_score INT, flood_depth_m REAL, response_time_ms INT, timestamp TIMESTAMP). risk_score_cache in Redis: key=coord_hash:{lat3dp}:{lon3dp}:{gauge_version}, value=JSON risk score object, TTL=300.
target_output.api_design: OpenAPI 3.1 spec-first. Base path /v1/. GET /v1/risk-score?lat={float}&lon={float} returns {risk_score, flood_zone_type, flood_depth_m, nearest_gauge_name, gauge_stage_ft, gauge_staleness_minutes, confidence_interval}. GET /v1/inundation?lat={float}&lon={float}&scenario={100yr|500yr} returns {inundation_polygon_geojson, scenario, data_vintage}. GET /v1/gauges?bbox={minlon,minlat,maxlon,maxlat} returns array of gauge status objects. GET /v1/historical?bbox={bbox}&from={ISO8601}&to={ISO8601}&page_cursor={token} returns paginated flood events. POST /v1/webhooks {gauge_id, stage_threshold_ft, callback_url} (v1.1). All endpoints require OAuth2 Bearer token with appropriate scope. Rate limits: 429 with Retry-After on threshold breach.
target_output.security_and_compliance: TLS 1.3 enforced at ALB; HTTP requests rejected. OAuth2 client credentials via Cognito app client per partner organization; JWT validated at Gin middleware using Cognito JWKS public keys cached for 1 hour. Scope-based authorization: flood:read for scoring and gauge endpoints, flood:history for historical queries. IP allowlisting enforced for insurance tier clients via Cognito resource server condition policy. All scoring requests logged to audit_log asynchronously with client_id, truncated coordinates (3 decimal places, approximately 100m precision), and response time. Audit log retained 2 years. WAF rules on ALB blocking common API abuse patterns. NOTE: This plan is missing the compliance_context field in the frozen_requirement_contract despite risk_level=high and data_sensitivity=confidential -- FEMA NFIP data use agreement and insurance regulatory data handling requirements should have been captured as a required compliance_context field. This is a contract gap that would be flagged by the auditor.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: go test (unit + integration with testcontainers PostGIS), golangci-lint, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with minimum healthy percent 100. Staging deploy requires manual approval gate. golang-migrate database migrations run as ECS one-off task before API deploy. ECS Fargate api-service: minimum 2 tasks (prevents cold start p99 spikes), max 8 tasks, CPU 60% scale-out. Gauge-poller: single Fargate Spot task via ECS Scheduled Tasks (5-minute EventBridge cron). RDS PostgreSQL 16 multi-AZ in us-east-1a/1c. ElastiCache Redis 7 with automatic failover. Secrets (Cognito client secrets, DB password) in AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM distributed traces on all Gin handlers with custom spans for PostGIS queries and Redis reads. Metrics: flood_risk_score_latency_ms P50/P95/P99 histogram, gauge_cache_hit_rate (target > 95%), usgs_nwm_api_success_rate, audit_log_write_success_rate, active_partner_request_rate by client_id. PagerDuty P1 alerts: p99 latency > 400ms, error rate > 0.5%, gauge staleness > 15 minutes. Structured JSON logs with request_id, client_id, lat/lon truncated, risk_score, and response_time_ms on every scoring request.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate 2-8 tasks at $0.04048/vCPU/hr and $0.004445/GB/hr: approximately $180/month at 2 baseline tasks. RDS PostgreSQL 16 multi-AZ db.r6g.large: approximately $220/month. ElastiCache Redis 7 cache.r6g.large: approximately $110/month. Total estimated $600/month for 100K daily scoring requests scaling to $1200/month at 500K. PostGIS spatial query compute scales linearly; Redis gauge cache absorbs proportional gauge read costs.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Go Gin API with FloodRiskEngine (PostGIS ST_DWithin, elevation lookup), GaugeFeedPoller (USGS NWM every 5 minutes), Redis gauge cache, OAuth2 Cognito client credentials, audit logging, OpenAPI 3.1 spec, ECS Fargate deploy, Datadog APM, PagerDuty alerts, GiST spatial indexes on flood_zone and elevation_model. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Historical flood event query endpoint, inundation polygon endpoint, Redis risk_score caching layer, IP allowlisting for insurance clients, partner onboarding workflow. Phase 3 (8 weeks): Webhook subscriptions for municipal planner gauge threshold events, ML risk score enhancement with storm surge forecast integration, multi-region active-passive for 99.9% SLA.
target_output.development_guidelines: OpenAPI 3.1 spec must be written before handler implementation; oapi-codegen generates request/response types enforcing schema compliance. PostGIS queries must use ST_DWithin with GiST-indexed geometry columns -- full table scans are a CI performance regression gate (EXPLAIN ANALYZE in migration tests). All gauge Redis writes use SETEX with 300-second TTL. Audit log writes are asynchronous via Go goroutine with a buffered channel (size 1000) to avoid adding latency to the scoring response path. Integration tests use testcontainers to spin up PostGIS and Redis locally.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: USGS NWM API SLA is not guaranteed; prolonged outages (>15 min) cause gauge staleness alerts to fire for all partners -- mitigate by caching last-known gauge readings in PostgreSQL as a fallback when Redis TTL expires. Risk: FEMA NFIP flood zone polygon data has known inaccuracies in urbanized areas with post-Katrina remapping gaps; risk scores in affected areas may be systematically underestimated -- document data vintage and known gap areas in the API response metadata. NOTE: This plan omitted compliance_context from the contract (ISS candidates for next audit round: FEMA NFIP data use agreement terms, state insurance department data accuracy disclosure requirements). Tradeoff: Synchronous PostGIS query vs pre-computed risk score grid -- synchronous query chosen for MVP to avoid the operational complexity of a tile-based precomputed index, accepting that at very high request rates a precomputed geohash index may be needed.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Go + Gin over Python + FastAPI for sub-500ms latency target (Go garbage collector pauses more predictable under sustained load). Confirmed PostGIS over ElasticSearch spatial queries (PostGIS ST_DWithin faster for polygon intersection workloads). Chosen OAuth2 client credentials over API keys for partner authentication to enable token rotation without partner re-integration. Redis TTL of 5 minutes chosen to match USGS NWM polling cadence exactly.

backend.ingestion: Separate Go ingestion service consuming MQTT from sensor stations

open_questions.forecast_model: In-house ML vs third-party forecast provider for 24h AQI prediction

reasoner_reviews.product: The AQI current-reading and 24-hour forecast are the anchor features for consumer adoption. Sensor station listing and historical readings are important for developer integration workflows. Usage analytics per API key enables the freemium pricing model.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Separating the IoT ingestion service from the API serving service on separate Fargate task definitions is the right isolation boundary — ingestion bursts during weather events cannot degrade API response latency. Redis geographic cell cache with a 5-minute TTL balances freshness with the p99 200ms SLA. ClickHouse MergeTree tables will handle the analytical aggregation queries without impacting low-latency reads.
reasoner_reviews.security: API key scoping with per-tier rate limits is appropriate. Sensor station ingestion should use short-lived JWTs signed by the station's identity certificate rather than static API keys to prevent key leakage from field devices. DDoS protection at the ALB/WAF layer is essential for a public endpoint.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 50K concurrent consumers with p99 < 200ms requires Redis cache hit rate above 95% for the AQI endpoint. Cache invalidation strategy after sensor writes must be precise to avoid stale reads. ClickHouse replication across two nodes prevents a single point of failure for analytics queries.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The previous round 1 plan did not specify the geographic cell model — it was unclear how lat/lng queries resolve to a sensor cluster and how cache keys are structured. The forecast endpoint also lacked a description of whether forecasts are pre-computed or on-demand.

backend.service_design: Two Go services: api-service (Gin, handles consumer requests, reads from Redis and ClickHouse) and ingestion-service (Gin, receives station POST payloads, validates, writes to ClickHouse, invalidates Redis cells). Shared Go module for AQI calculation logic.
backend.api_patterns: Versioned REST under /v1/: GET /aqi?lat=&lng= (returns current AQI + dominant pollutant), GET /forecast?lat=&lng= (returns 24h hourly AQI array pre-computed by forecast job), GET /stations (paginated sensor_station list with lat/lng and last_seen), GET /readings?station_id=&from=&to= (historical ClickHouse query)
backend.scaling_notes: API service scales on CPU 50% target; ingestion service scales on SQS queue depth for station payloads buffered during surge events

security.auth_design: Consumer API: API key in Authorization: Bearer header validated against Redis key store with tier metadata. Ingestion: station submits JWT signed with RS256 private key provisioned at station manufacture.
security.abuse_prevention: Token bucket rate limiter per API key (configurable per tier: free 60/min, pro 600/min, enterprise custom); WAF rule set on ALB for SQL injection and oversized payloads; IP-level block after 10 failed auth attempts in 60 seconds.
security.secrets_management: API master signing key in AWS Secrets Manager; station RSA key registry in DynamoDB; Secrets Manager rotation for ClickHouse credentials every 30 days.

data.entities: sensor_station (station_id, name, lat FLOAT8, lng FLOAT8, geographic_cell_id, owner_org, last_seen_at), air_quality_reading (time DateTime, station_id String, pm25 Float32, pm10 Float32, no2 Float32, o3 Float32, co Float32, aqi_value UInt16, aqi_category LowCardinality(String)) — ClickHouse MergeTree partitioned by toYYYYMM(time), forecast_model_run (run_id, generated_at, geographic_cell_id, forecast_json, model_version), api_consumer (api_key_hash, tier, created_at, rate_limit_rpm)
data.storage_design: ClickHouse ReplicatedMergeTree with 2-shard 2-replica cluster for air_quality_reading; Redis Hash per geographic_cell_id storing current AQI JSON with 5-min TTL; DynamoDB for api_consumer metadata (low-latency key lookup)
data.analytics_events: api_request_log (api_key_hash, endpoint, lat, lng, response_time_ms, cache_hit, timestamp) written to ClickHouse for per-consumer usage analytics and billing aggregation

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate in us-east-1 with two service definitions: api-service (min 4, max 20 tasks) and ingestion-service (min 2, max 10 tasks). ALB routes /v1/* to api-service; ingestion-service behind internal ALB. ClickHouse on EC2 i4i.2xlarge x2 in separate VPC peered to Fargate VPC.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Go lint (golangci-lint), unit tests, build Docker image, push to ECR, deploy ECS rolling update with 50% minimum healthy. Integration tests against staging ClickHouse before prod deploy.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM agent sidecar on each Fargate task; custom metrics: aqi_cache_hit_rate, station_ingestion_rate_per_min, api_latency_p99_ms, forecast_staleness_minutes. PagerDuty alert when cache_hit_rate < 90% or p99 > 180ms.

previous_plan.title: Air Quality Index Public API
previous_plan.executive_summary: A high-scale Go Gin API service delivering current AQI and 24h forecasts to thousands of consumer clients, backed by ClickHouse time-series storage and Redis geographic cell caching, ingesting from a distributed IoT sensor network.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Two Fargate services: api-service and ingestion-service. ClickHouse for analytics storage. Redis for current AQI cache. API keys for consumer auth.
previous_plan.data_model: sensor_station, air_quality_reading (ClickHouse MergeTree), api_consumer. No forecast_model_run entity. No geographic_cell schema.
previous_plan.workflows: Sensor station POSTs reading to ingestion-service; ingestion writes to ClickHouse. Consumer calls AQI endpoint; API reads Redis cache. Cache miss triggers ClickHouse query.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for a high-scale public AQI API. ISS-001 requires defining the H3 geospatial cell model and Redis cache key structure so the lat/lng-to-AQI lookup path is unambiguous. ISS-002 requires specifying forecast pre-computation via a batch job rather than on-demand inference. Quality is weak per manifest so I will be precise on the fixed areas and keep other sections at adequate depth.

fix_report.action_taken: Adopted Uber H3 hexagonal grid at resolution 7 (avg cell area ~5.16 km2) for geographic cell assignment. Each sensor_station is assigned a geographic_cell_id (H3 index string) at registration. Redis cache key pattern: aqi:cell:{h3_index}. On AQI query, Go backend converts lat/lng to H3 resolution-7 index, looks up Redis key, returns cached AQI JSON. Cache invalidated on ingestion write to cells with active stations. Described in data_model, system_components, and workflows.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Geographic resolution model is unambiguous; Redis cache key structure enables deterministic cache hit/miss behavior and precise invalidation.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined forecast_model_run as a pre-computed batch artifact. A forecast-worker ECS task runs every 30 minutes: it queries ClickHouse for the last 6 hours of AQI readings per H3 cell, applies an ARIMA-based trend extrapolation to produce 24 hourly AQI estimates, and writes the forecast_json payload to ClickHouse forecast_model_run. The /v1/forecast endpoint reads the latest forecast_model_run for the resolved H3 cell from a Redis forecast cache (30-min TTL).
fix_report.expected_outcome: Forecast endpoint has a defined data source and computation schedule; consumers receive pre-computed 24h forecasts with predictable staleness bounded by 30 minutes.

target_output.title: Air Quality Index Public API — Rev 2
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale Go API service delivering current AQI readings and 24-hour pollutant forecasts to thousands of consumer apps and smart home devices, backed by ClickHouse columnar storage and Redis H3-cell caching, ingesting from a distributed IoT sensor network. This revision defines the H3 geospatial cell model for geographic resolution and the pre-computed forecast batch pipeline to address round 1 gaps.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two independent ECS Fargate services on AWS: api-service (Go Gin, handles consumer GET requests) and ingestion-service (Go Gin, receives sensor station POST payloads). A third forecast-worker Fargate task runs on a 30-minute schedule. ClickHouse ReplicatedMergeTree cluster stores raw sensor readings and forecast outputs. Redis 7 holds H3-cell AQI cache (5-min TTL) and forecast cache (30-min TTL). Consumer API keys are validated against DynamoDB with per-tier rate limits enforced by a token bucket middleware. WAF on the public ALB protects against abuse.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin HTTP framework, uber-go/h3-go for hexagonal indexing, ClickHouse Go driver. Database: ClickHouse 24.x ReplicatedMergeTree (2-shard 2-replica on EC2 i4i.2xlarge). Cache: Redis 7 on ElastiCache. Key store: DynamoDB for API consumer metadata. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate (api-service, ingestion-service, forecast-worker), ALB with WAF, AWS Secrets Manager, ECR, GitHub Actions CI.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Current AQI Endpoint: resolves lat/lng to H3 resolution-7 cell, returns cached AQI with dominant pollutant and AQI category. Forecast Endpoint: returns pre-computed 24-hour hourly AQI array for the resolved H3 cell, bounded staleness 30 minutes. Sensor Ingestion: authenticated station POSTs raw pollutant readings; ingestion-service validates, writes to ClickHouse, invalidates affected H3 cell Redis key. Station Listing: paginated directory of sensor_station with coordinates and last_seen_at. Historical Readings: ClickHouse range query for a station over a date range. API Key Management: issuance, tier assignment, usage analytics per key.
target_output.system_components: API Service (Go Gin, Fargate, auto-scaling 4-20 tasks): handles consumer requests; resolves lat/lng to H3 index via h3-go; reads from Redis AQI cache; falls back to ClickHouse query on cache miss. Ingestion Service (Go Gin, Fargate, auto-scaling 2-10 tasks): validates station JWT, inserts air_quality_reading to ClickHouse, calls Redis DEL on the station's H3 cell key. Forecast Worker (Go, ECS scheduled task every 30 min): queries ClickHouse for last 6h per H3 cell, runs ARIMA extrapolation, writes forecast_model_run, updates Redis forecast cache. ClickHouse Cluster: 2-shard 2-replica ReplicatedMergeTree for air_quality_reading and forecast_model_run. Redis ElastiCache: two key namespaces — aqi:cell:{h3_index} and forecast:cell:{h3_index}.
target_output.workflows: AQI Request: Consumer sends GET /v1/aqi?lat=37.77&lng=-122.41 with API key. api-service validates key against DynamoDB, applies rate limit. Converts lat/lng to H3 res-7 index (e.g., '872830828ffffff'). Checks Redis key aqi:cell:872830828ffffff. Cache hit: returns AQI JSON in < 5ms. Cache miss: queries ClickHouse for most recent air_quality_reading from stations in that H3 cell, computes AQI, writes to Redis with 5-min TTL, returns result (< 200ms p99 target). Sensor Write: Station POSTs to ingestion-service with JWT. Service validates JWT signature against DynamoDB key registry. Inserts air_quality_reading row to ClickHouse. Issues Redis DEL for aqi:cell:{station.geographic_cell_id}. Forecast Batch: Every 30 min, forecast-worker fetches last 6h hourly avg AQI per H3 cell from ClickHouse. Runs Go ARIMA implementation. Writes forecast_model_run row. Updates Redis forecast:cell:{h3_index} with 30-min TTL.
target_output.data_model: sensor_station (station_id UUID PK, name TEXT, lat FLOAT8, lng FLOAT8, geographic_cell_id TEXT — H3 res-7 index, owner_org TEXT, last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ). air_quality_reading (time DateTime, station_id String, pm25 Float32, pm10 Float32, no2 Float32, o3 Float32, co Float32, aqi_value UInt16, aqi_category LowCardinality(String)) — ClickHouse ReplicatedMergeTree ORDER BY (station_id, time) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(time). forecast_model_run (run_id UUID, generated_at DateTime, geographic_cell_id String, forecast_json String — 24-element JSON array of hourly AQI estimates, model_version String) — ClickHouse MergeTree. api_consumer (api_key_hash String, tier Enum('free','pro','enterprise'), rate_limit_rpm UInt32, created_at DateTime) — DynamoDB table keyed by api_key_hash.
target_output.api_design: GET /v1/aqi?lat={float}&lng={float} — returns {cell_id, aqi, dominant_pollutant, category, station_count, cached_at}. GET /v1/forecast?lat={float}&lng={float} — returns {cell_id, generated_at, hourly:[{hour, aqi, category}x24]}. GET /v1/stations?page=&limit= — returns paginated sensor_station array. GET /v1/readings?station_id={uuid}&from={ISO}&to={ISO} — ClickHouse range query, max 7-day window. POST /v1/ingestion/reading — station JWT required; body: {station_id, pm25, pm10, no2, o3, co, recorded_at}. Auth: Authorization: Bearer {api_key} for consumer endpoints; Authorization: Bearer {jwt} for ingestion endpoint.
target_output.security_and_compliance: API key validation against DynamoDB with tier-based token bucket rate limiting (free: 60 req/min, pro: 600 req/min). WAF on public ALB: AWS managed rules for common web attacks plus rate-based rule blocking IPs exceeding 1000 req/min. Station ingestion uses RS256 JWT; station private keys provisioned at manufacturing and stored in AWS IoT Core certificate store. HTTPS enforced; HTTP redirected at ALB. ClickHouse credentials rotated every 30 days via Secrets Manager. DDoS protection via AWS Shield Standard.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate in us-east-1 with three service definitions: api-service (public ALB, CPU auto-scaling target 50%, min 4 max 20 tasks), ingestion-service (internal ALB, scales on CloudWatch MQTT queue metric, min 2 max 10 tasks), forecast-worker (EventBridge Scheduler every 30 min, single task). ClickHouse deployed on 2x EC2 i4i.2xlarge in VPC private subnet, peered to Fargate VPC. GitHub Actions pipeline: golangci-lint, go test -race, docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with 50% minimum healthy. Staging environment mirrors production topology at 25% scale.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on all Fargate tasks. Custom metrics: aqi_cache_hit_rate (target > 95%), api_request_latency_p99_ms (SLA < 200ms), station_ingestion_rate_per_second, forecast_staleness_minutes (max 30), clickhouse_query_duration_ms. PagerDuty escalation: cache hit rate < 90%, p99 > 180ms, forecast staleness > 45 min, ingestion-service error rate > 1%. Datadog dashboard: real-time AQI request volume by endpoint and H3 region heatmap.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $3,200/month at 50K concurrent consumers with 60% cache hit rate. ECS Fargate costs scale linearly with task count. ClickHouse i4i.2xlarge instances dominate fixed cost at $800/month. Redis ElastiCache r7g.large at $180/month. Cost optimization: ClickHouse native compression reduces storage 5-10x; tiered S3 archive for readings older than 1 year; reserved Fargate baseline for api-service at 4 minimum tasks.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (Weeks 1-5): ClickHouse schema, H3 cell assignment at station registration (ISS-001 fix), ingestion-service with JWT auth, api-service with Redis AQI cache, API key issuance and rate limiting, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (Weeks 6-9): Forecast-worker batch job with ARIMA extrapolation (ISS-002 fix), /v1/forecast endpoint, historical readings endpoint, Datadog observability setup. Phase 3 (Weeks 10-12): Station directory endpoint, usage analytics per API key, WAF tuning, load testing to validate p99 SLA, public beta launch.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go modules with golangci-lint in CI; go test -race for all service packages. H3 resolution constants defined in shared config package — no magic numbers. ClickHouse schema migrations via Atlas. Redis key naming convention documented in ADR-001 (aqi:cell:{h3} and forecast:cell:{h3}). Integration tests run against dockerized ClickHouse and Redis in CI. API versioning via URL path prefix /v1/ with semver-documented breaking change policy.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: H3 cell boundary effects — stations near cell boundaries may produce inconsistent AQI for users just across the boundary. Mitigation: return nearest-station count in response; future enhancement to blend adjacent cells. Risk: ARIMA forecast accuracy degrades for cells with fewer than 3 active stations. Mitigation: flag forecast confidence level in response; fall back to nearest high-station-density cell forecast. Tradeoff: Pre-computed forecasts over on-demand inference accepts up to 30-minute staleness in exchange for sub-10ms forecast endpoint latency. Tradeoff: ClickHouse on EC2 over managed service for cost control, accepting higher operational burden for schema migrations.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: H3 resolution 7 selected (avg 5.16 km2 cell) — coarse enough to aggregate multiple nearby stations, fine enough for urban differentiation. ARIMA chosen over third-party forecast provider to avoid external API dependency and latency; model accuracy reviewed after 60 days of production data. Redis ElastiCache selected over self-managed Redis for automated failover without operational overhead.

metadata.notes: Round 2 high-scale AQI API; defines H3 cell model and forecast pre-computation pipeline.

project_goal.value: Harden the cloud network and data security posture of a climate risk analytics firm by implementing private VPC architecture, secrets rotation, network segmentation, and audit logging across all production workloads
project_goal.rationale: Board-mandated security posture improvement following SOC 2 gap assessment

target_users.value: Platform engineering and security operations teams maintaining production AWS infrastructure
target_users.rationale: Internal operators responsible for ongoing security posture

project_class.rationale: Pure infrastructure hardening initiative; no application frontend or business logic

capabilities.rationale: Terraform IaC, Vault secrets management, and EKS cluster hardening

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-layer network segmentation, secrets automation, and policy-as-code in an existing production environment

risk_level.rationale: Changes to production network topology carry high blast radius; existing workloads process proprietary climate model outputs

data_sensitivity.rationale: Proprietary climate risk model outputs and client portfolio exposure data

external_exposure.rationale: Platform access restricted to authenticated engineers via Keycloak SSO and VPN

access_model.value: Keycloak SSO with OIDC for platform tooling; IAM role-based access for AWS resources; Vault AppRole for service-to-Vault authentication
access_model.rationale: Zero-trust identity model for infrastructure access

feature_scope.rationale: Full scope of SOC 2 gap remediation work

mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum to close the most critical SOC 2 findings

constraints.change_control: All Terraform applies via PR with security review

open_questions.vault_ha: Vault HA with DynamoDB backend vs Raft integrated storage

reasoner_reviews.product: The SOC 2 gap remediation scope is well-bounded with clear MVP (VPC migration, Vault, OPA gate) and future scope (GuardDuty, SIEM). Zero-downtime constraint is the primary delivery risk and must drive the migration sequencing strategy.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The VPC migration requires a blue-green subnet approach: provision new private subnets alongside existing ones, migrate EKS node groups via rolling node replacement, then decommission legacy public-facing subnets. Vault Raft integrated storage is preferred over DynamoDB backend for reduced operational complexity and latency. OPA conftest integration in GitHub Actions plan evaluation is the correct gate placement.
reasoner_reviews.security: CloudTrail must be enabled in all regions including us-east-2 and ap-southeast-1 if any global services are used, not just primary regions. Security Hub FSBP standard should be the baseline; CIS AWS Foundations standard adds coverage for IAM. Vault audit log must capture all secret access events, not just rotation events.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Zero-downtime EKS migration is achievable with node group blue-green replacement over two sprints. Terraform state migration from existing backend to new S3 bucket with DynamoDB lock requires a state pull/push operation during a maintenance window — document this in the runbook.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan resolved the Vault Raft vs DynamoDB question but still lacks specificity on the EKS CIS benchmark hardening steps and does not describe how Security Hub findings are triaged or escalated. The OPA policy library scope is also undefined.

backend.service_design: Terraform module hierarchy: root module per AWS account, child modules for vpc, vault-cluster, eks-hardening, security-hub-config, cloudtrail-config. Vault deployed as a 3-node Raft cluster on dedicated EKS namespace with persistent volumes on EBS gp3.
backend.failure_handling: Vault auto-unseal via AWS KMS; Raft quorum maintained across 3 availability zones. EKS cluster autoscaler on node groups. Terraform apply failures roll back via plan idempotency.
backend.backend_risks: Blue-green VPC migration risk: ENI attachment failures during node group replacement. Mitigation: pre-validate security group rules in staging before production apply.

security.auth_design: IAM roles for EKS service accounts (IRSA) replace static IAM keys. Vault Kubernetes auth method issues short-lived tokens to application pods. Keycloak OIDC integrated with AWS IAM Identity Center for console access.
security.secrets_management: Vault dynamic secrets engine for PostgreSQL: roles generate 1-hour TTL credentials. Static secrets (API keys, TLS certs) in Vault KV v2 with 24h rotation via Vault Agent. Vault audit log streams to CloudWatch Logs.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudTrail multi-region trail logging to KMS-encrypted S3 bucket with 7-year lifecycle. Security Hub FSBP + CIS AWS Foundations standards. OPA decision logs (terraform plan evaluation results) to S3 audit bucket. EKS control plane logs (API, audit, authenticator) to CloudWatch.
security.security_risks: Vault seal status is a blast radius risk: KMS key deletion would render Vault inaccessible. Mitigation: KMS key deletion prevention policy and daily Vault snapshot to S3.

data.entities: cloudtrail_event (stored in S3 as compressed JSON, Athena table: account_id, event_time, event_name, source_ip_address, user_identity, request_parameters), security_hub_finding (finding_id, severity, status, resource_arn, compliance_standard, created_at, updated_at), vault_audit_event (time, request_path, operation, auth_method, client_token_hash)
data.storage_design: CloudTrail S3 bucket with KMS SSE-S3, object versioning, MFA delete, 7-year lifecycle to Glacier. Athena external table on cloudtrail partition by account and date. Security Hub findings in native Security Hub store; exported to S3 monthly for long-term retention.
data.retention_and_deletion: 7-year CloudTrail retention via S3 Intelligent-Tiering to Glacier. Security Hub findings: active findings retained indefinitely; resolved findings archived to S3 after 1 year.

devops.deployment_topology: Two AWS accounts: production-us (us-east-1 EKS) and production-eu (eu-west-1 EKS). Shared services account hosts Vault cluster (EKS namespace), Keycloak (EKS namespace), and Security Hub aggregator. Terraform Cloud workspaces per account with OIDC authentication to AWS.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions pipeline: terraform fmt check, tflint, OPA conftest evaluate plan JSON against policy library (deny rules for: public S3 buckets, security groups with 0.0.0.0/0 ingress on non-443 ports, unencrypted EBS volumes). On pass: Terraform Cloud run triggered. Manual apply approval gate for production workspaces.
devops.observability_stack: AWS CloudWatch dashboards: EKS node CPU/memory, Vault request rate and error rate, Security Hub finding severity distribution. CloudWatch Alarms: Security Hub critical finding count > 0 (PagerDuty), Vault seal status change (PagerDuty), IAM policy attachment to root account (PagerDuty).
