previous_plan.title: Climate Risk Analytics Infrastructure Security Hardening — Rev 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Terraform-driven VPC hardening, Vault 3-node Raft cluster deployment, and OPA policy gate for EKS production infrastructure across us-east-1 and eu-west-1. Round 2 resolved OPA scope and Vault HA design.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Private VPC with three-tier subnet segmentation. Vault Raft on EKS shared services namespace. OPA conftest in GitHub Actions CI. CloudTrail multi-region to KMS S3. Security Hub FSBP + CIS AWS Foundations.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Vault dynamic secrets for PostgreSQL with 1h TTL. IAM IRSA for EKS service accounts. CloudTrail 7-year retention. OPA deny rules: public S3, open security groups 0.0.0.0/0 non-443, unencrypted EBS.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: Terraform Cloud workspaces per AWS account. GitHub Actions pipeline with OPA gate. Manual approval for production applies. Two AWS accounts: production-us, production-eu.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision for an infrastructure security hardening project targeting SOC 2 Type II compliance. ISS-001 requires enumerating specific CIS EKS Benchmark v1.4 controls and describing kube-bench verification in CI. ISS-002 requires defining Security Hub finding triage with role assignment, SLA timers, and escalation path. Plan quality is strong per manifest so I need rich, specific content across all sections.

fix_report.action_taken: Enumerated CIS EKS Benchmark v1.4 controls applied: 5.1.1 (restrict RBAC to least-privilege roles), 5.1.6 (disable automounting service account tokens for default SA), 5.2.1-5.2.6 (Pod Security Admission enforced in 'restricted' mode for all namespaces except kube-system), 5.4.1 (restrict EKS node IAM role to required policies only), 5.5.1 (encrypt Kubernetes secrets with KMS envelope encryption). kube-bench runs as a Kubernetes Job in CI after each EKS cluster change, emitting pass/fail JSON evaluated by OPA conftest. Described in security_and_compliance, deployment_and_operations, and system_components sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CIS EKS hardening steps are specific and auditable; kube-bench in CI provides continuous compliance verification that can be cited in SOC 2 audit evidence.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined Security Hub finding triage workflow: EventBridge rule on SecurityHub.Finding fires for severity CRITICAL and HIGH findings; Lambda function maps findings to JIRA issues with component tag and assigns to SecOps queue; PagerDuty SLA timer starts on creation (critical: 24h, high: 72h). SecOps engineer acknowledges, investigates via Athena CloudTrail query, remediates, and closes the Security Hub finding. Weekly Security Hub finding review in SecOps standup. Described in deployment_and_operations and observability sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Security Hub findings have a defined lifecycle from detection to resolution with SLA accountability; meets SOC 2 incident response evidence requirements.

target_output.title: Climate Risk Analytics Infrastructure Security Hardening — Rev 3
target_output.executive_summary: A Terraform-driven AWS infrastructure hardening initiative for a climate risk analytics firm, implementing private VPC architecture, HashiCorp Vault 3-node Raft cluster with dynamic credential rotation, OPA policy-as-code Terraform gates, and CIS EKS Benchmark v1.4 hardening across production clusters in us-east-1 and eu-west-1. This revision adds explicit CIS EKS control enumeration with kube-bench CI verification and a Security Hub finding triage and escalation workflow, completing the SOC 2 gap remediation design.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three AWS accounts: production-us (us-east-1), production-eu (eu-west-1), and shared-services. Each production account hosts an EKS cluster in a private VPC with three-tier subnet segmentation (public NAT/ALB, private application, isolated database). HashiCorp Vault 3-node Raft cluster runs in the shared-services EKS namespace and is consumed by both production accounts via VPC peering. OPA conftest evaluates Terraform plans in GitHub Actions CI, rejecting changes that violate the policy library. CloudTrail multi-region trails in both production accounts aggregate to a central S3 bucket in the shared-services account. Security Hub aggregates findings from all accounts with EventBridge-driven triage to JIRA and PagerDuty.
target_output.technology_stack: Infrastructure: Terraform 1.8, HashiCorp Vault 1.16 (Raft), OPA 0.65 (conftest), kube-bench v0.7. AWS services: EKS 1.30 (managed node groups), VPC, Security Hub, CloudTrail, KMS, S3, IAM Identity Center, EventBridge, Lambda (finding triage), Athena. Identity: Keycloak 24 (OIDC). CI: GitHub Actions with Terraform Cloud OIDC integration.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Private VPC Topology: three-tier subnet architecture replacing existing flat VPC; NAT gateway in public subnet; EKS nodes in private subnet; RDS/ElastiCache in isolated subnet with no internet route. Vault Secrets Automation: dynamic PostgreSQL credentials (1h TTL), static API key rotation (24h), Vault Agent sidecar for pod secret injection. OPA Terraform Gate: deny rules enforced before every plan apply. EKS CIS Hardening: CIS Benchmark v1.4 controls applied at cluster and node level, verified by kube-bench in CI. Security Hub Triage: EventBridge-Lambda-JIRA pipeline for finding assignment and SLA tracking. CloudTrail Audit: multi-region trail with 7-year S3 retention and Athena query capability.
target_output.system_components: VPC Module (Terraform): 3-tier subnets in 3 AZs per region, NAT gateway, VPC endpoints for S3/ECR/STS to eliminate internet traversal for EKS node traffic. EKS Hardening Module (Terraform): applies CIS 5.1.1 RBAC policies, CIS 5.1.6 SA token automount disable, CIS 5.2.1-5.2.6 Pod Security Admission 'restricted' mode, CIS 5.4.1 node IAM role scope restriction, CIS 5.5.1 KMS envelope encryption for etcd secrets. kube-bench Job: runs after EKS changes in CI, emits JSON report evaluated by OPA conftest; CI fails on CIS HIGH findings. Vault Cluster: 3-node Raft on shared-services EKS (dedicated namespace, node affinity, PodDisruptionBudget min 2 available), KMS auto-unseal. Security Hub Triage Lambda: triggered by EventBridge finding rule; creates JIRA issue with severity, resource ARN, and remediation guide link; starts PagerDuty SLA timer. Terraform Cloud: OIDC-authenticated workspaces per AWS account; plan output in JSON evaluated by OPA conftest before apply.
target_output.workflows: Terraform Change Pipeline: Engineer opens PR with Terraform changes. GitHub Actions runs: terraform fmt, tflint, terraform plan (Terraform Cloud), OPA conftest evaluate plan.json against policy library (public_s3_deny, open_ingress_deny, unencrypted_ebs_deny, public_subnet_workload_deny). OPA pass triggers senior engineer review gate. On approval: Terraform Cloud applies. Post-apply: kube-bench Job runs in affected EKS cluster; OPA evaluates kube-bench JSON report; CIS HIGH failures create a JIRA ticket and block the next deployment. Vault Credential Rotation: Vault Agent sidecar on application pods requests dynamic PostgreSQL credentials via Vault Kubernetes auth method (service account bound to Vault role). Credentials auto-renew before TTL expiry. On rotation, old lease revoked in Vault. Rotation event logged to Vault audit log and forwarded to CloudWatch. Security Hub Finding Triage: EventBridge rule fires on SecurityHub.Findings.Imported for severity CRITICAL or HIGH. Lambda triage function: parses finding, maps to JIRA project (SecOps backlog), creates issue with SLA due date (critical: now+24h, high: now+72h), posts to PagerDuty service. SecOps engineer receives PagerDuty alert, queries Athena CloudTrail table for relevant API calls, investigates, remediates. On remediation: Security Hub finding suppressed or resolved, JIRA issue closed, PagerDuty incident resolved.
target_output.data_model: CloudTrail audit store: S3 bucket (KMS SSE-KMS, versioning, MFA-delete, 7-year lifecycle Intelligent-Tiering to Glacier). Athena external table: cloudtrail_logs (eventVersion, eventTime, eventName, awsRegion, sourceIPAddress, userAgent, userIdentity STRUCT, requestParameters STRING, responseElements STRING) partitioned by account_id/year/month/day. Security Hub findings: native SecurityHub store + EventBridge export. Vault audit log: CloudWatch log group /vault/audit with retention 365 days; fields: time, type, auth.accessor, request.path, request.operation. kube-bench results: S3 object per run with cluster_id and timestamp prefix; Athena table for trend queries.
target_output.api_design: No external API surface. Internal tooling interfaces: GitHub Actions OPA conftest exit codes (0=pass, 1=policy violation) with JSON policy evaluation output. kube-bench JSON report schema: {Controls: [{id, text, tests: [{result: 'PASS'/'FAIL'/'WARN', scored: bool}]}]}. Vault Kubernetes auth endpoint: POST /v1/auth/kubernetes/login with {role, jwt} returns {client_token, lease_duration}. EventBridge rule pattern: {source: ['aws.securityhub'], detail-type: ['Security Hub Findings - Imported'], detail: {findings: [{Severity: {Label: ['CRITICAL','HIGH']}}]}}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SOC 2 Type II controls addressed: CC6.1 (logical access via IRSA and Keycloak OIDC), CC6.3 (Vault dynamic credentials eliminate standing access), CC6.7 (CloudTrail immutable audit log), CC7.2 (Security Hub finding triage with 24h/72h SLA). ISO 27001 A.12.4 (logging and monitoring via CloudTrail and CloudWatch). GDPR data residency: eu-west-1 EKS cluster processes EU client data; VPC peering to shared-services uses private IPs; no EU data traverses us-east-1. CIS EKS Benchmark v1.4: CIS 5.1.1 RBAC least-privilege (cluster-admin bound only to platform-admin group), CIS 5.1.6 automountServiceAccountToken: false on default SA, CIS 5.2.1-5.2.6 Pod Security Admission restricted mode, CIS 5.4.1 node IAM role with minimal EC2/ECR/CloudWatch permissions only, CIS 5.5.1 KMS envelope encryption for etcd secrets. kube-bench verifies compliance after each cluster change.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform Cloud workspaces: production-us, production-eu, shared-services (each OIDC-authenticated to respective AWS account). GitHub Actions CI stages: fmt → lint → plan → OPA gate → manual approval → apply → kube-bench verification → OPA kube-bench evaluation. VPC migration strategy: provision new private subnets alongside existing; migrate EKS node groups via rolling replacement (one node group at a time, drain old nodes); decommission legacy subnets after all workloads confirmed healthy. Vault deployment: Helm chart to shared-services EKS, PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable: 2, nodeAffinity to dedicated node group, KMS auto-unseal key in AWS KMS. Security Hub triage Lambda: Python 3.12 on arm64, EventBridge trigger, JIRA Cloud API and PagerDuty Events v2 API integrations. All Lambda environment variables sourced from Secrets Manager at invocation.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch dashboards: EKS node CPU/memory per cluster, Vault request rate and error rate (from Vault /v1/sys/metrics), Security Hub finding count by severity over time. CloudWatch Alarms (all to PagerDuty): Security Hub CRITICAL finding count > 0 (immediate), Vault seal status change (immediate), kube-bench CIS HIGH failure in CI (Slack notification), CloudTrail logging disabled in any region (immediate). OPA conftest decision log forwarded to S3 audit bucket for compliance evidence. Weekly Security Hub finding review in SecOps standup with JIRA burndown chart for open findings.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Infrastructure hardening is a one-time capital expenditure with ongoing operational costs. Vault cluster on 3x m6i.large EKS nodes: ~$320/month. Lambda triage function: negligible cost at Security Hub finding volumes. CloudTrail S3 storage: ~$40/month at estimated log volume. Terraform Cloud: Business tier at $20/seat for 6 engineers. Total ongoing delta: ~$400/month above existing EKS baseline. Cost optimization: Vault nodes on Reserved Instances after initial deployment validation.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - MVP (Weeks 1-4): Private VPC topology with three-tier subnet segmentation, EKS node group blue-green migration to private subnets, Vault 3-node Raft deployment with KMS auto-unseal, first Vault dynamic secret role for PostgreSQL, OPA Terraform gate in GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (Weeks 5-8): CIS EKS Benchmark v1.4 hardening module deployment (ISS-001 fix), kube-bench CI integration with OPA evaluation, Security Hub EventBridge-Lambda-JIRA triage pipeline (ISS-002 fix), CloudTrail multi-region trail with Athena table. Phase 3 (Weeks 9-12): KMS envelope encryption for EKS etcd secrets, Vault Agent sidecar rollout to all production pods, SOC 2 evidence collection and documentation, dry-run audit with external auditor. Phase 4 (Weeks 13-16): Remediate any audit findings, GuardDuty enablement, final SOC 2 readiness sign-off.
target_output.development_guidelines: Terraform code follows Google module structure convention. All modules have a terraform-docs generated README. OPA policy library in a dedicated rego/ directory with unit tests using opa test. No Terraform resources with count = 0 as dead code; use feature flags via variables. kube-bench Job definition versioned in the eks-hardening-module. All Vault policies follow least-privilege: path patterns limited to specific secret paths per application role. ADR documents for: Vault Raft vs DynamoDB (ADR-001, resolved), OPA policy scope (ADR-002), CIS control exclusions with justification (ADR-003).
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: EKS node group blue-green migration during business hours could cause brief pod rescheduling disruption. Mitigation: schedule migration windows during off-peak hours; pre-validate PodDisruptionBudgets on all production workloads. Risk: Vault seal event renders all dynamic credentials inaccessible until unseal. Mitigation: KMS auto-unseal with key deletion prevention; Vault snapshot to S3 daily; documented manual unseal runbook. Risk: CIS Pod Security Admission 'restricted' mode may break existing pods using hostPath or privileged containers. Mitigation: audit all pods in staging with Pod Security Admission dry-run mode before enforcing in production. Tradeoff: Terraform Cloud over self-managed Terraform state accepts SaaS dependency for improved plan visibility and team collaboration. Tradeoff: Single shared-services Vault cluster for both regions over per-region Vault accepts cross-region latency on secret reads in eu-west-1 (< 80ms measured in testing).
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Vault Raft integrated storage selected over DynamoDB backend: lower latency, no additional AWS service dependency, Raft quorum provides HA equivalent. OPA conftest selected over Sentinel for Terraform gate: open source with active community, integrates with GitHub Actions without Terraform Enterprise requirement. CIS EKS Benchmark v1.4 selected as the hardening baseline: most current EKS-specific guidance, kube-bench tooling aligns with v1.4 check IDs.

project_goal.value: Launch landing page for a direct-air carbon capture startup to generate corporate off-take inquiries and communicate the project's technology, timeline, and science credibility
project_goal.rationale: Primary conversion goal is signed off-take agreement pipeline

target_users.value: Corporate sustainability directors and Chief Sustainability Officers at Fortune 500 companies seeking to purchase permanent carbon removal
target_users.rationale: Primary buyer segment identified in sales strategy

project_class.rationale: Single-page marketing site with lead capture; no backend application logic

capabilities.rationale: Static marketing page with conversion tracking

complexity_level.rationale: Static site with a lead capture form; no server-side logic

risk_level.rationale: No user accounts, no payment processing, no personal health or financial data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Off-take inquiry form collects business contact info only; no regulated data

external_exposure.rationale: Public marketing page accessible to anyone

access_model.value: No authentication; publicly accessible to all visitors
access_model.rationale: Marketing landing page with no gated content

feature_scope.value: Hero section with DAC technology animation, science explainer with process diagram, project timeline with construction milestones, team and advisory board section, off-take inquiry form, FAQ, partner/investor logo strip
feature_scope.rationale: Full launch page content scope

mvp_scope.value: Hero, science explainer, off-take inquiry form with CRM webhook, Vercel Analytics
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum to begin generating off-take pipeline

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Vercel, Content Security Policy headers, no third-party tracking scripts beyond Vercel Analytics
security_baseline.rationale: Privacy-respecting analytics and standard CSP for a public page

frontend_stack.rationale: Static export capability; Vercel native deployment; excellent image optimization

backend_stack.value: None (static site with Vercel serverless form handler)
backend_stack.rationale: No backend runtime needed; form submission via Vercel Edge Function webhook

data_platform.value: None (no database; form submissions forwarded to HubSpot CRM via webhook)
data_platform.rationale: CRM is the system of record for off-take inquiries

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network
hosting_target.rationale: Zero-config CDN deployment with Next.js static export

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data stored on the site; HubSpot CRM governed by separate data processing agreement
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Data stays in CRM; site does not retain visitor data

future_scope.value: Investor relations section, interactive plant capacity calculator, customer testimonials page

constraints.value: Page load time under 2.5s on 4G mobile; no client-side analytics that require cookie consent banners
constraints.rationale: Performance and GDPR compliance requirements

observability_baseline.value: Vercel Analytics for page views, conversion events, and Core Web Vitals
observability_baseline.rationale: Privacy-friendly analytics sufficient for marketing insights

execution_preference.value: Content-first build: design system established first, then sections composed iteratively with stakeholder review gates
execution_preference.rationale: Marketing team needs review touchpoints before each section goes live

project.goal: Generate corporate off-take inquiry pipeline for a direct-air carbon capture startup
project.target_users: Corporate sustainability directors and CSOs

frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with custom brand tokens
frontend.animation: Framer Motion for DAC process animation

security.analytics: Vercel Analytics (no cookies, no consent banner required)

open_questions.form_handler: Vercel serverless function vs Formspree for inquiry form submission

reasoner_reviews.product: The off-take inquiry form is the single conversion goal; all page sections should funnel toward it. The science explainer and project timeline are credibility signals that reduce friction for high-value corporate buyers. Logo strips (partners, investors) increase trust for a startup with limited brand recognition.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 14 static export on Vercel is correct for this use case. The DAC process animation using Framer Motion should lazy-load to protect LCP. The off-take inquiry form should use a Vercel Edge Function to forward submissions to HubSpot via API, avoiding third-party form script payloads that would violate the CSP baseline.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP header must be strict: script-src 'self'; no inline scripts; no third-party JS from ad networks. Vercel Analytics is script-injected server-side so it does not violate CSP. Form submission to HubSpot should be a server-side proxy to avoid exposing the HubSpot API key in client-side code.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: LCP under 2.5s on 4G mobile requires the hero image to use Next.js Image with priority=true, preload hint, and a WebP source set. Framer Motion animations must be below-the-fold only or triggered after LCP to avoid render-blocking.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 app router static export; single page with section anchors: /#hero, /#science, /#timeline, /#team, /#inquiry, /#faq
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single-page layout with sticky nav. Hero: full-bleed video loop of DAC plant with animated CO2 capture counter. Science explainer: step diagram (air intake → electrochemical capture → compression → geological storage) with scroll-triggered Framer Motion transitions. Off-take inquiry form: company name, contact name, email, annual tonne volume, preferred delivery year — submits to /api/inquiry Edge Function.
frontend.performance_notes: hero image: Next.js Image priority=true, WebP, 1200w/800w/400w srcset. Framer Motion animations wrapped in lazy dynamic imports. All section images use next/image with blur placeholder. Target LCP < 2s.

security.auth_design: No authentication required
security.abuse_prevention: Inquiry form: honeypot field for bot detection; Vercel Edge Function rate-limits to 10 submissions per IP per hour via KV store
security.secrets_management: HubSpot API key stored in Vercel environment variable (server-side only); never exposed to client bundle

data.entities: offtake_inquiry (forwarded to HubSpot as CRM contact + deal: company, contact_name, email, annual_tonnes, delivery_year, submitted_at), analytics_event (Vercel Analytics: page_view, inquiry_form_start, inquiry_form_submit, cta_click — privacy-preserving, no PII)
data.storage_design: No database on the site. HubSpot is sole data store for inquiries. Vercel Analytics stores aggregated event data without individual visitor identification.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel project connected to GitHub repo. Next.js static export deployed to Vercel Edge Network (global CDN). Each PR generates a preview deployment URL shared with marketing stakeholders.

previous_plan.title: Direct Air Capture Startup Launch Landing Page
previous_plan.executive_summary: Next.js 14 static landing page on Vercel for a DAC startup generating corporate off-take inquiries via a lead capture form forwarded to HubSpot CRM.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Static Next.js export on Vercel Edge CDN. Framer Motion animations for DAC process diagram. Vercel Analytics for privacy-preserving conversion tracking.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: CSP headers in next.config.js. Vercel Analytics (no cookies). HubSpot form handler via Edge Function — API key handling not specified.
previous_plan.workflows: Visitor arrives via LinkedIn or conference sponsorship link. Reads hero and science sections. Clicks 'Request Off-Take Terms' CTA. Submits inquiry form. Form POSTs to /api/inquiry Edge Function. Edge Function forwards to HubSpot.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for a simple DAC startup landing page. ISS-001 is the only focus issue: the HubSpot API key handling in the Vercel Edge Function was not specified. I need to explicitly document that HUBSPOT_API_KEY is a server-side Vercel environment variable (no NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix), describe the Edge Function as the server-side proxy, and add IP rate limiting via Vercel KV. Plan quality is moderate per manifest so sections are good but not exhaustive.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified that HUBSPOT_API_KEY is stored as a Vercel environment variable without the NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix, making it server-side only and absent from the client bundle. The /api/inquiry Vercel Edge Function reads the key via process.env.HUBSPOT_API_KEY server-side and proxies the POST to HubSpot Contacts API. The client-side inquiry form submits only to /api/inquiry — it never directly calls HubSpot. Added IP-based rate limiting via Vercel KV: the Edge Function checks a counter key for the submitting IP and returns 429 after 10 submissions per hour.
fix_report.expected_outcome: HubSpot API key is never exposed in the client bundle; server-side proxy pattern is the sole integration path; rate limiting prevents inquiry form abuse.

target_output.title: Direct Air Capture Startup Launch Landing Page — Rev 2
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 static landing page deployed on Vercel for a direct-air carbon capture startup, converting corporate sustainability leaders into off-take inquiry leads via a science explainer, project timeline, and a CTA-gated inquiry form that server-side proxies submissions to HubSpot CRM. This revision explicitly documents the secure HubSpot API key handling pattern.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 static export deployed to Vercel Edge Network. All page content is statically generated at build time — no server-side rendering except the /api/inquiry Edge Function which acts as a server-side proxy to HubSpot. Framer Motion handles the DAC electrochemical capture animation and scroll-triggered section transitions. Vercel Analytics tracks conversion events without cookies or consent banners. GitHub Actions runs Lighthouse CI to enforce LCP < 2.5s before every Vercel deployment.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 (app router, static export), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS with custom brand color tokens, Framer Motion for animations, next/image for WebP srcsets. Form: /api/inquiry Vercel Edge Function proxying to HubSpot Contacts API v3. Analytics: Vercel Analytics (privacy-preserving, no cookies). CI: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI action. Hosting: Vercel Edge Network with automatic PR preview deployments.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: full-bleed WebP image of DAC plant facade with animated CO2 tonnes-captured counter (JavaScript IntersectionObserver counter animation). Science Explainer: four-step process diagram (atmospheric air intake → electrochemical sorbent capture → CO2 desorption → geological compression and injection) with scroll-triggered Framer Motion step reveals. Project Timeline: horizontal milestone timeline showing site preparation, first module construction, commissioning, and commercial operations target dates. Team and Advisory Board: headshots and bios for scientific credibility with institutional affiliations. Off-Take Inquiry Form: company, contact name, email, annual tonne volume (dropdown: 1K-10K, 10K-50K, 50K+ tCO2/year), preferred delivery year, message — submits to /api/inquiry. FAQ: expandable accordion addressing permanence, verification methodology, and delivery risk. Partner and Investor Logos: strip of recognizable climate investors and technology partners.
target_output.system_components: Next.js Static Export: HTML/CSS/JS bundles built at deploy time; all sections are static React components. /api/inquiry Edge Function: receives form POST, validates fields (company name, email format, tonne volume), checks Vercel KV rate limit for submitting IP, calls HubSpot Contacts API v3 with HUBSPOT_API_KEY from server-side environment variable, returns 200 on success. Vercel Edge Network: serves static assets globally from CDN POPs. Vercel Analytics: event tracking via server-side injection (no client-side script tag; no cookie dependency). GitHub Actions + Lighthouse CI: LCP and performance gate on every PR.
target_output.workflows: Visitor Conversion: Corporate sustainability director arrives via LinkedIn sponsored post or conference partner link. Reads hero with animated CO2 counter, scrolls through science explainer diagram (Framer Motion step animations trigger on scroll). Views project timeline showing construction milestones. Reads team advisory board credentials. Clicks 'Request Off-Take Terms' sticky CTA button. Fills inquiry form specifying 10K-50K tCO2/year and 2027 delivery. Submits form. Off-Take Inquiry Submission: Browser POSTs form fields to /api/inquiry Edge Function. Edge Function validates required fields. Checks Vercel KV key rate_limit:{ip}: if count >= 10 in the last hour, returns 429. Otherwise increments counter (TTL 3600s). Calls POST https://api.hubapi.com/crm/v3/objects/contacts with contact and deal properties using HUBSPOT_API_KEY from server-side env. On HubSpot 201 response, returns {success: true} to browser; form shows success state. Vercel Analytics tracks inquiry_form_submit event.
target_output.data_model: No database on the site. Data entities exist only in downstream systems: offtake_inquiry in HubSpot CRM (company STRING, contact_name STRING, email STRING, annual_tonnes_tier ENUM, delivery_year INT, submitted_at DATETIME, utm_source STRING, utm_campaign STRING). Vercel Analytics aggregated events: page_view (path, referrer), cta_click (section, variant), inquiry_form_start, inquiry_form_submit (no PII in event payload). Vercel KV: rate_limit:{ip_address} counter (TTL 3600s).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/inquiry (Vercel Edge Function): request body {company: string, contact_name: string, email: string, annual_tonnes_tier: '1k-10k'|'10k-50k'|'50k+', delivery_year: number, message?: string}. Validates fields; checks Vercel KV rate limit. Proxies to HubSpot Contacts API v3 POST /crm/v3/objects/contacts using server-side HUBSPOT_API_KEY. Returns {success: true} or {error: string}. Rate limit: 10 submissions per IP per hour. No other API routes on the site.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Vercel (all HTTP redirected). Content-Security-Policy header set in next.config.js headers(): script-src 'self' (no inline scripts, no third-party ad scripts); img-src 'self' data: blob:; connect-src 'self' https://api.hubapi.com (Edge Function only, not client). HSTS: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains. X-Frame-Options: DENY. HUBSPOT_API_KEY stored as Vercel environment variable without NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix — server-side only, absent from client bundle. Edge Function rate limits via Vercel KV to prevent inquiry form abuse (10 per IP per hour). Honeypot hidden field in form detects bot submissions before proxying to HubSpot. Vercel Analytics is privacy-preserving: no cookies, no fingerprinting, GDPR-compliant without consent banner.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Vercel project linked to GitHub repository. Production deployment: automatic on merge to main branch. Preview deployments: automatic on each PR, URL shared in PR comment for stakeholder content review. GitHub Actions CI on each PR: next lint (ESLint with Next.js config), tsc --noEmit (TypeScript strict), next build (verifies static export compiles without errors), Lighthouse CI action targeting preview deployment URL (LCP < 2500ms, Performance score > 90, Accessibility score > 90). Deployment environment variables: HUBSPOT_API_KEY set in Vercel project settings (production and preview scopes). KV_REST_API_URL and KV_REST_API_TOKEN for Vercel KV (rate limiting).
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics dashboard: daily page views, inquiry form conversion rate (inquiry_form_submit / page_view), CTA click-through rate by section. Core Web Vitals tracking: LCP, FID, CLS per deployment. Vercel build and deployment failure alerts via email. Lighthouse CI regressions block PR merge. HubSpot CRM provides lead pipeline visibility; no additional analytics tooling required.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Hobby plan (free) covers the initial launch; Pro plan ($20/month) if team collaboration features or higher build concurrency needed. Vercel KV free tier (30K requests/day) sufficient for rate limiting at expected inquiry volumes. HubSpot CRM Starter tier. Total estimated cost: $0-$20/month. Scaling is handled by Vercel Edge CDN; no infrastructure scaling decisions needed for a static site.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - MVP (Week 1-2): Next.js project setup, Tailwind brand token configuration, Hero + Science Explainer sections, /api/inquiry Edge Function with HubSpot proxy and rate limiting, Vercel Analytics, GitHub Actions CI with Lighthouse gate, Vercel deployment. Phase 2 (Week 3-4): Project Timeline section, Team and Advisory Board section, FAQ accordion, Partner logo strip, full page design polish, stakeholder review cycle, launch to production. Phase 3 (Post-launch): A/B test CTA copy variants using Vercel Edge Config, add investor relations section, interactive plant capacity calculator.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode throughout. Tailwind CSS via cn() utility for conditional class merging (clsx + tailwind-merge). All images through next/image; no raw img tags. Framer Motion imports via dynamic(() => import('framer-motion'), {ssr: false}) to exclude from server-side rendering. NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix forbidden for any API keys — enforced by ESLint rule. Each PR must pass Lighthouse CI LCP gate before merge. Content managed in a /content/data.ts file as typed TypeScript objects for easy stakeholder updates without touching component code.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: HubSpot API v3 rate limit (100 requests/10 seconds) could be hit if inquiry form goes viral after a press mention. Mitigation: inquiry volume unlikely to approach that threshold; Edge Function returns 503 gracefully if HubSpot returns 429. Risk: Vercel free tier limits (6000 Edge Function invocations/month) could be exceeded under heavy traffic. Mitigation: upgrade to Pro tier if needed; low cost relative to off-take deal value. Tradeoff: no database on the site keeps architecture simple and eliminates data retention compliance obligations, at the cost of losing inquiry data if HubSpot webhook fails; mitigated by checking HubSpot API response before returning success.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Vercel Edge Function selected over Formspree: avoids third-party script in CSP, keeps HubSpot key server-side, and provides more control over rate limiting and error handling. Vercel Analytics confirmed as sufficient for conversion tracking without cookie consent banner requirement. Static export over server-side rendering selected for maximum CDN cache efficiency and zero cold-start latency.

project.goal: Interactive SME green loan lead generation landing page
project.target_users: SME business owners and CFOs

frontend.framework: Astro 4 with React 18 island (calculator component) and TypeScript
frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with green finance brand palette

security.analytics: Plausible Analytics (self-hosted or Plausible Cloud; cookieless)

constraints.legal: Illustrative estimate disclaimer on calculator output

open_questions.plausible: Plausible Cloud vs self-hosted on Hetzner

reasoner_reviews.product: The loan calculator is the core engagement mechanism; the eligibility score output is the key hook that differentiates this from a generic contact form. The funnel from calculator completion to lead form submission is where conversion optimization will happen post-launch. Partner bank logos are trust signals critical for SME audiences unfamiliar with the brand.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 with a React island for the calculator is architecturally correct: zero JS shipped for static sections, React only loaded for the interactive calculator component. The green eligibility score algorithm should be purely client-side JavaScript — no server round-trip needed for an illustrative estimate. Netlify Function as form proxy keeps CRM API key server-side.
reasoner_reviews.security: The calculator must prominently display 'These estimates are illustrative and do not constitute financial advice or a loan offer' to meet FCA guidance for financial promotions. CSP must allowlist the Plausible Analytics script source. The Netlify Function proxy for the HubSpot CRM submission must store the API key as a Netlify environment variable (not prefixed for client exposure).
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse > 95 on Astro 4 is achievable with the island architecture. The React island for the calculator should be client:visible (loaded when scrolled into view) rather than client:load (loaded immediately) to preserve LCP.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 static site with a single index.astro page. React island: <LoanCalculator client:visible /> component encapsulates all interactive calculator and eligibility scoring logic.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Page structure: hero (value proposition, Plausible goal tracking on CTA clicks), product tier table (green bond vs SLL vs green loan), calculator island (revenue bracket, business sector dropdown, loan amount slider → rate range estimate + green eligibility score + disclaimer), eligibility pre-screening (sector-based green criteria checklist, score visualization), lead capture form (company name, contact, email, phone optional, preferred product tier), partner bank logo strip, sustainability credentials section
frontend.state_management: React useState for calculator inputs (revenue_bracket, sector, loan_amount) and derived estimates. No external state library needed for a single-island page.
frontend.performance_notes: Astro zero-JS static shell for all non-calculator sections. Calculator island: client:visible directive defers React load until scroll. Tailwind CSS purged at build. Images via Astro Image integration (WebP, responsive srcsets). Target LCP < 1.5s.

security.abuse_prevention: Netlify Function rate-limits lead form submissions to 5 per IP per 15 minutes via Netlify Edge Functions KV store; honeypot field for bot detection
security.secrets_management: HUBSPOT_API_KEY in Netlify environment variables (not prefixed VITE_ or ASTRO_ which would expose to client)

data.entities: lead_submission (forwarded to HubSpot: company_name, contact_name, email, phone, loan_amount_requested, preferred_product STRING, sector STRING, green_eligibility_score INT, submitted_at, utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign), plausible_event (goal: calculator_start, calculator_complete, lead_form_submit — cookieless, no PII)
data.storage_design: No database. Netlify CDN serves static assets. Netlify Function proxies form data to HubSpot Contacts API. Plausible Cloud aggregates anonymized events.

devops.deployment_topology: Netlify project connected to GitHub. Deploy on push to main. PR deploy previews for stakeholder review.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: astro check (TypeScript), eslint, astro build, Lighthouse CI action on Netlify preview URL (Performance > 95 gate). Netlify deploy on merge to main.
devops.observability_stack: Plausible Analytics: daily funnel report (calculator_start → calculator_complete → lead_form_submit conversion rates), UTM campaign breakdown. Netlify analytics for bandwidth and 404 monitoring.

previous_plan.executive_summary: Astro 4 static landing page with a React island loan calculator generating green eligibility scores and forwarding qualified leads to HubSpot CRM via Netlify Function proxy.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static shell. React client:visible island for calculator. Netlify CDN. Plausible Analytics for funnel tracking. Netlify Function for form proxy.
