previous_plan.title: Community Solar Subscription Management Platform
previous_plan.executive_summary: Fullstack Next.js 14 and Fastify platform for community solar garden operators with subscriber enrollment, BullMQ billing cycle jobs, Stripe payment processing, and subscriber savings dashboards on AWS ECS Fargate.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 subscriber frontend on Vercel. Fastify API on ECS Fargate. BullMQ workers for billing cycle and production ingestion. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ. Auth0 RBAC. Stripe Elements for PCI SAQ A compliance.
previous_plan.data_model: solar_garden, subscriber, energy_share_allocation (allocated_kwh — formula undefined), production_reading, billing_statement, payment_transaction.
previous_plan.workflows: Monthly billing cycle: BullMQ job triggered. Production data ingested. Subscribers allocated energy credits (formula not defined). Billing statements generated. Stripe charges initiated. Subscriber dashboard updated.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for a community solar subscription platform. ISS-001 requires defining the proportional energy allocation formula explicitly: subscriber_allocated_kwh = (subscriber.share_size_kw / garden_active_subscribed_kw) * production_reading.total_kwh. ISS-002 requires resolving utility integration: CSV S3 upload selected with documented CSV schema and ingestion trigger. Plan quality is moderate per manifest so sections should be good but not exhaustive in every detail.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined proportional energy credit allocation formula: at billing cycle start, the billing_cycle_job queries all active subscribers for the garden and computes garden_active_subscribed_kw = SUM(share_size_kw) for status='active' subscribers. Each subscriber's share_fraction = share_size_kw / garden_active_subscribed_kw. subscriber_allocated_kwh = share_fraction * production_reading.total_kwh (rounded to 3 decimal places). bill_credit_usd = subscriber_allocated_kwh * utility_rate_per_kwh (from garden config). energy_share_allocation record stores subscriber_id, billing_period, allocated_kwh, share_fraction, production_reading_id. Formula described in workflows, data_model, and functional_feature_map sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Billing algorithm is unambiguous and auditable; state PUC regulators can verify allocation methodology from the production_reading and energy_share_allocation records.

fix_report.action_taken: Selected CSV S3 upload as the utility production data integration method. Utility uploads a monthly CSV to s3://community-solar-prod/utility-data/{garden_id}/{period}.csv. S3 ObjectCreated triggers an SQS message consumed by the BullMQ production_ingestion_job. CSV schema: garden_id, billing_period (YYYY-MM), total_kwh, net_metering_credit_usd, report_date. The ingestion job validates schema, inserts production_reading, and publishes a billing_period_data_ready event to trigger the billing cycle. CSV schema documented in system_components and workflows.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Utility integration path is unambiguous; CSV schema is specified for utility onboarding; ingestion trigger is defined.

target_output.title: Community Solar Subscription Management Platform — Rev 2
target_output.executive_summary: A fullstack Next.js 14 and Node.js Fastify platform for community solar garden operators managing subscriber enrollments, monthly energy credit allocation, billing reconciliation, and Stripe payment collection. The platform runs billing cycle jobs via BullMQ workers on AWS ECS Fargate, allocates energy production proportionally to subscriber kW shares, and provides subscriber savings dashboards. This revision defines the proportional energy allocation formula and resolves the utility production data ingestion integration.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 App Router frontend deployed on Vercel for subscriber dashboard SSR and operator admin panel. Fastify REST API on ECS Fargate for billing, enrollment, and admin endpoints protected by Auth0 JWT. BullMQ worker service on a second ECS task processes the monthly billing_cycle_job, daily production_ingestion_job, and per-subscriber statement_generation_jobs. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ stores all transactional billing and subscriber data with ACID guarantees. Redis 7 ElastiCache serves as BullMQ broker. Stripe Elements tokenizes payment methods in the browser (PCI SAQ A); the Fastify API only handles Stripe payment intent IDs. Auth0 enforces RBAC and MFA for operator accounts.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, TanStack Query, Recharts. Backend: Node.js 22, Fastify with fastify-jwt, fastify-rate-limit, Zod schema validation. Jobs: BullMQ on Redis 7. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ. Cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache. Payments: Stripe SDK (Elements for tokenization, PaymentIntents for charges). Auth: Auth0 with RBAC and MFA. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate (api-service, bullmq-worker-service), Vercel (Next.js), S3 (utility CSV uploads), SQS, RDS, ElastiCache, Datadog.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Subscriber Enrollment: public enrollment flow with share size selection (0.5kW increments up to garden remaining capacity), Stripe Elements payment method setup, Auth0 account creation, enrollment confirmation. Energy Credit Allocation: monthly billing_cycle_job computes share_fraction = share_size_kw / garden_active_subscribed_kw per subscriber, calculates subscriber_allocated_kwh = share_fraction * total_production_kwh, stores in energy_share_allocation. Billing Statement Generation: bill_credit_usd = allocated_kwh * utility_rate_per_kwh; previous balance carried; Stripe PaymentIntent created for net amount due. Subscriber Savings Dashboard: current month allocated_kwh, bill_credit_usd, year-to-date savings sum, production chart (monthly bars). Operator Admin Panel: garden production data management, subscriber enrollment list with payment status, billing cycle trigger, utility off-take reconciliation report. Utility Production Ingestion: S3 CSV upload triggers SQS → BullMQ job validating and inserting production_reading.
target_output.system_components: Next.js Frontend (Vercel): subscriber dashboard, enrollment wizard with Stripe Elements iframe, operator admin panel. Fastify API (ECS Fargate, auto-scaling 2-6 tasks): enrollment, billing, subscriber, admin, and webhook plugins. BullMQ Worker (ECS Fargate, 2-4 tasks): billing_cycle_queue, production_ingestion_queue, statement_generation_queue. Billing Coordinator: billing_cycle_job fetches production_reading, computes garden_active_subscribed_kw, fans out 50-subscriber statement_generation batches to statement_generation_queue. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ: core data store; billing_statement and payment_transaction partitioned by billing_period. Redis 7 ElastiCache: BullMQ broker (DB 0,1,2) and Auth0 session cache (DB 3). S3 + SQS: utility CSV upload trigger path. Stripe: PaymentIntents API for billing charges; webhook events (payment_intent.succeeded, payment_intent.payment_failed) to Fastify webhook endpoint.
target_output.workflows: Monthly Billing Cycle: Operator clicks 'Run Billing Cycle' in admin panel for March 2026. Fastify POST /api/v1/billing/cycle enqueues billing_cycle_job to BullMQ. billing_cycle_job: queries production_reading for garden_id + billing_period='2026-03'; aborts if no production_reading found. Queries active subscribers: SELECT id, share_size_kw FROM subscriber WHERE garden_id = ? AND status = 'active'. Computes garden_active_subscribed_kw = SUM(share_size_kw). For each subscriber: share_fraction = share_size_kw / garden_active_subscribed_kw; subscriber_allocated_kwh = share_fraction * production_reading.total_kwh; bill_credit_usd = allocated_kwh * garden.utility_rate_per_kwh. Fans out 50-subscriber batches to statement_generation_queue (10 batches for 500 subscribers). Each statement_generation_job: INSERT INTO energy_share_allocation; INSERT INTO billing_statement; CREATE Stripe PaymentIntent for amount due. 10 parallel batches complete in ~15 minutes total. Coordinator job updates billing_cycle log. Utility Production Ingestion: Utility uploads CSV to s3://community-solar-prod/utility-data/garden-a/2026-03.csv. S3 ObjectCreated fires SQS message. BullMQ production_ingestion_job: downloads CSV from S3, validates columns (garden_id, billing_period, total_kwh, net_metering_credit_usd, report_date), validates total_kwh > 0. Inserts production_reading (garden_id, billing_period='2026-03', total_kwh=485230, net_metering_credit_usd=32150.00, source='utility_csv'). Publishes billing_period_data_ready event; operator notified in admin panel. Subscriber Dashboard Load: Subscriber logs in via Auth0. Next.js App Router fetches GET /api/v1/subscribers/:id/dashboard for current period. Fastify queries energy_share_allocation and billing_statement for subscriber; returns allocated_kwh, bill_credit_usd, year_to_date_savings. Recharts renders monthly savings bars.
target_output.data_model: solar_garden (id UUID PK, name TEXT, state CHAR(2), utility_id TEXT, total_capacity_kw NUMERIC, utility_rate_per_kwh NUMERIC, active_since DATE). subscriber (id UUID PK, garden_id UUID FK, auth0_sub TEXT UNIQUE, name TEXT, email TEXT NOT NULL, address TEXT, share_size_kw NUMERIC NOT NULL CHECK(share_size_kw > 0), enrollment_date DATE, status ENUM('active','suspended','cancelled'), stripe_customer_id TEXT). energy_share_allocation (id UUID PK, subscriber_id UUID FK, billing_period DATE NOT NULL, allocated_kwh NUMERIC(10,3) NOT NULL, share_fraction NUMERIC(8,6) NOT NULL, bill_credit_usd NUMERIC(10,2), production_reading_id UUID FK, computed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). production_reading (id UUID PK, garden_id UUID FK, billing_period DATE NOT NULL, total_kwh NUMERIC NOT NULL, net_metering_credit_usd NUMERIC, source ENUM('utility_csv','edi'), ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE(garden_id, billing_period)). billing_statement (id UUID PK, subscriber_id UUID FK, billing_period DATE NOT NULL, allocated_kwh NUMERIC(10,3), bill_credit_usd NUMERIC(10,2), previous_balance_usd NUMERIC(10,2) DEFAULT 0, amount_due_usd NUMERIC(10,2), payment_status ENUM('pending','paid','failed','void'), stripe_payment_intent_id TEXT, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ) PARTITION BY RANGE (billing_period). payment_transaction (id UUID PK, billing_statement_id UUID FK, stripe_charge_id TEXT, amount_usd NUMERIC(10,2), status TEXT, processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). utility_off_take (id UUID PK, garden_id UUID FK, billing_period DATE, total_production_kwh NUMERIC, net_metering_credit_usd NUMERIC, reconciled BOOLEAN DEFAULT false).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/enrollments — subscriber signup {garden_id, share_size_kw, stripe_payment_method_id}; Auth0 account created, subscriber record inserted. GET /api/v1/subscribers/:id/dashboard — returns {billing_period, allocated_kwh, bill_credit_usd, ytd_savings_usd, monthly_production_chart}. GET /api/v1/subscribers/:id/statements — paginated billing_statement history. POST /api/v1/billing/cycle — operator role; body {garden_id, billing_period}; enqueues billing_cycle_job. GET /api/v1/admin/gardens/:id/production — operator role; lists production_reading records with ingestion status. POST /api/v1/webhooks/stripe — Stripe signature verified; handles payment_intent.succeeded and payment_intent.payment_failed. POST /api/v1/webhooks/utility-upload — S3→SQS trigger processed by BullMQ production_ingestion_job. Auth: Auth0 JWT in Authorization: Bearer header; rate limiting per role.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PCI-DSS SAQ A: Stripe Elements renders card input in a Stripe-hosted iframe; no raw card numbers reach Fastify or PostgreSQL. stripe.webhooks.constructEvent validates Stripe webhook signatures. Auth0 MFA policy enforced for operator and admin application clients. RBAC: subscribers access only their own data (Fastify preHandler asserts jwt.sub = subscriber.auth0_sub); operators access their garden only (jwt.garden_id = garden.id); admin cross-garden. CCPA: DELETE /api/v1/subscribers/:id endpoint anonymizes PII fields (name → 'Deleted User', email → deleted_{uuid}@void.invalid, address → null) while retaining billing_statement records with subscriber_id for 7-year tax retention. PostgreSQL row-level security mirrors RBAC. HTTPS enforced at ALB; HTTP redirected. TLS 1.3 on all database connections.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate in us-east-1 VPC: api-service (public ALB, auto-scale 2-6 tasks on CPU 60%), bullmq-worker-service (internal, 2-4 tasks auto-scale on BullMQ queue depth). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ db.r7g.large. ElastiCache Redis r7g.medium cluster mode. S3 utility-data bucket with per-garden IAM prefix policies. GitHub Actions CI: eslint, tsc --noEmit, vitest (billing allocation unit tests, statement generation tests), docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Staging mirrors production at 50% scale with seeded test garden and 20 test subscribers for integration test billing cycle run.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on all ECS tasks. Custom metrics: billing_cycle_duration_seconds (target < 1800), statement_generation_queue_depth, payment_failure_rate_percent, production_ingestion_lag_hours (time from upload to production_reading insert), subscriber_allocation_total_kwh per garden per period. PagerDuty: billing_cycle_duration > 1800s (SLA breach), payment_failure_rate > 5% in 1h window, BullMQ DLQ size > 0 (any failed job), production_ingestion_lag > 48h. Datadog dashboard: monthly billing cycle status per garden, payment status distribution, subscriber enrollment velocity.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $850/month for 5 gardens, 500 subscribers. RDS Multi-AZ db.r7g.large $350/month, ElastiCache r7g.medium $70/month, ECS Fargate api + worker ~$180/month, S3/SQS negligible. Stripe fees ~$250/month at $50 average monthly bill per subscriber. Scaling to 50 gardens / 5000 subscribers: horizontal ECS scaling, RDS read replica for subscriber dashboard queries, ClickHouse for analytics reporting layer.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - MVP (Weeks 1-5): PostgreSQL schema, Fastify enrollment and billing APIs, BullMQ billing_cycle_job with proportional allocation formula (ISS-001 fix), utility CSV S3 ingestion with SQS trigger (ISS-002 fix), Stripe Elements enrollment flow, Auth0 RBAC, subscriber savings dashboard (current month). Phase 2 (Weeks 6-9): Multi-billing-period statement history, operator admin panel (production management, subscriber list, billing trigger), utility off-take reconciliation report, CCPA data deletion endpoint, Datadog observability setup. Phase 3 (Weeks 10-12): Staging integration test suite with seeded test garden, load test billing cycle for 500 subscribers, PagerDuty alert tuning, pilot solar garden onboarding.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode. Zod schemas defined alongside Fastify route handlers for request/response validation. BullMQ job definitions export typed JobData interfaces. Billing allocation formula unit-tested with known production/subscriber fixtures: 3 active subscribers at 2kW, 3kW, 5kW shares summing to 10kW, garden produces 10,000 kWh — expected allocations 2000, 3000, 5000 kWh. PostgreSQL migrations via node-pg-migrate. No business logic in database stored procedures — all allocation logic in BullMQ job TypeScript for testability. Auth0 tenant configuration versioned in Terraform.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Subscriber share_size_kw denominator changes mid-billing-period (new enrollments or cancellations after production data arrives). Mitigation: billing_cycle_job snapshots garden_active_subscribed_kw at job start; no enrollment changes accepted for a billing_period after the production_reading is inserted. Risk: Stripe PaymentIntent creation failure for a subset of subscribers during billing cycle leaves statements in pending state. Mitigation: statement_generation_job marks statement payment_status=failed and logs to BullMQ DLQ; operator reviews and retriggers payment via admin panel. Tradeoff: CSV S3 upload over EDI feed for utility integration accepts manual utility upload workflow in exchange for simpler integration complexity and lower development cost; EDI support deferred to future scope. Tradeoff: Vercel for Next.js over Fargate deployment accepts Vercel as a deployment dependency for the subscriber frontend in exchange for zero-config CDN and preview deployments.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: CSV S3 upload selected over EDI for utility integration: EDI requires SFTP gateway and EDI parsing library complexity; CSV with S3 is achievable in MVP timeline and utilities can export CSV from their billing systems. Proportional allocation formula defined as share_fraction = share_size_kw / SUM(active subscriber share_size_kw) — pro-rata by kW capacity rather than nominal share count, correctly reflecting actual production entitlement. Auth0 selected over self-managed Keycloak for MFA enforcement at low operational burden for a startup.

project_goal.value: Landing page for an AI-powered wildfire risk prediction SaaS platform targeting insurance carriers and CAL FIRE-style agencies, showcasing model accuracy benchmarks, a live risk map preview, and a demo request flow
project_goal.rationale: Primary conversion goal is qualified demo requests from insurance carriers and public safety agencies

target_users.value: Catastrophe risk analysts at insurance carriers, underwriters at reinsurers, and risk officers at state wildfire management agencies
target_users.rationale: Two primary buyer segments identified in sales strategy

project_class.rationale: Single-page marketing site with demo request lead capture; no backend application logic

capabilities.rationale: Interactive risk map widget and Vercel Analytics for conversion tracking

complexity_level.rationale: Static site with embedded map widget; no server-side processing

risk_level.rationale: No user accounts, no payment data, no regulated personal information

data_sensitivity.rationale: Demo request form collects business contact info only; no health, financial, or sensitive data

external_exposure.rationale: Public marketing page; accessible to anyone

access_model.value: No authentication; publicly accessible to all visitors
access_model.rationale: Marketing landing page with no gated content

feature_scope.rationale: Full launch page content for two buyer segments

mvp_scope.value: Hero with risk map preview, model benchmarks, demo request form, Vercel Analytics
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum to begin demo pipeline generation

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Vercel, Content Security Policy headers, no third-party tracking pixels
security_baseline.rationale: Privacy-respecting marketing page baseline

frontend_stack.rationale: Static export with excellent image optimization for map tile assets; Vercel native deployment

backend_stack.value: None (Vercel serverless Edge Function for demo request form handler)
backend_stack.rationale: No backend runtime needed beyond form proxy

hosting_target.rationale: Zero-config CDN deployment; Vercel Analytics included

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data stored on site; Salesforce CRM governed by separate DPA; Vercel Analytics is cookieless
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No retention obligations on the site

project.goal: Generate qualified demo requests from insurance carriers and wildfire management agencies
project.target_users: Catastrophe risk analysts and agency risk officers

frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with wildfire brand palette (ember orange, charcoal, off-white)
frontend.map: Static pre-rendered Mapbox tile mosaic image (not interactive Mapbox GL JS) for LCP compliance

security.analytics: Vercel Analytics (no cookies, no consent banner)

devops.hosting: Vercel (automatic deploy on main, preview on PR)

confirmed_decisions.map_approach: Static tile mosaic (pre-rendered image, not Mapbox GL JS)

reasoner_reviews.product: The live risk map preview is the primary emotional hook for insurance analysts — seeing California ignition probability heat zones immediately communicates the product's value. Model accuracy benchmarks must be specific (actual precision/recall numbers vs named competitors) to be credible with technical buyers. The dual use-case section for insurance vs agency buyers is critical for messaging alignment.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The static pre-rendered Mapbox tile mosaic is the correct decision for the LCP target — Mapbox GL JS bundles are 400KB+ and would destroy the 2.0s LCP requirement. A high-quality WebP image of the risk map at 1200px wide, with Next.js Image priority=true, will load in under 500ms on 4G. The demo request form should use a Vercel Edge Function to proxy to Salesforce Lead API rather than a third-party form service to maintain CSP compliance.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP must allowlist Vercel Analytics script source only — no Mapbox GL JS CDN, no Salesforce chat widget, no LinkedIn Insight tag. The Salesforce API key must be a server-side Vercel environment variable. Demo request form should have a honeypot field and IP rate limiting via Vercel KV.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: LCP < 2.0s requires the hero risk map image to be the priority image, loaded with fetchpriority='high' and served from Vercel CDN with a preload link hint. All Framer Motion animations must be off-screen only.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 static export; single page with section anchors: /#hero, /#benchmarks, /#use-cases, /#testimonials, /#pricing, /#demo
frontend.pages_and_flows: Hero: full-width risk map mosaic image (WebP, pre-rendered from Mapbox API at build time), headline 'Predict wildfire ignition before it starts', subhead for insurance and agency audiences, dual CTA buttons ('Request Demo' scrolls to form, 'View Benchmark Report' downloads PDF). Benchmarks: precision/recall comparison table with named competitors (if approved by legal). Use Cases: two-column insurance vs agency section. Testimonials: quote cards from 2 reference customers. Pricing: three-tier table (Starter/Pro/Enterprise). Demo Request Form: company, name, title, email, geographic footprint (state multi-select), buyer type (insurance/agency), message.
frontend.performance_notes: Risk map image: next/image with priority=true, sizes='100vw', WebP, 1200x630 exported from Mapbox Static Images API at build time. Fetch priority high. LCP target < 2.0s. Framer Motion only on below-the-fold sections. Target Lighthouse Performance > 95.

security.abuse_prevention: Demo request form: honeypot field, Vercel KV rate limit 5 submissions per IP per 30 minutes
security.secrets_management: SALESFORCE_ACCESS_TOKEN in Vercel environment variables (no NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix); refreshed via OAuth2 connected app client credentials flow in Edge Function

data.entities: demo_request (forwarded to Salesforce Lead: company STRING, contact_name STRING, title STRING, email STRING, geographic_footprint STRING, buyer_type ENUM, submitted_at, utm_source, utm_campaign), vercel_analytics_event (page_view, risk_map_viewed, demo_request_submit — no PII, cookieless)
data.storage_design: No database. Salesforce Lead object is sole store. Vercel Analytics aggregates events without individual tracking.

previous_plan.title: Wildfire Risk Prediction SaaS Landing Page — Rev 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Next.js 14 static landing page for a wildfire risk AI SaaS, featuring a pre-rendered Mapbox Static Images risk map, model accuracy benchmarks, and a Vercel Edge Function demo request proxy to Salesforce CRM.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 static export on Vercel. Mapbox Static Images API called at build time to generate the risk map WebP. Vercel Edge Function /api/demo-request forwards form submissions to Salesforce Leads API using a stored access token (refresh mechanism undefined).
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: CSP headers in next.config.js. SALESFORCE_ACCESS_TOKEN in Vercel environment variable (no NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix). Honeypot field. Vercel KV rate limiting.
previous_plan.workflows: Visitor views risk map (pre-rendered WebP loaded with next/image priority). Reads benchmarks. Fills demo request form. Form POSTs to /api/demo-request Edge Function. Edge Function calls Salesforce Leads API with stored access token (expiry unaddressed in round 2).

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision for a wildfire risk AI SaaS landing page. Only ISS-002 remains: the Salesforce OAuth2 token refresh mechanism. I need to replace the static access token pattern with an OAuth2 client credentials flow invoked per Edge Function call, storing SALESFORCE_CLIENT_ID and SALESFORCE_CLIENT_SECRET as env vars and calling the Salesforce token endpoint on each demo request submission to get a fresh token. Plan quality is strong per manifest so all sections need to be rich and specific.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced static SALESFORCE_ACCESS_TOKEN env var with OAuth2 client credentials flow. The /api/demo-request Edge Function now calls POST https://{sf_instance}/services/oauth2/token with grant_type=client_credentials, SALESFORCE_CLIENT_ID, and SALESFORCE_CLIENT_SECRET (both server-side Vercel env vars). The token response returns a fresh access_token valid for 2 hours. The Edge Function immediately uses this token to call POST /services/data/v59.0/sobjects/Lead/ and then discards it — no token is stored between invocations. This eliminates the token expiry problem: every demo request gets a fresh token. Described in security_and_compliance, workflows, and api_design sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Salesforce integration never fails due to token expiry; client credentials flow is the correct OAuth2 pattern for server-to-server integrations without user context; sensitive credentials are server-side only.

target_output.title: Wildfire Risk Prediction SaaS Landing Page — Rev 3
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 static landing page deployed on Vercel for an AI-powered wildfire risk prediction SaaS, targeting catastrophe risk analysts at insurance carriers and wildfire management agencies. The page features a pre-rendered California ignition probability risk map (Mapbox Static Images API, built at deploy time), model accuracy benchmarks against named competitors, dual buyer-segment use case sections, and a demo request form that proxies submissions to Salesforce Leads API via OAuth2 client credentials in a Vercel Edge Function. This revision resolves the Salesforce token refresh mechanism.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 static export on Vercel Edge Network. All page content is statically generated at build time, including the risk map WebP image fetched from Mapbox Static Images API during the Next.js build. A Vercel Edge Function at /api/demo-request handles form submissions using a stateless OAuth2 client credentials flow to obtain a fresh Salesforce access token per invocation. Vercel Analytics tracks demo_request_submit and risk_map_viewed goals without cookies. GitHub Actions enforces a Lighthouse CI gate (Performance > 95, LCP < 2000ms) on every PR before Vercel production deployment.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 (static export), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS (ember-600 brand orange, charcoal-900, off-white). Risk map: Mapbox Static Images API called via fetch() in next.config.js generateStaticParams or a build script, exported as WebP. Form proxy: Vercel Edge Function (Node 18 edge runtime). Analytics: Vercel Analytics (cookieless). CI: GitHub Actions, Lighthouse CI action. Hosting: Vercel Edge Network with automatic PR previews.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: full-width pre-rendered California wildfire ignition probability heat map (WebP 1400x700, ember-to-charcoal color gradient), headline 'Predict wildfire ignition. Before it spreads.', dual CTAs (insurance: 'Protect Your Portfolio' and agency: 'Defend Your District' — both scroll to demo form). Model Benchmarks Section: precision and recall comparison table for Q4 2025 validation dataset: platform (Precision 0.91, Recall 0.87), Competitor A (0.83, 0.79), Competitor B (0.78, 0.74), FARSITE baseline (0.71, 0.68) — data from published model validation report. Use Cases: two-column section; Insurance column: 'Wildfire ignition probability at 250m resolution for portfolio-level cat modeling'; Agency column: '72-hour dispatch pre-positioning based on ignition risk forecasts'. Testimonials: two quote cards (Carrier cat model director and state agency GIS lead). Pricing Plans: Starter (county-level resolution, $5K/yr), Pro (250m resolution, API access, $25K/yr), Enterprise (custom). Demo Request Form: company, name, title, work email, buyer type (Insurance Carrier / Reinsurer / State Agency / Federal Agency), geographic portfolio (state multi-select for US states), message. Use Case Tagging on submit for Salesforce lead routing.
target_output.system_components: Next.js Static Build: all HTML/CSS/JS generated at build time; risk map WebP fetched from Mapbox Static Images API during build (fetch in a /scripts/generate-map.ts script run by npm run prebuild). next/image serves WebP with priority=true, fetchpriority=high, preload link hint in <head>. /api/demo-request Edge Function: validates form fields, checks Vercel KV rate limit, executes Salesforce OAuth2 client credentials flow, creates Salesforce Lead record, returns success. Vercel Edge Network: global CDN; automatic HTTPS; image optimization CDN for WebP srcsets. Vercel Analytics: server-side injected event tracking without client JS dependencies.
target_output.workflows: Visitor Journey: Catastrophe risk analyst at a large P&C carrier clicks a conference email sponsorship link (UTM: utm_source=rms_conference utm_campaign=wildfire-risk-2026). Hero loads with ignition probability heat map visible above fold within 1.5s (Vercel CDN hit). Analyst immediately sees California county-level ember gradient — recognizes the value proposition without scrolling. Reads benchmarks table: platform Precision 0.91 vs FARSITE 0.71 — compelling delta for a cat modeler. Clicks 'Protect Your Portfolio'. Scrolls to demo form. Selects buyer_type='Insurance Carrier', selects California and Oregon in geographic portfolio multi-select. Fills work email. Submits. Demo Request Submission: Browser POSTs to /api/demo-request Edge Function. Edge Function validates required fields (company, name, email, buyer_type). Checks Vercel KV: rate_limit:{ip} counter — if >= 5 in last 30 minutes, returns 429. Calls POST https://company.my.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token with {grant_type: 'client_credentials', client_id: SALESFORCE_CLIENT_ID, client_secret: SALESFORCE_CLIENT_SECRET}. Salesforce returns fresh access_token. Immediately calls POST https://company.my.salesforce.com/services/data/v59.0/sobjects/Lead/ with {FirstName, LastName, Company, Title, Email, Lead_Buyer_Type__c: 'Insurance Carrier', Lead_Geographic_Scope__c: 'CA, OR', LeadSource: 'Website', UTM_Campaign__c: 'wildfire-risk-2026'}. On Salesforce 201: returns {success: true} to browser. Token discarded. Vercel Analytics records demo_request_submit goal. Mapbox Build Script: at next.js build time, scripts/generate-map.ts calls GET https://api.mapbox.com/styles/v1/mapbox/dark-v11/static/[-125,32,-114,42]/1400x700@2x?access_token={MAPBOX_TOKEN} with a custom style layer showing wildfire ignition probability tiles. Saves response as /public/images/ca-ignition-risk-map.webp.
target_output.data_model: No database on site. Data entities exist only in downstream Salesforce: Lead object (FirstName, LastName, Company, Email, Lead_Buyer_Type__c custom field, Lead_Geographic_Scope__c custom field, UTM_Source__c, UTM_Campaign__c, LeadSource='Website', CreatedDate). Vercel Analytics aggregated events: page_view, risk_map_viewed (fires when risk map section enters viewport via IntersectionObserver), demo_request_form_start (first field focus), demo_request_submit (successful Edge Function response). Vercel KV: rate_limit:{ip_address} counter, TTL 1800s.
target_output.api_design: POST /api/demo-request (Vercel Edge Function): body {company: string, first_name: string, last_name: string, title: string, email: string, buyer_type: 'insurance_carrier'|'reinsurer'|'state_agency'|'federal_agency', geographic_states: string[], message?: string}. Validates required fields. Checks Vercel KV rate limit (5/IP/30min). Executes Salesforce OAuth2 client credentials: POST /services/oauth2/token → {access_token}. Creates Salesforce Lead: POST /services/data/v59.0/sobjects/Lead/ with access_token in Authorization header. Returns {success: true} or {error: 'validation'|'rate_limit'|'salesforce_error'}. Mapbox build script: GET https://api.mapbox.com/styles/v1/mapbox/dark-v11/static/{bbox}/{size}@2x with MAPBOX_PUBLIC_TOKEN (public token — only Mapbox Static Images scope, no write access).
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Vercel. Headers in next.config.js: Content-Security-Policy (script-src 'self'; no third-party ad scripts or LinkedIn insight tag; Vercel Analytics is server-side injected); HSTS max-age=63072000 includeSubDomains; X-Frame-Options DENY; Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin. Salesforce integration: SALESFORCE_CLIENT_ID and SALESFORCE_CLIENT_SECRET stored in Vercel environment variables (no NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix). Client credentials OAuth2 flow per invocation — no stored access token that can expire or leak. MAPBOX_PUBLIC_TOKEN is a Mapbox public token scoped to Static Images API read-only; safe to use in build script output. Demo form honeypot field (hidden input must be empty). Vercel KV rate limiting (5 submissions per IP per 30 minutes). Vercel Analytics cookieless — no consent banner required.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Vercel project connected to GitHub main branch. Production deploy on merge to main. PR preview deployments auto-generated with URL in PR comment for stakeholder and legal review of content. Environment variables in Vercel project settings: SALESFORCE_CLIENT_ID, SALESFORCE_CLIENT_SECRET, MAPBOX_PUBLIC_TOKEN (all in production and preview scopes). GitHub Actions CI on each PR: next lint, tsc --noEmit, next build (verifies static export including Mapbox build script), Lighthouse CI action targeting PR preview URL with thresholds LCP < 2000, Performance > 95, Accessibility > 90. Merge blocked on Lighthouse failure. Vercel deployment webhook to Slack on production deploy success.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics dashboard: daily page views, unique visitors, UTM campaign attribution (conference, email, organic), Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS) per deployment version. Custom goals: risk_map_viewed (IntersectionObserver fires when hero map section enters viewport), demo_request_form_start, demo_request_submit. Funnel view: risk_map_viewed → demo_request_form_start → demo_request_submit. Conversion rate target > 3% of risk_map_viewed to demo_request_submit. Vercel build deployment alerts on failure via email and Slack webhook. Salesforce lead pipeline provides downstream funnel visibility.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro plan $20/month for team collaboration and higher build concurrency. Vercel Analytics included in Pro. Vercel KV free tier sufficient for rate limiting at expected demo request volumes (< 500/month). Mapbox Static Images API: free tier covers 2500 requests/month — build script calls it once per deployment; cost negligible. Salesforce CRM: existing subscription. MAPBOX_PUBLIC_TOKEN for Static Images API: $0 at build frequency. Total: $20/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - MVP (Week 1): Next.js project setup, Tailwind brand configuration, Mapbox build script for risk map WebP, hero section with priority image, model benchmarks section, demo request form with Vercel Edge Function and Salesforce OAuth2 client credentials (ISS-002 fix), Vercel Analytics goals, GitHub Actions CI with Lighthouse gate, Vercel deployment. Phase 2 (Week 2): Use case sections (insurance vs agency columns), testimonials, pricing plan table, partner/customer logo strip, full page design polish and legal review of benchmark data attribution, production launch. Phase 3 (Post-launch): A/B test buyer segment CTAs using Vercel Edge Config split testing, interactive county-level risk map drill-down (Mapbox GL JS lazy-loaded below the fold in a React island), ROI calculator for insurance cat model impact.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode. next.config.js security headers tested with securityheaders.com scan in CI. Mapbox build script cached: if /public/images/ca-ignition-risk-map.webp exists and is < 7 days old, skip Mapbox API call to avoid unnecessary API usage during local dev. SALESFORCE_CLIENT_ID and SALESFORCE_CLIENT_SECRET never logged: Edge Function uses a try/catch that logs only 'Salesforce API error: {status}' without credentials in the error message. All Vercel environment variables documented in .env.example with placeholder values and security notes. Lighthouse CI thresholds checked against Vercel preview URL after each build; PR merge blocked on regression.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Mapbox Static Images API call in build script fails if MAPBOX_PUBLIC_TOKEN is revoked, breaking the Next.js build. Mitigation: fallback to a committed placeholder risk map image if Mapbox API call fails; CI alerts on fallback use. Risk: Salesforce OAuth2 client credentials flow adds ~200ms latency to each demo form submission. Mitigation: acceptable for a once-per-session form submission; add a loading spinner during submission to mask latency. Risk: Competitor benchmark data in the precision/recall table may require legal sign-off or may be disputed by competitors. Mitigation: attribute data to 'Internal Q4 2025 Validation Dataset' with a footnote explaining methodology; legal review gate before production launch. Tradeoff: client credentials OAuth2 flow per invocation over cached tokens accepts extra Salesforce API call per submission in exchange for stateless simplicity and elimination of token expiry issues.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Salesforce OAuth2 client credentials flow selected over static access token: eliminates token expiry as a failure mode; simpler than refresh token flow (no refresh token storage needed); appropriate for server-to-server M2M integration without user context. Mapbox Static Images API confirmed as the correct approach for LCP compliance: pre-built WebP loaded as a standard next/image eliminates the 400KB Mapbox GL JS bundle. Vercel Pro plan selected over Hobby: team preview deployments and Slack webhook integration required for marketing team review workflow.

metadata.notes: Round 3 wildfire risk AI landing page; resolves Salesforce OAuth2 client credentials token refresh pattern.

target_users.rationale: Primary users handling emissions reporting workflows

access_model.rationale: CLI tool on trusted engineer machines
