project.goal: Validate GHG emissions data files before submission to CDP/GRI reporting platforms
project.target_users: Sustainability engineers and ESG analysts

backend.framework: Click 8.x for command routing, Pandera for DataFrame schema validation
backend.parsing: openpyxl for XLSX, built-in csv module for CSV

security.auth: None -- local execution only
security.filesystem: Read-only access to input files, write to --output path only

data.db: SQLite 3 embedded for emission factor library and schema version cache

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for lint, test, and PyPI publish

constraints.offline: Must run with no network access

reasoner_reviews.product: Validation against GHG Protocol, GRI 305, and CDP rules addresses a real pain point where sustainability teams discover format errors only after attempting platform submission. The --fix mode for unit conversions adds significant value for teams with inconsistent upstream data sources.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Pandera for DataFrame schema validation is the right choice -- it provides declarative column-level rules that map naturally to GHG Protocol field definitions. SQLite as an embedded rule cache avoids runtime network dependencies and keeps the tool fully offline-capable. Packaging as a standard pip distribution with pyproject.toml ensures easy CI integration.
reasoner_reviews.security: Local-only execution with no credentials or network endpoints makes this tool low-risk. The main concern is ensuring input file paths are validated to prevent path traversal when running in CI environments with untrusted input paths.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Offline-first constraint is achievable by bundling the GHG Protocol 2024 emission factor tables into the SQLite DB at package build time and versioning them. Update workflow needs documentation for when IPCC factors change with new AR releases.

backend.service_design: Single Python package ghg-validator with Click command group: validate (main validation flow), fix (auto-correction mode), update-factors (refresh SQLite cache). Module layout: cli.py (Click entrypoint), validator.py (Pandera schema engine keyed by framework), factor_lookup.py (SQLite emission factor queries), fixer.py (unit conversion and rounding logic), reporter.py (JSON and text serializers).
backend.api_patterns: CLI flags: --framework [ghg_protocol|gri|cdp], --input FILE, --output FILE, --format [json|text], --fix, --verbose. Exit codes: 0 (no errors), 1 (validation errors found), 2 (fatal parse error).
backend.failure_handling: Parse errors caught and reported per row with line number; fatal file-open errors exit with code 2 and stderr message; SQLite cache miss falls back to bundled defaults.
backend.business_modules: Validator module loads framework-specific Pandera schema (scope_1_schema, scope_2_schema, scope_3_schema), validates each row, collects ValidationError objects. Factor lookup queries emission_factor_library by gas_type and IPCC AR version.

security.auth_design: No authentication; tool runs as invoking OS user with no privilege escalation.
security.abuse_prevention: Input path validated against allowed extensions (.csv, .xlsx) and 50MB size limit to prevent processing unexpected file types in CI pipelines.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: --verbose flag enables structured JSON logging to stderr with row counts, rule IDs triggered, and transformation counts applied in --fix mode.

data.entities: emission_factor_library (id, gas_type, gwp_value, ipcc_version, scope_category, unit, updated_at), schema_version (id, framework_name, version_string, active, bundled_at), validation_rule (id, schema_version_id, field_name, rule_type, threshold_min, threshold_max, allowed_values JSON)
data.storage_design: SQLite DB bundled in package data directory at ghg_validator/data/rules.db; read-only at runtime; updated via update-factors command from versioned GitHub release asset.
data.retention_and_deletion: No user data stored; output reports written to filesystem at user-specified path only; SQLite DB contains reference data only.

devops.deployment_topology: Distributed as ghg-validator on PyPI; installed via pip install ghg-validator; no server deployment.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint with ruff, type-check with mypy, test with pytest covering all three framework validators and --fix mode, build wheel with hatch, publish to PyPI on semver tag push.
devops.observability_stack: stderr logging only; machine-readable JSON output for CI integration; no remote telemetry.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing an offline CLI tool for GHG emissions data validation. Key decisions: Click for ergonomic command routing with explicit exit codes for CI integration, Pandera for declarative DataFrame schema validation mapping to GHG Protocol field rules, SQLite bundled in the package for offline emission factor lookups. No network calls during validation satisfies air-gapped environment constraints. Packaging via pyproject.toml with hatch enables clean PyPI distribution.
target_output.title: GHG Emissions Data Validator CLI Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A pip-distributable Python CLI tool that validates corporate GHG emissions inventory files (CSV/XLSX) against GHG Protocol Corporate Standard, GRI 305, and CDP schema rules using Pandera for DataFrame validation and a bundled SQLite emission factor library. Supports --fix mode for auto-correcting unit conversions and decimal precision, outputs structured JSON error reports for CI integration, and runs entirely offline with no network dependencies.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-process Python CLI using Click command groups for validate, fix, and update-factors commands. Pandera schema objects encode GHG Protocol Corporate Standard field rules for Scope 1, 2, and 3 categories, with column-level validators for gas type codes, GWP conversion factors, and activity unit constraints. An embedded SQLite database bundled in the package data directory provides offline emission factor lookups by gas type and IPCC AR version. The reporter module serializes ValidationError collections to JSON or plain-text formats. No network I/O occurs during validation -- the tool is fully air-gap safe.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12, Click 8.x for CLI command routing and option parsing, Pandera 0.19 for DataFrame schema validation, pandas 2.x for CSV/XLSX parsing, openpyxl for XLSX read support, SQLite 3 via Python stdlib sqlite3 module for emission factor DB. Build: hatch with pyproject.toml. Testing: pytest with pytest-click for CLI integration tests. Linting: ruff plus mypy. CI: GitHub Actions for test, build, and PyPI publish.
target_output.functional_feature_map: validate command: reads input CSV/XLSX, accepts --framework flag (ghg_protocol/gri/cdp), loads Pandera schema, validates each row, outputs error report with row index and rule ID. fix command: runs validation then applies auto-corrections for unit mismatches (MWh to kWh, lbs to kg) and decimal rounding per framework precision rules, writes corrected file to --output with transformation log. update-factors command: downloads versioned emission factor table from GitHub release asset and repopulates SQLite cache. --verbose flag: emits JSON log lines to stderr with row count, rules evaluated, and errors per scope category.
target_output.system_components: cli.py (Click command group, argument parsing, exit code logic), validator.py (Pandera schema registry keyed by framework name, validate_dataframe entry point), factor_lookup.py (sqlite3 queries against emission_factor_library, GWP resolution by gas type and AR version), fixer.py (unit conversion rules, decimal normalization, transformation log builder), reporter.py (JSON and plain-text serializers for ValidationError lists), data/rules.db (bundled SQLite with emission_factor_library, schema_version, validation_rule tables).
target_output.workflows: Validation Workflow: Engineer runs 'ghg-validator validate --input q1_scope1.csv --framework ghg_protocol --format json --output report.json'. CLI reads file into pandas DataFrame, loads GHG Protocol Scope 1 Pandera schema, checks gas_type against IPCC AR6 codes, activity_data is numeric and positive, emission_factor within GWP bounds. Errors collected as ValidationError objects with row index, column name, failed rule ID, and observed value. Reporter serializes to JSON with summary counts by scope and severity. Exit code 1 returned if errors found, 0 if clean. Fix Workflow: Engineer runs 'ghg-validator fix --input q1_scope1.csv --framework ghg_protocol --output q1_fixed.csv'. Fixer applies unit conversion (MWh to kWh for electricity entries), rounds CO2e values to 2 decimal places per GHG Protocol precision rules, writes corrected DataFrame to output path, logs each transformation as structured JSON to stderr.
target_output.data_model: emission_factor_library (id INTEGER PK, gas_type TEXT [CO2/CH4/N2O/HFCs], gwp_value REAL, ipcc_version TEXT [AR5/AR6], scope_category TEXT [1/2/3], emission_source TEXT, unit TEXT, updated_at TEXT). schema_version (id INTEGER PK, framework_name TEXT [ghg_protocol/gri/cdp], version_string TEXT, active INTEGER, bundled_at TEXT). validation_rule (id INTEGER PK, schema_version_id INTEGER FK, field_name TEXT, rule_type TEXT [required/enum/range/numeric], threshold_min REAL, threshold_max REAL, allowed_values TEXT JSON). All tables bundled in package at build time from authoritative GHG Protocol 2024 factor tables.
target_output.api_design: CLI surface: 'ghg-validator validate [--input FILE] [--framework TEXT] [--format json|text] [--output FILE] [--verbose]'. 'ghg-validator fix [--input FILE] [--framework TEXT] [--output FILE] [--verbose]'. 'ghg-validator update-factors [--source-url URL]'. 'ghg-validator version'. Exit codes: 0 (no errors), 1 (validation errors found), 2 (fatal parse/IO error). JSON output schema: {summary: {total_rows, error_count, warning_count, framework, scopes_validated}, errors: [{row, column, rule_id, severity, observed_value, message}]}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Input path validated to resolve only .csv or .xlsx extensions under 50MB before processing to prevent inadvertent handling of unexpected file types in CI pipelines. No credentials, tokens, or network calls during validation. Output files written only to user-specified --output path. SQLite DB opened read-only at runtime via URI mode. update-factors command validates downloaded file SHA256 checksum against published manifest before writing. GHG Protocol Corporate Standard, GRI 305, and CDP supply chain categorization rules embedded as versioned rule sets in SQLite schema_version table.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Distributed on PyPI as ghg-validator; installed with 'pip install ghg-validator' or 'uv add ghg-validator'. GitHub Actions pipeline: ruff lint on PR, mypy type check, pytest suite covering validate/fix/update-factors commands against fixture CSV files for each framework, hatch build producing wheel and sdist, PyPI publish via trusted publisher on semver tag push. Version pinning: pyproject.toml specifies minimum Python 3.11 and pinned dependency ranges for pandas, Pandera, and openpyxl. Release notes document emission factor DB version and GHG Protocol ruleset version bundled in each release.
target_output.observability: stderr logging via Python logging module at DEBUG level when --verbose flag is set. Log lines are JSON-formatted with keys: timestamp, command, input_file, framework, rows_processed, errors_found, duration_ms. Machine-readable JSON output to --output file or stdout enables downstream CI tools to parse results. No remote telemetry or crash reporting -- offline-first design excludes analytics SDKs.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost -- distributed as a pip package with negligible PyPI storage. For large files (100K rows), Pandera validation runs in-memory on pandas DataFrame; 100K rows of Scope 1 data validates in under 10 seconds on a modern workstation. SQLite rule cache queries are sub-millisecond. No horizontal scaling considerations apply for a local CLI tool.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (2 weeks): CSV validation for GHG Protocol Corporate Standard Scope 1 and 2 rules, emission factor range checks against IPCC AR6 GWP values, JSON and plain-text error report output, pip-installable package with GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (2 weeks): XLSX ingestion via openpyxl, GRI 305 and CDP supply chain framework rule sets added to SQLite, --fix mode for unit conversions and decimal normalization, update-factors command. Phase 3 (future): Plugin API for custom rule sets, pre-commit hook integration, Scope 3 category-by-category validation routing.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with strict type annotations throughout. ruff for linting and import sorting, mypy for type checking -- both enforced in CI. pytest with minimum 85% coverage including CLI integration tests via pytest-click. Each framework rule set lives in its own Pandera schema module (schemas/ghg_protocol.py, schemas/gri.py, schemas/cdp.py) for independent versioning. Conventional Commits for changelog generation. Semantic versioning tied to bundled GHG Protocol ruleset version.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GHG Protocol emission factor tables change with new IPCC AR releases, making bundled SQLite DB stale. Mitigation: version DB with bundled_at timestamp and emit a warning when factors are older than 18 months. Tradeoff: bundling factors in the package increases wheel size by approximately 2MB but satisfies the offline constraint. Risk: Pandera validation is memory-bound for very large XLSX files over 500K rows. Mitigation: chunked processing via pandas read_csv chunksize in Phase 2. Tradeoff: SQLite over a remote rules API keeps tool air-gap safe but requires manual update-factors run for new rulesets.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose Pandera over Great Expectations for lighter dependency footprint appropriate for a CLI distribution. Chose SQLite over JSON flat files for emission factor storage to enable SQL-based range queries and versioning. Confirmed offline-first constraint rules out any runtime API calls to IPCC or GHG Protocol endpoints.

metadata.notes: GHG emissions data validation CLI with offline SQLite rule cache and Pandera schema validation.

project_goal.value: Microservices-decomposed API powering a biodiversity credit marketplace with separate services for project registry, credit issuance, buyer matching, and audit trail consumed by multiple frontend portals and third-party brokers
project_goal.rationale: Separate services enable independent scaling and auditability of each marketplace domain

target_users.value: Land stewards submitting biodiversity projects, corporate buyers purchasing credits, accredited verifiers, and broker-partner intermediaries
target_users.rationale: All marketplace participant roles identified in discovery

project_class.rationale: Backend API with no first-party frontend; consumed by multiple clients

capabilities.rationale: API-first with OAuth2, partner broker integrations, and public REST endpoints

complexity_level.rationale: Microservices decomposition with cross-service transactions and audit trail requirements

risk_level.rationale: Financial transactions and regulatory audit trail for biodiversity credits

data_sensitivity.rationale: Credit transactions and buyer financial data require financial data handling controls

external_exposure.rationale: Public REST API consumed by third-party brokers and buyer portals

access_model.value: OAuth2 with Auth0, role-based: steward/buyer/verifier/broker/admin
access_model.rationale: Each marketplace participant role has distinct API permissions

feature_scope.value: Project registry CRUD and verification workflow, credit issuance and batch management, buyer matching and transaction settlement, audit trail with tamper-evident logging
feature_scope.rationale: Four core marketplace domains each implemented as independent services

mvp_scope.value: Project registry service, credit issuance service, basic buyer matching, and append-only audit trail
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for pilot launch with early land stewards and buyers

security_baseline.value: OAuth2 with JWT, mTLS between services, encrypted PostgreSQL at rest, TLS 1.3 public endpoints, rate limiting per client ID
security_baseline.rationale: Financial and partner-facing API requires strong inter-service and external security controls

frontend_stack.value: None -- API-only service; frontends are separate consumer applications
frontend_stack.rationale: No first-party frontend in scope

backend_stack.rationale: High-throughput async I/O for API traffic, team expertise

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 per service (schema-per-service isolation), Redis 7 for caching and idempotency keys
data_platform.rationale: Relational data with service-level schema isolation, Redis for sub-ms idempotency checks

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot on GCP
hosting_target.rationale: Managed Kubernetes reduces ops overhead for multi-service deployment

privacy_retention_policy.value: Transaction records retained 7 years per financial regulation; buyer PII retained per GDPR right-to-erasure for EU participants
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Financial transaction retention and GDPR erasure obligations

future_scope.value: Secondary market trading, carbon co-benefit bundling, price discovery API, ISO 14064 verification integration

constraints.value: Each service must be independently deployable; no shared databases across service boundaries; audit trail must be append-only
constraints.rationale: Microservices isolation and audit integrity requirements

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Operations Suite for logs and traces, Prometheus metrics per service, PagerDuty alerts on transaction failure rates
observability_baseline.rationale: GCP-native observability aligned with hosting platform

execution_preference.value: Service-by-service delivery with registry service first, followed by issuance, matching, and audit
execution_preference.rationale: Incremental rollout reduces integration risk

compliance_context.value: Biodiversity Credit Alliance standards for credit issuance, financial transaction audit requirements for buyer settlements, GDPR Article 17 erasure for EU participant data
compliance_context.rationale: Marketplace operates under BCA framework and handles EU buyer financial data

project.goal: Deploy microservices API for biodiversity credit marketplace
project.target_users: Land stewards, corporate buyers, verifiers, broker partners

backend.framework: Fastify with JSON Schema validation, Prisma ORM
backend.inter_service: gRPC for synchronous calls, Pub/Sub for async events

security.auth: Auth0 OAuth2 with JWT, mTLS between services
security.rate_limiting: Per client_id via Redis token bucket

data.cache: Redis 7 for idempotency keys and buyer matching cache
data.events: GCP Pub/Sub for cross-service async events

constraints.isolation: No shared DB across service boundaries
constraints.audit: Append-only audit_trail_entry table, no UPDATE or DELETE

confirmed_decisions.service_mesh: Istio for mTLS and traffic management

reasoner_reviews.product: The four-service decomposition aligns well with marketplace domain boundaries -- registry, issuance, matching, and audit are genuinely distinct bounded contexts. The broker partner integration model needs explicit API versioning to avoid breaking third-party consumers on deployment.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: gRPC for synchronous cross-service calls and Pub/Sub for async events is the right pattern. The main risk is distributed transaction consistency when credit issuance and audit trail writes must succeed atomically across service boundaries -- the outbox pattern on the issuance service should be specified explicitly to handle Pub/Sub delivery failures.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS enforcement via Istio service mesh is correctly specified. The prior round left an open issue around the audit_trail_entry table needing cryptographic hash chaining to prove tamper-evidence -- a naive append-only constraint in PostgreSQL is insufficient if DB admin credentials are compromised.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Service isolation constraint is well-enforced. The GDPR erasure requirement conflicts with the 7-year financial retention for buyer accounts -- this needs a pseudonymization strategy that satisfies both obligations, which was flagged in round 2 and remains unresolved.

backend.service_design: Four Fastify services: registry-service (biodiversity_project CRUD, verification workflow state machine), issuance-service (credit_issuance batch creation with outbox pattern for Pub/Sub), matching-service (buyer order matching algorithm, transaction settlement), audit-service (append-only audit_trail_entry ingestion from all services via Pub/Sub subscription).
backend.api_patterns: RESTful JSON API at /v1. Registry: POST /v1/projects, GET /v1/projects/:id, PATCH /v1/projects/:id/submit-verification. Issuance: POST /v1/credits/issue, GET /v1/credits/:batch_id. Matching: POST /v1/transactions, GET /v1/transactions/:id. Audit: GET /v1/audit?entity_id=&entity_type=.
backend.failure_handling: Outbox pattern on issuance-service ensures credit issuance events are durably written to PostgreSQL before Pub/Sub publish, preventing credit creation without audit trail. Idempotency keys stored in Redis for transaction endpoints with 24-hour TTL.
backend.scaling_notes: Matching service horizontally scales independently for high buyer request periods. Audit service uses Pub/Sub pull subscription with batch acknowledgement to handle burst event ingestion without data loss.

security.auth_design: Auth0 machine-to-machine credentials for broker partners, user JWT for steward/buyer/verifier roles. Istio enforces mTLS for all pod-to-pod communication within GKE cluster.
security.authorization_model: Fastify guard middleware checks JWT claims: stewards can only write to projects they own, buyers to their own transactions, verifiers only to assigned project verification workflow steps, brokers scoped to their registered client_id.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Audit service ingests audit_trail_entry events from all services via Pub/Sub. Each entry includes: entity_type, entity_id, action, actor_id, timestamp, previous_hash (SHA-256 of prior entry for chain integrity). Hash chain enables tamper detection.
security.security_risks: GDPR erasure for buyer PII conflicts with 7-year financial retention. Pseudonymization strategy required to satisfy both obligations.

data.entities: biodiversity_project (id, steward_id, project_type, geography_polygon, status ENUM draft/submitted/verified/rejected, created_at). credit_issuance (id, project_id, batch_serial, vintage_year, units_issued, units_available, issued_at). transaction_record (id, buyer_account_id, credit_batch_id, units_transferred, settlement_price, status ENUM pending/settled/failed, idempotency_key, settled_at). audit_trail_entry (id, entity_type, entity_id, action, actor_id, payload_hash, previous_hash, recorded_at). buyer_account (id, pseudonym_id, organization_name, contact_email_encrypted, gdpr_erasure_requested_at).
data.consistency_notes: Outbox table on issuance-service DB ensures at-least-once delivery to Pub/Sub. Deduplication on audit-service via idempotency_key on audit_trail_entry.
data.migration_notes: Prisma migrations per service; no cross-service schema migrations. Schema changes require service-level API version bump.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot with Istio service mesh. Four namespaces (registry, issuance, matching, audit) each with independent Deployment, HPA, and Service objects. Ingress via GCP Cloud Load Balancer with TLS termination.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build trigger per service on path-based change detection. Each pipeline: lint (ESLint), test (Vitest), container build and push to Artifact Registry, Helm chart deploy to GKE with canary rollout strategy.
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm rollback per service to previous chart version. Prisma migration rollback via down-migration scripts stored alongside up-migrations.
devops.devops_risks: GKE Autopilot node provisioning latency can cause cold-start delays for matching-service during sudden buyer traffic spikes. HPA target set to 60% CPU to leave headroom.

ISS-001.title: Audit trail lacks cryptographic hash chaining
ISS-001.detail: Prior plan described audit_trail_entry as append-only but did not include previous_hash column for chain integrity. A compromised DB admin could insert or modify records without detection.

history.note: SecurityAgent flagged; ArchitectAgent acknowledged but deferred to Phase 2

ISS-002.title: GDPR erasure conflicts with 7-year financial retention
ISS-002.detail: buyer_account table stores PII that must be erased on GDPR request, but transaction_record links to buyer_account and must be retained 7 years. No pseudonymization strategy defined.

history.note: Legal flagged; architecture did not address resolution

ISS-003.title: No API versioning strategy for broker partner consumers
ISS-003.detail: Broker partners consume the public API; breaking changes on deploy will break third-party integrations. No version header or URL versioning policy specified.

history.note: Raised by product reviewer in round 1
URL versioning /v1 enforced via Fastify route prefix

focus_issues.title: Audit trail lacks cryptographic hash chaining
focus_issues.detail: Prior plan described audit_trail_entry as append-only but did not include previous_hash column for chain integrity.

focus_issues.title: GDPR erasure conflicts with 7-year financial retention
focus_issues.detail: No pseudonymization strategy defined for buyer_account PII erasure while retaining transaction_record.

latest_recommendations: Add previous_hash SHA-256 chain to audit_trail_entry and document verification procedure
Define pseudonymization strategy for buyer PII erasure that retains financial records under pseudonym_id reference
Specify URL versioning /v1 enforced via Fastify route prefix -- resolved in round 2
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Biodiversity Credit Marketplace Microservices API Round 2

convergence_state.reason: Two high-severity issues unresolved across two rounds

previous_audits.summary: Architecture is structurally sound with good service decomposition and gRPC/Pub/Sub event patterns. Two high-severity issues remain: audit trail lacks hash chaining and GDPR erasure strategy is undefined.
strengths: Four-service decomposition aligns with marketplace bounded contexts
Outbox pattern for issuance-to-audit reliability is well-specified
Istio mTLS between services is correctly enforced
concerns: Append-only PostgreSQL constraint is insufficient for tamper-evidence without cryptographic chaining
Buyer PII erasure on GDPR request will violate financial retention if not pseudonymized first
blocking_issues: ISS-001: audit_trail_entry must include previous_hash for tamper detection
ISS-002: buyer_account PII erasure strategy conflicts with 7-year transaction retention
recommendations: Add previous_hash CHAR(64) column to audit_trail_entry, computed as SHA-256 of the prior row concatenation
Introduce pseudonym_id UUID on buyer_account; on GDPR erasure, null out PII columns and set gdpr_erased_at; transaction_record retains pseudonym_id reference satisfying financial retention

issue_updates.title: Audit trail lacks cryptographic hash chaining
issue_updates.detail: Data model shows audit_trail_entry without previous_hash; append-only constraint alone is insufficient

issue_updates.title: GDPR erasure conflicts with 7-year financial retention
issue_updates.detail: No pseudonymization approach documented

previous_plan.title: Biodiversity Credit Marketplace Microservices API Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Microservices API with registry, issuance, matching, and audit services on GKE Autopilot. gRPC for synchronous cross-service calls, Pub/Sub for async audit events. Auth0 OAuth2 with Istio mTLS. Append-only audit_trail_entry table.
previous_plan.data_model: biodiversity_project, credit_issuance, transaction_record, audit_trail_entry (id, entity_type, entity_id, action, actor_id, recorded_at -- missing previous_hash), buyer_account (id, contact_email -- no pseudonym_id).
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: OAuth2 JWT, Istio mTLS, TLS 1.3 public endpoints. Audit trail append-only via PostgreSQL role restriction. GDPR erasure and 7-year retention conflict not resolved.
previous_plan.api_design: RESTful /v1 endpoints per service. URL versioning enforced via Fastify route prefix.

best_plan.title: Biodiversity Credit Marketplace Microservices API Round 2
best_plan.executive_summary: Microservices API with registry, issuance, matching, and audit services on GKE Autopilot with gRPC and Pub/Sub.
best_plan.data_model: Four-service schema isolation; audit_trail_entry append-only without hash chain.
best_plan.security_and_compliance: Auth0 OAuth2, Istio mTLS, no pseudonymization strategy.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision addressing two high-severity issues: audit_trail_entry needs cryptographic hash chaining via SHA-256 previous_hash to provide tamper evidence beyond a PostgreSQL append-only constraint, and buyer PII GDPR erasure must pseudonymize the buyer_account record while retaining transaction_record linked via pseudonym_id to satisfy 7-year financial retention. Both fixes require data model changes and corresponding security section updates.

fix_report.action_taken: Added previous_hash CHAR(64) column to audit_trail_entry data model, computed as SHA-256(previous_row.entity_id || previous_row.action || previous_row.actor_id || previous_row.recorded_at). Added hash chain verification procedure to security_and_compliance section. Audit service now computes and publishes hash value with each event ingestion.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Tamper detection enabled: any modification or deletion of audit entries will break the hash chain, detectable by the chain verification job.

fix_report.action_taken: Introduced pseudonym_id UUID column on buyer_account. On GDPR erasure request, contact_email_encrypted and organization_name are nulled, gdpr_erased_at set; transaction_record retains pseudonym_id reference only. Added pseudonymization procedure to security_and_compliance and data_model sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GDPR Article 17 erasure satisfied by removing PII from buyer_account while 7-year financial retention preserved via transaction_record pseudonym_id linkage.

target_output.title: Biodiversity Credit Marketplace Microservices API
target_output.executive_summary: A four-service microservices API on GKE Autopilot powering a biodiversity credit marketplace: registry-service for project submission and third-party verification workflow, issuance-service for credit batch creation with outbox pattern, matching-service for buyer order settlement with idempotency, and audit-service ingesting tamper-evident SHA-256 hash-chained audit trail entries via Pub/Sub. Auth0 OAuth2 with Istio mTLS, GDPR pseudonymization for buyer PII on erasure request, and 7-year financial transaction retention via pseudonym_id linkage.
target_output.architecture_overview: Four independently deployable Fastify (Node.js 22) services in GKE Autopilot namespaces, communicating via gRPC for synchronous calls and GCP Pub/Sub for async audit events. Istio service mesh enforces mTLS for all pod-to-pod traffic. Each service owns its PostgreSQL 16 schema with no cross-service DB joins. Redis 7 provides idempotency key storage for transaction endpoints. The audit-service ingests events from a Pub/Sub subscription and writes SHA-256 hash-chained audit_trail_entry records, enabling tamper detection. Auth0 OAuth2 issues role-scoped JWTs validated at Fastify middleware layer per service.
target_output.technology_stack: Node.js 22 with Fastify 4.x and JSON Schema validation, Prisma ORM for PostgreSQL access, gRPC via grpc-js for inter-service synchronous calls, GCP Pub/Sub client for async event publishing. Databases: PostgreSQL 16 per service on Cloud SQL. Cache: Redis 7 on Memorystore. Service mesh: Istio 1.21 on GKE Autopilot. Auth: Auth0 machine-to-machine and user JWT. CI: Cloud Build per service. Observability: Google Cloud Operations Suite, Prometheus sidecar, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Registry Service: biodiversity project CRUD, steward submission workflow, third-party verifier assignment and approval state machine. Issuance Service: credit batch issuance from verified projects, outbox-based Pub/Sub event publishing, credit availability tracking. Matching Service: buyer purchase order creation, credit batch matching by project type and vintage year, atomic settlement with idempotency. Audit Service: Pub/Sub subscriber ingesting all cross-service events, SHA-256 hash chain computation and storage, audit query API for compliance officers and chain verification endpoint.
target_output.system_components: registry-service (Fastify, Prisma, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL), issuance-service (Fastify, Prisma, outbox table, Pub/Sub publisher), matching-service (Fastify, Prisma, Redis idempotency, Pub/Sub publisher), audit-service (Fastify, Prisma, Pub/Sub pull subscriber, hash chain verifier). Istio ingress gateway for external TLS termination. Cloud Load Balancer for public API routing. Artifact Registry for container images. GCP Secret Manager for DB credentials and Auth0 secrets.
target_output.workflows: Credit Issuance Workflow: Verifier approves project via PATCH /v1/projects/:id/approve on registry-service, which publishes project_verified event to Pub/Sub. Issuance-service subscriber picks up event, creates credit_issuance batch record and writes issuance_outbox row in same PostgreSQL transaction. Background worker polls outbox, publishes credit_issued event to audit Pub/Sub topic, deletes outbox row on acknowledgement. Audit-service subscriber computes entry_hash = SHA-256(entity_type || entity_id || action || actor_id || recorded_at || previous_hash) and inserts new audit_trail_entry with previous_hash. Buyer Transaction Settlement Workflow: Buyer POSTs /v1/transactions with idempotency_key, credit_batch_id, units, buyer_account_id. Matching-service checks Redis for duplicate idempotency_key, validates credit availability, performs atomic PostgreSQL UPDATE on credit_issuance units_available and INSERT on transaction_record in single transaction, publishes transaction_settled event to audit topic. GDPR Erasure Workflow: Auth0 webhook triggers DELETE /v1/buyers/:id/gdpr-erase on matching-service, which nulls contact_email_encrypted and organization_name on buyer_account, sets gdpr_erased_at timestamp, retains pseudonym_id. Transaction records linked via pseudonym_id remain intact for 7-year financial retention.
target_output.data_model: biodiversity_project (id UUID PK, steward_id FK, project_type TEXT, geography_polygon GEOGRAPHY, status ENUM draft/submitted/verified/rejected, verifier_id, created_at). credit_issuance (id UUID PK, project_id FK, batch_serial TEXT, vintage_year INT, units_issued INT, units_available INT, issued_at TIMESTAMPTZ). transaction_record (id UUID PK, pseudonym_id UUID FK buyer_account, credit_batch_id FK, units_transferred INT, settlement_price NUMERIC(12,4), status ENUM pending/settled/failed, idempotency_key CHAR(64) UNIQUE, settled_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_trail_entry (id UUID PK, entity_type TEXT, entity_id UUID, action TEXT, actor_id UUID, payload_json JSONB, previous_hash CHAR(64), entry_hash CHAR(64), recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- append-only, no UPDATE/DELETE grants to service role. buyer_account (id UUID PK, pseudonym_id UUID UNIQUE NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), organization_name TEXT NULLABLE, contact_email_encrypted TEXT NULLABLE, gdpr_erased_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: All services under /v1 URL prefix. Registry-service: POST /v1/projects, GET /v1/projects/:id, PATCH /v1/projects/:id/submit-verification, PATCH /v1/projects/:id/approve (verifier role). Issuance-service: POST /v1/credits/issue (admin role, requires verified project_id), GET /v1/credits/:batch_id. Matching-service: POST /v1/transactions (buyer role, idempotency_key required header), GET /v1/transactions/:id, DELETE /v1/buyers/:id/gdpr-erase (admin role). Audit-service: GET /v1/audit?entity_id=&entity_type=&from=&to= (compliance officer role), GET /v1/audit/verify-chain?from=&to= (returns chain integrity report). Auth: Bearer JWT validated per service. Rate limit: 200 req/min per client_id via Redis token bucket.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth0 OAuth2 with role-scoped JWTs (steward/buyer/verifier/broker/admin). Istio mTLS enforced cluster-wide. TLS 1.3 on public Load Balancer ingress. PostgreSQL at rest encrypted via Cloud SQL CMEK. Redis Memorystore with in-transit encryption. Audit trail tamper detection: audit-service computes entry_hash = SHA-256(entity_type || entity_id || action || actor_id || recorded_at || previous_hash) on each entry; GET /v1/audit/verify-chain re-computes and compares hashes for a date range, returning any broken chain positions. GDPR pseudonymization: buyer_account PII nulled on erasure, pseudonym_id retained for transaction linkage satisfying 7-year financial retention. BCA compliance: credit issuance gated on verified project status. Auth0 machine-to-machine for broker clients with client_id rate limiting.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot with four namespaces (registry, issuance, matching, audit). Istio 1.21 installed via Fleet. Each service: Deployment with 2-4 replicas, HPA on CPU 60% target, PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable 1. Cloud Build triggers per service on path-scoped changes to monorepo. Pipeline: ESLint, Vitest unit tests, container build to Artifact Registry, Helm chart deploy with canary strategy (10% traffic split for 10 minutes before full rollout). Prisma migrations run as Kubernetes Job pre-deployment hook. Secrets in GCP Secret Manager mounted via Kubernetes External Secrets Operator. Rollback: Helm rollback to previous revision.
target_output.observability: Google Cloud Operations Suite: structured JSON logs from each Fastify service with trace_id correlation. Cloud Trace for distributed request tracing across gRPC service calls. Prometheus sidecar per pod. Custom metrics: transaction settlement rate, audit event lag (time from Pub/Sub publish to audit_trail_entry insert), credit issuance throughput, hash chain verification pass/fail rate. PagerDuty alerts: transaction failure rate >2%, audit event lag >30s, hash chain verification failure.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot billing per pod CPU/memory request. Estimated baseline 4 services at 2 replicas each: approximately $280/month. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL four instances: approximately $400/month. Redis Memorystore: $80/month. Pub/Sub: negligible for marketplace event volumes. Cost controls: Autopilot scales to zero for dev/staging namespaces overnight. Cloud SQL read replicas added per service when query latency exceeds 200ms p99.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (6 weeks): registry-service with project CRUD and verification state machine, issuance-service with credit batch creation and outbox pattern, audit-service with hash-chained entry ingestion, Auth0 OAuth2 and Istio mTLS baseline, Cloud Build CI per service. Phase 2 (4 weeks): matching-service with buyer transaction settlement and idempotency, Redis rate limiting, GDPR erasure workflow on buyer_account pseudonymization, audit chain verification API. Phase 3 (4 weeks): broker partner machine-to-machine credentials, secondary market endpoints, performance load testing for matching service at 1000 concurrent transaction requests.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode across all four services. ESLint with import/no-cycle rule to enforce no cross-service module imports. Vitest with minimum 80% coverage including gRPC handler tests and Pub/Sub subscriber unit tests. Each service has its own package.json and Prisma schema -- no shared ORM models across service boundaries. API contract changes require Fastify JSON Schema version bump and consumer notification. Conventional Commits with independent semantic versioning per service.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Pub/Sub delivery failure between issuance-service and audit-service breaks audit trail completeness. Mitigation: outbox pattern ensures at-least-once delivery; audit-service idempotency_key deduplication prevents duplicate entries. Tradeoff: hash chain on audit_trail_entry adds approximately 5ms per write but is required for tamper evidence. Risk: GKE Autopilot node provisioning cold-start affects matching-service latency during sudden buyer traffic bursts. Mitigation: maintain minimum 2 replicas via HPA minReplicas. Risk: Prisma migration failures mid-deployment corrupt service schema. Mitigation: pre-deployment Kubernetes Job with migration dry-run and rollback on failure.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected gRPC over REST for inter-service synchronous calls for lower overhead and strong typing. Confirmed GCP Pub/Sub over Kafka to reduce operational burden on GKE Autopilot. Chose schema-per-service PostgreSQL isolation over separate Cloud SQL instances for cost efficiency at current scale. Hash chain algorithm fixed as SHA-256 for audit trail tamper detection.

metadata.notes: Biodiversity credit marketplace microservices API; round 3 resolves audit hash chain and GDPR pseudonymization issues.

profile.domain: Wind Farm Yaw Alignment Optimization Automation

project_goal.value: Automation tool for large-scale wind farm operators to optimize turbine yaw alignment by processing high-frequency SCADA performance logs, detecting wake interference patterns, and generating actionable yaw offset recommendations
project_goal.rationale: Operational efficiency improvement for wind farm energy yield

target_users.value: Wind farm operations teams and SCADA engineers reviewing turbine performance
target_users.rationale: Primary operators of the yaw optimization workflow

project_class.rationale: Batch processing pipeline with no interactive frontend

capabilities.rationale: SCADA log ingestion, wake model computation, and SCADA write-back integration

complexity_level.rationale: Dask parallel processing of 10Hz SCADA time-series with physics-based wake models

risk_level.rationale: Internal tool with confidential turbine performance data but no public exposure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Turbine SCADA performance data is commercially sensitive operational IP

project.goal: Automate yaw offset optimization for 200-turbine wind farm using SCADA data
project.target_users: Wind farm operations teams and SCADA engineers
project.timeline: Phase 1 nightly batch in Q3, SCADA write-back in Q4

backend.framework: Dask 2024.x for distributed DataFrame operations, floris 3.x for Jensen wake model

devops.hosting: Azure Batch pool with Python 3.12 custom image

constraints.window: 4-hour nightly processing window for 200 turbines at 10Hz
constraints.safety: Operator approval gate before any SCADA control system writes

reasoner_reviews.product: Nightly batch yaw optimization is operationally sound for a first deployment. The 4-hour processing window for 200 turbines at 10Hz is approximately 288,000 10-second intervals per turbine -- Dask parallel partitioning should handle this comfortably. The operator approval gate before SCADA write-back is correct from a safety perspective.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Dask on Azure Batch is appropriate for the batch processing scale. Key architectural gap in prior rounds: the plan did not specify how Dask workers coordinate across Azure Batch nodes or how partial job failures are handled. The recommendation is to use dask.distributed with an AzureBatch cluster backend and a robust task retry policy.
reasoner_reviews.security: Azure AD managed identity is correctly specified. The SCADA write-back via mTLS is appropriate but the prior plan did not specify certificate management -- certificates should be stored in Azure Key Vault and rotated on a 90-day schedule.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 4-hour window constraint should be validated against Dask partition sizing. For 200 turbines at 10Hz with 24 hours of data, partitioning by turbine ID across 8 Azure Batch nodes should complete well within 4 hours based on floris benchmarks.

backend.service_design: Single Python application orchestrated as an Azure Batch job. Entry point: run_yaw_optimization.py triggered nightly via Azure Logic Apps. Modules: scada_ingestor.py (reads Parquet SCADA partitions from Blob Storage into Dask DataFrame), wake_analyzer.py (applies Jensen model per farm_cluster using floris 3.x), recommendation_writer.py (writes yaw_offset_recommendation measurements to InfluxDB), scada_integrator.py (Phase 2 SCADA API write-back).
backend.api_patterns: No HTTP API -- batch job reads from Blob Storage and writes to InfluxDB. SCADA write-back uses proprietary SCADA vendor REST API with mTLS client certificate.
backend.failure_handling: Dask task retry policy: 3 retries with exponential backoff for transient Blob Storage read failures. Partial batch failures logged and skipped without failing entire job. Job-level failure triggers Azure Monitor alert and stops before InfluxDB writes to prevent partial state.
backend.background_jobs: Azure Logic Apps triggers the Azure Batch job at 02:00 local farm time nightly. Job pulls previous day SCADA Parquet files from Blob Storage hot tier, processes in parallel Dask tasks partitioned by wind_turbine.id, writes results to InfluxDB within the 4-hour window.

security.auth_design: Azure AD managed identity assigned to Azure Batch pool. Blob Storage and InfluxDB accessed via private endpoints -- no public internet egress. SCADA API mTLS certificates stored in Azure Key Vault; loaded at job start via managed identity credential.
security.secrets_management: Azure Key Vault for mTLS client certificate and InfluxDB token. Key Vault access policy scoped to batch job managed identity only. Certificate rotation triggered by Azure DevOps pipeline on 90-day schedule.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Structured JSON logs to Azure Log Analytics workspace for each turbine analysis: turbine_id, wake_deficit_computed, recommended_offset_degrees, baseline_power_kw, analysis_duration_ms. Job-level audit log includes total turbines processed, failures, and InfluxDB write count.

data.entities: wind_turbine (turbine_id, farm_cluster_id, latitude, longitude, hub_height_m, rotor_diameter_m). scada_log stored as Parquet: turbine_id, timestamp, wind_speed_ms, wind_direction_deg, active_power_kw, yaw_position_deg, rotor_rpm, pitch_angle_deg. wake_interference_event (turbine_id, upstream_turbine_id, wind_direction_deg, wake_deficit_pct, detected_at). yaw_offset_recommendation InfluxDB measurement: tags (turbine_id, farm_cluster_id, batch_run_id), fields (recommended_offset_deg, expected_gain_pct, confidence_score). performance_baseline InfluxDB measurement: tags (turbine_id), fields (baseline_aep_kwh, measurement_period_days).
data.storage_design: Azure Blob Storage: raw SCADA logs as Parquet partitioned by farm_cluster_id/turbine_id/date. InfluxDB: yaw_offset_recommendation and performance_baseline measurements keyed by turbine_id tag. Blob hot tier 30 days, cold tier 5 years lifecycle policy.
data.analytics_events: Grafana dashboard reads from InfluxDB: recommended offsets per turbine, expected energy gain heatmap by farm layout, historical recommendation acceptance rate by operator.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure Batch pool with 8 dedicated D4s_v3 nodes running custom Python 3.12 container image. Azure Logic Apps HTTP trigger calls Azure Batch job submission API. InfluxDB hosted on Azure Container Instances within same VNET.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipeline: pytest with mock SCADA fixtures, mypy type check, Docker build of batch container image, push to Azure Container Registry, update Batch pool image version. Triggered on main branch merge.
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor: Batch job metrics (task success/failure counts, duration). Log Analytics workspace: structured application logs. Grafana for yaw recommendation review by operators.
devops.devops_risks: Azure Batch pool cold-start (node provisioning approximately 5 minutes) reduces effective processing window. Mitigation: keep pool at minimum 2 warm nodes via low-priority allocation.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified dask.distributed Client initialized with AzureBatch cluster via dask-cloudprovider, retries=3 per task with exponential backoff base 2s. Partial turbine failures caught at task level, logged to Log Analytics, and skipped without aborting overall job. Job-level success threshold set at 95% of turbines processed before InfluxDB write proceeds.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Individual turbine analysis failures no longer abort the nightly job; operators see per-turbine failure counts in Log Analytics; job completes within 4-hour window for 200 turbines.

fix_report.action_taken: Removed React 18 and TypeScript references from technology_stack entirely. Clarified that operators consume yaw recommendations via existing Grafana dashboards connected to InfluxDB, not a first-party frontend. Aligns with contract frontend_stack field value 'None'.

target_output.title: Wind Farm Yaw Alignment Optimization Automation Tool
target_output.executive_summary: A nightly Azure Batch automation tool that ingests 10Hz SCADA performance logs for a 200-turbine wind farm, applies the Jensen wake deficit model via the floris library using Dask distributed processing on 8 Azure Batch nodes, and writes per-turbine yaw offset recommendations to InfluxDB for operator review before SCADA write-back. No first-party frontend -- operators consume recommendations via existing Grafana dashboards. Dask distributed with task-level retries and partial-failure tolerance completes the 200-turbine analysis within the 4-hour nightly window.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-purpose Azure Batch job written in Python 3.12. An Azure Logic Apps timer trigger submits the job nightly at 02:00 farm local time. The job initializes a dask.distributed Client backed by the Azure Batch pool (8 D4s_v3 nodes via dask-cloudprovider AzureBatch), reads previous-day SCADA Parquet files from Azure Blob Storage partitioned by farm_cluster_id/turbine_id/date, distributes turbine-level analysis tasks across Dask workers, applies the Jensen wake model via floris, and writes yaw_offset_recommendation measurements to InfluxDB via private endpoint. Partial turbine failures are logged and skipped; job succeeds if 95% or more of turbines complete. No HTTP endpoints, no frontend.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12, Dask 2024.x with dask.distributed for parallel task execution, dask-cloudprovider for Azure Batch worker backend, floris 3.x for Jensen wake model computation, pandas for single-turbine DataFrame operations within each Dask task, pyarrow for Parquet I/O from Azure Blob Storage, influxdb-client-python for time-series writes. Infrastructure: Azure Batch pool (8 D4s_v3 nodes, custom Python 3.12 Docker image), Azure Blob Storage (SCADA archive), InfluxDB on Azure Container Instances (VNET-private). CI: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Monitoring: Azure Monitor, Log Analytics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: SCADA Ingestion: reads previous-day Parquet partitions from Blob Storage hot tier for all 200 turbines, validates schema (wind_speed_ms, wind_direction_deg, active_power_kw, yaw_position_deg columns required). Wake Analysis: per wind_turbine, computes Jensen wake deficit from upstream neighbors based on wind direction histogram using floris WakeDeflection model, identifies yaw misalignment patterns. Recommendation Generation: produces yaw_offset_recommendation (recommended_offset_deg, expected_gain_pct, confidence_score) per turbine per analysis run, persists to InfluxDB. Operator Review Gate: InfluxDB data surfaced in Grafana dashboard with approve/reject workflow managed by operations team before SCADA write-back. SCADA Write-back (Phase 2): approved recommendations sent to SCADA vendor REST API via mTLS.
target_output.system_components: run_yaw_optimization.py (Batch job entrypoint, Dask cluster initialization with retries=3 per task), scada_ingestor.py (Blob Storage Parquet reads via pyarrow, schema validation), wake_analyzer.py (floris WakeDeflection model per farm_cluster, wind direction sector binning), recommendation_writer.py (InfluxDB batch write of yaw_offset_recommendation measurements), partial_failure_handler.py (catches per-turbine task exceptions, logs to Log Analytics, accumulates failure count for 95% threshold check). Azure Logic Apps (nightly job trigger). Azure Batch pool (compute). Azure Blob Storage (SCADA archive). InfluxDB ACI instance (private VNET). Azure Monitor and Log Analytics (observability).
target_output.workflows: Nightly Batch Workflow: Azure Logic Apps fires at 02:00, submits Batch job via Azure Batch REST API. Job entrypoint starts dask.distributed Client on 8-node AzureBatch pool. Dask map call distributes analyze_turbine(turbine_id, date) tasks across workers. Each task reads turbine Parquet from Blob, validates schema, loads floris farm layout for that farm_cluster, runs Jensen wake analysis for 24-hour wind direction distribution, computes recommended yaw offset per direction sector, returns recommendation dict. Failed tasks retried 3 times with 2/4/8s backoff; persistent failures logged and counted. After all tasks complete, recommendation_writer batches InfluxDB writes. If 95% or more turbines succeeded, job exits 0; else exits 1 and triggers PagerDuty. Operator Approval Workflow: Operations engineer opens Grafana dashboard, reviews per-turbine yaw recommendations with expected energy gain percentages. Approves via separate SCADA team process before any control system adjustments (Phase 2 SCADA write-back not automated in Phase 1).
target_output.data_model: wind_turbine (turbine_id TEXT PK, farm_cluster_id TEXT FK, latitude FLOAT, longitude FLOAT, hub_height_m FLOAT, rotor_diameter_m FLOAT, nacelle_offset_deg FLOAT). scada_log stored as Parquet in Blob: columns (turbine_id, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, wind_speed_ms FLOAT, wind_direction_deg FLOAT, active_power_kw FLOAT, yaw_position_deg FLOAT, rotor_rpm FLOAT, pitch_angle_deg FLOAT). wake_interference_event (turbine_id, upstream_turbine_id, wind_direction_sector_deg, wake_deficit_pct, detected_in_run_id, detected_at). yaw_offset_recommendation InfluxDB measurement: tags (turbine_id, farm_cluster_id, batch_run_id), fields (recommended_offset_deg, expected_gain_pct, confidence_score, wind_direction_sector_deg), timestamp. performance_baseline InfluxDB measurement: tags (turbine_id), fields (baseline_aep_kwh, measurement_period_days), timestamp.
target_output.api_design: No HTTP API surface. Internal interfaces: Azure Batch job submission via Azure SDK (azure-batch). SCADA write-back (Phase 2): PATCH to SCADA vendor REST API /api/v2/turbines/{turbine_id}/yaw-offset with mTLS client certificate loaded from Azure Key Vault. InfluxDB write API via influxdb-client-python write_api with VNET private endpoint. Azure Blob Storage reads via azure-storage-blob with managed identity credential. All interfaces are internal or outbound-only; no inbound HTTP endpoints.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD managed identity for Batch pool with no static credentials. Blob Storage and InfluxDB accessed exclusively via Azure Private Endpoints. SCADA API mTLS: client certificate stored in Azure Key Vault, loaded at job start via DefaultAzureCredential, rotated on 90-day schedule via Azure DevOps certificate renewal task. Log Analytics workspace receives structured JSON logs including turbine_id, analysis duration, and recommendation values -- no raw SCADA data logged to prevent sensitive IP leakage. Compliance context: internal operational data classification policy applied (vague -- no specific regulatory framework cited, which is a gap in this plan). Azure VNET NSG rules restrict all inbound traffic to Batch nodes.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure Batch pool: 8 dedicated D4s_v3 nodes (4 vCPU, 16GB RAM each), minimum 2 always-on nodes to reduce cold-start latency. Custom Docker image: python:3.12-slim with Dask, floris, pyarrow, influxdb-client pre-installed, pushed to Azure Container Registry on CI merge. Azure DevOps Pipeline: pytest with mock SCADA Parquet fixtures for 10-turbine subset, mypy type check, Docker build and push to ACR, Logic Apps update if job template changes. Logic Apps timer: nightly at 02:00 farm local time. Azure Monitor: Batch job duration, task success/failure counts. Log Analytics queries for per-run recommendation counts and partial failure rates.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor Batch metrics: task_success_count, task_failure_count, job_duration_seconds per run_id. Log Analytics structured logs: per-turbine JSON records with turbine_id, wake_deficit_pct, recommended_offset_deg, expected_gain_pct, confidence_score, analysis_duration_ms, error field (null or exception message). Alert rules: job_duration_seconds > 14400 (4-hour window breach), task_failure_count / total_tasks > 0.05, job exit code != 0. PagerDuty integration for critical alerts during night processing window.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure Batch 8 D4s_v3 nodes at approximately $0.38/hour each: $3.04/hour during nightly 4-hour window equals roughly $12/day or $360/month. Azure Blob Storage 5TB cold tier for 5-year SCADA archive: approximately $50/month. InfluxDB ACI (2 vCPU, 4GB): approximately $60/month. Estimated total: $470/month. Scaling for larger farms (500+ turbines): increase pool to 20 nodes and partition by farm_cluster -- Dask task graph scales linearly.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (4 weeks): nightly Azure Batch Dask job for 200-turbine SCADA ingestion and Jensen wake analysis, yaw_offset_recommendation writes to InfluxDB, Grafana dashboard for operator review, Azure Monitor and Log Analytics observability, CI/CD pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): SCADA write-back via mTLS API with Key Vault certificate management, operator approval workflow formalized, performance_baseline automated monthly recalculation. Phase 3 (future): real-time Event Hubs streaming for sub-hourly yaw updates, ML-based wake model replacing Jensen, closed-loop automatic yaw control with safety interlocks.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with type annotations. mypy strict mode enforced in CI. pytest with minimum 80% coverage; mock Parquet fixtures for 10-turbine farm subset used in all unit and integration tests. Dask task functions must be pure (no shared mutable state) for safe distributed execution. floris farm layout configurations stored as YAML in repository, versioned alongside the codebase. Conventional Commits; semver releases tied to floris library version updates.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Azure Batch node provisioning cold-start approximately 5 minutes reduces effective processing window. Mitigation: minimum 2 always-on nodes. Risk: floris Jensen model accuracy degrades for complex terrain farms; Gaussian wake model is more accurate but 3x slower. Mitigation: Jensen for MVP, Gaussian optional in Phase 3. Tradeoff: Dask distributed adds coordination overhead versus single-threaded processing; break-even is approximately 50 turbines. Risk: SCADA Parquet schema changes from vendor firmware updates break ingestion silently. Mitigation: schema validation step at ingestion rejects files with unexpected column sets and alerts on failure.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Dask over Ray for Azure Batch integration via dask-cloudprovider native support. Chose InfluxDB over TimescaleDB for yaw recommendations due to native time-series query optimization for tag-based turbine lookups. Confirmed Jensen wake model via floris for Phase 1 speed; Gaussian model deferred to Phase 3.

metadata.notes: Wind farm yaw optimization; round 4 fixes React frontend violation and Dask coordination but retains vague compliance_context as intentional flaw.

profile.domain: Invasive Plant Species Identification Mobile App

project_goal.value: Consumer mobile app using on-device ML to identify invasive plant species from citizen scientist photos, logging GPS sightings and contributing validated observations to a national biodiversity database
project_goal.rationale: Citizen science data collection at scale with on-device privacy-preserving ML inference

target_users.value: Citizen scientists and naturalists photographing plants in the field; volunteer botanist reviewers validating observations
target_users.rationale: Two distinct user roles: field reporters and expert validators

project_class.rationale: Primary user interface is a smartphone camera-based mobile app

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, analytics, integrations
capabilities.rationale: On-device TFLite model, FastAPI backend, Firebase Auth, national DB integration

complexity_level.rationale: On-device ML model deployment, GPS privacy controls, multi-role review workflow

risk_level.rationale: Personal location data for citizens; no financial or health data

data_sensitivity.rationale: GPS coordinates and user identity qualify as personal data under GDPR and CCPA

external_exposure.rationale: Consumer app with public signup; FastAPI backend accessible from app clients

access_model.value: Firebase Auth with email and Google OAuth; role-based: citizen_scientist / reviewer / admin
access_model.rationale: Public signup for citizens, invite-only for botanist reviewers

feature_scope.value: On-device species identification from camera photo, GPS sighting submission, confidence score display, reviewer validation queue, national database sync via nightly integration job, user observation history
feature_scope.rationale: Core citizen science loop from capture to validated record

mvp_scope.value: On-device inference with TFLite model (50 invasive species), photo and GPS submission, reviewer validation queue, basic observation history
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for pilot launch with conservation partner

security_baseline.value: Firebase Auth JWT, HTTPS for all API calls, GPS coordinates encrypted at rest in PostgreSQL, user PII subject to GDPR/CCPA controls
security_baseline.rationale: Personal location data requires encryption and user rights compliance

frontend_stack.value: Flutter with Dart for iOS and Android cross-platform
frontend_stack.rationale: Single codebase for iOS and Android, mature camera and GPS plugin ecosystem

backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async I/O for photo upload and observation submission endpoints

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for observations and user data; Redis 7 for reviewer queue cache
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for observation review workflow; Redis for low-latency reviewer batch queuing

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for FastAPI backend
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless scaling for variable citizen science submission bursts

privacy_retention_policy.value: GPS coordinates retained with precision reduced to 100m radius; user account data deleted within 30 days of deletion request; observation data anonymized and retained indefinitely for scientific record
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR Article 5 data minimization for location precision; scientific value of anonymized observations retained

future_scope.value: Model update push mechanism for new invasive species, audio-based plant sound identification, integration with iNaturalist API

constraints.value: On-device inference must run without internet connection; TFLite model size under 30MB for reasonable app download size
constraints.rationale: Field use in areas with no cell coverage; app store size constraints

observability_baseline.value: Firebase Crashlytics for crash reporting; Grafana on Cloud Run metrics; custom model inference latency tracking
observability_baseline.rationale: Mobile crash monitoring and backend API observability

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with biweekly conservation partner demos
execution_preference.rationale: Regular stakeholder feedback during citizen science feature development

compliance_context.value: GDPR Article 6 lawful basis for location data processing (legitimate interest for conservation), GDPR Article 17 right to erasure for user accounts, CCPA opt-out for California users, data minimization via GPS precision reduction for sensitive species locations
compliance_context.rationale: EU and California users submit personal GPS data requiring explicit compliance framework

llm_integration.value: On-device TFLite MobileNetV3 model (30MB) fine-tuned on 50 invasive plant species; no cloud inference for privacy and offline operation; model updates delivered via OTA versioned asset download in app
llm_integration.rationale: Offline inference requirement and privacy constraints mandate on-device model only; no cloud AI API

project.goal: Deploy citizen science invasive plant identification app with on-device ML
project.target_users: Citizen scientists and volunteer botanist reviewers
project.timeline: MVP in Q3, national DB integration in Q4

backend.framework: FastAPI with async SQLAlchemy
backend.storage: GCS for photo uploads via signed URLs
backend.auth: Firebase Admin SDK for JWT validation

security.auth: Firebase Auth JWT validated at FastAPI middleware
security.location_precision: GPS rounded to 100m radius before storage

data.cache: Redis 7 on Memorystore for reviewer queue
data.photos: Google Cloud Storage with lifecycle policy

constraints.app_stores: iOS App Store and Google Play submission

reasoner_reviews.product: The two-role model (citizen_scientist and reviewer) maps well to the citizen science workflow. The reviewer queue with batch assignment by geographic region reduces reviewer fatigue and improves validation accuracy. The national DB sync integration is the right target for scientific impact.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: TFLite MobileNetV3 at 30MB is feasible for app store distribution. The model OTA update mechanism needs careful versioning -- the app must handle the case where a user has an older model version and the backend expects newer species codes. Model version should be included in every sighting submission.
reasoner_reviews.security: GPS precision reduction to 100m radius is an important privacy control, especially for sensitive species. The prior round fully resolved the GDPR erasure workflow -- anonymization on account deletion is correctly specified. Firebase Crashlytics should be configured to exclude GPS coordinates from crash reports.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: TFLite model update OTA should not be a background auto-update on metered connections -- opt-in model update with size display is better UX for field users on limited data plans.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with route modules: /sightings (submission, photo URL generation, queue insertion), /reviews (reviewer assignment, approve/reject), /species (species catalog, model version metadata), /users (profile, deletion request). Async SQLAlchemy for PostgreSQL. Background task for national DB sync (nightly Cloud Run job).
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/sightings (submit observation with photo GCS key, GPS rounded coords, model_version, species_predictions), GET /v1/reviews/queue (reviewer fetches batch by region), PATCH /v1/reviews/:id (approve or correct species), GET /v1/species/:id (species details and threat level), DELETE /v1/users/me (triggers anonymization workflow).
backend.failure_handling: GCS signed URL generation fails gracefully -- client retries with exponential backoff. Reviewer queue Redis cache miss falls back to PostgreSQL query. National DB sync failures logged and retried next night.
backend.scaling_notes: Cloud Run scales to 0 between submission bursts. Minimum 1 instance to avoid cold-start for reviewer workflows. Reviewer queue uses Redis sorted set keyed by geographic region for O(log n) batch pop.

frontend.app_structure: Flutter app with feature-based module structure: camera_capture, species_inference, sighting_submission, reviewer_queue, observation_history, user_profile. Riverpod for state management across async inference and submission flows.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Camera flow: camera preview -> capture -> TFLite inference (offline) -> species result with confidence -> GPS attach -> submit or discard. Reviewer flow: queue list -> observation detail with photo, GPS map, submitted species, confidence -> approve or correct species via dropdown -> submit review.
frontend.state_management: Riverpod StateNotifier for inference results, submission status, and reviewer queue. sqflite for local pending_sighting persistence. connectivity_plus for network status detection -- submission queued locally when offline and synced on reconnection.
frontend.performance_notes: TFLite inference runs on isolate thread to avoid UI jank. Image downsampled to 224x224 before inference per MobileNetV3 input spec. Photo compressed to JPEG 85% before GCS upload.

security.auth_design: Firebase Auth handles email and Google OAuth registration. Firebase JWT validated on every FastAPI request via firebase_admin SDK. Reviewer role assigned by admin via custom claim.
security.privacy_controls: GPS coordinates rounded to nearest 0.001 degree (approximately 100m) on Flutter client before transmission. Sensitive species flag triggers additional 1km precision reduction. Firebase Crashlytics initialized with setCrashlyticsCollectionEnabled(false) for EU users until GDPR consent collected.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: sighting_submission_log records user_id, species_predicted, gps_precision_level, model_version, submitted_at. Reviewer decision log records reviewer_id, sighting_id, action, corrected_species, decided_at.

data.entities: citizen_scientist (id UUID PK, firebase_uid, display_name, region, gdpr_consent_at, anonymized_at). sighting_report (id UUID PK, user_id FK, species_id FK, photo_gcs_key, latitude_rounded FLOAT, longitude_rounded FLOAT, confidence_score FLOAT, model_version TEXT, submitted_at, validation_status ENUM pending/approved/rejected). invasive_species_record (id INT PK, common_name, scientific_name, threat_level ENUM high/medium/low, sensitive_location BOOL). validation_review (id UUID PK, sighting_id FK, reviewer_id FK, action ENUM approved/corrected/rejected, corrected_species_id, decided_at). national_db_sync_record (id UUID PK, sighting_id FK, external_record_id, synced_at, sync_status). pending_sighting sqflite local (id INTEGER PK autoincrement, photo_bytes_compressed BLOB, latitude_rounded REAL, longitude_rounded REAL, species_predictions TEXT JSON, confidence_score REAL, model_version TEXT, created_at TEXT, upload_attempts INTEGER DEFAULT 0).
data.retention_and_deletion: On user deletion: citizen_scientist anonymized (display_name nulled, firebase_uid replaced with anon_id), sighting_report user_id set to anon_id. Photos in GCS deleted within 30 days. national_db_sync_records retained with anon_id for scientific provenance.
data.analytics_events: Firebase Analytics: species_identified (species_id, confidence, model_version), sighting_submitted, offline_queue_flush_completed. Grafana: daily submission count, validation queue depth, model inference p99 latency by device_model.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run service for FastAPI in us-central1. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with private IP. Redis Memorystore in same VPC. GCS bucket for photos with lifecycle: delete after 30 days for anonymized accounts. Cloud Run scheduled job for national DB nightly sync.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Flutter analyze and flutter test, Python pytest with 80% coverage, Docker build and push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy with 10% canary traffic split. Fastlane for App Store and Play Store submissions on semver tags.
devops.backup_and_recovery: Cloud SQL automated daily backups with 7-day retention. GCS versioning on photo bucket. Point-in-time recovery for PostgreSQL up to 7 days.

ISS-001.title: Model version compatibility between app and backend not handled
ISS-001.detail: Backend receives sighting submissions but does not validate model_version field, so older app versions submitting deprecated species codes cause silent data corruption.

history.note: Raised by architect_reasoner
model_version field added to sighting_report and validated against supported_model_versions table

ISS-002.title: Firebase Crashlytics collecting GPS coordinates in crash reports
ISS-002.detail: Default Crashlytics configuration captures device state including location data, violating GDPR for EU users.

history.note: Security reviewer flagged
setCrashlyticsCollectionEnabled(false) until GDPR consent; location excluded from custom keys

ISS-003.title: Offline submission queue not durable on app crash
ISS-003.detail: Sightings captured offline held in memory and lost if the app crashes before network reconnection.

history.note: Raised by product reviewer when offline use case stress-tested

focus_issues.title: Offline submission queue not durable on app crash
focus_issues.detail: Sightings captured offline held in memory and lost on app crash; field scientists lose data.

latest_recommendations: Persist offline submission queue to SQLite via sqflite on device so sightings survive app crash and are uploaded on next launch
Add queue length indicator in app UI so field scientists know how many pending submissions are waiting for connectivity
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Invasive Plant Species Identification App Round 3

convergence_state.reason: One medium issue unresolved; score stable at 8.1 but ISS-003 affects field data quality

previous_audits.summary: Strong architecture with resolved GPS privacy and model version issues. Offline durability for the submission queue is the remaining gap -- offline-captured sightings lost on app crash affects the core citizen science value proposition.
strengths: On-device TFLite inference with model_version compatibility checking is well-specified
GDPR anonymization workflow on account deletion is complete
GPS precision reduction with sensitive species additional reduction is a strong privacy control
concerns: Offline submission queue uses in-memory Riverpod state -- crashes during offline sessions lose captured sightings
SQLite local persistence for offline queue not specified
recommendations: Use sqflite to persist pending_sighting table locally; upload on reconnect; delete local record after successful backend acknowledgement
Display pending upload count badge in app to reassure field users their offline sightings are queued

issue_updates.title: Offline submission queue not durable on app crash
issue_updates.detail: Offline queue held in Riverpod state only; not persisted to device storage
