target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 resolves ISS-003 by adding a durable local SQLite queue via sqflite that persists pending sighting submissions across app crashes. The key decision is to store the full sighting payload in a pending_sighting table on device, attempt upload on every network reconnect, and delete the local record only after the backend returns HTTP 201. The existing architecture is strong (score 8.1); this round upgrades the offline submission flow and adds a pending upload count indicator.

fix_report.action_taken: Added sqflite dependency to Flutter app. Defined local pending_sighting table (id, photo_bytes_compressed BLOB, latitude_rounded, longitude_rounded, species_predictions JSON, confidence_score, model_version, created_at, upload_attempts). SightingSubmissionNotifier writes to sqflite before attempting network upload. ConnectivityService watches connectivity_plus stream; on reconnect, flushes pending_sighting queue with exponential backoff. Backend acknowledgement (HTTP 201) triggers deletion of local record. Added PendingUploadBadge widget on home screen showing count of locally stored sightings.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Sightings captured during offline field sessions survive app crash and are uploaded when connectivity is restored; no citizen science data lost from offline use.

target_output.title: Invasive Plant Species Identification Mobile App Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Flutter cross-platform mobile app enabling citizen scientists to photograph plants and receive real-time invasive species identification from an on-device TFLite MobileNetV3 model (30MB, 50 species) without internet connectivity. Sightings with rounded GPS coordinates are queued in a local sqflite SQLite database and uploaded to a FastAPI backend on Cloud Run when connectivity is available, surviving app crashes and offline field sessions. Volunteer botanist reviewers validate pending observations via a region-scoped Redis queue. Validated records sync nightly to the national biodiversity database. GDPR and CCPA compliant with GPS precision reduction, Crashlytics consent gating, and account anonymization on deletion.
target_output.architecture_overview: Flutter app communicates with a Python 3.12 FastAPI backend on GCP Cloud Run. On-device TFLite inference (MobileNetV3, 30MB) runs on an isolate thread with no network dependency. Captured sightings are persisted to a local sqflite SQLite database before network transmission, ensuring durability across crashes and offline sessions. ConnectivityService monitors network state via connectivity_plus and triggers queue flush on reconnect. Photos uploaded to GCS via signed URLs. FastAPI validates Firebase JWT, stores observations in Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with GPS rounded to 100m, and pushes pending review assignments to Redis reviewer queues by geographic region. A nightly Cloud Run job syncs approved observations to the national biodiversity database.
target_output.technology_stack: Flutter 3.x with Dart for iOS and Android. Packages: tflite_flutter (on-device inference), camera, geolocator, sqflite (local offline queue), flutter_riverpod (state management), connectivity_plus (network monitoring), firebase_auth, firebase_crashlytics. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, async SQLAlchemy, Pydantic v2, firebase_admin, google-cloud-storage. Database: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (private IP). Cache: Redis 7 Memorystore. Storage: GCS with signed URLs. Hosting: Cloud Run (min 1 instance). CI: GitHub Actions. Mobile distribution: Fastlane for App Store and Play Store. Monitoring: Firebase Crashlytics, Grafana on Cloud Run metrics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Camera Capture and Inference: camera plugin -> capture JPEG -> downsample to 224x224 -> TFLite isolate inference -> ranked species list with confidence scores (offline). Sighting Submission: GPS attach (rounded to 100m) -> write to sqflite pending_sighting -> attempt network upload -> on HTTP 201 delete local record; on failure retain for retry. Offline Queue Flush: ConnectivityService detects online state -> iterate pending_sighting table -> POST each with exponential backoff -> show PendingUploadBadge count. Reviewer Workflow: region-scoped queue from Redis via GET /v1/reviews/queue -> view photo, map pin, submitted species, confidence -> approve or correct species -> PATCH /v1/reviews/:id. National DB Sync: nightly Cloud Run scheduled job queries approved sightings and POSTs to national biodiversity API. Account Deletion: anonymizes citizen_scientist record, queues GCS photo deletion, retains national_db_sync_records with anon_id.
target_output.system_components: Flutter app (camera module, TFLite inference isolate, sqflite offline queue, Riverpod state, ConnectivityService, PendingUploadBadge widget). FastAPI on Cloud Run (sightings router, reviews router, species router, users router, Firebase JWT middleware). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16. Redis 7 Memorystore (reviewer queue sorted sets by region). GCS (photo uploads with lifecycle). Firebase Auth. Firebase Crashlytics (GPS excluded). Cloud Run scheduled job (national DB sync).
target_output.workflows: Offline Capture Workflow: Citizen scientist in field with no cell signal opens app, taps identify. TFLite inference runs on isolate thread in under 300ms, returns ranked species list. User confirms sighting; geolocator returns GPS rounded to 100m. sqflite inserts row into pending_sighting with photo_bytes_compressed, GPS, species_predictions JSON, model_version. PendingUploadBadge increments. When connectivity restored, ConnectivityService fires; SightingSubmissionNotifier iterates pending_sighting, POSTs each to /v1/sightings with GCS signed URL upload for photo, retries up to 5 times with 2/4/8/16/32s backoff. On HTTP 201, sqflite deletes local row. Badge decrements. Reviewer Validation Workflow: Botanist reviewer opens app (reviewer Firebase JWT), navigates to queue. GET /v1/reviews/queue returns 20 pending sightings in reviewer's registered region from Redis sorted set. Reviewer views photo, map pin, submitted species and confidence. Taps Approve (PATCH action=approved) or selects correct species (PATCH action=corrected). Approved sightings update validation_status to approved, eligible for national DB sync.
target_output.data_model: citizen_scientist (id UUID PK, firebase_uid TEXT UNIQUE, display_name TEXT NULLABLE, region TEXT, gdpr_consent_at TIMESTAMPTZ, anonymized_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE). sighting_report (id UUID PK, user_id UUID FK, species_id INT FK, photo_gcs_key TEXT, latitude_rounded FLOAT, longitude_rounded FLOAT, confidence_score FLOAT, model_version TEXT, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, validation_status ENUM pending/approved/corrected/rejected). invasive_species_record (id INT PK, common_name TEXT, scientific_name TEXT, threat_level ENUM high/medium/low, sensitive_location BOOL, added_at DATE). validation_review (id UUID PK, sighting_id FK, reviewer_id FK, action ENUM approved/corrected/rejected, corrected_species_id INT NULLABLE, decided_at TIMESTAMPTZ). national_db_sync_record (id UUID PK, sighting_id FK, external_record_id TEXT, synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ, sync_status ENUM pending/synced/failed). sqflite local: pending_sighting (id INTEGER PK autoincrement, photo_bytes_compressed BLOB, latitude_rounded REAL, longitude_rounded REAL, species_predictions TEXT JSON, confidence_score REAL, model_version TEXT, created_at TEXT, upload_attempts INTEGER DEFAULT 0).
target_output.api_design: FastAPI under /v1. POST /v1/sightings (body: photo_gcs_key, latitude_rounded, longitude_rounded, species_predictions[], confidence_score, model_version; returns 201 with sighting_id). POST /v1/sightings/photo-url (returns GCS signed PUT URL). GET /v1/reviews/queue?region= (reviewer role; returns 20 pending sightings). PATCH /v1/reviews/:id (body: action, corrected_species_id optional). GET /v1/species?q= (search invasive species catalog). GET /v1/users/me/sightings (observation history). DELETE /v1/users/me (triggers anonymization). GET /v1/models/latest (current model version, size, OTA download URL). Auth: Firebase JWT Bearer on all endpoints. Rate limit: 60 req/min per user_id.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth JWT validated via firebase_admin on every FastAPI request. GPS precision reduced on Flutter client to nearest 0.001 degree (approximately 100m) before any transmission; sensitive_location species trigger round to 0.01 degree (approximately 1km). Firebase Crashlytics: setCrashlyticsCollectionEnabled(false) at startup until GDPR consent received; location excluded from all custom crash keys. sqflite local DB stores photo bytes and GPS -- Flutter encryption level set to DataProtectionLevel.complete on iOS. GDPR Article 17 erasure: DELETE /v1/users/me anonymizes citizen_scientist, queues GCS photo deletion within 30 days, national_db_sync_records retained with anon_id for scientific provenance. CCPA opt-out: identical pseudonymization flow triggered for California users.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Run FastAPI service in us-central1, min-instances=1, max-instances=20, memory 1Gi. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 private IP. Redis Memorystore 2GB. GCS photo bucket with object lifecycle rule deleting objects where metadata.anonymized=true after 30 days. GitHub Actions CI: flutter analyze, flutter test, Python pytest 80% coverage, Docker build and push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy with 10% canary traffic split 15 minutes. Fastlane lanes for App Store and Play Store on semver tag. National DB sync: Cloud Run scheduled job via Cloud Scheduler at 03:00 UTC nightly. Rollback: Cloud Run revision traffic rollback in under 2 minutes.
target_output.observability: Firebase Crashlytics: crash-free session rate per app version, GPS and PII excluded. Grafana on Cloud Run metrics: request rate, error rate, p99 latency per endpoint. Custom metrics: pending_sighting_queue_depth (polled from Redis), national_db_sync_success_count, review_queue_depth_by_region. Firebase Analytics events: species_identified (species_id, confidence, model_version), sighting_submitted, offline_queue_flush_completed (count flushed). Alert rules: review queue depth > 500 for any region, Cloud Run error rate > 2%, national DB sync failure.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run pay-per-request: estimated $40/month for 10K daily submissions. Cloud SQL db-n1-standard-2: $120/month. Redis Memorystore 2GB: $50/month. GCS photo storage with 30-day lifecycle: approximately $20/month. Firebase Auth and Crashlytics free tier sufficient for MVP. Total estimate: approximately $230/month. Cloud Run auto-scales to 20 instances for daily submission bursts. Reviewer queue Redis sorted sets scale to 100K pending items without degradation.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Flutter app with TFLite inference, sqflite offline queue, GPS precision reduction, Firebase Auth, sighting submission to FastAPI, reviewer validation queue via Redis, GDPR anonymization, GitHub Actions CI and Cloud Run deploy. Phase 2 (4 weeks): National DB nightly sync, model OTA update mechanism with version compatibility check, Grafana observability dashboard, Fastlane App Store and Play Store submission. Phase 3 (future): iNaturalist API observation sharing, audio-based plant identification module, reviewer gamification and accuracy scores.
target_output.development_guidelines: Flutter: dart analyze with strict lints, flutter_lints package enforced in CI. Dart code coverage minimum 70% with flutter_test. Python: ruff and mypy strict, pytest 80% coverage. TFLite model updates versioned in GCS model-assets bucket with semantic version in filename (invasive_v2.3.0.tflite). sqflite migrations managed via DatabaseHelper versioned onCreate/onUpgrade callbacks. Firebase JWT validation uses firebase_admin cached key fetching to avoid per-request network calls. Feature flags via Firebase Remote Config for rolling out new species catalog additions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: TFLite model accuracy degrades for novel invasive species not in training set (50 species MVP). Mitigation: low-confidence submissions (under 0.5) shown as 'Unknown -- submit for expert review' rather than a species name. Risk: sqflite local database on Android unencrypted on rooted devices. Mitigation: flutter_secure_storage encryption keys protect sensitive photo bytes. Tradeoff: GPS precision reduction to 100m prevents exact location reconstruction for some scientific research applications; accepted to comply with GDPR data minimization. Risk: GCS signed URL expiry (15 minutes) during poor connectivity causes photo upload failure. Mitigation: pending_sighting retry logic requests fresh signed URL on each upload attempt.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected sqflite over Hive for local offline queue because sqflite provides SQL queries enabling ordered retry by created_at and upload_attempts count. Chose GPS rounding on Flutter client (not server) to prevent raw coordinates ever leaving the device. Confirmed Firebase Auth over Supabase Auth for better Flutter SDK maturity and Crashlytics integration. Model OTA via GCS signed download URLs avoids App Store review cycles for model updates.

metadata.notes: Invasive plant species ID mobile app; round 4 adds durable sqflite offline submission queue resolving field data loss risk.

project_goal.value: AI system ingesting multi-decade climate reanalysis datasets and satellite land-use change records to generate long-range drought risk forecasts for agricultural insurance and food security planning at the watershed level
project_goal.rationale: Watershed-level drought probability distributions support agricultural insurance premium pricing and food security policy decisions

target_users.value: Agricultural insurers pricing seasonal drought risk, food security analysts at government agencies and NGOs
target_users.rationale: Two distinct consumer roles with different query patterns and risk tolerance

project_class.rationale: Core value is ML ensemble model producing drought forecasts; analytics and forecast API are delivery mechanisms

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, analytics, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: React dashboard for analysts, FastAPI forecast API, SageMaker model training, ClickHouse analytics, weekly batch retraining

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-decade climate reanalysis at watershed granularity generates terabyte-scale training datasets requiring SageMaker distributed training

risk_level.rationale: Internal analytical tool; forecast errors have economic impact but no direct physical harm

data_sensitivity.rationale: Climate datasets are public; derived forecasts are internal business intelligence

external_exposure.rationale: Forecast API and dashboard require authenticated login; no public access

access_model.value: Cognito with role-based access: insurer_analyst / food_security_analyst / model_engineer / admin
access_model.rationale: Insurers and food security analysts have different forecast query scopes; model engineers need SageMaker experiment access

feature_scope.value: Weekly ensemble model retraining pipeline on SageMaker, watershed-level drought probability forecast API, React dashboard with map visualization and time-series charts, historical forecast accuracy tracking, insurer portfolio query interface
feature_scope.rationale: Full system from data ingestion through model training to forecast delivery and accuracy monitoring

mvp_scope.value: Weekly SageMaker batch retraining, 3-month drought probability forecast per watershed, FastAPI query endpoint, basic React dashboard with ClickHouse-backed charts
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for pilot with two insurer clients and one government agency

security_baseline.value: Cognito MFA for all users, HTTPS/TLS 1.3 for all endpoints, VPC isolation for SageMaker and ClickHouse, IAM role-based S3 access for training data
security_baseline.rationale: Internal system with authentication requirements; no public access

frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; Mapbox GL JS for watershed map visualization

backend_stack.rationale: Async FastAPI for forecast query endpoints; consistent with data science team stack

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for forecast time-series analytics; S3 for climate reanalysis archive and SageMaker training data
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse sub-second aggregation over 40 years of watershed forecasts; S3 for petabyte-scale climate archive

hosting_target.value: AWS SageMaker for model training and batch inference; ECS Fargate for FastAPI backend
hosting_target.rationale: SageMaker managed ML infrastructure; Fargate for API and dashboard serving

privacy_retention_policy.value: Climate reanalysis data retained indefinitely (public datasets); derived watershed forecasts retained 10 years for model accuracy validation; user query logs retained 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Long forecast retention supports retrospective accuracy studies; query logs for audit and debugging only

future_scope.value: Sub-weekly forecast cadence, crop-type adjusted drought impact models, API access for third-party insurer platforms, integration with commodity futures pricing feeds
future_scope.rationale: Post-pilot expansion of forecast resolution and distribution channels

constraints.value: SageMaker retraining must complete within 6-hour window on weekly schedule; forecast query p99 latency under 500ms; ClickHouse cluster sized for 40-year historical query range
constraints.rationale: Weekly cadence timing constraint and real-time query performance requirements

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards for SageMaker training job metrics, FastAPI request latency, ClickHouse query performance; Prometheus for API metrics; CloudWatch for infrastructure
observability_baseline.rationale: Hybrid observability stack covering ML pipeline and API serving

execution_preference.value: ML pipeline first (SageMaker training and batch forecast), then API and dashboard delivery
execution_preference.rationale: Validated model forecasts must exist before API and UI can be meaningfully built

compliance_context.value: Internal AI governance policy requiring model explainability documentation, forecast accuracy validation before production deployment, and audit trail for model version used per forecast query
compliance_context.rationale: Insurer clients require documentation of model methodology and accuracy statistics for regulatory filings

llm_integration.value: Ensemble of XGBoost and LSTM models trained on ERA5 climate reanalysis and MODIS NDVI land-use change time series; no generative LLM or cloud AI API; SageMaker-managed distributed training with ml.p3.8xlarge instances
llm_integration.rationale: Physics-informed ensemble with interpretable XGBoost component for insurer regulatory explainability; LSTM captures long-range temporal drought signal

project.goal: Weekly ensemble drought forecast system at watershed granularity for agricultural insurance and food security
project.target_users: Agricultural insurers and food security analysts
project.timeline: ML pipeline MVP in Q2, API and dashboard in Q3

frontend.mapping: Mapbox GL JS for watershed choropleth maps
frontend.charting: Recharts for time-series drought probability curves
frontend.state: React Query for forecast data fetching

backend.framework: FastAPI with async SQLAlchemy for user and query log tables
backend.ml_serving: SageMaker batch transform for weekly forecasts; no real-time inference

security.auth: Cognito user pools with MFA
security.vpc: VPC for SageMaker, ClickHouse, and Fargate in same private subnet

data.timeseries: ClickHouse for drought_index and forecast_run measurements
data.archive: S3 for ERA5 climate reanalysis and MODIS tiles

reasoner_reviews.product: Weekly retraining cadence is appropriate for climate model updates -- ERA5 reanalysis data updates on a monthly basis, so the training data only changes meaningfully once a month. The weekly cadence provides flexibility to retrain with updated MODIS NDVI satellite composites. The 3-month forecast horizon is well-matched to agricultural insurance underwriting cycles.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The XGBoost plus LSTM ensemble is a sound choice: XGBoost provides interpretable feature importance (precipitation deficit, SPEI, soil moisture anomaly) for insurer regulatory documentation, while LSTM captures 12-18 month lagged drought signal from the climate reanalysis. The prior round's gap was insufficient ClickHouse schema specificity -- the schema needs to support efficient watershed-range queries across the 40-year historical baseline.
reasoner_reviews.security: Cognito MFA with VPC isolation is correctly specified. Model version audit trail was flagged in round 2 as missing -- each forecast query must log the model_version used to the audit table so insurer clients can reconstruct which model produced a given premium pricing input.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: SageMaker ml.p3.8xlarge training completion within 6 hours for 5000 watersheds requires careful data pipeline optimization. ERA5 feature extraction from NetCDF to SageMaker Feature Store is the likely bottleneck -- Spark on EMR Serverless for the feature engineering step should be benchmarked.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with routers: /forecasts (watershed drought probability queries against ClickHouse), /watersheds (watershed metadata and geometry), /models (model version catalog and accuracy metrics), /audit (query audit log). Async SQLAlchemy for user and audit tables in PostgreSQL. ClickHouse async client for forecast time-series queries.
backend.api_patterns: GET /v1/forecasts?watershed_id=&horizon_months=&as_of_date= returns drought probability distribution (p10/p50/p90). GET /v1/forecasts/portfolio?watershed_ids=[] batch query for insurer portfolio. GET /v1/models/current (model version, accuracy metrics, training date). GET /v1/watersheds/:id/history?from=&to= (historical drought index from ClickHouse).
backend.scaling_notes: FastAPI on Fargate scales to 10 tasks at CPU 70% target. ClickHouse query p99 under 200ms for single watershed queries using watershed_id and forecast_date composite index. Batch portfolio queries for 500 watersheds resolved via ClickHouse IN clause under 500ms p99.
backend.failure_handling: Stale forecast detection: if latest forecast_run for watershed is older than 10 days, response includes staleness_warning flag. SageMaker training job failure sends SNS alert and retains previous model version in production.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with route-based code splitting. Pages: WatershedMap (Mapbox GL choropleth), ForecastDetail (watershed time-series charts), PortfolioRisk (insurer batch query interface), ModelInfo (current model version and accuracy metrics), Admin.
frontend.pages_and_flows: WatershedMap: Mapbox GL choropleth of 3-month drought probability per watershed, color scale green to red. Click watershed opens ForecastDetail panel with 12-month probability time-series (Recharts line chart) and historical SPEI index. PortfolioRisk: insurer pastes list of watershed IDs, fetches batch forecast, downloads CSV for actuarial modeling.
frontend.state_management: React Query with 1-hour stale time for forecast data. Zustand for map viewport state and selected watershed.
frontend.performance_notes: Mapbox GL vector tiles from S3 watershed geometry; not inline GeoJSON. React Query prefetches ForecastDetail data on watershed hover. PortfolioRisk CSV export streams from API.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with TOTP MFA. Cognito JWT validated at ALB and FastAPI guard middleware. Insurer role scoped to read-only forecast queries; model_engineer role can trigger manual SageMaker retraining.
security.authorization_model: FastAPI Depends injection per route: insurer_analyst can query /forecasts and /watersheds, food_security_analyst same plus /models read, model_engineer adds /models write for manual retrain trigger, admin full access.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: forecast_query_log table (user_id, watershed_id, forecast_horizon, model_version_used, queried_at) written on every /forecasts request. Retained 90 days. Insurer clients can request a signed audit report for regulatory filings.

data.entities: watershed (id INT PK, name TEXT, geometry GEOMETRY, area_km2, country, admin_region, agro_ecological_zone). climate_reanalysis_record stored in SageMaker Feature Store: watershed_id, date, precip_mm, temp_avg_c, soil_moisture, spei_3m, spei_12m. drought_index ClickHouse table: watershed_id, date, spi_3m, spi_12m, pdsi. forecast_result ClickHouse table: forecast_run_id, watershed_id, forecast_date, horizon_months, drought_prob_p10, drought_prob_p50, drought_prob_p90. model_version PostgreSQL: id, training_completed_at, mae_score, rmse_score, registry_status, deployed. forecast_query_log PostgreSQL: id, user_id, watershed_id, forecast_horizon, model_version_used, queried_at.
data.storage_design: ERA5 climate reanalysis: S3 zarr store partitioned by year and variable. MODIS NDVI composites: S3 GeoTIFF by month. ClickHouse: drought_index and forecast_result with ORDER BY (watershed_id, forecast_date) and toYYYYMM(forecast_date) partition for 40-year range query performance.
data.analytics_events: Grafana: weekly training job duration, mae_score trend per model_version, forecast query p99 by endpoint, watershed query frequency heatmap.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: FastAPI service (2-10 tasks) and React static assets via CloudFront/S3. SageMaker Pipelines for weekly retraining. ClickHouse on EC2 m6i.4xlarge cluster (3 nodes) in private subnet.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest 80% coverage, mypy, React build, Docker push to ECR, Fargate deploy. SageMaker Pipeline triggered weekly by EventBridge and on-demand via POST /v1/models/trigger.
devops.observability_stack: Grafana: SageMaker training metrics, Fargate CPU/memory, ClickHouse query performance. Prometheus sidecar on Fargate tasks. Weights and Biases for ML experiment tracking.
devops.devops_risks: ClickHouse on EC2 is operational burden. Mitigation: evaluate ClickHouse Cloud at scale milestone. SageMaker spot interruption can fail weekly training. Mitigation: SageMaker managed spot training with S3 checkpoint.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 resolves the two remaining medium issues: adding model_version_used to forecast_query_log with automatic injection of the currently deployed SageMaker Model Registry endpoint version on every /forecasts call, and specifying SageMaker Model Registry approved/staging channels with explicit fallback logic -- on training failure, production remains on the most recent approved model version and all forecast responses include model_age_days with staleness_warning when age exceeds 10 days.

fix_report.action_taken: Added model_version_used TEXT NOT NULL column to forecast_query_log table. FastAPI /forecasts endpoint dependency injects current_deployed_model_version from SageMaker Model Registry describe_model_package call (cached 5 minutes in Redis). model_version_used written on every INSERT to forecast_query_log. Audit report endpoint GET /v1/audit/forecast-log returns paginated log with model_version_used for insurer regulatory export.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Every forecast query linked to the model version that produced it; insurer clients can retrieve a signed audit log including model_version_used for regulatory filings.

fix_report.action_taken: Configured SageMaker Model Registry with drought-forecast-approved and drought-forecast-staging model package groups. Weekly training pipeline: if new model mae_score improves over approved model by more than 2%, promote to approved channel; else retain existing approved model. EventBridge rule monitors SageMaker Pipeline execution failure and publishes SNS alert. FastAPI /forecasts response includes model_age_days (days since training_completed_at) and staleness_warning (bool, true when model_age_days > 10).
fix_report.expected_outcome: Training job failure does not disrupt forecast service; production model version retained until a better model is promoted; API consumers aware of model staleness via response field.

target_output.title: Watershed Drought Risk Forecast AI System Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: An AI system ingesting ERA5 climate reanalysis and MODIS land-use change data to train weekly ensemble XGBoost+LSTM drought forecast models on SageMaker, storing 3-month watershed-level drought probability distributions in ClickHouse for sub-second query. A FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate serves forecast queries to agricultural insurers and food security analysts via a React dashboard with Mapbox GL watershed maps. SageMaker Model Registry manages approved/staging channels with fallback to previous model on training failure. Forecast query audit log includes model_version_used for insurer regulatory filing support.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier ML system: (1) Weekly SageMaker Pipeline for feature engineering (ERA5 NetCDF extraction to Feature Store via EMR Serverless Spark), XGBoost and LSTM training on ml.p3.8xlarge spot instances, ensemble evaluation, and Model Registry promotion if mae_score improves more than 2% over current approved model. (2) FastAPI on ECS Fargate serving forecast queries from ClickHouse, with Cognito JWT authentication, model version injection from SageMaker Model Registry (Redis-cached), and PostgreSQL audit logging. (3) React 18 SPA with Mapbox GL watershed choropleth and Recharts time-series visualization, served via CloudFront. ClickHouse EC2 cluster (3 nodes, private subnet) stores 40 years of drought_index and all forecast_result partitions with ORDER BY (watershed_id, forecast_date) for efficient per-watershed temporal range queries.
target_output.technology_stack: ML: Python 3.12, XGBoost 2.x, PyTorch LSTM, SageMaker Pipelines, SageMaker Feature Store, SageMaker Model Registry, EMR Serverless Spark for ERA5 feature extraction. Backend: FastAPI, async SQLAlchemy, aiochclient for async ClickHouse queries, boto3. Frontend: React 18 TypeScript, Mapbox GL JS 3.x, Recharts, React Query, Zustand. Database: ClickHouse EC2 cluster (m6i.4xlarge x3), PostgreSQL 16 on RDS. Cache: ElastiCache Redis (model version cache 5-min TTL). Auth: AWS Cognito with TOTP MFA. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, SageMaker Pipelines EventBridge schedule. Monitoring: Grafana, Prometheus, Weights and Biases, CloudWatch.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Weekly Retraining Pipeline: EventBridge triggers SageMaker Pipeline Sunday 22:00 UTC. EMR Serverless Spark extracts ERA5 features (SPEI-3, SPEI-12, soil moisture anomaly, precipitation deficit) per watershed to SageMaker Feature Store. XGBoost and LSTM trained in parallel on Feature Store dataset. Ensemble evaluation on 2020-2024 held-out validation set. If mae_score better than approved model by more than 2%, promote to drought-forecast-approved channel. Forecast Query API: GET /v1/forecasts returns drought_prob_p10/p50/p90 with model_age_days and staleness_warning. Portfolio Batch Query: POST /v1/forecasts/portfolio with up to 500 watershed_ids returns batch drought probabilities. React Dashboard: WatershedMap choropleth colored by 3-month p50 drought probability; click watershed for 12-month time-series; PortfolioRisk insurer interface for bulk CSV export. Audit: GET /v1/audit/forecast-log returns paginated query log with model_version_used per query.
target_output.system_components: SageMaker Pipeline (EMR feature extraction step, XGBoost training step, LSTM training step, ensemble eval step, model registry promotion step). SageMaker Feature Store (watershed climate feature vectors). SageMaker Model Registry (drought-forecast-approved and drought-forecast-staging groups). ECS Fargate (FastAPI service 2-10 tasks). ClickHouse EC2 cluster (3 nodes, private subnet, Keeper replication). RDS PostgreSQL 16 (user accounts, forecast_query_log, model_version). ElastiCache Redis (model version cache). CloudFront + S3 (React SPA). Cognito user pool. Grafana + Prometheus (observability). Weights and Biases (ML experiment tracking).
target_output.workflows: Weekly Retraining Workflow: EventBridge rule fires Sunday 22:00 UTC, starts SageMaker Pipeline. EMR Serverless Spark reads new ERA5 zarr files from S3, computes SPEI and soil moisture anomaly per watershed for the latest week, upserts SageMaker Feature Store. XGBoost job trains on 1980-2023 Feature Store data (spot ml.p3.8xlarge, 3h budget). LSTM job trains in parallel. Ensemble evaluation step runs on 2020-2024 held-out set, computes mae_score. Model Registry step: if mae_score improves more than 2% over current approved model, promote to drought-forecast-approved and archive previous; else register as staging only. On pipeline failure, EventBridge SNS alert fires to model_engineer PagerDuty. Forecast Query Workflow: Insurer analyst calls GET /v1/forecasts?watershed_id=7342&horizon_months=3. Cognito JWT validated. FastAPI dependency fetches current approved model version from Redis (5-min TTL) or SageMaker Model Registry on miss. ClickHouse query: SELECT drought_prob_p10, drought_prob_p50, drought_prob_p90 FROM forecast_result WHERE watershed_id=7342 AND forecast_run_id=current_run_id AND horizon_months=3. Response returned with model_age_days and staleness_warning bool. forecast_query_log INSERT with user_id, watershed_id, horizon_months, model_version_used, queried_at.
target_output.data_model: watershed (id INT PK, name TEXT, geometry GEOMETRY, area_km2, country, admin_region, agro_ecological_zone, centroid_lat, centroid_lon). drought_index ClickHouse table (watershed_id UInt32, date Date, spi_3m Float32, spi_12m Float32, pdsi Float32, soil_moisture_anomaly Float32) ORDER BY (watershed_id, date) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(date). forecast_result ClickHouse table (forecast_run_id UUID, watershed_id UInt32, forecast_date Date, horizon_months UInt8, drought_prob_p10 Float32, drought_prob_p50 Float32, drought_prob_p90 Float32) ORDER BY (watershed_id, forecast_date) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(forecast_date). forecast_run PostgreSQL (id UUID PK, model_version TEXT FK, training_completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, era5_through DATE, mae_score FLOAT, n_watersheds INT, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). model_version PostgreSQL (id TEXT PK, training_completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, mae_score FLOAT, rmse_score FLOAT, registry_status ENUM approved/staging/archived, deployed BOOL). forecast_query_log PostgreSQL (id UUID PK, user_id UUID FK, watershed_id INT, forecast_horizon_months INT, model_version_used TEXT NOT NULL, queried_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- retained 90 days, partitioned by month.
target_output.api_design: FastAPI /v1. GET /v1/forecasts?watershed_id=INT&horizon_months=INT&as_of_date=DATE returns {watershed_id, horizon_months, drought_prob_p10, drought_prob_p50, drought_prob_p90, model_version, model_age_days, staleness_warning}. POST /v1/forecasts/portfolio body {watershed_ids: [INT], horizon_months: INT} returns array of forecast objects. GET /v1/watersheds/:id/history?from=DATE&to=DATE returns drought_index time-series from ClickHouse. GET /v1/models/current returns {model_version, training_completed_at, mae_score, model_age_days}. POST /v1/models/trigger (model_engineer role only, triggers manual SageMaker Pipeline execution). GET /v1/audit/forecast-log?from=DATE&to=DATE (admin role, paginated, returns model_version_used per query). Auth: Cognito JWT Bearer. Rate limit: 300 req/min for insurer_analyst, 60 req/min otherwise.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito TOTP MFA required for all user roles. Cognito JWT validated at ALB listener rules and FastAPI Depends middleware. VPC private subnets: ClickHouse EC2, RDS PostgreSQL, ElastiCache Redis, SageMaker VPC-mode jobs. S3 bucket policies restrict ERA5 and MODIS data access to SageMaker execution role IAM only. Model version audit: forecast_query_log.model_version_used populated on every /forecasts call; GET /v1/audit/forecast-log provides paginated CSV for insurer regulatory filings. AI governance: SageMaker Model Registry approved channel requires mae_score improvement threshold before promotion; model cards documented per version with training data date range, mae_score, and XGBoost SHAP feature importance rankings for regulatory explainability.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate: FastAPI service 2-4 tasks, HPA on CPU 70%, ALB with Cognito auth integration. CloudFront with S3 React SPA. ClickHouse: 3 EC2 m6i.4xlarge in private subnet, Keeper replication. SageMaker Pipelines: EventBridge schedule Sunday 22:00 UTC, managed spot training with S3 checkpointing, 6-hour maximum job duration limit. Model Registry: drought-forecast-approved and drought-forecast-staging groups; automated promotion on mae threshold. GitHub Actions CI: pytest 80% coverage, mypy, React build, Docker push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy. Terraform modules for VPC, Fargate service, ClickHouse EC2, SageMaker pipeline definitions.
target_output.observability: Grafana dashboards: SageMaker Pipeline execution status and training mae_score per run, FastAPI p99 request latency per endpoint, ClickHouse query duration p99 target under 200ms, model_age_days gauge. Prometheus sidecar on ECS Fargate tasks. Weights and Biases for XGBoost and LSTM experiment tracking with loss curves, validation mae, and feature importance. CloudWatch Logs for SageMaker container stdout. Alerts: SageMaker Pipeline failure (EventBridge SNS PagerDuty), model_age_days > 10 (staleness email to model_engineer team), ClickHouse query p99 > 500ms, Fargate error rate > 1%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: SageMaker ml.p3.8xlarge spot weekly approximately $12 per run times 52 equals roughly $624/year. EMR Serverless Spark feature extraction approximately $8 per run equals $416/year. ClickHouse EC2 m6i.4xlarge times 3 approximately $700/month. ECS Fargate 2 tasks baseline approximately $80/month. RDS PostgreSQL db-t3.medium approximately $50/month. ElastiCache Redis cache.t3.small approximately $25/month. CloudFront and S3 approximately $30/month. Total approximately $950/month. SageMaker spot training saves approximately 65% versus on-demand. ClickHouse scales vertically before horizontal -- single shard handles 5000 watersheds times 40 years comfortably.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 ML Pipeline (6 weeks): ERA5 and MODIS S3 data lake setup, SageMaker Feature Store with watershed feature vectors, XGBoost baseline model training on 1980-2020 historical data, SageMaker Model Registry configuration with approved/staging channels, weekly EventBridge schedule, ClickHouse cluster with forecast_result schema and correct ORDER BY indexing. Phase 2 API and Dashboard (5 weeks): FastAPI /forecasts and /portfolios endpoints with Cognito auth, forecast_query_log with model_version_used, model staleness detection and staleness_warning response field, React WatershedMap and ForecastDetail pages, PortfolioRisk CSV export. Phase 3 (4 weeks): LSTM component added to ensemble, Weights and Biases experiment tracking, audit log export endpoint, Grafana observability dashboards, Terraform IaC for full environment reproducibility.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 type annotations on all FastAPI routes and SageMaker step functions. pytest with 80% coverage including mock ClickHouse responses. mypy strict enforced in CI. React TypeScript strict mode, ESLint, Vitest for component tests. SageMaker Pipeline steps defined as Python functions decorated with @step for reproducibility and unit testability. Model cards required in Git for each approved model version: training data range, mae_score, SHAP feature importance top-10. ClickHouse schema migrations versioned as numbered SQL files executed on cluster upgrade.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ERA5 reanalysis data availability lag -- Copernicus publishes with 5-day delay, so very recent climate events are underrepresented in weekly retraining. Mitigation: document ERA5 currency lag in model card and set staleness_warning threshold accordingly. Risk: ClickHouse EC2 self-managed cluster requires manual upgrade and failover. Mitigation: evaluate ClickHouse Cloud at 6-month review. Tradeoff: XGBoost plus LSTM ensemble improves mae_score 12% over XGBoost alone but doubles training time -- acceptable within 6-hour window at current watershed count. Risk: SageMaker spot interruption during LSTM training (3-4 hour job) without checkpointing loses run. Mitigation: SageMaker managed spot training with S3 checkpoint every 30 minutes.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected ClickHouse over TimescaleDB for forecast storage because ClickHouse ORDER BY MergeTree engine provides better query performance for 5000-watershed times 40-year range scans. Chose SageMaker Model Registry over manual S3 versioning for model promotion gates and rollback. Confirmed EMR Serverless Spark for ERA5 feature extraction over Pandas due to terabyte-scale NetCDF processing requirement.

metadata.notes: Watershed drought forecast AI system; round 3 resolves model audit trail and SageMaker Model Registry fallback issues.

profile.domain: Research prototype streaming real-time ocean buoy sensor telemetry into a live dashboard for marine scientists studying sea surface temperature anomalies and microplastic concentration drift patterns.

project_goal.rationale: Core research objective defined in project charter

target_users.rationale: Primary end-users identified in stakeholder workshops

access_model.rationale: Internal research prototype, no sensitive data requiring access control

project.goal: Ingest MQTT telemetry from ocean buoys and display live SST and microplastic concentration data to marine scientists
project.target_users: Marine scientists at internal institute

backend.framework: FastAPI with WebSockets and asyncio MQTT client

security.auth: None for prototype, intranet access only

open_questions.mqtt_broker: Evaluate Mosquitto vs EMQX for 50 buoy scale

reasoner_reviews.product: The MVP scope is well-scoped for a 3-buoy pilot. SST heatmap and threshold alerting are the core research tools scientists need immediately. The 30-day historical playback is correctly deferred to post-MVP.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI async WebSocket broadcast to multiple researcher sessions is the right pattern here. Consider a connection manager that tracks active sessions per buoy subscription to avoid broadcasting all buoy updates to all clients. TimescaleDB continuous aggregates will be essential for the historical playback chart performance once buoy count scales beyond 20.
reasoner_reviews.security: No authentication is acceptable for this internal prototype given data sensitivity is none. Ensure nginx enforces HTTPS even on intranet to prevent passive monitoring of sensor feeds.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The EUR 200/month Hetzner budget is sufficient for a Hetzner AX41 dedicated server running the full Docker Compose stack with TimescaleDB. At 50 buoys transmitting every 60 seconds, the write throughput is trivial for TimescaleDB.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The first-round plan had thin coverage of the WebSocket connection management strategy. With 10+ concurrent researcher sessions each subscribing to 50 buoy feeds, the broadcast fanout logic needs explicit design to avoid N*M message duplication.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with three routers: /api/buoys for REST queries, /ws/buoys/{buoy_id} for per-buoy WebSocket subscriptions, and /api/anomalies for threshold configuration. A background asyncio task runs the aiomqtt subscriber consuming from topic buoys/+/telemetry.
backend.api_patterns: REST endpoints for historical queries with cursor pagination on sensor_readings. WebSocket upgrade path uses FastAPI WebSocket class with a ConnectionManager singleton tracking Set[WebSocket] per buoy_id. MQTT ingestion writes to TimescaleDB and triggers ConnectionManager.broadcast(buoy_id, payload) for live push.
backend.background_jobs: Anomaly checker runs as an asyncio periodic task every 60s evaluating current SST against alert_thresholds table; emits alert events to connected WebSocket sessions for affected buoys.
backend.failure_handling: aiomqtt subscriber reconnects with exponential backoff on broker disconnect. WebSocket clients receive a reconnect hint message and re-subscribe on reconnect.

frontend.app_structure: Svelte 4 SPA with three views: LiveMap (Leaflet with buoy markers), BuoyDetail (time-series charts for selected buoy), and AnomalyLog (list of triggered threshold events).
frontend.pages_and_flows: LiveMap loads all buoys via GET /api/buoys, renders markers colored by SST range, subscribes to /ws/buoys/{id} for each visible buoy. BuoyDetail shows Chart.js line charts for temperature_celsius and microplastic_ppm over configurable time windows.
frontend.state_management: Svelte stores for buoyState (Map of buoy_id to latest reading), alertQueue (recent anomaly events), and selectedBuoyId. WebSocket messages mutate buoyState triggering reactive map marker updates.
frontend.performance_notes: Leaflet marker updates throttled to 5s intervals via Svelte reactive statement debounce to avoid map thrashing on high-frequency MQTT bursts.

security.auth_design: No authentication for prototype phase. nginx enforces HTTPS with self-signed certificate on intranet. Direct bare-metal port 8000 is blocked by iptables, forcing all traffic through nginx on 443.
security.abuse_prevention: nginx rate-limit on /ws/ WebSocket upgrade endpoint: max 10 concurrent WebSocket connections per source IP to prevent accidental fan-out storms from misconfigured clients.

data.entities: ocean_buoy (id UUID, buoy_code VARCHAR, lat DECIMAL, lon DECIMAL, deployment_date DATE, status ENUM active/inactive/maintenance). sensor_reading (time TIMESTAMPTZ, buoy_id UUID, temperature_celsius FLOAT8, salinity_ppt FLOAT8, microplastic_ppm FLOAT8, battery_voltage FLOAT4) partitioned as TimescaleDB hypertable on time. alert_threshold (id UUID, buoy_id UUID, metric VARCHAR, threshold_value FLOAT8, direction ENUM above/below, enabled BOOL). temperature_anomaly (id UUID, buoy_id UUID, observed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, peak_celsius FLOAT8, duration_minutes INT, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: TimescaleDB hypertable chunk_time_interval set to 1 week. Continuous aggregate cagg_hourly_avg computes mean temperature_celsius and microplastic_ppm per buoy per hour, refreshed every 15 minutes for dashboard chart performance.
data.retention_and_deletion: sensor_reading data retained indefinitely per research policy. TimescaleDB compression policy activates on chunks older than 30 days, achieving ~10x compression on float columns.
data.data_risks: TimescaleDB disk fill on Hetzner if buoy count scales to 200+ without compression policy. Mitigate with chunk compression and monitoring disk utilization via Prometheus node_exporter.

devops.deployment_topology: Docker Compose on single Hetzner AX41: mqtt-broker (Mosquitto), api (FastAPI), timescaledb, nginx (reverse proxy with HTTPS), grafana, prometheus, node-exporter.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint (ruff + mypy), pytest with TimescaleDB test container, Docker build + push to GitHub Container Registry, SSH deploy via docker compose pull and docker compose up -d on Hetzner host.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes FastAPI /metrics (prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator) and node-exporter. Grafana dashboard with panels for MQTT message rate, WebSocket active connections, TimescaleDB insert latency, and disk usage.
devops.rollback_strategy: Git tag on each deploy; rollback via SSH docker compose pull ghcr.io/org/buoy-api:previous-tag and docker compose up -d api.

ISS-001.title: WebSocket connection manager lacks per-buoy subscription filtering
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan broadcast all buoy updates to all connected researcher sessions regardless of which buoys the session had subscribed to, causing unnecessary data transfer and making per-buoy chart views inaccurate.

history.note: Identified in critic review: N*M broadcast pattern

ISS-002.title: No TimescaleDB continuous aggregate for historical chart queries
ISS-002.detail: The historical playback feature queries raw sensor_reading rows over 30-day windows which will degrade as data accumulates. Continuous aggregates were not specified in round 1 plan.

history.note: Identified in architect_reasoner review

focus_issues.title: WebSocket connection manager lacks per-buoy subscription filtering
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan broadcast all buoy updates to all connected researcher sessions regardless of which buoys the session had subscribed to.

latest_recommendations: Design ConnectionManager with per-buoy Set[WebSocket] subscriptions rather than global broadcast
Add TimescaleDB continuous aggregate for hourly averages to support historical chart queries
Document Mosquitto vs EMQX decision and 50-buoy scale rationale
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Ocean Buoy Telemetry Live Dashboard - Round 1

convergence_state.reason: High severity WebSocket broadcast issue unresolved

previous_audits.summary: Solid technology choices for a research prototype but the real-time architecture has a critical broadcast design flaw that would make per-buoy dashboards unusable at scale.
strengths: TimescaleDB hypertable choice is well-justified for sensor time-series
Svelte reactive stores are a good fit for high-frequency WebSocket updates
Docker Compose on Hetzner is correctly scoped to research budget
concerns: WebSocket broadcast sends all buoy updates to all researcher sessions; a session viewing one buoy receives telemetry for all 50 buoys
No continuous aggregate defined for historical chart queries
MQTT broker selection left as open question without evaluation criteria
blocking_issues: WebSocket connection manager must filter broadcasts to sessions subscribed to each buoy_id
recommendations: Implement ConnectionManager with Dict[str, Set[WebSocket]] keyed by buoy_id
Define TimescaleDB continuous aggregate cagg_hourly_avg refreshed every 15 minutes
Document Mosquitto selection rationale with connection count and persistence requirements

issue_updates.title: WebSocket connection manager lacks per-buoy subscription filtering
issue_updates.detail: Plan described a single broadcast to all connected clients without subscription routing

issue_updates.title: No TimescaleDB continuous aggregate for historical chart queries
issue_updates.detail: Historical queries will scan raw hypertable rows; no pre-aggregation strategy defined

previous_plan.title: Ocean Buoy Telemetry Live Dashboard - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: FastAPI WebSocket backend ingesting MQTT buoy telemetry into TimescaleDB and pushing live updates to a Svelte dashboard showing SST heatmap and microplastic concentration charts for marine researchers.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Single Hetzner server running Docker Compose with Mosquitto MQTT broker, FastAPI backend subscribing to buoy topics, TimescaleDB for persistence, and Svelte frontend served via nginx.
previous_plan.workflows: MQTT ingest writes sensor_reading rows; FastAPI broadcasts all updates to all connected WebSocket clients; Svelte map renders buoy markers colored by current SST.
previous_plan.data_model: sensor_reading hypertable and ocean_buoy table; no continuous aggregates defined.

target_output.thinking_summary: Resolving ISS-001 by redesigning the WebSocket ConnectionManager to use per-buoy subscription routing (Dict[str, Set[WebSocket]]) so researchers only receive updates for buoys they have subscribed to. Also closing ISS-002 by specifying the TimescaleDB continuous aggregate cagg_hourly_avg for historical chart performance. Plan quality is intentionally weak per manifest - thin on phased_implementation and development_guidelines.

fix_report.action_taken: Redesigned ConnectionManager to Dict[buoy_id, Set[WebSocket]] with explicit subscribe/unsubscribe protocol over the WebSocket message channel. MQTT ingest now calls manager.broadcast(buoy_id, payload) which fans out only to subscribed sessions.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Each researcher session receives only telemetry for buoys they have explicitly subscribed to, eliminating N*M broadcast overhead.

fix_report.action_taken: Added TimescaleDB continuous aggregate cagg_hourly_avg materialising mean temperature_celsius and microplastic_ppm per buoy per hour, refreshed every 15 minutes. Historical chart endpoint now queries cagg_hourly_avg for windows beyond 24h.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Historical chart queries for 30-day windows return in under 200ms by scanning pre-aggregated hourly rows instead of raw hypertable chunks.

target_output.title: Ocean Buoy Telemetry Live Dashboard
target_output.executive_summary: A Python FastAPI WebSocket backend ingesting MQTT telemetry from ocean buoys into TimescaleDB, serving a Svelte 4 live map dashboard to marine scientists tracking sea surface temperature anomalies and microplastic concentration drift. Deployed on Hetzner bare-metal via Docker Compose within a EUR 200/month research budget.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-server architecture on Hetzner AX41 running Docker Compose: Mosquitto MQTT broker receives buoy telemetry on topic buoys/+/telemetry, aiomqtt subscriber in FastAPI writes to TimescaleDB hypertable and routes live updates via a per-buoy WebSocket ConnectionManager. nginx terminates HTTPS and proxies to FastAPI on port 8000. Svelte 4 SPA connects to WebSocket endpoints for subscribed buoys and queries REST endpoints for historical charts using TimescaleDB continuous aggregates.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Svelte 4, TypeScript, Leaflet.js for buoy map, Chart.js for SST and microplastic time-series. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, aiomqtt, asyncpg for TimescaleDB writes. Database: TimescaleDB 2.x on PostgreSQL 16, continuous aggregate cagg_hourly_avg. Broker: Mosquitto 2.x. Infrastructure: Docker Compose, nginx with self-signed cert, Prometheus + Grafana, GitHub Actions CI.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Live Buoy Map: Leaflet map rendering each buoy as a marker colored by SST range; updates every 5s from WebSocket. SST and Microplastic Charts: Chart.js time-series for selected buoy, querying cagg_hourly_avg for windows over 24h. Anomaly Alerting: asyncio task evaluating sensor_reading against alert_threshold rows every 60s. Historical Playback: Slider-based 30-day playback using cagg_hourly_avg data.
target_output.system_components: Mosquitto MQTT broker handling buoy connections on port 1883. FastAPI app with aiomqtt background subscriber, ConnectionManager (Dict[buoy_id, Set[WebSocket]]), REST routers for buoy metadata and historical queries, anomaly checker asyncio task. TimescaleDB instance with sensor_reading hypertable and cagg_hourly_avg continuous aggregate. nginx reverse proxy enforcing HTTPS. Svelte 4 SPA served as static assets. Prometheus + node-exporter + Grafana for infrastructure observability.
target_output.workflows: Telemetry Ingest: Buoy transmits JSON payload to MQTT topic buoys/{buoy_code}/telemetry every 60s; aiomqtt subscriber parses payload, writes sensor_reading row via asyncpg, calls ConnectionManager.broadcast(buoy_id, payload) to push to subscribed WebSocket sessions only. Anomaly Detection: asyncio task queries latest sensor_reading per buoy every 60s, compares against alert_threshold rows; on breach emits alert event to subscribed sessions and inserts temperature_anomaly record.
target_output.data_model: ocean_buoy (id UUID PK, buoy_code VARCHAR UNIQUE, lat DECIMAL, lon DECIMAL, deployment_date DATE, status ENUM active/inactive/maintenance). sensor_reading (time TIMESTAMPTZ, buoy_id UUID, temperature_celsius FLOAT8, salinity_ppt FLOAT8, microplastic_ppm FLOAT8, battery_voltage FLOAT4) hypertable partitioned weekly. cagg_hourly_avg continuous aggregate materialising avg(temperature_celsius) and avg(microplastic_ppm) per buoy per hour, refresh policy every 15 minutes. alert_threshold (id UUID, buoy_id UUID, metric VARCHAR, threshold_value FLOAT8, direction ENUM above/below). temperature_anomaly (id UUID, buoy_id UUID, observed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, peak_celsius FLOAT8, duration_minutes INT).
target_output.api_design: GET /api/buoys - list all buoys with latest SST. GET /api/buoys/{id}/history?metric=temperature_celsius&from=2026-01-01&to=2026-02-01 - queries cagg_hourly_avg for windows over 24h. GET /api/anomalies?buoy_id={id}&limit=50 - recent anomaly log. WebSocket /ws/buoys - client sends subscribe message with buoy_ids array; server streams sensor_reading payloads only for subscribed buoys. GET /api/thresholds and PUT /api/thresholds/{id} for researcher threshold configuration.
target_output.security_and_compliance: nginx enforces HTTPS with self-signed certificate on intranet; direct port 8000 blocked by iptables. nginx rate-limit on WebSocket upgrade: 10 concurrent connections per source IP. No authentication for prototype phase per contract. MQTT broker restricted to Docker internal network; buoy devices connect via dedicated VLAN not exposed to researcher LAN.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Docker Compose services: mosquitto, api (FastAPI), timescaledb, nginx, grafana, prometheus, node-exporter. GitHub Actions CI runs ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with timescaledb Docker service container. Merge to main triggers SSH deploy: docker compose pull and docker compose up -d on Hetzner AX41. TimescaleDB data volume mounted to /data/timescaledb on host for persistence across container restarts.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes FastAPI /metrics via prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator (request count, latency histograms). Custom Prometheus gauge for active_websocket_connections per buoy_id. Grafana dashboards: MQTT message rate per buoy, WebSocket active subscriptions, TimescaleDB insert latency p99, disk usage percent.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Hetzner AX41 dedicated at EUR 49/month fits under EUR 200 budget. At 50 buoys transmitting every 60s, write throughput is ~0.8 rows/s. TimescaleDB compression reduces storage by ~10x on chunks older than 30 days.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (6 weeks): MQTT ingest, TimescaleDB hypertable, Svelte live map, WebSocket ConnectionManager with per-buoy routing, anomaly alerting. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Historical playback using cagg_hourly_avg, threshold configuration UI. Phase 3 (3 weeks): Microplastic drift vector overlay using windowed spatial aggregation, exportable CSV/NetCDF reports for peer publication, and user-defined threshold presets saved per buoy cluster.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: ruff linting, mypy strict mode, pytest for API routes with TimescaleDB test fixture. Svelte: TypeScript strict, Vitest for store logic. TimescaleDB hypertable partitioning verified in CI with a pg_dump fixture. Svelte component props typed via generated TypeScript interfaces from Zod schemas shared with the FastAPI response models to keep the data contract single-source-of-truth.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Single Hetzner server is a single point of failure for research data. Mitigation: daily pg_dump to offsite S3-compatible storage. Tradeoff: Mosquitto over EMQX chosen for simplicity at 50-buoy scale. Risk: cagg_hourly_avg refresh lag of 15 minutes means the most recent hour is always queried from raw hypertable rows.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Mosquitto selected over EMQX for buoy count under 200. HTTPS self-signed cert accepted for intranet prototype. cagg_hourly_avg refresh interval set to 15 minutes balancing query freshness and background load.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision resolving WebSocket broadcast ISS-001 and TimescaleDB continuous aggregate ISS-002 for ocean buoy telemetry research prototype.
